### PaulG — 2014-03-07T03:01:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-chronic-fatigue-syndrome-cfs-myalgic-encephalomyelitis-me-thread.10052/post-113881>

There seems to be a few people on this forum who have CFS/ME. A quick search of the forums has revealed some posts but I thought it might be good to have a thread of useful resources and Jack Kruse inspired thoughts/truth bombs.

I will kick off this as I found some amazingly honest, funny and intelligent videos by [Howard Bloom](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Howard_Bloom)

Bloom was diagnoses with CFS since 1988 and worked with musicians such as Michael Jackson and Prince but has since conquered his CFS after 15 years being trapped in a bedroom. His is now able to do between 360 and 520 pushups without stopping, He walks 3.5 miles a day and gives lectures in Moscow, Amsterdam, Paris, Kuala Lumpur, Seoul, and Chengdu

He recorded a number of video interviews about his CFS
[http://www.healtheo360.com/stories/chapter-1-the-origins-of-howard-bloom/#.UxkFWH8aySN](http://www.healtheo360.com/stories/chapter-1-the-origins-of-howard-bloom/#.UxkFWH8aySN)

Also the [Phoenix Rising forum](http://forums.phoenixrising.me/index.php) has much useful information

There is also a new film called ["Canary in the Coalmine](http://www.canaryinacoalminefilm.com/)" that is looking for support/funding.

I have also used the Poll feature of the new forum to quantify (privately) people's improvements or not.

### Martin — 2014-11-21T15:47:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-chronic-fatigue-syndrome-cfs-myalgic-encephalomyelitis-me-thread.10052/post-148072>

Some Dr. Kruse quotes that I've collected...
_(emphasis mine)_

"The more omega 6 's in your Cell Membranes (CM) the more GABA you need to allow the brain to work. The more DHA in CM and inner mitochondrial membrane (MINOS) the less GABA you need. People with poor thinking, mental illness, Eating Disorders, MS, AI's, mycoplasma, and **CFS** have moved away from a DHA laden template. Same is true for those with **chronic pain** and sleep apnea. This is why opiate addiction is associated with excessive O6/O3 ratio's. No resolvins and protectins from DHA to blunt the pain........O6's magnify the the pain response because their breakdown products are all tied to excessive creation of inflammatory prostaglandins in the face of no resolvins and protectins from DHA in cell membranes. [http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/8397297](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/8397297) "

"You also need DHA in those circuits to capture electrons and build up working memory to move electrons to and fro. This is why so many people complain of a cognitive haze with AF, menopause, and **CFS.** Then you need to be able to move those electrons in that neural pathway to create consciousness. Consciousness is not all or none.......it is a continuum tied to electron collection and flow in these circuits."

"Why does cortisol help AI folks for s short time? Cortisol removes electrons from the mix so it stops immune activation of the T regulator cells in the local immune reactions. This is the fundamental physical break in AI disease as a common tie. Get your microbiome tested I bet you see it simplified and altered. I'd be surprised if you don't have dysbiosis. **The cause is likely Calcium efflux** from your environment. Most cases are. Every study on Autoimmune disease **(including fibro)** that has investigated dysbiosis and leaky gut has found it."

"@Christy I cant practice medicine on here but the treatment hack I use for **Fibromyalgia** is among the most extensive I do and it requires 18-36 months to reverse. It is a complete system failure and dosing is all custom done to blood work."

**"Let’s talk about chronic fatigue patients.** Do they suffer from the same condition but on a more subacute level? Yes they do. Patients with **ME/CFS** patients usually have dramatic increases than normal levels of ventricular lactate in the CSF, and 36% less GSH in the cortical regions of the brain. The cortex is bathed in surface CSF that usually is not able to “proton superconduct” for many reasons. Fluoride is a dielectric blocker in CSF. The industrial omega 6 PUFA fats in the surface of their cell membranes in their cortical neurons are the most common reasons for avalanche collapse of proton super conduction in “CSF water” by displacing iodine and causing an up regulation of lactate production to use as a fuel when ketones are not present for energy production to make maximum ATP. When we have an energy inefficiency in the brain, we get neurodegenerative disorders soon following. This is why Alzhemer’s disease and metabolic syndrome are bedfellows. It is also why so many **fibromyalgia** and fatigue patients also have similar phenotypes.

Neurons in the CNS and PNS **love lactate,** especially when they are suboptimal in ATP production or suffering from a neurodegenerative disease. Neurons become inefficient because of poor mitochondrial function due to lowered ATP production. This poor ATP recycling is the signal to the neurons and the brain mitochondria that they are trying to increase proton superconduction in CSF (density of CSF water) to improve CMRO2 and the current in the brain’s other semiconductor, water. The higher CMRO2 is, the less the brain relies on ATP and the more it uses water superconduction. It is like a high octane fuel for neurons. Patients with **ME/CFS** are very energy inefficient and subsequently they have high lactate levels in their CSF because they ARE energy inefficient and poor ATP recyclers. The lactate supply in the brain is made by up-regulation of MCT1 which is called mono carboxylic acid transporter 1. People with **ME/CFS generally generate a lot of superoxide in the brain** as the cause of their disease and not being able to make a lot of ATP and this is why they eat way to many carbs to fast recycle ATP. This never allows them to open their proteins fully to open the maximum amounts of water binding sites to foster water/proton super conduction.

Neurons in the brain try to avoid superoxide generation at all costs and neurons usually attempt to increase lactate production as a consequence to offset superoxide generation to use lactate as a secondary fuel source to ketones. Most **people with ME/CFS do not use ketones** for electrons along their inner mitochondrial membrane for ECT; **instead they use glucose** for fuels because they are no longer metabolically flexible due to the defects of protein folding at cytochrome 1 (NADH) because of what I wrote about in [EMF 4](http://www.jackkruse.com/emf-4-why-might-you-need-carbs-for-performance/). They **use too much complex one** because of glucose and glutamate fuels, and they try to run their mitochondria on NADH complex one. It can lead to mis-folding if they cannot make enough ATP to complete the task. When this fails, you activate apoptosis pathway and some of the things I spoke about in the recent [webinar](http://www.jackkruse.com/april-webinar-2013/) I did.

When you have metabolic syndrome you really want to access ECT at FADH2, or complex two in electron chain transport on the inner mitochondrial membrane instead using FFA or the ketogenic diet to bypass this issue in these diseases. When one fuels their diet with glucose/carbs, you are increasing ROS and superoxide as a consequence. The more you use these fuels, the more you increase ROS and superoxide and limit your ability to make ATP. I covered this in big detail in [EMF 4](http://www.jackkruse.com/emf-4-why-might-you-need-carbs-for-performance/).

_**(note to self: check out EMF 4)**_

**Electron dense foodstuffs** supply the best possible neuronal FADH2:NADH (F/N) ratio of 0.2 to minimize ROS and superoxide generation. Moreover, this is why this pathway of electrons gives one FADH2 for 5 NADHs when you do this to minimize superoxide maximally. I talked about this very issue in the [quantum electron blog post](http://www.jackkruse.com/do-food-electrons-impart-a-quantum-effect/) over two years ago. The more NADH you make the higher lactate will be made to offset the glucose in the brain. This shows you why the brain really **does not like nor need a lot of glucose** as a first line fuel contrary to what most believe or regurgitate. Ketones and lactate support fat metabolism optimally in neuronal use from foods/fuels to protect the brain."

### Martin — 2014-11-26T19:47:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-chronic-fatigue-syndrome-cfs-myalgic-encephalomyelitis-me-thread.10052/post-148578>

**Onset**

_"The majority of CFS cases **start suddenly**,\[34\] usually accompanied by a "[**flu-like illness**](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Flu-like_illness)"\[5\] while a significant proportion of cases begin within several months of severe adverse stress.\[34\]\[35\]\[36\] An Australian prospective study found that after infection by viral and non-viral [pathogens](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pathogen), a sub-set of individuals met the criteria for CFS, with the researchers concluding that "post-infective fatigue syndrome is a valid illness model for investigating one pathophysiological pathway to CFS".\[37\] However, accurate prevalence and exact roles of infection and stress in the development of CFS are currently unknown.."_

I'm a little embarrassed.

I missed the connection as to why my problems came on _suddenly_.

When I crashed over five years ago, it started me on a research journey that brought me to know so many fundamental things about health and this site is far and away the most valuable. I knew one of the keys was the fact that one week I was doing very well, and the next, very poorly.

How did I miss it? Poor search terms? Assumptions about "chronic fatigue" and "fibro" that stopped me searching because those are such popular catch-all terms?

I went in another direction at first, thinking it was Lyme's, then discovered the mold connection, and began to think that biotoxin illness was at the root of my sickness.

I mentioned that "sudden" event it to all my docs, but it raised no discussion about it having precipitated my CFS/ME/MCS. I had to start somewhere so I went going after mold after the HLA DR results came back and some of Shoemaker's tests showing elevated results that indicated mold, but some of the markers were negative. Remediated my home at great expense. Changed my habits. Took CSM for five months with no change in symptoms. Went down the methylation trail. Not much difference, but some.

It's all good, but it still _nagged_ at me that that viral event caused such a huge drop in the quality of my life. While searching one day last week I came across "post-exertional malaise" and "80% of CFS patients had flu-like illness", and I was stunned at how a simple wikipedia entry described my experience almost perfectly. I'm beginning to think that mold, methylation, SNP's, et al, are _aggravated_ by this fall off the cliff, not the cause, per se.

My curiosity about how things fail has been with me since I was a child, and I love to learn how things work and how to fix them. I'm looking for a pathophysiological reason why a virus would hammer someone **_so suddenly_**. Huge methylation crash? I'd welcome any thoughts on this.

NSH

Martin, have you tries autovaccines? I have met quite a number of people who saw great results with this very old approach. Mainly viral diseases such as eppstein-baar virus etc, obviously chronic stage issues like CFS and fibro. My doctor is now using it for chronic lyme s well. Some really bad cases and the response is there and the change of direction slowly follows the response. Youcan do it at home at almost no costs and longterm. My doctor uses 5ml of blood, freeze it for 5 min to desintegrate the cells and inject it as intramuscular injection. She does regimen every 2-3 days and combines it case depending with herbs. as said, it is a very old approach, proven by centuries. My exboss daughter contracted in India some unknown infection and spent 5 years with her body covered in abscesses, quite terrible case of very young bright lady. She finally found her cure in autovaccines. I am wondering myself how it happened I forgot abut this case when I got sick. I cannot offer personal experience because I got pregnant when i was about to start, but it is on my list as soon as I will be in condition and situation.

# Thread: Reductive science?  Reductio ad absurdum
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/reductive-science-reductio-ad-absurdum.10053/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Gagnrad — 2014-03-07T08:38:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/reductive-science-reductio-ad-absurdum.10053/post-113944>

Here's an example of the scientific method gone mad.

You start from the reasonable premise that it's best to try to limit variables; you pursue that to the point of absurdity; you end up with a totally artificial result that tells you nothing useful.

I got this one from Peter's blog:

[http://high-fat-nutrition.blogspot.co.uk/2014/03/would-you-like-soya-oil-poured-over.html](http://high-fat-nutrition.blogspot.co.uk/2014/03/would-you-like-soya-oil-poured-over.html)

As he puts it:

> ... the same Aussie pile of wallanga pertaining to report science

I think we've got a malapropism there, and by "pertaining" he means "purporting". But never mind, I haven't Peter's skills: I can spot that kind of thing, but I can't do something as complex as operate on a cat with appendicitis. And we get the drift.

Why is it a pile of codswallop? Well the the media story he links:

[http://mobile.news.com.au/national/...s-bad-as-smoking/story-e6frfku9-1226845436762](http://mobile.news.com.au/national/breaking-news/protein-diets-nearly-as-bad-as-smoking/story-e6frfku9-1226845436762)

says:

> They tested 25 different diet combinations on 900 mice to see what happened to their appetite, metabolic health, ageing and lifespan.

Uh, uh. It _wasn't_ just "mice". Peter looked it up and it turns out they used:

> the standard crippled C57BL/6 mouse strain.

I seem to recall the notorious T. Colin Campbell has pulled the trick of swapping out the mice strains when he doesn't get the result he wants. Here they've cut straight to the chase and started with a crippled strain. Not only is this mice not humans, _it's not even normal mice_.

So what did they do. In Peter's words:

> They fed umpteen macronutrient ratios to umpteen mice and looked at the longevity.

Here's where it gets interesting -- and here's what the Australian news report _doesn't_ tell you. This special crippled strain of mice can't eat saturated fat: it "breaks their brains". No problem …

> A brief trip to supplementary data gives what the diets were made of:
>
> "Diets varied in content of P (casein and methionine), C (sucrose, wheatstarch and dextrinized cornstarch) and F (soya bean oil)".

In fine, the study tells us nothing about humans; it wasn't even done with normal mice; they weren't even fed a normal diet. Who among us breakfasts on a mixture of casein, sucrose, and soya oil?

OK, they tested 25 different macronutrient ratios in their _reductio ad absurdum_ experiment, but they might as well have not bothered.

Final quote from Peter:

> DO NOT CONSUME BULK CALORIES AS PUFA, ESPECIALLY OMEGA-6 PUFA.

Final quote from the news source:

> "\[This study\] is an enormous leap in our understanding of the impact of diet quality and diet balance on food intake, health, ageing and longevity," Prof Simpson says.

An enormous leap? What utter nonsense! I think people in the biological sciences really must be out to make themselves a laughing stock among real scientists.

# Thread: Ketone Testing
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ketone-testing.10058/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### healthman — 2014-03-07T13:57:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ketone-testing.10058/post-113979>

Hi
I have started testing my Ketone levels first thing in the morning but I keep getting negative results? I have tried various times of the day still negative? Does anyone have any idea why I am not going into ketosis? I hardly eat any carbs and have fat for breakfast & dinner and seafood for lunch?

### nicld — 2014-03-07T20:52:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ketone-testing.10058/post-114032>

Good question, I have the same issue.

From some of the research that I have done, you hydration level has some effect. If you drink as much water as Dr. K recommends, one might now see it. There are some times when I get it to register small on the sticks but that is rare.

I did see in my GI tests that I have poor fat absorption so I am wondering if that is having an effect on the results too and have methylation issues too.

Hope other people chime in.

Nicole

### cantweight — 2014-03-08T04:00:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ketone-testing.10058/post-114093>

Are you using the keto urine stix? I find them really unreliable. I have the opposite issue. Shows me high in ketones after just a day or two and even a high carb mishap day does not knock me out of ketosis according to the strips.
Ketone blood test would be more accurate...but the test stick things are pricey!

### healthman — 2014-03-08T20:31:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ketone-testing.10058/post-114183>

Hi cantweight
Thanks for the reply
Yes have been using the keto stix?
Mine are showing nothing whatsoever no matter what time if day I use them!
Do you think I may not be absorbing fat very well and this could be the reason why?

Thanks

### Cristi Vlad — 2014-03-16T21:45:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ketone-testing.10058/post-115825>

when did you start the keto diet?

### seanb4 — 2014-03-07T14:05:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnet-therapy-applied-to-stomach.10059/post-113980>

Hi guys,

I have had some success with using the magnetico sleep pad for my issues. In particular I have noticed better sleep, increased energy and lower heart palpitations. This has confirmed for me that the use of a magnetic field to the body can be beneficial.

The same people that make the magnetico also are looking into using real strong magnets to parts of the body to increase healing ([http://www.amri-intl.com/](http://www.amri-intl.com/)). I think this works by vastly increasing the DC current in the specific area.

I'm wondering if anyone knows more information about this. I'm hopeful that maybe if this real strong magnet is applied to my stomach then it will allow my body to heal itself. This treatment sounds unavailable and probably real expensive however.

Do you think it will work, what's your opinion on it?

### Tanya — 2014-03-07T17:49:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnet-therapy-applied-to-stomach.10059/post-114007>

Sean, I don't have any information about it, but they are using similar to the MRI technology, except that you will not need to have a dye contrast for that. So, if you can afford it (I think, you are right to assume that it will be expensive), I would go for it. I find magnet therapy very helpful, but this would take it on a so much higher level.

### seanb4 — 2014-03-07T18:27:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnet-therapy-applied-to-stomach.10059/post-114012>

It's definitely something to keep my eye on for the future. I'm just wondering how legitimate the theory is. Will using 5000 gauss actually provide 5000x the benefit of 1 gauss or is there a cut off point?

I also need to wait for the results of the preliminary tests they are running.

Does anyone know any other similar treatments that are available now?

@[Tanya](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/6641/) You say you find magnet therapy helpful. How as it helped you, and is it just the magnetico or are you using other techniques?

### Tanya — 2014-03-07T19:27:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnet-therapy-applied-to-stomach.10059/post-114017>

> [seanb4 said:](/goto/post?id=114012)
>
> @[Tanya](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/6641/) You say you find magnet therapy helpful. How as it helped you, and is it just the magnetico or are you using other techniques?

Sean, i found magnets to be extremely effective for my pain resulting from a severe RA. I was not taking any pharmaceuticals for it, including any pain killers. I was suffering from a lot of structural damage at that time and could not walk. I was doing many other things, but I relied mostly on magnets to control my pain. That was the time before I learned about earthing. I noticed that magnetico was making it somewhat easier for me during the night, but it was still not enough. So, I started placing individual magnets on all of my injured areas and found a significant pain relief that way. Made a believer out of me, for sure.

### Tanya — 2014-03-07T19:50:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnet-therapy-applied-to-stomach.10059/post-114020>

Magnets have been used with great effect over acupuncture points. In fact, that is how I came to learn about their use while having the acupuncture treatment. I replaced acupuncture with my own magnet therapy, and it worked. No worries whatsoever, just had to keep in mind that the negative poll should always face the body and I was on my way....

### Josh — 2014-03-08T18:19:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnet-therapy-applied-to-stomach.10059/post-114162>

I will attempt not to shoot myself in the foot here...but it will probably happen anyway...

1.  Magnetico is a broad treatment for moving and balancing qi/energy. Something like needling many points on the body...It is sophisticated in how it does this, but subject to the same limitations and issues that challenge one with all broad treatments. It is all in the physics. Essentially when you move qi/energy, you need energy...think redox potential. So if one has a functioning water battery with near 100% efficiency that will supply the energy, everything may go "well". This will mean different things for different people based on how they have framed the experiment/treatment...all treatment is an "experiment" because as we know, N=1. In addition, the Magnetico seems to facilitate the functioning of people's "water batteries" to a point...
2.  There are issues with broadly moving qi/energy; such as running out of qi/energy, depleting it in the weaker organ systems, blocked areas-think collapsed matted collagen that will not conduct piezoelectric current or support nerve conduction or worse, and there is the perennial question of why isn't the body doing this itself??????
3.  So why would one have hypofunction in an organ system in the digestive tract? Vagal tone perhaps? Cerebellar degeneration due to a factor such as GAD65 antibodies that are triggered by gluten and things that cross react with gluten such as caesin and others? Cerebellar degeneration=decreased vagal tone due to lower Purkinje cell generation of piezo electricity for vagal tone/stimulation that triggers peristalsis in the stomach fro example? This is not a complete model as I am not a neurosurgeon...
4.  There are no generalizations about treatment in Chinese/Oriental Medicine because it is an N=1 system. I cringe every time I hear a Western trained scientist/doctor or anyone else make general statements about treatments because the most interesting thing about the system is its N=1 approach within a sophisticated conceptual framework (s). One might say that "generally a magnet of such and such strength applied to an individual with the following characteristics in such and such an environment at such and such time of year might be governed by the following parameters..." I will not even get into the possible politics and cultural issues about why a Chinese practitioner at a conference in Europe may or may not have made the statement the Dr. Bonlie quoted...
5.  Practically, Oriental Medicine has a lot to offer people with issues such as these. Typically points distal to the area affected are treated. There are also subsystems on the hands, face, head, feet and ears that map the whole body and can treat with varying efficacy...think the holographic stuff in the 2014 Q&A if you have heard it...Magnets would work to some extent in all of these systems and I have used them with varying efficacy along with all of the other ways of "stimulating" a point which is probably a vortex at a junction in the semiconducting collagen matrix that Dr. Kruse is illuminating so nicely for all of us...I am learning right along with everyone else...I have so much "phenomena" to explain...it is not funny...
6.  The famous example that is similar to this issue that is taught about the history of what we might call "modern" Oriental Medicine comes from the 1950's when a condition was presented to the allopathic and oriental practitioners in China for treatment. The story goes (I will call it a "story" because I was not there) that uterine prolapse was the condition and each group presented their treatment and results. The allopathic community returned with surgical approaches that bound the prolapsed organ up in more or less the proper place. This has longterm negative repercussion for health and does not fix the root of the problem which is "why can't the body just hold the organ in the right place and how does it do that anyway?" The oriental practitioners said we strengthen and balance the systems that support the function of the organ and hold it in place and the body pulls it back up by itself. Their demonstration of this was so effective that many allopathic doctors reputedly switched over and or at least embraced traditional treatment. We can now translate this as they restored the redox potential and the ability to maintain it so that the body could activate the muscles and possibly it is really the hydrated 3D collagen matrix to restore the position and function of the uterus. Doctors are still sewing uterus's and bladders back in place (think Da Vinci Robot) like crazy and oriental and functional medicine practitiners are still having decent results with compliant patients getting them to go back on their own. In comparison, improving peristalsis is easier.

I have probably said too much already, but the wind is blowing with a warm sun and it is spring and the yang is rising disturbing my shen and overacting on my stomach making it harder for me to descend my food in the proper manner (this model is a few thousand year old...) and making me a little "hutu" so take what you like and leave the rest.....

### seanb4 — 2014-03-08T18:42:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnet-therapy-applied-to-stomach.10059/post-114164>

Okay so the essence of what you are saying, Josh, is that you can't make the blanket statement that using individual magnets is bad for more than an hour per week because for some people it might be good, you need to try it for yourself.

Whilst I do agree with this on some level, it also concerns me that there is a logical reason / theory why return field might be bad and you can explain people get good results as short term results.

Whilst it is possible that Bonlie could be "making it up" or wrong to sell his sleep pads over cheaper versions, why would Jack back him up on this. Jack could be wrong too but he's been right on most things so far.

I just don't want to spend my money on something that has a decent chance of hurting me in the long run.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnet-therapy-applied-to-stomach.10059/post-114178>

1.  No "good or bad", only "heaven or hell" based on your N=1. I have heard stories of people who drank poison such as phenol or bleach and it caused such a cathartic detox that it reset their entire system and cleared up major dis harmonies changing their lives.

    > “_**It is the dose that makes the poison.**_”

    One of the semi-mythical origin of acupuncture stories is that someone got speared or shot with an arrow and it "cured" their sore back. Someone noticed it and started treating people with sharp instruments in similar places and it evolved from there...
2.  The Magnetico is a well though out and constructed device. My dad and stepmother sleep on one and have talked with Dr. Bonlie. Their N=1's have given them different experiences as I would have predicted...
3.  Magnet therapy is not a small subject, the truth is what works for your goals and purposes now...based on what you understand now....subject to continued inquiry and refinement...
4.  For me, the fundamental question for "health" is understanding, measuring and perceiving the difference between increasing/improving my "resonance/qi/redox potential" and sensations and experiences from things that change these but do not maintain or increase/improve them....

### Tanya — 2014-03-09T19:27:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnet-therapy-applied-to-stomach.10059/post-114338>

Audrey, I don’t know how much more is there really to say about it, but I will try.

First of all, I should emphasize that the magnet therapy was only a part, albeit a significant part of my entire treatment process.

As you already know, there was a time when I could not even walk anymore because of all the irreparable damage due to my RA and I already had a magnetic pad

My luck began to change when someone mentioned to me about one chinese man who was able to help some people with very serious health problems. Actually, at first, I was rather skeptical about it, because I have already tried acupuncture several times before from a few different practitioners (2 of them also being chinese) and it was not helping me. But after we were told that he and his family had plans to move to CA, I decided that I should give it a try before it is too late.

It was not your typical health provider office (he had a massage table right in the middle of his living room), this older man and his wife were one of the nicest and genuine people you would ever hope to meet.

Even though he did not speak much English, I could tell right away how experienced and knowledgeable he was. I was getting so much from him while his fee was so little (he would not take any extra money when I tried to give it to him). I probably would still be in that wheelchair, if it would not have been for him and I was so upset when it came time for them to move - I was afraid that I will loose all that progress that I have made. Well, before they left, he had a present for me - a set of magnets. He showed me the points where I needed to place them and told me to continue massaging and exercising as he already taught me to do so.

That is exactly what I’ve been doing since then. I have purchased some more magnets and books about the magnet therapy since then. These magnets are such a small investment to make when you are getting so much more back from them and the books will describe the magnet therapy way better then I ever could do it justice....

> [seanb4 said:](/goto/post?id=114363)
>
> How many months did you use the spot magnets for?
>
> How long before you were able to comfortably walk again?

Sean, I am still not walking as comfortably as I would have liked - slight limp still remains, but I continue working on it.

I was in a wheelchair for several long month before I started improving and moved to using a walker and later a cane. Now all of that equipment is in the basement...

I have a structural damage in both of my ankles with no hope for it being repaired, I was told. They say that the body cannot repair the damaged cartilage. I might need to get another MRI done (have not had one since the first ones showed all the damage) to see what is happening there to explain that I am walking so well. Knowing now what I do about Robert O. Becker’s experiments, I really wonder how accurate all these doctors’ assessments have been.....

### Josh — 2014-03-09T23:21:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnet-therapy-applied-to-stomach.10059/post-114384>

Inspiring story, thank you.

### seanb4 — 2014-03-10T09:06:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnet-therapy-applied-to-stomach.10059/post-114484>

Cool, I hope that the MRI shows it is growing back!

### caroline — 2014-03-10T09:43:42+0200

Inspiring indeed ...thanks Tanya for sharing that. I suppose because he didn't speak much English - he couldn't really explain much? where did he go?

You are one determined women .... but there really is no other option is there.....

I love what Mr. Pinkies says \[Kate} Impossible is just an option! Have you read her journal?

### caroline — 2014-03-10T09:28:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/this-is-cool.10060/post-114487>

but he isn't smiling!! what's up with that? maybe his budgie smugglers are too tight?

### Gagnrad — 2014-03-10T11:34:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/this-is-cool.10060/post-114506>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=114487)
>
> but he isn't smiling!! what's up with that?

Looking into the sun, isn't he? It's making him screw his eyes up, and I guess that affects his expression. Since he wants the background ...

But you can get nice portraits taken into the sun, if it's not too bright and you use fill-in flash.

### caroline — 2014-03-10T11:50:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/this-is-cool.10060/post-114507>

you seem to know a little about everything..... how the heck do you do that?????

I gotta go to bed!

### Gagnrad — 2014-03-10T12:26:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/this-is-cool.10060/post-114512>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=114507)
>
> you seem to know a little about everything..... how the heck do you do that?????

The occasional photography book -- and just noticing things. Just seeing the eyes. AFAIK, photographers would often take a model and put her the other way round with her back to the sun. Now she won't screw her eyes up, but her face might be rather in shadow, so you throw a little light back the other way. Either fill-in flash, or an assistant holding up a reflector -- even a white sheet or a newspaper.

Talking of noticing things, this should amuse you. I'm just reading Eric Newby's _Love and War in the Apennines_ about when he was hiding from the Germans in Italy during WWII. (Very enjoyable, and said to be his masterpiece.) Newby said it was very much harder to escape in Italy than in Germany. This wasn't because the Italians were keen on the War -- far from it, many helped Allied troops stranded behind the lines. It was because Italians were very observant and very interested in clothes and fashions. A POW could saunter past Germans dressed in a "civilian coat" he'd made from a blanket but you'd never be able to get away with that with Italians.

> I gotta go to bed!

You've been up all night? You'd best do that!

### caroline — 2014-03-10T23:52:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/this-is-cool.10060/post-114613>

That is very funny about Italians and clothing! I always love all your observations........

# Thread: OMG my Hair!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/omg-my-hair.10063/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 38
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Shijin13 — 2014-03-07T21:46:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/omg-my-hair.10063/post-114037>

I'm sure many of you have a history of dull lifeless hair. I know I do! Add to it, I always had horrible cystic acne on my scalp. Fast forward to last fall when I went searching through a great thread (For some reason I can't find it!) but it was on optimal beauty products. I believe Fitness@home mentioned using Morroco method products. I'd used them in the past w/o great success...

we'll I've been using them for the past 8mths. my cystic acne is gone. I can get away with washing my baby fine hair once a week.

Yesterday instead of washing my hair I decided to do a hair mask, I added some lavender and tea tree oil to the zen detox last night instead of my usual. put it on, covered my hair w/plastic wrap, then wrapped a towel around my head. rinsed it out an hour later.... very few tangles (I get tangles in my hair no matter how often I wash/condition regardless of the product I use) there are times I want to take clippers to my hair and shave it all off...

today I woke up and my hair had this amazing wave in it. The henna I use to color my greys - is rich and deep. OMG what Happen? I have sex kitten hair!!!! WOOT!

### Tanya — 2014-03-07T22:22:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/omg-my-hair.10063/post-114040>

Gretchen, that is a great news - I am happy for you. I went to Morroco's website and there is, of course a choice of products... Is there a particular one that you liked better then the other? As far as coloring your greys - hmmmm, you don't look old enough to have any

### Shijin13 — 2014-03-07T22:45:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/omg-my-hair.10063/post-114042>

> [Tanya said:](/goto/post?id=114040)
>
> Gretchen, that is a great news - I am happy for you. I went to Morroco's website and there is, of course a choice of products... Is there a particular one that you liked better then the other? As far as coloring your greys - hmmmm, you don't look old enough to have any

Tanya I bought the healthy hair starter pack. One thing I did was mix the shampoos/conditioners with RO water to stretch it further. [https://www.morroccomethod.com/raw-store/packages/healthy-hair-starter-package-detail](https://www.morroccomethod.com/raw-store/packages/healthy-hair-starter-package-detail)

the first time around these products didn't work all that well for me... but then I wasn't paleo either... Its took my scalp about 6 wks to fully transition to the products and get to the point where I could easily wash my hair only 1x/week. I have found that the longer I use the products the more I don't use the conditioner. I will tell you that my favorite shampoo is different from Chaos's based upon the smell....

Since my hair is now past shoulder length, many times I just put it up to keep it out of my way.

I have grey around my entire face - I've been dying it for years. But I went to henna back in the fall and Haven't looked back... beautiful rich color.
here's where I buy it... [http://www.hennaforhair.com/](http://www.hennaforhair.com/)

### Inger — 2014-03-09T09:33:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/omg-my-hair.10063/post-114272>

I use Ghassul.. it is from Marocco too I think. It is meant for washing, hair and body. It is kind of slippery, like soap a little bit. Use it since many years and I love it. My hair gets better and better! I am growing it out now, it already have reached my shoulder and even a bit longer than that. If I feel my hair is too "dry" after washing and the curls are not too organized, I just brush a bit to straighten and spray with rain water. I so love how it makes my hair!

Also a mask once in a while with Silica gel, raw egg yolk, lemon juice and some EVOO makes my hair feel great. I put it on my face too. Raw egg yolk for face is pretty amazing

I wash my hair every other day.

### Shijin13 — 2014-03-11T01:01:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/omg-my-hair.10063/post-114630>

Audrey those morrocco method products are all mud/clay based... they really are lovely... don't know if you can get them down under.....

### caroline — 2014-03-11T01:53:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/omg-my-hair.10063/post-114647>

I suppose you will need to bring me some.........

### Inger — 2014-03-11T09:11:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/omg-my-hair.10063/post-114754>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=114621)
>
> lovely Inger .......I wish I could get my hair longer too but every once in a while it gets so sun damaged and we have to give it the snip!!
> what does the mud do for your hair? what products would you suggest I start with?

I would suggest, just start washing with ghassul and then do a mask with silica, egg yolk, EVOO and lemon juice before washing once in a while... and spray your hair with rainwater often and rinse it in rainwater or soft water from a river or lake or even well/tap if it is soft (hard water does not work..) and then just let it grow

You will need some transition time to get used to the ghassul I think. I will send you some to try, just PM or mail me your address, ok! It is very cheap so I would gladly send you some! Sure you will find it is Australia too if you search around, it can be spelled Rhassoul too I have seen.

The mud makes my hair feel thicker, and it just have made it healthier over all. I used to use loads of products and sprays to keep my hair looking "normal"... that is how thin it is.
If you feel the sun damages your hair just do masks more often in summer  You might try out different kind of home made masks that suits your hair the best but the mask mentioned above is great for me and I think we have pretty same kind of hair....
You could also use a at to cover your hair from the sun when it is strongest/at the beach. But I do not do that I like how the sun bleaches my hair

Funny, I still have days when my hair looks better or worse... must be the hormones or hydration level?

### Lahelada — 2014-03-11T11:35:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/omg-my-hair.10063/post-114776>

If you cannot find that shampoo try looking for soapwort or saponaria oficinalis. You boilit uplike a tea and it gets very slightly foamy. Great for hair.I sold natural shampoo for a while based on this. looking at this thread I wonder what would happen if you added some clay to it.

### kathylu — 2014-03-13T05:57:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/omg-my-hair.10063/post-115123>

Wow...I am still using baking soda in RO water to wash and an AC vinegar rinse. I wash once a week and every other week I use about a teaspoon of baby shampoo before the baking soda. I admit to using about a 1/2 teaspoon conditioner on the lower 2 inches of my hair (I have long hair). That takes care of any tangles. I use Avalon Organics Rosemary conditioner. My hair is better than it has ever been. I really like only needing to shampoo once per week!

# Thread: Piezoluminescent CO???
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/piezoluminescent-co.10073/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### RobH — 2014-03-09T03:16:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/piezoluminescent-co.10073/post-114241>

While digging into my coconut oil jar to brush my teeth last night... in total darkness ... the sun was down after all -- I noticed that mechanically agitating the relatively solid CO (was about 55F in the room) with my toothbrush actually caused a brief brief whitish blue flash.

Did it 5 different times. Very repeatable.

[http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Piezoluminescence](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Piezoluminescence)

It doesn't mention CO ... but I swear it happened. Will try again tonight. I was not intoxicated!

All those electron clouds in the shells of the MCT / Fatty acids actually conduct with mechanical stress, like a piezoelectric crytals, and give of photons perhaps? I'm kind of a QED / QM novice, but it sort of makes sense to me that way.

Anybody else noticed this?

RobH

### nonchalant — 2014-03-11T01:02:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/piezoluminescent-co.10073/post-114631>

Neat, Rob! No, I haven't noticed this, but kudos for not using regular toothpaste, being dark after sunset, and having your room at 55F! ;-)

### Shijin13 — 2014-03-11T01:04:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/piezoluminescent-co.10073/post-114633>

sweet... Now I'm going to have to try to duplicate this... and share it with the kids... they'd totally dig it...

### Jude — 2014-03-11T01:32:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/piezoluminescent-co.10073/post-114641>

I tried it lots last night, in the dark as well, but my co was most probably not firm enough so didn't get that reaction...but will still keep on trying as the days get a little cooler.....

### sjoshua — 2014-03-12T20:27:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/piezoluminescent-co.10073/post-115048>

> [RobH said:](/goto/post?id=114241)

# Thread: Notes from a hospital... "Something in the Air?"
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/notes-from-a-hospital-something-in-the-air.10078/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### NeilBB — 2014-03-09T19:51:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/notes-from-a-hospital-something-in-the-air.10078/post-114342>

Without practicing medicine day-in-and-day-out in the insane environment that we have today in America, you would not fully know just how bad things have gotten and how much worse it's going to be soon, in every way. It's indescribably discouraging from my vantage point. Everyone's only real hope is to absorb what Jack is saying and then to fully take your health into your own hands and to do everything possible to avoid becoming dependent on the medical system, for as long as you possibly can. It doesn't matter how hard it is, how much it costs, or how weird other people think you look. It's what you've absolutely got to do if you want to have a chance. Got this video on in the background, looking for a spark of life somewhere. Seems appropriate...

### prAna303 — 2014-03-09T20:04:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/notes-from-a-hospital-something-in-the-air.10078/post-114348>

I would say that the situation looks very much the same here in Sweden.

When i do my "santas little helper", giving advice on gait and posture related problem, i tend to meet the ones that modern medicin has given up upon. Many times they are broken by a system that is more like shipping gods thru a warehouse aka logistics. Not that i think doctors and their staff is bad, they have such weird work situation today. First stressed and then teached a method that is not working, can you not stress?

The weird things is that i have helped quite a lot of people that is coming back saying i changed their life. Many ask how i see things when i observe their gait for example when doctors do not, i say it is partly because i do not have a doctors education. Nowdays, thanks to Jack and others on this forum i can "cure" many problem related to tendons and other stuff, payback is great.

I do think that many more can help others, it would be great for all parts. Doctors could do what they perhaps should do, not struggling with paperworks and bad science. Everyone must become more willing to learn and try it live.

The funny things is when people ask about my education, they think i joke... Semiconductors, electronics and digital technology.

All of you doctors here, keep on fighting, that is what makes changes.

### Gracelind — 2014-03-09T22:16:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/notes-from-a-hospital-something-in-the-air.10078/post-114378>

Hi Neil

My DIL is a PA in an urban ER in a very rough city. What she sees daily is horrendous. Now with more insurance folks come in to ER with the most minute issues such as corns. They use ambulances like taxies.
They eat SAD with a vengeance. Then they want to be fixed by an ER visit.

She is a gifted healer who I'm gradually bringing around toKruse world. There's a lot of conventional med residue from med school to overcome. Epipaleo eating seems to be key.

We need the enlightened docs and med folk to hang in there and help us survive the current madness.

### Josh — 2014-03-09T23:51:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/notes-from-a-hospital-something-in-the-air.10078/post-114388>

For my 50th birthday, I was just informed by Blue Cross of AZ, I will be blessed with a 25% rate increase, just because....for a policy that gives me no access to basic healthcare. Sometimes I feel that if another healthcare practitioner alternative or allopathic tells me they do not have the time to review Functional Medicine and it is not their responsibility to know this stuff anyway, I will gouge their eyes out.....Traditionally being a healthcare provider/doctor was a lifelong commitment to learning, experimentation and self-cultivation with rewards and frustrations. Two of my heroes are depicted in "4th Uncle in the Mountain". Both he and his father embody this. He lives in Manchester, VT now....

### NeilBB — 2014-03-10T00:42:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/notes-from-a-hospital-something-in-the-air.10078/post-114404>

People need to realize that from the perspective of the modern "healthcare system" (and modern society at large really), we are all considered to be fungible, that is, easily replaced by an "equivalent unit," and therefore not very important as individuals. We are all (doctors and patients alike) cogs in a machine. An enormous, all powerful, malfunctioning, irresponsible machine.

Bottom line is this. If you consider yourself to be important, you have to learn to extricate yourself from the machine whenever and to whatever extent you are able.

Yeah, I've had a pretty rough week at work. But nonetheless, it's true.

### caroline — 2014-03-10T01:45:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/notes-from-a-hospital-something-in-the-air.10078/post-114422>

I have been very lucky to have many caring, wonderful Docs as friends over the years. At least 20 years ago I was talking about this with one of them and asking what to do ....and he told me to make sure I stay well and out of the hospital at all costs! he got it all those years ago! He also said to never leave a loved one alone in a hospital.
I never left my DH's side - I slept in a chair or on the floor or what ever ....they knew I wouldn't leave and so they cared for me too!
I think almost all of you professionals are amazing ....and now that you know better - you are doing better!

### Vinje — 2014-03-09T20:10:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/top-cities-areas-to-live-in.10079/post-114349>

Hey guys, I posted this on the Facebook page but I think I can get more recommendations here.
What would be your guys top ten places to live in the US as far as low emf, quality food, good farming practices, good people ect. Looking to make a move out of my city and into a more biology friendly city. Open to any suggestions. Thanks guys in advance, you all kick ass!

### nicld — 2014-03-10T16:04:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/top-cities-areas-to-live-in.10079/post-114532>

Jack would say where he lives is the best, close to fresh seafood and the whole crater things (by New Orleans). I think he has also said that Colorado is not too bad either (I vote for there). There are places you definitely DON'T want to live (large cities like San Fran, New York City on the 15th floor). Living in the country surrounded by pine trees with great well or spring fed water would be good but somewhere by the ocean for grounding CT would be better. I have been watching a bunch of shows about people who live in the remote areas and that just looks so appealing to me. I would prefer the mountains over the swamps though.

Hope this helps a bit.

Nicole

Working on thyroid issues as low T3.

Environment - small town living but grew up on a farm so I have some concerns about pesticides and detox.

### prAna303 — 2014-03-10T08:16:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kids-and-technology.10088/post-114476>

Once again i chime in...

I observe technology usage, mostly because i come from a business where i was part of manufacturing and later on selling, to today, quite hostile agains the trends.

I have friends who give their 1 year old baby iPhone entertainment BECAUSE they can not stand having babies play around and make noices. In many cases the parents are high on medicin for ADHD and other problems, they can not stand the chaos. Also friends that make kids to show that they are perfect couples, trust me, they even confess to this. Often hear, but I want a silent moment, it will not harm the baby, look how smart he/she/it is.

My point to many is that because the parents are so low on energy they go the easy way, do not take the discussion when their kids want gadgets, they look at me and say that they have it all but you (me), what do i have. They can be really rude but i do not care, i take a stand and do not change my opinion.

For ten years i have seen the gear i worked with, yes WORKED with, transformed into entertainment. Everything must be rotating GIFS and effects. Everything is simplified into easy BUT stupid functions. I do not understand the reasons why schools use iPads, the books is transfered into light version with multitasking build in. When you read science you also should update Facebook, Twitter about that strange kid without iPhones, listen to Lady Gaga and look at cats on skateboards on youtube. Is this a great solution?

I remember even to this day some tests i did in a lab in 1989. At this place, a semiconductor factory in Sweden we tried some things that changed my life forever. In many of these, ESD (electrostatic discharge) was the main issue. For example, take a roll of regular office tape, pull a strip, just about 10 centimeters. The discharge would make changes to components 10 meters away, not completely kill it BUT make it just a little bit disturbed, weird, yes make it do weird things. Guess why humans is not allowed in a spot where they make components advanced like this? Yes you are right, we are trouble in a world of electronics BECAUSE we have tons of electronics in us also, easy described. We did tests with the tape and measured US, yes our skin surface react to that same roll of tape, we could even see changes in others parts in body also.

These tests makes me feel really bad when we come to children of today, the things that would slowly harm a grown up will change everything for kids in an instant, guess you guys, the doctors see this every day and me as a amateur see it in kids without ability to move in the real world...

### caroline — 2014-03-10T08:22:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kids-and-technology.10088/post-114478>

If this doesn't give you pause .... I don't know what would. Thanks Peter.

### cantweight — 2014-03-10T14:54:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kids-and-technology.10088/post-114518>

Wow the parents comments are just so typical....I'm surprised no one said they are not worried because a new drug will be developed to treat the kids.

I'm not even going to pretend to be the parent that did/does everything right, years ago when I was quite ill the tv was my surrogate babysitter while I laid on the floor with my toddlers, barely able to move from migraine, pain, and fever. But when you know better you do better right?
**
One thing I've learned in all of this is to never underestimate the power of telling kids the truth.**

Kids dont want to be ill, they dont want to be medicated, they dont want anxiety, they dont want their reproductive organs to fail, they dont want brain fog, they dont want depression, they dont want cancer.....more than anything they dont want these things at the hands of those that are to love and protect them. Kids trust that their parents will always do their best by them. Parents need to step up. Myself included.

Kids are smarter than we give them credit for, tell them what is in processed food and watch them stop eating it, tell them how playing video games affects their mood and energy for sports and watch them walk away from it, tell your son that keeping his iphone in his pocket is going to render his penis useless and watch that phone live on the charging dock, tell your daughter that using her phone at night destroys her sleep and causes acne and that phone ends up right next to her brothers....

Better yet...tell them the benefits of sunlight, water, magnetism, seafood, CT, and sleep and watch them blossom

Childrens instincts are still strong, they are resilient, and can still thrive when given the tools.....they have the power to change the future if we help them....now.

### Shijin13 — 2014-03-10T15:07:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kids-and-technology.10088/post-114520>

Yesterday when running around, Chaos asked me for iphone/ipod/ipad. to which I told her no. She asked why? I told her why. yet she was upset b/c all the other kids have this stuff. when I asked her if she wanted to be sick she said no. I told her these devises are like wheat. it doesn't seem bad initially, but in the long run they're not good for us.

My kids may want to have this stuff but they won't. They'll be better off w/o them. They understand we do things differently. they get that. At some point I anticipate they will rebel against this. but as we keep talking to them they'll continue to absorb this and internalize it.

I have several friends who have similar positions as we do regarding these devises/tv/computer etc for kids. and then I have those on the other side of the coin who've signed their kids up for computer programming classes at 6.

### nicld — 2014-03-10T15:49:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kids-and-technology.10088/post-114527>

I was a "mean" parent and never allowed video games in my house nor cable in the DD's room. The only computer she had to use with in the living room. She also did not get a cell phone until she was driving. Now she is 22 and I keep telling her how bad blue light is for you and what sleeping with the cell phone right by her bed is bad but she won't listen to me now. I just wish I could get her to at least look at some of this stuff since she is getting her RN degree in May and will soon be seeing sick people all the time.

### Gagnrad — 2014-03-10T15:49:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kids-and-technology.10088/post-114528>

> [Shijin13 said:](/goto/post?id=114520)
>
> My kids may want to have this stuff but they won't. They'll be better off w/o them. They understand we do things differently. they get that. At some point I anticipate they will rebel against this. but as we keep talking to them they'll continue to absorb this and internalize it.

They're lucky to have you as a mother. Most people, of course, have no idea. Hard-hitting recent stuff from Devra Davis here:

> In South Korea where there is an extremely high use of wireless technology, a syndrome has been observed which they call “digital dementia.” Also, South Korea has one of the highest rates of dementia in the world – one out of thirty eight. ...
>
>
> WHO has classified
> electromagnetic radiation as a class 2 B carcinogen. This is the same category as lead, engine exhaust and DDT. ...
>
>
> The results are even more severe for our young. The brain triples in the first two
> years of life, and the faster cells are growing, the greater damage Unborn fetuses are
> at the greatest risk followed by infants and young children. The young child’s brain
> continues to develop until adulthood, and damage of the early years can accumulate
> and magnify. iPads over bouncy seats or potties pose uncharted risks to future
> brains and bodies. The Health Ministry of Israel—a nation sophisticated in radar
> and microwave radiation—advises limited exposures to wireless for young children.

[www.svhi.com/wp-content/uploads/2014/02/SLF\_2014Feb20.pdf](http://www.svhi.com/wp-content/uploads/2014/02/SLF_2014Feb20.pdf)

I also took a peek at her twitter account. She's just linked a long article on a baseball player who's died young from brain cancer. Areas like snakes across the back of his head. He's not the only one. Why baseball -- it's not a sport involving a lot of head trauma.

Here's probably why:

> What most people don’t realize is that part of the action behind the plate and elsewhere involves radar. The radar is aimed at the pitcher from behind home plate and at times, in center field aimed at the catchers to get the velocities off of the bat. It is used on almost every ball that is pitched and caught, whether it be at practice, spring training or during regular season, a radar gun is in operation aimed directly at the pitcher. The catcher is also smack dab in the middle of the radar beam

[http://thefullertoninformer.com/the...t-we-can-learn-from-what-happened-to-the-kid/](http://thefullertoninformer.com/the-untimely-death-of-gary-carter-and-what-we-can-learn-from-what-happened-to-the-kid/)

Deeply serious stuff. Can this go on? 'Tis at the moment, anyway ...

She has also recently linked something of direct relevance to this thread:

[http://movingtolearn.ca/2014/ten-re...ld-be-banned-for-children-under-the-age-of-12](http://movingtolearn.ca/2014/ten-reasons-why-hand-held-devices-should-be-banned-for-children-under-the-age-of-12)

It says a lot for Jack that recently challenged in Twitter by one of these "can't be anything in it" people he just calmly said go and have a look at this and at that and then come back and talk. It could be irritating to know just how serious all this is and to have people who haven't made the effort and taken the time to research it pooh-pooh it.

### caroline — 2014-03-11T06:35:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kids-and-technology.10088/post-114745>

### Matty — 2014-03-10T12:39:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/re-artificial-means-of-grounding.10092/post-114515>

I asked in the EMF blog and was sent here, anyone care to answer?

This series is really phenomenal dr Kruse. I keep feeling the need to skip ahead to quiet the debate about grounding equipment. It takes me a long time to read these blogs and I want to know if I'm doing further harm using a electrical circuit ground based product. I assume for the reading I've done on your thoughts this far, your preference would be magnetically pads, then grounding rod based products, then if at all, electric circuit ground based product. I do not have $80 to drop on listening to the webinar to skip ahead either. Young family I'm trying to protect, and finances are tight. Can you shed some light for me so I can focus on the blog as you intended? If I plug in to a plug grounded strap when using an ipad at home, am I helping or making worse the EMFs? How about in an office building?

### Martin — 2014-03-10T14:56:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/re-artificial-means-of-grounding.10092/post-114519>

Hi Matty, without testing your home for what levels of magnetic, electrical, and radio frequencies you have, you don't know if your becoming an antenna for all those fields to come through you when you use a grounding/earthing electrical circuit-based product. It may be fine, but in this modern world, probably not. When you are on a computer, for instance, any EMF fields coming from it will pass THROUGH you if your feet/hands are on a grounding mat plugged into the wall. You need to ground the computer's metal frame directly into the earth with a wire and metal rod, according to many emf experts. Look up Michael Neuert's site and search around for a way to make a simple USB ground device for your computer. The office building? Probably loaded with EMF, but unless you have ways of testing, you can't be sure. If there are flourescent lights in it, more than likely you have fields. Every structure is different. Hope this helps.

NSH

I found an inexpensive multimeter, and have started testing everything as Jack recommends.

Should that read 0.00? I get the expected readings, similar to what I've seen in the how to videos. But no one in the vids mention the number illustrated on the reference. Anybody?

sorry matty, i cant help you (though i have packed up my earthing mat that i thought was gonna be so awesome, dirty electricity?! ugh.. ) but im curious now, how does an am radio help 'sniff' out emfs? Id like to do some 'sniffing' at my place!

Hi Margor,
Basically your listening for changes in the background static on the AM radio… the first 5 minutes of this video shows the process.

Bruce

### av8r — 2014-03-12T11:43:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/re-artificial-means-of-grounding.10092/post-114977>

> [Matty said:](/goto/post?id=114884)
>
> I found an inexpensive multimeter, and have started testing everything as Jack recommends.
>
> I wonder this about using the multimeter. The black wire connects to CON plug and to the ground, and then you hold the probe of the red lead. They say the CON is your reference, the earth. Should that read 0.00? I get the expected readings, similar to what I've seen in the how to videos. But no one in the vids mention the number illustrated on the reference. Anybody?
>
> The multimeter shows a major drop in body voltage when I connect to my electric based ground, darn near zero. So should I still be wary of this set up over a dedicated ground rod system?

Hi Matty,
Yes, COM is the common lead to ground and measure it in VAC, volts AC. The closer to zero reading you get the better.

When it comes to grounding you, you’re much better off grounding to the earth, bare foot on the ground is the best and second to that is a ground rod to a grounding mat separate from your house ground… there are too many unknowns with house grounds, i.e. other currents caused by ground loops and dirty connections etc.

Don’t run your grounding wire from the grounding rod to your mat in close proximity to any house wiring too or other sources of EMF. If you can’t avoid it then run your grounding wire perpendicular over any house wiring paths you need to cross. Also, at the end of your grounding wire where it attaches to your grounding mat put in an RF choke to suppress any EMF that get on your grounding wire… a grounding wire is an antenna in theory! An RF choke is a simple toroid bead that the wire wraps a few turns around… radio shack or any electronics store sells them. [http://sigma.octopart.com/490833/image/RadioShack-RF-CHOKE.jpg](http://sigma.octopart.com/490833/image/RadioShack-RF-CHOKE.jpg)
Bruce

### Matty — 2014-03-12T12:29:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/re-artificial-means-of-grounding.10092/post-114983>

Fantastic, Bruce! Thanks you for your input, very helpful!

"Blocked plug-in" _sounds_ like a security message. Maybe Apple have tightened up the security in the latest iteration, so you can only send plain text or HTML. Or maybe you can send flash, but not run it, unless you specifically allow it in the settings. I don't know, though.

> Lots of industry people there.

Thank you, Mike for your reply. Could not search on arstechnica yet unless I am registered there....
I am sure that eventually I will find the answer....I was just trying to avoid spending too much time on it. Coupled that with having a diminished brain power, I was looking for a quick shortcut to solve my problem...

### Gagnrad — 2014-03-11T20:20:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-am-so-frustrated-with-my-computer-today-still-have-not-solved-the-problem.10100/post-114829>

Having a quick look round … it's kind of interesting that the upgrade apparently uninstalls Flash (and Java):

> Mavericks doesn't come packed with two pieces of software you'll probably need at some point, Flash and Java. In fact, if you had either installed in OS X Mountain Lion, Apple went ahead and deleted it when you upgraded to Mavericks. ...

[http://lifehacker.com/how-to-fix-os-x-mavericks-biggest-annoyances-1450220339](http://lifehacker.com/how-to-fix-os-x-mavericks-biggest-annoyances-1450220339)

Presumably, you re-installed Flash, or you'd say it wasn't working in the browser, and you say it is. And the dialog box say "Plug-in blocked" NOT "Plug-in missing".

Basically, they don't want it there in the OS at all. It's not good software; historically, it's been notoriously flaky, slow and inefficient on OS X; it's also been a frequently exploited security hole. Better really if we all went over to doing videos via HTML5. And there's a further reason … but a short digression first:

You may already have the ClickToFlash Safari extension installed, which prevents Flash content auto-running (think ads, for example) and replaces it with HTML H.264 video where available:

> ClickToFlash prevents the Flash plug-in from running. It replaces any Flash element on a webpage with a placeholder; simply click the placeholder to load the Flash content. ClickToFlash also lets you replace Flash videos from select websites (such as YouTube) with H.264 video. Other optional features include advanced whitelisting.

[https://extensions.apple.com](https://extensions.apple.com)

If not you might want that.

(I think Adobe have got a bit worried and they've put a little more effort into the Mac version of Flash these days. It's updated so often, that in itself has become a pain in the neck.)

Anyway, the further reason is this: Flash was absolute garbage on mobile versions. A total resource hog. You probably remember how, until a year or two back, Flash content could get the fan going and play merry hell on a Mac. It was really no-go on handhelds. Allowing it on iOS would not only have ruined Apple's nice new toy (the iPhone) but have handed Adobe a barrel to put Apple over. Apple would then be dependent on Adobe to update Flash for iPhone. No way Steve Jobs would ever allow that. The battle was already lost on the desktop, but they weren't going to sell the pass on a new platform.

In short: _they don't want it there_. They have to put up with it on OS X, but they'd really rather run OS X and iOS under similar policies. If you think of yourself imbedding Flash content in a mail message and sending it to someone with an iPhone … well, they'd not be able to see it (because there's no Flash for iOS, for good reasons).

I'm not surprised the latest iteration of the OS strips out Flash -- and Java, to which many of the same problems apply.

I expect it's policy not to allow Flash to run in email messages -- by default at least -- under Mavericks. But I don't know, and I'm still running 10.7 here myself.

But the guys at Ars Technica will know what's going on.

### Tanya — 2014-03-11T20:58:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-am-so-frustrated-with-my-computer-today-still-have-not-solved-the-problem.10100/post-114832>

Thank you so very much, Mike.  I followed your advice and with ClicktoFlash was finally able to get it done. I suspect that you are correct in your estimate of what was happening with it... I was so reluctant to switch to Mavericks to begin with, and now that I looked at the list of "mavericks' biggest annoyances" in the link that you provided, I wonder if I should have done it at all.... Thanks again, Mike.

# Thread: Scallop week in Rye
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/scallop-week-in-rye.10123/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Gagnrad — 2014-03-12T10:32:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/scallop-week-in-rye.10123/post-114972>

> Back in the Middle Ages, the French made several forays across the English Channel to sack the picturesque little Sussex town of Rye.
>
> These days they arrive on a more frequent basis – to buy the most succulent scallops I’ve ever tasted, because the arc of Rye Bay is to this prized mollusc what crabs are to Cromer and oysters to Galway.
> This pre-eminence is celebrated each February at the annual Rye Bay Scallop Week, which this year celebrated its seventh annual staging.
>
> Scallops with black pudding bon-bons and roast parsnip purée at the ancient Mermaid Inn; wood roast scallops flamed on the shell with pastis and shaved fennel, dill and lemon at the elegant George Grill; seared scallops with Jerusalem artichoke, Serrano ham and squid ink sauce at the homely Ship Inn; scallop, potato and watercress chowder at trendy Webbe’s In Rye – the choices were certainly multiple, with 24 venues across the district taking part ...

[http://www.belfasttelegraph.co.uk/life/travel/ryes-shellfish-intentions-30062410.html](http://www.belfasttelegraph.co.uk/life/travel/ryes-shellfish-intentions-30062410.html)

### Tanya — 2014-03-12T20:08:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/scallop-week-in-rye.10123/post-115046>

Mike, if I ever visit England again, this town would definitely be on my itinerary - beautiful place with so much history and now my favorite food - scallops.

# Thread: Huevos rancheros
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/huevos-rancheros.10124/>
**Subforum:** Epi-Paleo Recipes
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Gagnrad — 2014-03-12T15:14:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/huevos-rancheros.10124/post-114999>

Perhaps this would be more of a summer/autumn dish. If there are any tomatoes around where I am right now then they're grown under glass.

But anyway, does anyone do a version of these?

The first I heard of this dish was when the Two Fat Ladies cooked it on the TV. They used chilli con carne over the eggs, which seems not to be authentic ... but a pretty good idea.

I came across a version in the recipe section of Dr. Perlmutter's wonderful new book. And I just tried googling "paleo huevos rancheros". Loads of hits. This was the top one:

[http://janeshealthykitchen.com/paleo-huevos-rancheros/](http://janeshealthykitchen.com/paleo-huevos-rancheros/)

There she's paleoized it by replacing the tortillas with a kind of stew of sweet potatoes used as a base. Looks OK. I don't think I'd bother with sweet potatoes, though, myself.

If I make it, I'll usually have a couple of fried eggs topped with some chilli and with some salsa and some gucamole on the side -- or just some avocado if I can't be bothered to make gucamole.

What do others do?

### Lahelada — 2014-03-12T15:40:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/huevos-rancheros.10124/post-115003>

I lived in Mexico for a good while and this is how you cook them there.
The sauce is made with chile ancho (dried chile) and some jalapeño or chile arbol (lady finger chili) for heat. Last I heard you could get them in the UK. harrods foodhall has them for sure. A sauce that you can make anywhere and store is this:
I litre of passata, as many finely choppedgreen or red chiles as you can stand , 2 or three garlic cloves , 2 or three allspice berries ,a clove and oregano. Water to keep thinning the sauce.Fry up the chiles,garlic and the allspice and clove, lightly of course so the garlic does not go bitter. Add the passata , simmer on low for as long as you can frequently adding water.In the final simmer cook down the liquid and add the oregano. This sauce gos with any meat too.
Ladle on a plate,put your eggs on top and grate cheese over them.If your sauce is very spicy you could also add sour cream. The fried tortilla is essentially a filler in everyday cooking but serves the purpose of giving a neutral element to teh palate. The cheese and sour cream serve the same purpose to cool down to mitigate the effect on teh palate. You could also try a pairing with cauliflower cheese for a larger meal.

Edit:If you use a blender puree the sauce if you don't like lumpy bits
Edit 2: add note about chile arbol finger chile

### Shijin13 — 2014-03-12T16:41:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/huevos-rancheros.10124/post-115014>

When I worked at a local coffee shop after getting out of the AF - we made these every day. Called them Confetti eggs. We'd steam the eggs with the steaming wand from an espresso maker, add in cheese, diced tomatoes, green onions, jalapeños, and serve over refried black beans and with sour cream and guacamole.

here's a paleo recipe [http://janeshealthykitchen.com/paleo-huevos-rancheros/#.UyBx0\_SwKv0](http://janeshealthykitchen.com/paleo-huevos-rancheros/#.UyBx0_SwKv0)

# Thread: Greece and fish
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greece-and-fish.10130/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Gagnrad — 2014-03-12T20:55:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greece-and-fish.10130/post-115050>

Having a look around online.

I knew seafood was much prized in Ancient Athens. I was looking to see what I could find on that.

This from the American School of Classical Studies at Athens confirms that:

> Meat, except sausages, was rarely eaten by ordinary citizens, but it could be bought for important occasions at the cook shops. It was also distributed to the citizens after the sacrifices at festivals. Birds and **fish** (9) **were esteemed delicacies, also sea urchins, octopus, eels, and oysters.** The poor relied on unleavened bread, porridge, soup, eggs, greens, salt fish, tripe.

[http://www.agathe.gr/icons/pdfs/agorapicbk-12.pdf](http://www.agathe.gr/icons/pdfs/agorapicbk-12.pdf)

Here's a nice artifact:

> Athenian and Apulian Red-Figure Fish Plates Both of these footed plates were made to serve succulent morsels of grilled seafood, like the fish, mollusks, and other marine creatures that are painted on their surfaces. Greece and Italy are peninsulas projecting into seas brimming with marine life. A primary source of protein, seafood was a basic staple of the ancient Mediterranean diet; it remains so today. ...

Click on the photo for an enlargement.

[http://www.artic.edu/aic/collections/artwork/255](http://www.artic.edu/aic/collections/artwork/255)

Finally … here's a mouthwatering catalogue of the seafood available nowadays in Greece. Just a bewildering variety of stuff. And while the fish are cooking, there are appetisers:

> While the big fish is cooking we will have ordered some _mezedes_ to go along with it. Among them is _tarama salata_ (fish-\[roe\] salad-photo) usually made from the eggs of carp but if you are lucky, from kefalo (gray mullet) which is what it was originally made from ... You have to ask if the restaurant makes it or if it comes from a container. … A plate of fried gavros (anchovies) is as essential as a plate of fried potatoes and are eaten the same way, with lemon or vinegar squeezed on them.

A plate of fried anchovies instead of a plate of fried potatoes? Count me in.

[http://www.greecefoods.com/seafood/](http://www.greecefoods.com/seafood/)

I guess people from Greece are probably a "heart disease paradox" just like the French.

### caroline — 2014-03-13T01:57:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greece-and-fish.10130/post-115092>

Ricardo was just telling me that when he lived in the Matimes he ate almost exclusively fresh fish off the fishing boats ....... And has never been healthier.
