### Gagnrad — 2014-04-23T20:50:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-real-mediterranean-diet.10634/post-123221>

The Caveman Doctor reports on what (the very slim and active) people in Southern Italy are _actually_ currently eating.

> The amount of bread was ... minimal compared to what I expected. ...
>
> Finally, pasta, which I figured would be flowing out of the faucets, was consumed in much smaller amounts than I ever expected. ...
>
> Often there was a menu page or two devoted to meat, including beef, lamb, chicken, and a rare pork dish, with another page or two of fish, and a page or two of appetizers, which consisted of carpaccio, tuna, prosciutto and bufala mozzarella (ham and buffalo mozzarella). ...
>
> ... the food that dominated the menus and plates of most local restaurants along the coast was the calamari and octopus...

[http://www.myhealthwire.com/news/diet-nutrition/758](http://www.myhealthwire.com/news/diet-nutrition/758)

### sooperb — 2014-04-26T21:37:44+0300

# Thread: Fermented Cod Liver Oil Sources
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fermented-cod-liver-oil-sources.10635/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### persistence — 2014-04-23T21:39:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fermented-cod-liver-oil-sources.10635/post-123229>

I have been buying the Green Pastures BLUE ICE Royal Butter Oil/Fermented Cod Liver Oil Blend product for fat soluble vitamins, and supplementing that with the Life Extension vitamin K product. Does anyone have a cheaper source for the fermented cod liver oil? With shipping, a single small container of the Green Pastures product is $60. That's a $720 yearly habit just for fat soluble vitamins. There really should be a cheaper way to get this.

### sooperb — 2014-04-30T10:52:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fermented-cod-liver-oil-sources.10635/post-124011>

Mercola might do something.

### Gagnrad — 2014-04-30T11:03:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fermented-cod-liver-oil-sources.10635/post-124013>

AFAIK, Green Pastures is the only company in the world making a fermented CLO.

Is it any cheaper when not mixed with the butter oil? I can't recall whether it is or not over here. Because, of course, you can always include good quality butter in your diet even though it's not quite so rich a source of AA.

Is your reckoning based on a teaspoon a day or what? Since cost depends on how much you take, you could, even if you want to use it, use rather less but also supplement with D3 capsules.

I couldn't get get cheaper than that for fclo. They have a sale the last week of the year if you want to stock up. I did use Max DHA from Jarrow and got decent results. Now I just eat canned sardines for lunch 4 times a week. They are 2.50 a can, wild caught with bone in. You are getting iodine, dha, EPA, proline and all of the cofactors in an electron dense package.

# Thread: I need help!!!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help.10637/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### cmegan — 2014-04-24T01:03:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help.10637/post-123243>

Hi!

Here is my question in a nutshell...how do other members who cannot tolerate bone broths (secondary to histamine and glutamate issues) get their collagen? I remember that Seahorse and I think BJK77 have these issues. Do you focus more on eating cuts of meat that are higher in collagen like lamb/beef shoulders and shanks? Anything else? Pork skins?

I was diagnosed several weeks ago with frozen shoulder and am truly suffering with pain and horrible nightly insomnia. I am exhausted. Dr. Kruse and Josh posted that is was basically a vasculitis issue and RO water and bone broth is the way to go. I was started to have bone broth three times per day (either lamb or fish heads/frame) and boom, 4 days into it I developed large welts all over my face, palpitations, etc etc etc. Now at least I understand why I never did well on GAPS or with winter slow cooked stews, sauerkraut, etc. I am seeing my GP next week and will have all new bloods done including MTHFR, but for now I need to understand how to get some collagen in my system! Or any other ideas about talking this painful syndrome! Thanks! Chrissy

### cmegan — 2014-04-24T01:08:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help.10637/post-123244>

An interesting experience today related to this, I went to see the acupuncturist today for my shoulder...I've been seeing him in chinatown for about 25 years, he doesn't speak english and has never spoken to me. I just point to the part of my body that is ailing me and he assesses my tongue and pulse and gives me needles...he always helps me. When I went today and showed him my lack of ROM in my shoulder and suggested that I had pain, he called in one his assistants to interpret for him (which he's never done before!) She turned to me and said "he wants you to eat many many chicken wings"!

### cantweight — 2014-04-24T01:56:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help.10637/post-123245>

That is so wild...I guess you need to eat connective tissue to heal connective tissue. Too bad you cant do the broth, a nice big bowl of pho with tendon could be so good for you!!

### SeaHorse — 2014-04-24T02:59:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help.10637/post-123256>

I feel your pain cmegan…. I have to really limit the broths, but what I do is get pork hocks and cook them in water with bay leaf, onion and a clove, salt and pepper and then I eat them that same day, lots of cartilage and fat in the hocks…no reheating or long 5 hours cooking time this will cut down on the histamine and glutamate. I seem to be able to do this pretty regularly, but I don't have it every day. Also your idea of shin beef is right on…very good and with beef I'm happy to eat it raw still can't do that with pork. Pork skins from wellness are good too, but the heating part worries me a bit. Lamb parts are great.

Basically I try to eat my collagen instead of drink it.. As for your shoulder, what about buckets of RO water, a small amount of protein and lots of cartilage/fat for a week or so? ….do you have a red light you can use on your shoulder…although with dehydration that might be too much input.

Have you ever used Histame?…it can help to a degree. Best wishes.

### cmegan — 2014-04-24T03:15:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help.10637/post-123259>

thanks seahorse this is all good advice! I have been buying my lamb from a man in TX and he was explaining to me that the shoulder is as high in collagen as are the shanks. I bought a lamb shoulder chop in WF tonight (cheaper then most cuts) and grilled it and chewed a lot on the bone after eating the meat...I think your idea of lots of RO water, little protein, and more cartilage/fat is great. I'm not quite sure where seafood fits into this scenario right now as it is low in collagen and I have been craving lamb chops and poultry skin. I will look into pork skins from Wellness. It was so funny at my sister's house for Easter this weekend my nephew pulled out a bag of pork rinds (I haven't had them since my old Atkins days!). It was so much fun to eat something that reminded me of potato chips without the carbs! I haven't used histame, I will look into it! I like your advice of eating your collagen instead of drinking it! Thank you....

### Inger — 2014-04-24T08:36:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help.10637/post-123282>

Raw grass fed heart is awesome I would say, and very easy to eat too  remember to chew less

> Beef heart contains all essential amino acids, zinc, selenium and phosphorus. **It has more than double the elastin and collagen than other cuts of meat** and a highly concentrated source of coenzyme Q10, also known as CoQ10.

[http://www.livestrong.com/article/336563-is-beef-heart-healthy/](http://www.livestrong.com/article/336563-is-beef-heart-healthy/)

You have read the the gut-collagen blog too...? and seen Jacks advice to swallow pieces of raw "connective tissue" stuff....

[http://jackkruse.com/organizational-structural-failure-1-gutcollagen-link/#idc-cover](http://jackkruse.com/organizational-structural-failure-1-gutcollagen-link/#idc-cover)

I think I have the same issue as you Megan... my shoulder(s) is still not 100%. I am not surprised because I have done stupid things lately - ha!
My cure will be lots of grounding and sunshine, dips in the river as always, lots of raw heart - not much chewed - and also swallowing connective tissue and hardly chewable stuff and fish head smoothies and raw seafood and working on my mind

I love your acupuncturist - lucky you!

### cmegan — 2014-04-24T14:26:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help.10637/post-123301>

Inger, I am always so impressed with your ability to eat raw...I can only do cerviche now, but after looking at your post I think I will try some raw lamb heart. I think the fact that I don't have to "chew" it will make it so much easier!!! I am so happy that spring is here, I live 5 minutes from the ocean and go there everyday...last night I stood with my feet in the water, it was so cold but it felt so good! Thank you for your advice! I hope your shoulder gets better too!

### SeaHorse — 2014-04-24T14:35:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help.10637/post-123303>

Lamb heart is the easiest to start with….Sometimes I chop up heart, pour a bit of evo on it, salt, chopped herbs like cilantro, parsley and dill and a squeeze of lemon. As with the mhs and raw fish….lemon and cilantro go a long way to "normalizing" the taste until you can appreciate it for what it is (which is never with MHS, but always now with raw heart and beef) Bon Voyage!

### cmegan — 2014-04-24T16:08:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help.10637/post-123309>

Thanks seahorse, I agree lemon and cilantro and Evo are the way to go! My plan was to cut really small pieces, roll into a ball, and swallow like a pill!!'

### cantweight — 2014-04-24T16:12:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help.10637/post-123311>

> [cmegan said:](/goto/post?id=123309)
>
> Thanks seahorse, I agree lemon and cilantro and Evo are the way to go! My plan was to cut really small pieces, roll into a ball, and swallow like a pill!!'

I am posting this public service announcement for anyone on the forum with small children as well as to share with others they know. Two children a week die or become severely incapacitated from something so simply prevented through awareness.I hope that you all share this.

# Thread: Another quantum poem:  OCEANS
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/another-quantum-poem-oceans.10642/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### SeaHorse — 2014-04-24T16:02:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/another-quantum-poem-oceans.10642/post-123308>

I have a feeling that my boat
has struck,
down there in the depths,
against a great thing.

And nothing happens!
Nothing…Silence…Waves…

\--Nothing happen? Or has
everything happened, and are
we standing now, quietly,
in the new life?

Juan Ramon Jimenez

translated by Robert Bly

### sooperb — 2014-04-25T11:00:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/another-quantum-poem-oceans.10642/post-123408>

Isn't that a lovely challenging concept? I like it

# Thread: Turning off the juice....
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/turning-off-the-juice.10645/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### RobH — 2014-04-24T18:47:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/turning-off-the-juice.10645/post-123327>

For months I've been occasionally and intermittently (not often enough) turning off breakers to our bedroom at night. This has been a huge pain (thus the occasionally and intermittently) because of the location of our breaker box -- outside, around the house, through bushes, etc.

Had an electrician come out yesterday and install a 4-pole "contactor" that allows me to cut power to 3 circuits (the one feeding master bedroom / bath) area by simply flipping a cute little red switch. The switch is in master bath about 15 feet from bed (hope that's not too close...), and has MC (metal clad) shielded cable running to it. Now we can cut 3 circuits by just flipping a single switch in a convenient place. The hardware supposedly was less than $100 and it was about 2 hours of electrician time to install (waiting for bill). Wish I would have done it months ago.

My wife reports great sleep in a last night.  I slept pretty well after getting to bed at 2:30 after a shift.

The confounding factor is that the other thing the electricians were hooking up was the infrared sauna.... She got in that before bed and said it was wonderful.

I've messed with 2 variables at once so not such a good experimentalist I guess.

If you haven't looked into having something like this installed -- you should. It is very convenient and thus enhances compliance....

Highly recommended.

RobH

### SeaHorse — 2014-04-24T20:39:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/turning-off-the-juice.10645/post-123340>

We did the same thing and got a kill switch for our bedroom/bath/hallway around Christmas time and along with the magnetico our sleep has been super great since….

### Tanya — 2014-04-24T22:28:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/turning-off-the-juice.10645/post-123348>

> [RobH said:](/goto/post?id=123327)

> For months I've been occasionally and intermittently (not often enough) turning off breakers to our bedroom at night. This has been a huge pain (thus the occasionally and intermittently) because of the location of our breaker box -- outside, around the house, through bushes, etc.
>
> Had an electrician come out yesterday and install a 4-pole "contactor" that allows me to cut power to 3 circuits (the one feeding master bedroom / bath) area by simply flipping a cute little red switch. The switch is in master bath about 15 feet from bed (hope that's not too close...), and has MC (metal clad) shielded cable running to it. Now we can cut 3 circuits by just flipping a single switch in a convenient place. The hardware supposedly was less than $100 and it was about 2 hours of electrician time to install (waiting for bill). Wish I would have done it months ago.
>
> My wife reports great sleep in a last night.  I slept pretty well after getting to bed at 2:30 after a shift.
>
> The confounding factor is that the other thing the electricians were hooking up was the infrared sauna.... She got in that before bed and said it was wonderful.
>
> I've messed with 2 variables at once so not such a good experimentalist I guess.
>
> If you haven't looked into having something like this installed -- you should. It is very convenient and thus enhances compliance....

I am going to share with my husband what you had to do before you installed the kill switch, Rob Right now, he has to go down into our basement to get to our breaker box. Never forgets to remind me that he is doing it all just for me...If I don't want to hear him complaining anymore, we would definitely need to rewire. I know, it is not going to be as affordable as it was in your state, though - electricians here make a small fortune... I suppose, I could ask for that for my birthday... I need to start hinting it

### kyrakitty — 2014-04-25T05:15:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/beef-liver-p%C3%A2t%C3%A9-in-jacks-cook-book-question.10650/post-123378>

So, forgive the dumb question but I really want to make this. Do I cook the liver first or is the liver supposed to be raw and only warmed up? Thanks to whomever is kind enough to answer ))

Ingredients
• 3/4 cup ghee
• 1/2 cup onion, chopped
• 1 lb liver (beef, chicken or goose)
• 1 1/2 tsp cajun spice
• 1/8 tsp black pepper
• 1 tsp lemon juice
• 1 hard boiled egg
• 1/8 tsp cayenne pepper

Directions:
Melt 1/2 ghee and onions in pan until onions are soft for about 5 mins. Remove from heat. Add all other ingredients, stir until ghee is melted. Put mixture in a blender with egg. Blend at high speed untilpureed. Take out of blender and shape into a ball or log. Place in fridge until cool about 1 or 2 hrs.

### Inger — 2014-04-25T08:13:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/beef-liver-p%C3%A2t%C3%A9-in-jacks-cook-book-question.10650/post-123385>

It must be cooked liver - with raw it would end up as a bloody mess

### kyrakitty — 2014-04-25T08:35:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/beef-liver-p%C3%A2t%C3%A9-in-jacks-cook-book-question.10650/post-123388>

Thx Inger )) you're the best!

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/beef-liver-p%C3%A2t%C3%A9-in-jacks-cook-book-question.10650/post-123411>

# Thread: Wired DSL Modems and Accessories
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wired-dsl-modems-and-accessories.10652/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Chrysanthemama — 2014-04-25T18:24:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wired-dsl-modems-and-accessories.10652/post-123449>

Greetings Everyone!
I am looking for some advice that hopefully you all have.
I recently switched ISPs, and now I have DSL internet.
Of course the company only provided a wireless modem.

I was looking at this:
[http://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B0061308MA/ref=gno\_cart\_title\_1?ie=UTF8&psc=1&smid=A2L77EE7U53NWQ](http://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B0061308MA/ref=gno_cart_title_1?ie=UTF8&psc=1&smid=A2L77EE7U53NWQ)

And I'm wondering if others have used this system, and what I would need. I am assuming a wired DSL modem. A wired router, and then this?

Any advice is appreciated!

### RobH — 2014-04-26T03:04:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wired-dsl-modems-and-accessories.10652/post-123471>

I had ethernet cable runs in my home done. It was easy due to crawl space (ugh, not sitting on earth) My only concern with that is are you creating more "dirty electricity) going to every outlet in your house? Don't know.

RobH

### Jonathin — 2014-04-26T05:29:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wired-dsl-modems-and-accessories.10652/post-123482>

Ethernet cables have twisted conductors that limit radiation. If you use your house wiring with a powerline adapter network you don't have that protection plus your whole electrical power wiring has essentially dirty electricity. I would stick with a basic inexpensive router with cat 5e or 6 cable. Internet connect speeds are slow compared to computer and router capabilities so nothing fancy or expensive is necessary.

### ssj3 — 2014-04-26T12:11:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wired-dsl-modems-and-accessories.10652/post-123494>

If I am understanding your question correctly... most routers have additional network ports so you can just run additional Ethernet cables as needed to different areas. You could also consider a router that has a button to enable WIFI when you need it. I use a Netgear N150 which has this feature and works well.

[http://www.amazon.com/NetGear-DGN10...\_sbs\_pc\_6?ie=UTF8&refRID=16X5ZYSM031H8J0C7TN9](http://www.amazon.com/NetGear-DGN1000-Wireless-4-Port-Protected/dp/B002WOXYHS/ref=pd_sim_sbs_pc_6?ie=UTF8&refRID=16X5ZYSM031H8J0C7TN9)

### Shijin13 — 2014-04-26T18:52:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wired-dsl-modems-and-accessories.10652/post-123521>

We have a VPN Router (required for work stuff) and a basic modem that doesn't emit wifi. specifically went out and bought that. I'm currently using a Henge docking station for my old macbook Pro. When I get my next MBP I plan on using a docking station for that, and plug the current MBP with the HengeDock by the TV, and run ethernet to that system and hook it direct up to the TV so we can officially ditch cable - minus internet....

### JMO — 2014-04-27T04:46:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wired-dsl-modems-and-accessories.10652/post-123552>

I have DSL and an actiontec wireless modem. There is not a hardwired switch to turn wireless off but you can do it thru the software by going to the modem ip address 192.168.0.1 and change the configuration to turn wireless off.

### JackFrake — 2014-04-25T19:26:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ppp-in-the-early-ocean.10653/post-123453>

'Metabolism may have started in our early oceans':

[http://phys.org/news/2014-04-metabolism-early-oceans-life.html](http://phys.org/news/2014-04-metabolism-early-oceans-life.html)

_The findings suggests that metabolism predates the origin of life and evolved through the chemical conditions that prevailed in the worlds earliest oceans.

"Our results show that reaction sequences that resemble two essential reaction cascades of metabolism, glycolysis and the pentose-phosphate pathways, could have occurred spontaneously in the earth's ancient oceans," says Dr. Markus Ralser at the Department of Biochemistry at the University of Cambridge and the National Institute for Medical Research, who led the study.
_

# Thread: african origin of modern mankind  and cold thermogenesis
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/african-origin-of-modern-mankind-and-cold-thermogenesis.10654/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### callag — 2014-04-25T20:02:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/african-origin-of-modern-mankind-and-cold-thermogenesis.10654/post-123454>

Hi

sorry if this topic was already covered but can anyone explain how dr Kruse is reconciling cold thermogenesis and the fact that as a species we all descend by Africans so we all need and love hot ?

[http://www.reuters.com/article/2007/07/18/us-evolution-skulls-idUSL1885586220070718](http://www.reuters.com/article/2007/07/18/us-evolution-skulls-idUSL1885586220070718)

[http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Recent\_African\_origin\_of\_modern\_humans](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Recent_African_origin_of_modern_humans)

It looks like we should be doing saunas instead of cold bath

Many thanks

### NeilBB — 2014-04-25T21:07:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/african-origin-of-modern-mankind-and-cold-thermogenesis.10654/post-123459>

> [callag said:](/goto/post?id=123454)
>
> Hi
>
> sorry if this topic was already covered but can anyone explain how dr Kruse is reconciling cold thermogenesis and the fact that as a species we all descend by Africans so we all need and love hot ?
>
> [http://www.reuters.com/article/2007/07/18/us-evolution-skulls-idUSL1885586220070718](http://www.reuters.com/article/2007/07/18/us-evolution-skulls-idUSL1885586220070718)
>
> [http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Recent\_African\_origin\_of\_modern\_humans](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Recent_African_origin_of_modern_humans)
>
> It looks like we should be doing saunas instead of cold bath
>
> Many thanks

Complicated issue but briefly off top of my head, I'd say this...

There is an excess of artificial warmth in modern life due to clothes, heaters, and indoor living, and therefore an unnatural relative absence of intense cold exposure, especially in winter. Lack of a cold winter is a significant modern chronobiological mismatch for most and a major contributor to chronic disease including diabetes, obesity, autoimmune conditions. Therefore year round CT is likely beneficial for many to counteract this modern problem. Even if not in cold climate, the early humans in Africa also still experienced a much more normal native seasonal variation and were not subject to the specific inflammatory effects of modern life that we are trying to counteract now.

Cold has many pro-life effects. Read the CT series for more details. It is anti-inflammatory and can cause fat cell apoptosis among other things. Most fundamentally though, according to physics, cold is antientropic, which means essentially that it slows down random molecular motion, e.g., degeneration, inflammation, and aging. It can also enhance the speed of biological semiconduction as it slows randomness.

There is also a place for targeted heat, specifically infrared electromagnetic radiation, which can energize the body's water stores by separating its charges into positive and negative components, creating a biological battery. (See Pollack, 4th Phase of Water) To do this well though, you need to maintain optimal hydration status at all times, which is difficult today. When well hydrated, IR heat from sunlight, fire, or infrared saunas are also very beneficial. If dehydrated, like most today, heat may make you feel good but won't really help health or longevity much.

### NeilBB — 2014-04-25T21:51:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/african-origin-of-modern-mankind-and-cold-thermogenesis.10654/post-123460>

I would also add this...

Benefits of cold are not unique to humans, are common to all life, and especially eutherian mammals (hibernation). (See factor X story.) When you study evolution, look for these benefits after earth's extinction events and at the origin of life. Or in the polar oceans.

So, from an evolutionary perspective, it makes much more sense to examine the ecological factors present at the origin of man from a perspective of what is _unique_ to man, i. e., his greatly enhanced central nervous system. This leads to the uniqueness of DHA and the aquatic food chain in human brain development, which did come out of Africa! _This_ is what was most important advancement specifically of _human development_. Cold-adaptation for us, by contrast, is a dormant genetic program that we inherited from prehuman ancestors, that we are now able to reactivate to help us normalize the environmental mismatches of modern life.

### callag — 2014-04-25T22:59:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/african-origin-of-modern-mankind-and-cold-thermogenesis.10654/post-123465>

thanks it is starts making sense..

### NeilBB — 2014-04-25T23:48:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/african-origin-of-modern-mankind-and-cold-thermogenesis.10654/post-123468>

Also remember that in Africa at the dawn of humanity there was a particularly strong magnetic field present, partly due to the local intersection of three tectonic plates, but today we have a declining magnetic field on earth everywhere, and then consider that cold enhances the strength of diamagnetic and ferromagnetic materials. Which is probably another reason to keep the bedroom as cold as possible if you've got a Magnetico, I'd bet...

### Dali Dula — 2014-04-26T01:41:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/african-origin-of-modern-mankind-and-cold-thermogenesis.10654/post-123470>

Sweet synopsis, NeilBB.

### ssj3 — 2014-04-26T11:57:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/african-origin-of-modern-mankind-and-cold-thermogenesis.10654/post-123491>

# Thread: I know my husband loves me….
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-know-my-husband-loves-me%E2%80%A6.10656/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 15
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### SeaHorse — 2014-04-26T04:12:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-know-my-husband-loves-me%E2%80%A6.10656/post-123474>

He never blinked an eye as we went off this afternoon to look at used cars, me with my trusty tri-meter clutched in my hand. It was a very fun afternoon and by the end of the day the sale manager was getting me to test some of the newer vehicles to see what the differences were. These guys already knew my husband and I was just tickled to see how the whole question of emf and no "bells and whistles" was brought up. There was no eye rolling or condescension and one of the owners was actually saying that he didn't think all these electronics were good for people either. Now, I know they saw us as potential customers, but their interest went beyond the professional salesman behaviour. Anyway, it looks like I'll be trading in my Subaru for an older Honda…the readings were substantially lower and it had no satellite or bluetooth and computer stuff. YAY, maybe I'll enjoy driving again.

### cantweight — 2014-04-26T04:35:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-know-my-husband-loves-me%E2%80%A6.10656/post-123476>

Its an amazing feeling to be respected like that. The hoops I have made my hubby jump through with diet and lifestyle change....man....I dont know how he put up with it. The crazy is part is along he just wanted steak and veggies....should have followed his instincts instead of mine on that one. The poor man, I made him commit to veganism for a month and few years back....if he didn't divorce me over that we are golden!

He has bent over backwards to provide us with everything we could need to implement Jacks protocols simply because I believe in them. R/O faucet, cold tub and ice machine, sauna, magnetico, supplements, emf meters....you name it....monies we could have spent on so many other things we have gone without. Is a blessing indeed to have such a mate...although they are quite lucky themselves

For the record...10 yr old Suburbans test really well  and give the kids the opportunity to learn about cassette tape players!

### SeaHorse — 2014-04-26T04:58:44+0300

It is a great feeling to be in it together…I was just talking about HBOT with my husband tonight….and he asked me why HE wasn't doing it too! So far he's not jumped in the pond with me, but he's grounding and eating epi-paleo and enjoying the magnetico as much as I do…..

Thanks for the Suburban tip…have you see this site? [http://www.ahappyhabitat.com/cars.html](http://www.ahappyhabitat.com/cars.html)
Wish I still had my cassettes, most of them didn't make it in our last move.

### cantweight — 2014-04-26T17:14:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-know-my-husband-loves-me%E2%80%A6.10656/post-123513>

# Thread: Activated almonds, Mmmm :)
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/activated-almonds-mmmm.10660/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### sooperb — 2014-04-26T19:43:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/activated-almonds-mmmm.10660/post-123525>

I don't suppose I'm the first but today I discovered activated almonds, in a bag with raw chocolate in the health food shop. I have just sampled them and they are really good. Downside is that after I'd swallowed a few and marvelled at how good (and expensive) they were I checked the ingredients. Doh, they also have coconut flower nectar in them. Here's a link to anyone lucky enough to live in the UK - [http://www.rawecstasy.co.uk/shop/activated-almonds-raw-chocolate/](http://www.rawecstasy.co.uk/shop/activated-almonds-raw-chocolate/)

So, me being me, I set off to find out more about them. They are surprisingly easy to make, soak, cool roast or dehydrate and you have them. I just knew there was a reason I bought the dehydrator that is currently still in its box  I also just happen to have a half full (see I'm learning to be a half full person) bag of raw cocoa (which I have yet to sample, flipping pixies!) So I just need to work out how to coat them with it and make it stick. Maybe I could just melt some 85% chocolate over them. They've got to be better than jelly snakes

### sooperb — 2014-04-26T20:25:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/activated-almonds-mmmm.10660/post-123531>

### Albert83BCN — 2014-04-27T14:35:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sparkling-mineral-water-and-its-benefits.10661/post-123575>

If I were to find a brand that does not have it would be nice but I doubt I'd find it on every bar I got to, too much effort from my part to ask for the brand every time, I think I'll stick with plain water with a slide of lemon as my staple :/

# Thread: Persecution of the Bushmen
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/persecution-of-the-bushmen.10665/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Gagnrad — 2014-04-27T13:50:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/persecution-of-the-bushmen.10665/post-123565>

This sorry tale of the attempted extermination of one of the last hunter-gatherer peoples continues.

> ... that haunting tragedy followed by this column since 1996: the ruthless drive of the Botswanan government to expel the last survivors of the original human inhabitants of southern Africa from the Central Kalahari Game Reserve (CKGR) – the area where the Bushmen’s right to live undisturbed in perpetuity was established by Britain in colonial days and guaranteed by Botswana’s constitution when it won independence in 1966.
>
> In 2006, ... most of the Bushmen had already been forced out of their ancestral lands to live in a degrading settlement they called “the place of death” ...
>
> What made the story even more disturbing was that **this attempt to wipe out their traditional way of life was defended by the European Union and by successive British governments**, eager to keep in with a country that, thanks to its **diamond reserves** (including those discovered in the CKGR), is, per capita, the richest in Africa.
>
> In 2008, Botswana’s new half-English president, Ian Khama, who likes to pose on the international stage as a “conservationist”, nevertheless made it a personal crusade to continue the **persecution of the Bushmen, denying them their right to access water or hunt for food**. Last summer, just before Mr Bennett was due to make another legal bid to allow the Bushmen access to water, he was barred from entering the country ...
>
> **Britain ... has not uttered a squeak of protest** against this crushing of the rights of \[a small\] and ... defenceless minority – rights that we ourselves, in more honourable days, wrote into the law of their land.

[http://www.telegraph.co.uk/news/wor...-turned-our-back-on-the-Kalahari-Bushmen.html](http://www.telegraph.co.uk/news/worldnews/africaandindianocean/botswana/10789457/Weve-turned-our-back-on-the-Kalahari-Bushmen.html)

See also:

[http://www.amazon.co.uk/The-Bushmen-Southern-Africa-Slaughter/dp/0701169060/](http://www.amazon.co.uk/The-Bushmen-Southern-Africa-Slaughter/dp/0701169060/)

# Thread: Craniofacial Development and other things
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/craniofacial-development-and-other-things.10667/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Gagnrad — 2014-04-27T17:11:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/craniofacial-development-and-other-things.10667/post-123584>

New post from Sally Fallon at the WAPF:

> The narrowing of the face that occurs after a population has abandoned its native diet and embraced Western processed food continues to perplex the medical and dental establishments, which just can’t seem to make the connection between food and physical development. In Dr. Price’s day, scientists blamed lack of facial development on “race mixing,” a premise that Dr. Price soundly denounced. Today orthodontists blame crooked teeth on everything from genetics to thumb sucking to soft foods—that is, everything but the obvious decline in nutrient density in modern diets. ...

Ain't that the truth.

Well worth reading -- there's a round-up of a few pieces of news in this post, including:

> A growing number of farmers are abandoning genetically modified seeds, but it’s not because they are ideologically opposed to the industry. They are switching back to non- GMO crops because they are more productive and profitable (_Modern Farmer_, January 2014).
>
> … the GMO seeds are not working as well as they did originally. “Five years ago the \[GMO seeds\] worked,” said farmer Christ Huegerich, who along with his father planted GMO seeds. “I didn’t have corn rootworm because of the Bt gene, and I used less pesticide. Now, the worms are adjusting, and the weeds are resistant. Mother Nature adapts.”

This reminds me of the comment in _Jurassic Park_: "Life will find a way."

[http://www.westonaprice.org/caustic-commentary/caustic-commentary-spring-2014](http://www.westonaprice.org/caustic-commentary/caustic-commentary-spring-2014)

# Thread: Evanescent Light Waves A new twist in the properties of light
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/evanescent-light-waves-a-new-twist-in-the-properties-of-light.10672/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Gracelind — 2014-04-28T11:07:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/evanescent-light-waves-a-new-twist-in-the-properties-of-light.10672/post-123633>

[http://phys.org/news/2014-04-properties.html#ajTabs](http://phys.org/news/2014-04-properties.html#ajTabs)

**A new twist in the properties of light**

Light has some well-established dynamical properties that have defined our understanding of electromagnetic radiation for over a century. Two of the most fundamental of these properties are that photons of light carry momentum in the direction of propagation, and a 'spin' about the propagation axis defined by the electromagnetic wave's circular polarization. These properties play critical roles in a range of everyday phenomena and experimental interactions between light and matter.

Konstantin Bliokh from the RIKEN Interdisciplinary Theoretical Science Research Group (iTHES) and Aleksandr Bekshaev and Franco Nori from the RIKEN Center for Emergent Matter Science have now made the remarkable discovery that a particular type of [light](http://phys.org/tags/light/) known as evanescent waves possesses unexpected dynamical properties that are in sharp contrast with previous knowledge about light and photons.

# Thread: Phases of matter
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phases-of-matter.10673/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Gagnrad — 2014-04-28T13:27:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phases-of-matter.10673/post-123646>

> Melvyn Bragg and his guests discuss the science of matter and the states in which it can exist. Most people are familiar with the idea that a substance like water can exist in solid, liquid and gaseous forms. But as much as 99% of the \[visible\] matter in the universe is now believed to exist in a fourth state, plasma. Today scientists recognise a number of other exotic states or phases, such as glasses, gels and liquid crystals - many of them with useful properties that can be exploited.
>
> With:
> Andrea Sella
> Professor of Chemistry at University College London
>
> Athene Donald
> Professor of Experimental Physics at the University of Cambridge
>
> Justin Wark
> Professor of Physics and Fellow of Trinity College at the University of Oxford

[http://www.bbc.co.uk/programmes/b03zdbr4](http://www.bbc.co.uk/programmes/b03zdbr4)

# Thread: Mattress Springs Acting As A Radiation Antenna ?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mattress-springs-acting-as-a-radiation-antenna.10677/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Terminator — 2014-04-28T22:35:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mattress-springs-acting-as-a-radiation-antenna.10677/post-123752>

Can mattress springs act as a radiation antenna ? I don't recall this being discussed here.
Should we be concerned ? Should we change box springs and mattresses that contain metal to wood and foam ? Are Magnetico Pads affected by metal parts of box springs and mattresses ?

[http://www.europeanbedding.sg/your-...mful-by-acting-as-radiation-antenna-a-55.html](http://www.europeanbedding.sg/your-mattress-could-be-harmful-by-acting-as-radiation-antenna-a-55.html)

### Tanya — 2014-04-28T22:55:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mattress-springs-acting-as-a-radiation-antenna.10677/post-123761>

> [Terminator said:](/goto/post?id=123752)
>
> Can mattress springs act as a radiation antenna ? I don't recall this being discussed here.
> Should we be concerned ? Should we change box springs and mattresses that contain metal to wood and foam ? Are Magnetico Pads affected by metal parts of box springs and mattresses ?
>
> [http://www.europeanbedding.sg/your-...mful-by-acting-as-radiation-antenna-a-55.html](http://www.europeanbedding.sg/your-mattress-could-be-harmful-by-acting-as-radiation-antenna-a-55.html)

> Thus, as we sleep on our coil-spring mattresses, we are in effect sleeping on an antenna that amplifies the intensity of the broadcast FM/TV radiation. Asleep on these antennas, our bodies are exposed to the amplified electromagnetic radiation for a third of our life spans. As we slumber on a metal coil-spring mattress, a wave of electromagnetic radiation envelops our bodies so that the maximum strength of the field develops 75 centimeters above the mattress in the middle of our bodies. When sleeping on the right side, the body’s left side will thereby be exposed to field strength about twice as strong as what the right side absorbs.

# Thread: Weston Price, Gerald Cox and Fluoridation
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weston-price-gerald-cox-and-fluoridation.10679/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Gagnrad — 2014-04-29T08:54:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weston-price-gerald-cox-and-fluoridation.10679/post-123833>

The WAPF has been on a roll recently. This is the third interesting blog they've posted in just a few days.

Cox I hadn't heard of. Here's the whole sorry saga of fluoridation. Cox it seems, spoke up for sugar when employed by the sugar industry and for aluminium when employed by the Mellon Institute, which had connections with Alcoa Aluminum. He was also an enthusiast for fluoride, which was a waste product for Alcoa Aluminum. Cox ended his professional career working for Corn Products Refining Company, at which time he claimed that corn oil was a cure for just about everything.

Weston Price's genius aside, the important difference between the men seems to be that Weston Price served others and Gerald Cox served himself.

A final odd note:

> Cox’s professional papers have not been preserved in any archive. In a 1969 letter he says that he has to “get rid of the many papers” from his office.

[http://www.westonaprice.org/notes-from-yesteryear/nutrition-fluoridation-and-dental-health](http://www.westonaprice.org/notes-from-yesteryear/nutrition-fluoridation-and-dental-health)

Keep scrolling -- there's a list of references, and then the article restarts again.

### sooperb — 2014-04-30T20:29:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weston-price-gerald-cox-and-fluoridation.10679/post-124112>

So many of those cause junkies about. Whoever pays gets their support! Bit like politicians, Tony Blair hasn't looked back since he left politics. How to make a fortune, ruin a country's economy and then everyone wants to hear how you did it and will pay handsomely for the privilege.

# Thread: Amesbury oldest town
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/amesbury-oldest-town.10713/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Gagnrad — 2014-05-01T20:03:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/amesbury-oldest-town.10713/post-124377>

Investigation shows that Amesbury in Wiltshire, England has been occupied continuously since 8820 BC, making it the oldest town in Britain.

[http://www.bbc.co.uk/news/uk-england-wiltshire-27238503](http://www.bbc.co.uk/news/uk-england-wiltshire-27238503)

The significance of this is that people were not waiting for the Neolithic Revolution to take place before "staying put" as the archaeologist who's interviewed nicely and idiomatically puts it:

> "It provides evidence for people staying put, clearing land, building, and presumably worshipping, monuments.
>
> "The area was clearly a hub point for people to come to from many miles away, and in many ways was a forerunner for what later went on at Stonehenge itself. ...

I was reminded of a book by the distinguished South African scholar David Lewis-Williams in which he argues (among a myriad of other interesting things) that farming may actually have come about _as a result_ of ritual activity, something that was originally suggested a long time ago. For example, if you want to sacrifice wild cattle, you first have to corral them. (That of course, turns the Marxist view of "Historical Materialism", popular in archaeology at one time, on its head, since in that view technological and economic change happens first and people's ideas follow along afterwards -- "base/superstructure".)

[http://www.amazon.co.uk/Inside-Neolithic-Mind-Consciousness-Cosmos/dp/0500288275/](http://www.amazon.co.uk/Inside-Neolithic-Mind-Consciousness-Cosmos/dp/0500288275/)


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: Misty Williams Tribute
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/misty-williams-tribute.10733/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 21
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### PaulG — 2014-05-03T02:59:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/misty-williams-tribute.10733/post-124806>

Sad news that Misty has departed. She was a loyal long term member of this community so I wanted to honor her enormous contributions over the years.

Highlights for me were:

Implementing the new forum in a few weeks from initial suggestion
The Optimal reset programme
Facilitating the monthly webinars and Q and A's
Supporting others in their journey
Her passion and humor

So thanks Misty and her team you will be greatly missed.

I have worked in a few companies and always get sad when colleagues you know and respect leave but I have always deplored the way that their name and achievements become unmentionable after they leave. This is a great community let's not fall into such behaviors.

> Last month, I notified Dr. Kruse that I will not be continuing on as the business manager for JackKruse.com after today (April 30).
>
> It has been my pleasure to support Dr. Kruse and all of you on your journeys to optimal. This has been more than a project for me … I have been on this journey with you. I’ve found a lot of joy in finding ways to bring in the right kind of support to help us along as we're decoding our health challenges and leveraging the incredible information Dr. Kruse is sharing with us. I’ve been moved by your stories and inspired by your resolve to change your lives!
>
> I’m especially grateful for the hard work my team has put in to improving this community and supporting each one of you. They’ve been champs.
>
> Thank you all for the love and gratitude and patience you’ve shown as we’ve all grown together. I will miss you!
>

### Nick — 2014-05-03T04:25:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/misty-williams-tribute.10733/post-124826>

Seeing Misty and Jack sipping from wine glasses, having lots of laughs, in some of the early webinar Q&A's will always be fondly remembered.

Thank you Misty for helping the journey begin for the community.

### tellmisty — 2014-05-03T20:14:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/misty-williams-tribute.10733/post-124903>

You guys make me teary.

Thank you so much ... I appreciate and respect each one of you, from the bottom of my heart. This journey is HARD. I know firsthand the dedication and commitment it takes to delve in and _sustain_ our journeys to optimal. I've heard from so many of you who have been beaten down and have felt hopeless as you try to get well, and it has been my honor to support you and to create space for all of us to get better together applying the revolutionary things Dr. Kruse has been teaching us over the last 3 years.

I am glad that you've noticed what we have worked so hard to do for you ... for all of us. It means so much; to my team, too. They are rock stars ... they really are. I don't even \*know\* of another team that does what we do. I really hope Dr. Kruse is able to find a great team to take things over. My team will continue to support Sandy (his wife), as needed, as she manages things until they have another team in place.

My next journey is packing up and moving to Austin ... I appreciate your good vibes; this is going to be a crazy month!

Namaste, my friends. xo

### Josh — 2014-05-03T20:33:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/misty-williams-tribute.10733/post-124904>

**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Lahelada — 2014-05-03T14:54:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/intl-emf-research.10736/post-124858>

Hope this is interesting despite the language barrier

### Lahelada — 2014-05-03T15:09:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/intl-emf-research.10736/post-124859>

[http://www.wavesguard.es/videos.html](http://www.wavesguard.es/videos.html)

More good videos. Some in English. I have no affiliation to the store

### Lahelada — 2014-05-07T21:09:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/intl-emf-research.10736/post-125677>

BAFFLED BIRDS...
[http://news.nationalgeographic.com/...ic-robins-henrik-mouritsen-science-broadband/](http://news.nationalgeographic.com/news/2014/05/140507-birds-migration-electromagnetic-robins-henrik-mouritsen-science-broadband/)

### Gagnrad — 2014-05-07T21:31:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/intl-emf-research.10736/post-125679>

The birds and the bees …

And bees?

> Early experiments revealed that bee colonies placed near electric fields became hyperactive. The bees, “in a strong electric field became aggressive, stinging each other to death.” They stopped producing brood and destroyed their own young. These bees would either completely abandon their hives or instead barricade every opening so none could enter or leave.
>
> Recent studies have found a correlation between colony collapse disorder and the EMF from cellular and wireless technology. A study was conducted where test honeybee colonies were subjected to cellular frequencies for 10 minutes daily. After only ten days of cellular EMF exposure, no worker bees returned to their hives and simply went missing. ...

[http://www.emfs.com/article/are-emfs-responsible-for-vanishing-bees](http://www.emfs.com/article/are-emfs-responsible-for-vanishing-bees)

That's from a site that sells EMF meters.

On the other hand, there's this:

> … there does not seem to be evidence of EMFs or power lines adversely affecting bees. Indeed, one study (abstract below) found that the strip of land along power lines in the USA, because it is protected from development, can be particularly attractive to bees.

[http://www.emfs.info/The+Science/Agriculture/bees/](http://www.emfs.info/The+Science/Agriculture/bees/)

That's from the UK National Grid.

Everyone has an interest ... but I guess in the end the truth will out. Of course, since bees are necessary for the pollination of crops, if they are being wantonly harmed we'll all be in trouble.

### Lahelada — 2014-05-07T22:13:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/intl-emf-research.10736/post-125683>

To continue the song:
And the flowers and the trees?
I unscientifically note that my i phone receives 3 wifis only whereas 5 metres fron rhe house the wifi stations already double.
How does it affect them and can they help us?
Any studies? My house is o vergrown with ivy and yucca.

### margor_j — 2014-05-04T06:41:08+0300

hi guys
Iv had my 23andme results come back and finally gotten to looking at them. Iv run them through 'genetic genie' and it comes up saying i have no active SNPs, but right at the top of the chart it says 'invalid file' - iv written to them but had no response. has anyone else had that experience? is this a good app? i was figuring the results would show me as messed up to buggery to, i dont believe i dont have something wrong with me, lol.

thanks!

### ssj3 — 2014-05-04T11:30:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/23andme-results.10744/post-124959>

Two things I can think of are:
1- File was corrupted when you downloaded it from 23andMe and you will need to re-download it.
2- From memory I think 23andMe data is downloaded as a zip file. If it is a zip file I think you will need to extract the text file from the archive and use that with genetic genie.

### fitness@home — 2014-05-06T12:34:21+0300

# Thread: Yikes! Med mix up.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yikes-med-mix-up.10745/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Dali Dula — 2014-05-04T16:48:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yikes-med-mix-up.10745/post-124998>

I am a 53 yr old male with T2D/Hashi's. So through some prescription error and me not checking labels I took what amounts to a double dose of Synthroid for six weeks. I am supposed to be taking 112 mcg Synthroid and 10mcg Cytomel. Cytomel scrip needed renewal my Doc's nurse ordered Synthroid instead of Cytomel. Scrip arrived in mail and I didn't check label. 50mcg Synthroid tabs look like 5mcg Cytomel. Thinking I was taking 10 mcg Cytomel I was taking 100 mcg Synthroid on top of my 112 mcg Synthroid for six weeks.
Thinking back I realized there were some symptoms like hot flashes, but the prominent effect was BG which has increased to it's highest level since going paleo 3 years ago. No heart palps though. I have also developed torturous acid reflux which massive amounts of betaine has done little to alleviate.

Endo said to lay off Synthroid for 3 days and resume. I have not taken it for 12 days now and except for bad heartburn every time I eat I feel okay and BG has come down some. Any thoughts?

### Shijin13 — 2014-05-04T16:52:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yikes-med-mix-up.10745/post-124999>

If BG has come backdown - I'd say you're ok to go back to your normal synthroid... though you might want to consider running thyroid labs to see where you're at before adding back in...

I'd also ask for the correct refill of cytomel.. and add them back in together....

I've also noticed that when my thyroid meds are too high or too low, my FBG goes nuts! I think its a great indicator to help thyroid patients zero in on the perfect dose for their meds...


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# Thread: summer beetroot brownies
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/summer-beetroot-brownies.10764/>
**Subforum:** Epi-Paleo Recipes
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### kovita — 2014-05-06T12:33:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/summer-beetroot-brownies.10764/post-125449>

this is not strictly epi-paleo recipe, but the period of carbs is coming with the long light cycles and these brownies are great and there are no nuts in them. I hope you will love it!
you need:

-   2 and half cups of grated beetroot
-   1 cup of honey
-   4 eggs
-   1/2 cup of coconut oil
-   a dash of salt

-   1/2 cup of raw cocoa powder
-   1/2 teaspoon of baking soda but works wihtout it
-   optionally handfull of raw cocoa nibs or freshly grated coconut

Cook the beetroot till it is soft, get rid of excess water and add the honey. in a blender blend together the beetroot with honey and all other ingredients till it has paste like consistency. Add cocoa, cocoa nibs or fresh coconut and stir well. Pour to the baking form over the baking paper. Bake for 40 minuts at 170 degrees Celsius. The brownies will be dense and wet so keep it in the fridge. Sometimes it has beefy colour,sometimes it is pinkish and sometimes more brownish depending on the beetroot quality and the amount of cocoa { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

### nicld — 2014-05-06T16:30:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/summer-beetroot-brownies.10764/post-125484>

Hmmm, I just planted beets again. Might have to try this. Thanks.

### caroline — 2014-05-07T01:27:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/summer-beetroot-brownies.10764/post-125555>

They do sound good .... but holey cow .....the sugar....

### nicld — 2014-05-07T04:11:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/summer-beetroot-brownies.10764/post-125576>

I am sure that you can cut down on the honey. I missed that the first time.

### caroline — 2014-05-07T04:38:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/summer-beetroot-brownies.10764/post-125582>

Beets have a lot of sugar too .....

### kovita — 2014-05-07T09:43:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/summer-beetroot-brownies.10764/post-125600>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=125582)
>
> Beets have a lot of sugar too .....

Caroline, this is summer recipe amd honey and beets are not too bad source of sugars. You can use stevia, but I personally dislike the taste. I usually add much less honey and it is still very good. I discovered during the past few weeks my body works better with 50-100g of some carbs per day, using vegetables and something like these brownies for example. My kidney function went to great from poor immediately when I changed to even more fat, low moderate protein and this amounts of carbs per day. I have A blood group and i never was really protein person, so it is confirmed now I do better on lower protein  It seems it may be helpfull for my serotonin in the toilet as well. As said many times each person is unique and diet is not static and must change as our body needs change.

### caroline — 2014-05-07T11:00:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/summer-beetroot-brownies.10764/post-125607>

I totally agree Kovita .... I only made the comment because some might not stop to think how much sugar is in beets.
It would not work for me - everyone needs to pay attention and figure out their own n=1.

I am so glad you are feeling better - I was worried about you.

High fat is so much better for me too ..... and low protein....

we need to pay attention to everything...

### bionaut — 2014-05-07T19:33:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bio-laser.10777/post-125659>

As cool as this looks I believe it is marketing. I bet you could achieve similar results with any 600-900nm laser. Water will charge separate in the red spectrum.

In the same vein, I purchased a intranasal light for hayfever that has given me decent results,
[http://www.amazon.co.uk/Medinose-45030-Hayfever-Allergy-Treatment/dp/B000BI69UQ](http://www.amazon.co.uk/Medinose-45030-Hayfever-Allergy-Treatment/dp/B000BI69UQ)

50$ vs a 400$-500$ vielight is a deal. It is simply an led attached to a circuitboard, so long as you can get lights within a certain spectrum why would we have to pay so much out of pocket for simple tech.

# Thread: Creatine and Water
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/creatine-and-water.10780/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Fitness
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Josh — 2014-05-07T21:26:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/creatine-and-water.10780/post-125678>

Quote from CPC 9....Comments

> D\_Ray_· [5 days ago](http://jackkruse.com/hashimotos-and-melasma/#IDComment824932277)_
>
> Relative to intra-cellular water, I've been experimenting with creatine lately to support my SAMe levels and to help with chronic sarcopenia. The positive result was a rapid weight gain of 25 pounds and better strength. However, I've read that creatine increases the overall water content in the body and changes the ratio of water between extra-cellular water and intra-cellular water in a negative way if used chronically and can lead to severe muscle cramps with intensive exercise.
>
> My experience seems to confirm that. After a while I began to get intense muscle cramps while bike riding and even while sleeping when I would stretch or change sleeping positions.
>
> Any thoughts on creatine relative to cellular hydration, i.e., good or bad?
> [jackkruse](http://jackkruse.com/)_· [5 days ago](http://jackkruse.com/hashimotos-and-melasma/#IDComment824935435)_
>
> D-Ray **_Creatine does help your cells retain water, which many meat heads think is good for performance. One huge problem.......it is not bound intracellular water to K+ as it should be in a cell. Therefore is is not used in energy transduction. It just increases mass of the muscle. What does increasing mass do? It makes you look better but it also drop energy production because of Einstein's mass equivalence equation._** So I am not fan of its use. In fact, many body builders do thing to look good and slowly kill themselves. This is one of them. Water has to be bound to proteins to get the energy benefit. One thing it is good for though it helps athletes with chronic muscle spasms.........but the effect is short lived.

### Josh — 2014-05-24T01:29:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/creatine-and-water.10780/post-128144>

> [persistence said:](/goto/post?id=128128)
>
> I guess everyone just talks their position on this controversial issue:
>
> [http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/18184753](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/18184753)

There is a difference between "position" and sound quantum physiological mechanisms. I found creatine to be almost a miracle for me and my raw vegan friends and attributed it to lack of it in our diets. However, everyone plateaued and often began to decline, why? Because I am guessing we had all crashed our redox and thereby had developed methylation issues. The creatine bypassed all of this and fueled our brains and muscles for a while until we used up our methyl groups and got in new trouble...not really a "position" issue, rather a lack of sound physiological mechanistic understanding applied to various N=1's.

The abstract suggests that the authors do not understand these mechanisms well and saw enough short-term results affecting the things they listed, that they concluded that the cramping isuue, etc. was not a problem. Well I did cramp a great deal and needed potassium and magnesium to balance it out. Again, why? My mitochondria were shot and I did not have enough magnesium and or potassium in my system. I find studies such as this are too broad to be of any use for people with challenged N=1, and dangerous for the longterm viability of healthier people because they support things without appearing to drill down to the fundamental physiology.

Far and away the most dramatic imporvements I have seen with creatine have been cognitive and as Dr. Kruse points out, it is an expensive fuel to use.

### bionaut — 2014-05-24T01:51:53+0300

Part of the reason that supplements can sometimes be problematic. We do not know what we do not know :/. Thanks for the cautionary tale.

### persistence — 2014-05-24T01:58:39+0300

Well I did cramp a great deal and needed potassium and magnesium to balance it out. Again, why? My mitochondria were shot and I did not have enough magnesium and or potassium in my system. I find studies such as this are too broad to be of any use for people with challenged N=1, and dangerous for the longterm viability of healthier people because they support things without appearing to drill down to the fundamental physiology.

I agree with the spirit of this, but maybe you are making this more difficult than it needs to be. Creatine may just be causing you to hold more water and diluting electrolytes in the process. So take more of the extracellular sodium and more of the intracellular potassium to keep things in balance, and the body just shed the excess beyond its real needs. Probably getting some magnesium and some bicarbonates into the electrolyte solution would be important if you wanted a really balanced electrolyte.

Like you, I sense that the cramping is due to an electrolyte balance issue. Until my own health crisis of the last six months, I had no idea that electrolytes were so critical to health and even a small imbalance can leave you virtually disabled.

### persistence — 2014-05-24T03:05:39+0300

You can create thousands of intelligent speculations based on understanding chemical processes. That's not a double blind study. Most theories don't test as theorized.

Rather than reading about methylation pathways and speculating on leading edge biochemistry, how about running a good electrolyte panel that shows your current values for:

sodium
potassium
chloride
CO2 = bicarbonates
magnesium
calcium

And if you want to get fancy and spend a bit more money, add Magnesium RBC and Potassium RBC to get the intracellular values of those two.

My guess is that 90% of the time muscle cramps will show up in some way in that test and probably correcting the electrolyte imbalance directly will fix it.

Resort to fancy science only after simple science fails to solve the problem.

I need help understanding why my LDL P and small particle counts are so extremely high and testosterone low. I usually eat between 40 and 70g of carbs per day and keep my daily calories to between 1700 and 2200 (6' 2" male, 206 lbs). I do crossfit 2x per week, sprint once a week, walk 5 to 6 miles a day. I don't snack, don't drink caffeine, and drink one or two glasses of wine a week. All beef is grass fed, eat seafood 4 or 5 times per week, and don't have any issues with excess appetite, so I normally eat two meals per day. I've been on my diet for about 5 months and have lost 4" from my waist and about 40 pounds. Generally I feel extremely healthy. I supplement with lugols iodine, 10 drops per day, transdermal magnesium, 40 sprays per day, 5,000 IU of D3 on days I don't get enough sun, a one a day vitamin, and fish oil.

### Josh (Paleo Osteo) — 2014-05-08T13:22:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ldl-particle-issues.10783/post-125819>

ditch crossfit and any sort of stressor.
if the ASI shows altered cortisol, it is most likely pregnenolone steal syndrome. its in one of jacks blogs, [http://jackkruse.com/hormone-cascade-101/](http://jackkruse.com/hormone-cascade-101/)

here is a main quote

Leptin resistance occurs first. Then insulin resistance happens next. This eventually leads to adrenal resistance. Cortisol is the stress hormone that allows for fight or flight syndrome (life or death). This is the hormone that allows you to run away from a hungry lion fast and live. Evolution always makes sure cortisol production stays ready for action at the expense of the other hormones that also are made from the same precursors. That precursor is pregnenolone. Pregnenolone is made from cholesterol, and cholesterol is made from LDL. So anytime the body is stressed or inflamed, it up-regulates cholesterol production to make more lifesaving hormones. It requires T3 and vitamin A as cofactors to complete this step. Blocking cholesterol production will increase cellular stress. This is why biochemically, to me, no statin drugs have ever made any sense under any circumstance in medicine. Moreover, this is why there is a chronic association of cancer to low cholesterol levels in the literature. If your cholesterol levels are low, you can not proper construct a mitotic spindle to pull apart your chromosomes correctly. This is why cancer rates have been shown to be higher in 11 studies on statins. This has been documented in the literature and the Great Cholesterol Con, by Anthony Colpo. In times of infection or stress, LDL levels always need to rise to protect the cell to make more cholesterol to make hormones and improve intracellular signaling.

i would start by ditching physical stressors like HIT/crossfit

### Melanie Procter — 2014-05-12T12:40:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ldl-particle-issues.10783/post-126426>

I was reading the Tilted Quilt post as I was researching cholesterol. It seems as it is important to both generate and hold a charge I think it would be interesting to do daily long term readings on ph to measure your "proton presence". In the Tilted Quilt blog, Jack wrote, "pH is a measure of proton presence. Protons are acids. Positively charged (+) protons gobble up the negative (-) electrons in our body to turn oxygen into water in our mitochondria. Failed oxygen delivery to tissues is the beginning of a spiral of descent into neolithic disease. As the photoelectric charge becomes reduced, less and less oxygen penetrates cells, creating bad news for you, and good news for your doctor!
Stress lowers your pH because you are losing electrons to your environment in the form of bio-photons."

This is starting to make sense to us now due to current blogs.

**Subforum:** Adrenal Rx and Leaky Gut Rx

### Clayton — 2014-05-09T00:28:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/maral-root.10788/post-125943>

I learned about this herb yesterday and how it can be used for cancer patients following chemo or radiation for its anabolic effects. It also acts as an adaptogen and has come pretty cool properties not seen with other adaptogens. Chinese and Russian olympic athletes apparently use it regularly

[http://www.chagatrade.ru/leusea.html](http://www.chagatrade.ru/leusea.html)

[http://www.planetherbs.com/michaels-blog/maral-root-a-lesser-known-adaptogen-from-russia.html](http://www.planetherbs.com/michaels-blog/maral-root-a-lesser-known-adaptogen-from-russia.html)

### ssj3 — 2014-05-09T01:18:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/maral-root.10788/post-125949>

Use it and like it a lot. Of all the touted herbals, this is It is one of the few that has noticeable and positive effects. I limit its use to heavy leg days.

### Clayton — 2014-05-09T02:02:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/maral-root.10788/post-125955>

Very cool. Do you have a good source? It's certainly not a well known herb, at least where I live. I've checked a few herbal dispensaries in Vancouver and they haven't even heard of it.

### SeaHorse — 2014-05-09T02:10:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/maral-root.10788/post-125958>

It's interesting that the three adaptogens in the second link…rhodiola, eleurhero and leuzea or maral root all grow in extremely stressful climates, are cold tolerant and in the case of maral root are long lived and slow growing….sounds like a good model for plants and people.

### ssj3 — 2014-05-09T02:20:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/maral-root.10788/post-125960>

I use a Maral extract from Barlowe’s Herbal Elixirs. I use it for its support to aid training performance.
I also find that Moomiyo works when I am ending an over-reaching stage of training and starting to feel beat up. Take it for 3-4 days with good results.

# Thread: Radio 5 Live could send robins on the wrong flightpath
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/radio-5-live-could-send-robins-on-the-wrong-flightpath.10796/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### HoneyChild — 2014-05-10T12:39:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/radio-5-live-could-send-robins-on-the-wrong-flightpath.10796/post-126167>

**Electromagnetic noise from AM broadcast signal could be disrupting the migratory flight paths of birds, say scientists**

The popularity of stations such as Radio 5 Live could be causing migratory birds such as the robin to lose their way, scientists have suggested.

Researchers have found that robins' in-built compass is disturbed by electromagnetic "noise" emitted by AM radio transmitters.

Professor Henrik Mouritson, of the University of Oldenburg in Germany, said: “Every piece of equipment with a plug, when you plug it in, it’s going to make electromagnetic noise at some frequency range.

“If you could sense this it would probably be like some kind of rock concert going on all the time.”

Migratory birds can sense the Earth’s magnetic field and use it as an in-built compass to help them fly in the right direction.

It has long been suspected that electromagnetic noise made by humans could be interfering with their compass.

The team made the discovery after putting migrating birds in a cabin. Because birds are so eager to migrate, they will jump in the direction they want to fly.

However over three years, the team were surprised to find that none of the birds tried to fly in the correct direction.

But when they shielded the cabin against radio waves with aluminium, the birds began to jump in their usual migratory direction.

“The frequency that we found effects the birds is the radio frequency AM band, from about 50 kHz to about 5 mHz, and that means that it is not mobile phones and it’s not power line,” said Mouritson “It’s radio signals.”

“It may be a localised effect which only effects them when they are in towns and cities and as soon as they go out of town they are fine.”

The research was published in the journal Nature.

[http://www.telegraph.co.uk/journali...ould-send-robins-on-the-wrong-flightpath.html](http://www.telegraph.co.uk/journalists/sarah-knapton/10814764/Radio-5-Live-could-send-robins-on-the-wrong-flightpath.html)

### Lahelada — 2014-05-10T16:26:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/radio-5-live-could-send-robins-on-the-wrong-flightpath.10796/post-126186>

Interesting that he says that it is not power lines or cell phones. What does not make sense to me is that we have had AM radio for decades so why did they not get confused before. Has the AM frequency been in so much demand recently? Thanks for posting.

### sooperb — 2014-05-11T20:50:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/radio-5-live-could-send-robins-on-the-wrong-flightpath.10796/post-126319>

UK robins are not migratory so in the UK they wouldn't be affected (not effected!)

# Thread: Seed Crackers
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seed-crackers.10798/>
**Subforum:** Epi-Paleo Recipes
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### fitness@home — 2014-05-11T02:47:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seed-crackers.10798/post-126236>

I miss crackers.

I can’t eat flax seeds due to sensitivity, so I had to drop the Mary’s Gone Crackers variety. Tweaked this recipe and I like it.

Basic Recipe:
1 C water
½ C chia seeds
½ C pumpkin seeds
½ C sesame seeds (didn't have any so dropped this ingredient and increased sunflower/pumpkin seeds)
½ C sunflower seeds
½ tsp sea salt (dissolved in the water)
Freshly ground pepper to taste
1 TBL seaweed sprinkles
¼ C chopped chives

Pre-soak the pumpkin, sesame, and sunflower seeds in spring water overnight. Use enough water to cover them. (I do not soak the chia seeds overnight because they would turn into a gelatinous glob) .
Drain the soaked seeds well.

\- Put 1C water in a blender or Magic Bullet and add chopped chives, sea salt, & seaweed sprinkles and blend well.
\- Combine ALL the seeds and the 1 C blended water in a bowl and allow to sit for 10 minutes to let the chia seeds become somewhat gelatinous.
\- Spread the batter onto a cookie sheet lined with parchment paper using a spoon. It should be less than ¼” thick.
\- Bake in preheated 350\* oven for 30 minutes and then slice into crackers using a pizza wheel.
\- Turn them over carefully to the other side and transfer back into oven for another 20-30 minutes.
\- Allow to cool completely before eating. Store in an air-tight container and it will keep up to a week.

I store portions in the freezer for later use.

Ideas for flavors: Mexican – chili powder, cumin, & cayenne pepper (Nacho chip replacement)
Greek seasonings – great with zucchini hummus
Savory – garlic clove, rosemary, thyme Italian – Italian herbs and a little Parmesan cheese
Slightly Sweet – ¼ raw honey dissolved in the water, cinnamon, nutmeg, ginger.

Will be experimenting ^^^

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

# Thread: Protein Recycling
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/protein-recycling.10820/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### seanb4 — 2014-05-13T14:13:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/protein-recycling.10820/post-126619>

Hey,

Over the last couple of weeks I have dropped down to one meal, in the afternoon, per day as it allows me consume adequate amounts of water in the morning on an empty stomach. I have noticed since doing this that I am freezing cold all day up until I eat, then I get less cold for a few hours.

Am I right in thinking that, when I have just eaten, my body has enough protein, from the food, to fold with water and thus create energy/heat. However this only lasts a short amount of time because my body is poor at recycling the protein and therefore runs out of energy causing cold/hypothyroid throughout the day?

This is also why 3 meals per day is recommended on the leptin reset to give you a steady flow of protein for super conduction throughout the day. Would this also require protein to be eaten at all 3 meals? Would fat not have the same effect?
