### Eric — 2014-06-08T19:08:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-cfb-apoptosis-spot-reduction-and-raising-rmr-via-cold-adaptation.11011/post-130173>

> [Danco3636 said:](/goto/post?id=130108)
>
> Eric. Do you have any study's / pictures that show the fat cells actually die-ing off and disappearing? How has your progress been with fat cells going away, apoptosis perminantly? Do you have scans showing any of this?
>
> How long do you say it takes for the dead fat cells to actually be metabolized and go away permanently? I have heard it is a few months or more after a treatment.
> I would imagine it may depend on ones redox and autophagy potential.
>
> Going to try my n=1 with the Gut Buster for an hour a day and measure skin fold measurements. I will also still be doing some CT each day and eating a monitored Epi Paleo ketosis diet along with IF.

Those studies are rare... in fact, I found only one, where what they did, they took subcutaneous fat biopsies, literally counted the number of fat cells in the sample, then calculated the total number of cells (in conjunction w/ a DEXA scan). I actually tried to get the same scientists to work w/ me on the Gut Buster Apoptosis experiment, but couldn't get it set up in time. (this was the third and _FINAL_ time I fatten up for any studies LOL!)

Most of the reports claim a bulk of the dead fat cells are reabsorbed within the first month, then the remaining over the next 1-2 months (so 3 months in total). Interestingly, the effect seems to happen a lot easier/faster in children, as they apparently have more saturated fats composing their fat; we all know what bacon grease vs cooking oils look like at room temp; obviously easier to spot freeze stomach fat, were it made mostly of saturated fats.

Another thing to consider, supposedly a fair number (I forget the percentage) of customers who did the cosmetic cryolipolysis treatments were not satisfied w/ the results. I'm guessing several possible reasons: 1. for whatever reason, they didn't get much spot reduction, 2. they got spot reduction, but the effects take so long to gradually take place (reabsorption), they didn't notice the change, and/or 3. they spot reduced significant fat, but it wasn't noticable, as they're carrying too much fat in general.

That's the thing -- I doubt this spot reduction will make much visible difference unless one is somewhat lean, perhaps even on the verge of having abs. (males ~ 15% BF, females ~ 20%) That's why spot reduction isn't something one should probably sit around and 'wait for' while watching the clock. If it happens and is noticeable, great. If not, oh well, it's not really the goal anyhow, even for those whose main concern is fat burning / weight loss (since general CT is the prime mover in that).

### Eric — 2014-06-08T19:11:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-cfb-apoptosis-spot-reduction-and-raising-rmr-via-cold-adaptation.11011/post-130174>

BTW, about the "fattening up" phase... It's amazing, the process.

First, I dread eating the crap foods, feel like hell, miserable all day when eating like that. Heartburn, headaches, terrible energy fluctuations, emotional swings, on and on and on.
I remember saying to someone, _"How do you 'normal' people eat like this???"_

But then, after a few weeks, I start craving that crap! And you start to notice the subtle addiction-like nature of sugar, processed junk carbs, and emotional-eating.

EDIT: it's ironic, how much cheaper the garbage junk food is. You can get 10 packs of ramen noodles for $1. My grocery bills were so much cheaper during that fattening phase.

It immediately brought to mind, the food / medical / drug 'cartel' we got going on:

1\. Cheap processed carb foods are one of the biggest cash-crops in the US.
2\. 2/3 of people are obese, ~ 90% health problems are 'lifestyle' related. (see # 1)
3\. Billion-dollar-a-year drug companies sell chemical substances to mask symptoms of # 2.

A self-perpetuating system, a conspiracy hidden in plain site.

# Thread: Psychobiotics....
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/psychobiotics.11012/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Josh — 2014-06-06T22:41:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/psychobiotics.11012/post-130021>

[http://www.nutraingredients-usa.com...-s-really-an-exciting-frontier-says-professor](http://www.nutraingredients-usa.com/Research/Psychobiotics-can-feed-gut-brain-health-axis-It-s-really-an-exciting-frontier-says-professor)

> It’s really an exciting frontier in medicine right now to try and understand the potential of how the gut microbiome can play a role in brain
> development and behavior and therefore we might be able to develop novel strategies targeting the gut microbiome, what we call psychiobiotics
> that may be useful in the treatment of certain mental disorders,” said professor John Cryan.

[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/23759244](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/23759244)

> **Abstract**
> Here, we define a psychobiotic as a live organism that, when ingested in adequate amounts, produces a health benefit in patients suffering from psychiatric illness. As a class of probiotic, these bacteria are capable of producing and delivering neuroactive substances such as gamma-aminobutyric acid and serotonin, which act on the brain-gut axis. Preclinical evaluation in rodents suggests that certain psychobiotics possess antidepressant or anxiolytic activity. Effects may be mediated via the vagus nerve, spinal cord, or neuroendocrine systems. So far, psychobiotics have been most extensively studied in a liaison psychiatric setting in patients with irritable bowel syndrome, where positive benefits have been reported for a number of organisms including Bifidobacterium infantis. Evidence is emerging of benefits in alleviating symptoms of depression and in chronic fatigue syndrome. Such benefits may be related to the anti-inflammatory actions of certain psychobiotics and a capacity to reduce hypothalamic-pituitary-adrenal axis activity. Results from large scale placebo-controlled studies are awaited.

# Thread: This has to fit somewhere and or I missed it already: NMDA and Ryanodine Receptors
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/this-has-to-fit-somewhere-and-or-i-missed-it-already-nmda-and-ryanodine-receptors.11017/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Josh — 2014-06-07T03:54:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/this-has-to-fit-somewhere-and-or-i-missed-it-already-nmda-and-ryanodine-receptors.11017/post-130053>

[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/23085028](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/23085028)

> [Eur J Pharmacol.](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/23085028#) 2013 Jan 5;698(1-3):186-92. doi: 10.1016/j.ejphar.2012.10.010. Epub 2012 Oct 17.
> **Hypothermia reduces calcium entry via the N-methyl-D-aspartate and ryanodine receptors in cultured hippocampal neurons.**
> [Phillips KF](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed?term=Phillips%20KF%5BAuthor%5D&cauthor=true&cauthor_uid=23085028)1, [Deshpande LS](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed?term=Deshpande%20LS%5BAuthor%5D&cauthor=true&cauthor_uid=23085028), [DeLorenzo RJ](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed?term=DeLorenzo%20RJ%5BAuthor%5D&cauthor=true&cauthor_uid=23085028).
> **[Author information](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/23085028#)**
>
> **Abstract**
> Hypothermia is a powerful neuroprotective method when induced following cardiac arrest, stroke, and traumatic brain injury. The physiological effects of hypothermia are multifaceted and therefore a better knowledge of its therapeutic targets will be central to developing innovative combination therapies to augment the protective benefits of hypothermia. Altered neuronal calcium dynamics have been implicated following stroke, status epilepticus and traumatic brain injury. This study was therefore initiated to evaluate the effect of hypothermia on various modes of calcium entry into a neuron. Here, we utilized various pharmacological agents to stimulate major routes of calcium entry in primary cultured hippocampal neurons. Fluorescent calcium indicator Fura-2AM was used to compare calcium ratio under normothermic (37 °C) and hypothermic (31 °C) conditions. The results of this study indicate that hypothermia preferentially reduces calcium entry through N-methyl-D-aspartate receptors and ryanodine receptors. Hypothermia, on the other hand, did not have a significant effect on calcium entry through the voltage-dependent calcium channels or the inositol tri-phosphate receptors. The ability of hypothermia to selectively affect both N-methyl-D-aspartate receptors and ryanodine receptors-mediated calcium systems makes it an attractive intervention for alleviating calcium elevations that are present following many neurological injuries.
>
> Copyright © 2012 Elsevier B.V. All rights reserved.


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: Grounding, Grass, and Sunshine works for horses, too
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-grass-and-sunshine-works-for-horses-too.11024/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Gracelind — 2014-06-08T02:45:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-grass-and-sunshine-works-for-horses-too.11024/post-130126>

[http://enduranceridestuff.com/blog/2014/01/12-14-weeks-horses-fit/](http://enduranceridestuff.com/blog/2014/01/12-14-weeks-horses-fit/)
**After 12 to 14 weeks off, this is why my horses are still fit….**

_The team concluded that horses on stall rest for 14 weeks lost fitness, as indicated by the increase in heart rate and blood lactate levels after the final SET. But more importantly, they noted, pastured horses were able to maintain a similar level of fitness as the stalled, exercised horses **in addition to having greater bone mineral content** at the end of the study.

Graham-Thiers concluded, “Letting horses be horses—as in putting them in their natural setting—allows them to maintain fitness better, which may make their transition back to competition easier and likely more successful.”_

\~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
Imagine that!

### Gracelind — 2014-06-08T03:10:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-grass-and-sunshine-works-for-horses-too.11024/post-130131>

Mariska, Houdini horse had the right idea: [http://blog.petflow.com/we-call-her-houdini-horse-after-catching-this-trickster-on-camera/](http://blog.petflow.com/we-call-her-houdini-horse-after-catching-this-trickster-on-camera/)

# Thread: Topical Steriod Addiction/Withdrawal or Red Skin Syndrome
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/topical-steriod-addiction-withdrawal-or-red-skin-syndrome.11064/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### LaurenR — 2014-06-13T11:52:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/topical-steriod-addiction-withdrawal-or-red-skin-syndrome.11064/post-130732>

I am creating this thread for myself and any others who are dealing with Topical Steroid Addiction, Withdrawal and Red Skin Syndrome.

I have recently learned that my eczema is in fact not eczema and a skin condition cause by chronic and long term topical steroid use (maybe even abuse).

Dr. Rapaport a leading expert in this field, claims all eczema in people over the age of teens is not eczema and is a skin condition caused by the steroid cream itself.

As I plan my withdrawal and healing, I hope to combine information from Dr Kruse (magnetism/light/water), ITSAN and other leading Dr's in the field (Japan is leading the way) and find a gentler and faster healing.

I hope this is useful and would love to hear what Jack thinks about all of this.

Dr Rapaprt talks about the syndrome in this clip.

Another vid from another doctor:
[http://www.itsan.org/eczema\_specialist\_Dr\_2\_I24V.html](http://www.itsan.org/eczema_specialist_Dr_2_I24V.html)

ITSAN site: [http://www.itsan.org/eczema\_questions\_answered.html](http://www.itsan.org/eczema_questions_answered.html)

# Thread: Grounding mats and EMF

**Posts:** 10

### JasonB — 2014-06-17T06:45:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-mats-and-emf.11098/post-131123>

From what I've read of the earthing literature, a grounding (or earthing) mat grounds you to the Schumann resonance field and essentially surrounds your body like an aura thus negating the immediate effects of any unnatural EMFs in the vicinity. Plausible, or total phooey? I see merits to both sides but I don't have the gear to really test this properly.

You see the guys on youtube that have their multimeters showing voltage on the skin from being around EMF sources that go immediately to zero when they touch ground. I'm a bit skeptical but have not done further research.

### caroline — 2014-06-17T08:26:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-mats-and-emf.11098/post-131128>

There has been a lot said here about grounding mats etc. They can draw EMF to you - you can wind up being microwaved ... not good!

Michael Neurt did an EMF bootcamp for us ...... check out the home page.

There is a lot of info in the podcasts about EMF etc.

### Josh (Paleo Osteo) — 2014-06-17T10:52:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-mats-and-emf.11098/post-131134>

> [JasonB said:](/goto/post?id=131123)
>
> From what I've read of the earthing literature, a grounding (or earthing) mat grounds you to the Schumann resonance field and essentially surrounds your body like an aura thus negating the immediate effects of any unnatural EMFs in the vicinity. Plausible, or total phooey? I see merits to both sides but I don't have the gear to really test this properly.
>
> You see the guys on youtube that have their multimeters showing voltage on the skin from being around EMF sources that go immediately to zero when they touch ground. I'm a bit skeptical but have not done further research.

it can immediately ground you and make acute change as displayed in the videos, but contact with it long term in an environment polluted by EMF can see it act as an antenna, pulling the non-native EMF to ground (via you)

### nonchalant — 2014-06-17T15:48:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-mats-and-emf.11098/post-131149>

Jason, I've had good and bad effects with them. Here in the city, when I was having trouble with my back, a grounding sheet connected to a rod in the soil outside did wonders for me. But after about 6 months, while my back had improved tremendously, I started getting adverse effects in other areas. My back was in good enough condition to stop the use. But out in the country, with much lower nn EMF levels, I continued to use one with good results.

I guess you could say your results would depend on your environment, and also your state of health.

### JasonB — 2014-06-17T20:17:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-mats-and-emf.11098/post-131172>

Interesting to know about the antenna effect. In that case at work I'll stop grounding as I have two big monitors in front of me, two big monitors at my coworker's desk six feet from my head. A WiFi antenna eight feet away....

At home I have a desk with two big monitors and a Wifi router even closer. My wife will be hard pressed to move it out of that room.
The bedroom is in the basement and the Wifi signals are fairly weak down there because of the ceiling. All the plumbing and electrical tends to weaken those fields between floors. Yes, this does not discount the fact that there is an electrical field in the walls.

**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/metametrix-comprehensive-stool-test-dental-infection-question.11110/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### kyrakitty — 2014-06-18T08:48:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/metametrix-comprehensive-stool-test-dental-infection-question.11110/post-131238>

Does anyone recall what to look for on the metametrix comprehensive stool test that indicates dental infection?

I remember one of the docs, may Dr. Tim or one of the dentists said if you have a dental infection something would come up in the metatetrix comprehensive stool test indicating that... but I can't find a note on what that something is to look for on the test.

My report came back and I want to see if the "flag" came up that indicates dental infection.

Metametrix does give some general notes on what some of my results could mean but I don't know if metametrix is hip to pointing out that something flanged could indicate dental infection.

Fingers crossed someone has this info readily available.

Thanks so much!

### Josh — 2014-06-18T22:26:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/metametrix-comprehensive-stool-test-dental-infection-question.11110/post-131298>

[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2897872/](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2897872/)

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

Consider what SIgA represents, gut microbiome, enough energy for the immune system, regulation of the immune system....

> The two principal antibody classes present in saliva are secretory IgA (SIgA) and IgG; the former is produced
> as dimeric IgA by local plasma cells (PCs) in the stroma of salivary glands and is transported through
> secretory epithelia by the polymeric Ig receptor (pIgR), also named membrane secretory component (SC).
> Most IgG in saliva is derived from the blood circulation by passive leakage mainly via gingival crevicular
> epithelium, although some may be locally produced in the gingiva or salivary glands. Gut-associated
> lymphoid tissue (GALT) and nasopharynx-associated lymphoid tissue (NALT) do not contribute equally to
> the pool of memory/effector B cells differentiating to mucosal PCs throughout the body. Thus, enteric
> immunostimulation may not be the best way to activate the production of salivary IgA antibodies although
> the level of specific SIgA in saliva may still reflect an intestinal immune response after enteric immunization.
> It remains unknown whether the IgA response in submandibular/sublingual glands is better related to
> B-cell induction in GALT than the parotid response. Such disparity is suggested by the levels of IgA in
> submandibular secretions of AIDS patients, paralleling their highly upregulated intestinal IgA system, while
> the parotid IgA level is decreased. Parotid SIgA could more consistently be linked to immune induction
> in palatine tonsils/adenoids (human NALT) and cervical lymph nodes, as supported by the homing molecule
> profile observed after immune induction at these sites. Several other variables influence the levels of
> antibodies in salivary secretions. These include difficulties with reproducibility and standardization of
> immunoassays, the impact of flow rate, acute or chronic stress, protein loss during sample handling, and
> uncontrolled admixture of serum-derived IgG and monomeric IgA. Despite these problems, saliva is an easily
> accessible biological fluid with interesting scientific and clinical potentials.
> Keywords: IgA; IgG; mucosa-associated lymphoid tissue (MALT); gut-associated lymphoid tissue (GALT);
> nasopharynx-associated lymphoid tissue (NALT); salivary glands; crevicular fluid; polymeric Ig receptor (pIgR); secretory
> component (SC); mucosal vaccination
>
> **_Salivary immunoglobulins in relation to disease_**
> Numerous studies have attempted to relate salivary IgA
> levels to a variety of oral as well as systemic diseases.
> Several reviews of such reports have been published but
> far from all available data can be considered conclusive
> (151153). This is true also for studies of dental caries or
> periodontal disease in patients with primary immunodeficiencies,
> particularly selective IgA deficiency (154),
> although the results of early studies tended to suggest
> an inverse relationship between salivary IgA activity and
> caries susceptibility (151). Distinct results may be masked
> due to the fact that the immune defense operates in
> multiple layers so the possibility exists for various
> compensatory activities to come into play. Thus, it is
> well known that salivary SIgM is increased when SIgA
> is deficient (12), and the compensatory IgM reacts
> with commensal oral bacteria such as S. mutans (155).
> In mice it has been shown that adaptive and innate
> immunity cooperate flexibly to maintain hostmicrobiota
> mutualism (156); IgA deficiency will after some time
> be compensated by innate defense mechanisms which
> protect the gut epithelium from penetration of commensal
> bacteria (157).

[http://www.journaloforalmicrobiology.net/index.php/jom/article/download/20401/26368](http://www.journaloforalmicrobiology.net/index.php/jom/article/download/20401/26368)

Amazing article on the subject

### fitness@home — 2014-06-18T22:59:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/metametrix-comprehensive-stool-test-dental-infection-question.11110/post-131299>

I had Dr. Tim review my GI Effects test and this was his feedback.

From my journal:

> **Prevotella & Fusobacteria** – Indicate a possible dental infection (and I just got compliments at my semi-annual dental appt!). If my gut was working efficiently these little guys would likely not make it through alive.
>
> Recommendation to seek a biologic dentist for treatment with Perioprotect [http://perioprotect.com/](http://perioprotect.com/) or Oral Ozone therapy [http://www.ozonefordentistry.com/DentalO.html](http://www.ozonefordentistry.com/DentalO.html) A quick search and I’ve located dentists in my zip code that utilize these treatments.

### Grmoline — 2014-06-20T12:41:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/metametrix-comprehensive-stool-test-dental-infection-question.11110/post-131497>

I got the same message from Dr. Tim - no problem with Provotella, but the Fusobacteria was high and suggested a dental infection. There's only one practice that I can find in the UK that do Perio Protect and they are too far away. I've gone with Livionex toothpaste and Biotene mouthwash, both of which break up biofilm in the mouth. I'm hoping that this treatment will show an impact when I repeat the stool test.

# Thread: YUMMY eating raw seafood your thoughts please
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yummy-eating-raw-seafood-your-thoughts-please.11118/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 20
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### kyrakitty — 2014-06-19T04:28:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yummy-eating-raw-seafood-your-thoughts-please.11118/post-131348>

I have been wanting to eat more seafood raw besides oysters. I want to eat raw shrimp, salmon, scallops. I purchase only wild caught seafood and I try to get it from Alaska vs pacific. However, all the wild caught seafood from Alaska is frozen and I am unsure about eating it raw as it isn't technically fresh caught. I typically stick with non-predator fishes as these are technically lower in toxins.

Questions for any one that wants to share their personal raw seafood selections:

1.  What kind of seafood do you eat raw?
2.  Is it wild caught or farmed?
3.  Is it fresh or frozen?

4.  What part of the ocean do you prefer your seafood from and why?
5.  How do you prepare it meaning do you slice it, add lemon, hot sauce or straight up?

\*\*\*\*let's all agree that all that participate in this thread is NOT endorsing for others to eat raw seafood but rather simply sharing what they personally do, that way no one here can be accused of anything or feel like they might get into trouble. Eating raw seafood is a personal choice one must research and make on their own decision on whether or not to do it.

### nonchalant — 2014-06-19T04:39:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yummy-eating-raw-seafood-your-thoughts-please.11118/post-131352>

Wild caught frozen sockeye salmon, purchased from particular stores.
Dice it, chew the meaty part and swallow the associated skin whole. (Chunks too large to swallow whole.)

### caroline — 2014-06-19T06:50:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yummy-eating-raw-seafood-your-thoughts-please.11118/post-131381>

dont forget about cans of sardines ....... a little easier on the budget
I buy oysters and shrimp
and salmon and scallops for ceviche.

I buy what I can get and what I can afford at my local fish market ......

It is great to think about all the other stuff you asked about I guess - but my budget dictates what I can do and I just do what I can.

### yewwei.tan — 2014-06-19T14:25:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yummy-eating-raw-seafood-your-thoughts-please.11118/post-131397>

Recently, I've tended not to eat my seafood entirely raw, but cooked on the outside slightly. This is more for taste and textural preferences more than anything else, for example, prawns are amazing with a crispy shell but with a raw centre.

As for my usual fare, I would do the following completely raw:

-   salmon
-   ahi tuna
-   bonito tuna
-   saba (a type of mackerel)
-   anchovies (fresh as white bait, or dried ones sold at Asian grocers)

Any other seafood I like cooked on the surface, leaving 80-90% of the fish raw. Recently it's been:

-   snapper varieties
-   mackerel varieties
-   prawns
-   fish roe (which we can get year-long here in Australia thanks to the various fishing regions)
-   occasionally some stingray / skate
-   and of course, oysters

But to be honest, I've eaten salmon maybe 3 times in as many months. Same with ahi tuna. The rest of the list is pretty much consumed daily.

I shop at the markets, and tend to buy whole fish from the fishmonger. As with most Australian fish, they are caught and transported on the same day. I don't think any of the fish are frozen, but even if they are, I think the most important factor is the time they are killed to the time they are sold to you. By that metric, Aussie fish stocks are pretty damn fresh.

Most fish here is also wild caught. The prawns are all wild caught from all over the island.

I personally like to cut my fish sashimi style (I've got a japanese single-beveled yanagiba ) just for convenient consumption with chopsticks, and to practice my knife skills. Usually it's just some salt for me, and usually not much oil used during cooking (if there is, it would be coconut oil).

My general rule is to eat small and medium sized fish over large fish, partly because of the mercury bioaccumulation issue, partly because that's the sort of fish that is available here in australia, and partly because I think I did some back-of-the-knapkin calculations once, and figured that smaller fish have more DHA per unit mass (I'm not sure if that is true though). Same with prawns, I like the smaller varieties, mainly because they are easier to eat whole (shell, head, and all). These smaller fish then tend not to be deep water fish.

Budget wise, it hasn't been too difficult. Shopping at the fish markets is way cheaper. Typical prices would be:

-   $15/kg for a whole salmon
-   $25/kg for ahi tuna
-   $10/kg for bonito tuna
-   anywhere from $8-12/kg for mackerel
-   anywhere from $5-10/kg for snapper
-   $15-18/kg for wild caught prawns (I typically go for small tigers, endeavours, or vannemay prawns)
-   $7-8/kg for fish roe
-   $5/kg for "white fish" like blue grenadier, skate, etc ...
-   $12-13/dozen for fresh oysters, $9/lbs for raw frozen oysters

In any case, my monthly spend on food has consistently been about $350-400/month, and that's being a 81kg/180lbs male who's eating at least 0.5kg of seafood every single day for the last 8 months, with oysters being eaten 4-5 times a week in the last 2 months.

### Inger — 2014-06-19T20:45:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yummy-eating-raw-seafood-your-thoughts-please.11118/post-131422>

> Questions for any one that wants to share their personal raw seafood selections:
>
> 1.  What kind of seafood do you eat raw?
> 2.  Is it wild caught or farmed?
> 3.  Is it fresh or frozen?
>
> 4.  What part of the ocean do you prefer your seafood from and why?
> 5.  How do you prepare it meaning do you slice it, add lemon, hot sauce or straight up?

1\. Any seafood

2\. Both, I sure prefer wild if I get.. I eat some farmed Norwegian salmon once in a while, others are all wild except oysters and mussels

3\. Both. Again, if I get and can afford fresh, I buy that. But I cannot always so I have to do frozen Pollock.... raw. I also freeze mackerel if I get many.

4\. North Atlantic. Any place that is less populated and polluted. But I still eat raw Baltic Herring because they are cheap, especially when I am tight on money

5\. I slice, mostly add raw organic and unfiltered ACV, some sliced raw onion and little salt, sometimes seaweed too, sometimes avocado. If non fatty fish I use EVOO. Raw fermented sauerkraut actually is nice with raw salmon too!
Heads I make into smoothie if they are soft enough

I am very easy girl and eat like a caveman  and always the same stuff, I never get bored, even if same stuff every single day. lol

### yewwei.tan — 2014-06-20T09:49:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yummy-eating-raw-seafood-your-thoughts-please.11118/post-131484>

>
> "My general rule is to eat small and medium sized fish over large fish, partly because of the mercury bioaccumulation issue" Good to know thank you!

The way I like to cook prawns is just to take them directly from the freezer, and then cook then in a dry frying pan until the shell turns slightly brown. Because they are frozen, the inside is still nice and raw after cooking.

I personally find prawns pretty easy to chew whole. Any prawn that is smaller than 20 to a pound should be pretty manageable, though I've eaten of the huge tigers shell (like 5 to a pound) and all as well. Variety of prawn matters too, and I find the tiger prawns in Australia to have pretty soft shells relative to the endeavours. Then again, I've got a big mouth

Sidenote: I would reckon that the vast majority of DHA in a prawn is in their head. Eat the heads people! Snip of the head spike (if any) if you're afraid of getting cut.

I would have thought it would be a simple matter of taking 24.2 / 18.4 = 1.3152 which is a pretty low ratio.

### caroline — 2014-06-20T11:16:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yummy-eating-raw-seafood-your-thoughts-please.11118/post-131488>

I just ate raw octopus with some wasabi and ginger ...yum!

### nonchalant — 2014-06-20T16:25:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yummy-eating-raw-seafood-your-thoughts-please.11118/post-131524>

Kyra, if I cut up the fish in cubes so that each cube has some skin, I can't swallow the chunks. So I pick up the chunk, bite off most of the meat and chew that yummy stuff, then before swallowing I toss in the bit of skin (with scales). It's doable, but oysters are easier whole.

### Jonathin — 2014-06-30T05:46:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yummy-eating-raw-seafood-your-thoughts-please.11118/post-132694>

One experience - I got some particularly fatty salmon from Scotland. We ate it raw cut into cubes. It was like eating avocado! Nice texture. The skin was thick and rubbery unlike sockeye. Just cut that up in pieces and swallowed without chewing.

### margor_j — 2014-06-30T06:12:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yummy-eating-raw-seafood-your-thoughts-please.11118/post-132696>

WELL, here in Thailand... I went to the big organic supermarket last week, and found oysters!! theyre really big and meaty, but 55 baht per punnet (about a dozen) which is around $1.50 AUD.. yes, i eat them raw (no cooking facilities in accommodation with in the old city in Chiang Mai because its a protected 'site') wow, talk about gagging, lol.. i literally throw them up, its a big mental exercise, especially at 7am, but i refuse to let that stop me!!
Last nite, at the sunday markets, I had an octopus leg and two king prawns (cooked on a dry bbq) - I knew the ocotpus would be chewy, but its seafood! then i had some fish fillets from a guy.. wow, they were amazing! so good, i bought more (i had tin foil with me! sneaky aye!) and wrapped them up and had them for breakfast with my BPC! They were cooked..

Il go back to the organic store today and look at fish fillets, but from what i saw last week, its predominantly things like prawns / crustaceans / shell fish.. which i know il struggle to eat raw because i dont know how old they are, and il get a mental block..  i can try i guess..

### Josh (Paleo Osteo) — 2014-06-30T11:35:26+0300

# Thread: Mercury after two years epi-paleo.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mercury-after-two-years-epi-paleo.11119/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 10

### Dali Dula — 2014-06-19T15:21:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mercury-after-two-years-epi-paleo.11119/post-131400>

In discussions about the epi paleo diet and seafood consumptions concerns about mercury often arise. At 53 I had never had a mercury test, but after two years of epi paleo I thought I should. My result is 4 ng/ml. With a lab range of 0-9. I incorporate seaweed and many herbs reputed to have chelating properties in my food. Stay the course.

# Thread: Urine specific gravity
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/urine-specific-gravity.11150/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Melanie Procter — 2014-06-22T01:28:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/urine-specific-gravity.11150/post-131687>

Jack talks about this in the redox potential blog. Jack says when it is high, one's redox potential is low. Does this mean it is "heavy" or have a "high surface tension"?

### Melanie Procter — 2014-06-22T01:39:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/urine-specific-gravity.11150/post-131688>

Jack also talks in this blog about lacking infrared light in the cells can lead to low LDL under 200. I wonder if this is connected with lack of sunlight and even human touch. I though I had heard from a physicist before that we can collect infrared light from touch or even proximity to other humans or animals.

### nonchalant — 2014-06-22T03:38:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/urine-specific-gravity.11150/post-131705>

> [Melanie Procter said:](/goto/post?id=131687)
>
> Jack talks about this in the redox potential blog. Jack says when it is high, one's redox potential is low. Does this mean it is "heavy" or have a "high surface tension"?

I think it means the urine is dark and concentrated, like when one is dehydrated.

### Melanie Procter — 2014-06-22T03:57:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/urine-specific-gravity.11150/post-131706>

Wikipedia says this.
One of the main roles of the [kidneys](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kidney) in humans and other mammals is to aid in the [clearance](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Clearance_\(medicine\)) of various water-soluble molecules, including [toxins](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Toxins), [toxicants](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Toxicant), and [metabolic waste](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metabolic_waste). The body [excretes](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Excretion) some of these waste molecules via[urination](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Urination), and the role of the kidney is to [concentrate](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Concentration) the urine, such that waste molecules can be excreted with minimal loss of water and nutrients. The concentration of the excreted molecules determines the urine's [specific gravity](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Specific_gravity). In adult humans, normal specific gravity values range from 1.015 to 1.024.

But Jack is saying that the mitochondria and proteins can't transfer energy efficiently if the urine specific gravity or osmolality is high. Here is a photo but can't say I understand it.

[http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Osmoti...cytes\_OsmoticPressure\_PhaseContrast\_Plain.svg](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Osmotic_pressure#mediaviewer/File:Human_Erythrocytes_OsmoticPressure_PhaseContrast_Plain.svg)

Here is an explanation of tonicity.
**Tonicity\[[edit](http://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Osmotic_pressure&action=edit&section=5)\]**
Main article: [Tonicity](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tonicity)
Osmotic pressure is an important factor affecting cells. [Osmoregulation](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Osmoregulation) is the [homeostasis](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Homeostasis) mechanism of an organism to reach balance in osmotic pressure.

-   [Hypertonicity](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tonicity#Hypertonicity) is the presence of a solution that causes cells to shrink.
-   [Hypotonicity](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tonicity#Hypotonicity) is the presence of a solution that causes cells to swell.
-   [Isotonicity](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tonicity#Isotonicity) is the presence of a solution that produces no change in cell volume.

When a [biological](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Biological_tissue) [cell](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cell_\(biology\)) is in a hypotonic environment, the cell interior accumulates water, water flows across the [cell membrane](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cell_membrane) into the cell, causing it to expand. In[plant cells](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Plant_cell), the [cell wall](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cell_wall) restricts the expansion, resulting in pressure on the cell wall from within called [turgor pressure](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Turgor_pressure).

**Applications\[[edit](http://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Osmotic_pressure&action=edit&section=6)\]**

### Melanie Procter — 2014-06-22T04:05:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/urine-specific-gravity.11150/post-131707>

So, I am guessing if one has a lot of salt, or coffee or something to dehydrate the cell, they would have hypertonicity. The cells would then shrink. Hypotonicity would cause the cells through something like hydration. So, I am guessing that this is why high osmolality would indicate something like cellular dehydration. I am just taking a shot at this.

### Jonathin — 2014-06-22T07:02:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/urine-specific-gravity.11150/post-131714>

For awhile I was checking my urine with a refractometer. That gives you a refractive index that pretty closely corresponds to the percent of dissolved solids. I've used it in agriculture for years determining the sugar content of plant sap and fruit. Anyhow, I've had a lousy BUN/creatinine ratio for a long time and when I started drink lots of water, I could monitor some changes. If I drank about 1.5 qts. of water before breakfast I could get my urine down to about 1 or slightly lower. First pee in the morning was about 4 typically. Now I more often rely on the amount of free liquid under my tongue as an indicator to remind me to have a pint or two to drink right now especially in the summer. I live in a high and dry climate so it seems doubley important.

### Melanie Procter — 2014-06-22T07:59:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/urine-specific-gravity.11150/post-131715>

This is a great measure Jonathin. I am guessing this refractometer is a color guide online. How did u check your bun/creatine ratio? We can only do this with a doctor, right?

### Melanie Procter — 2014-06-22T08:00:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/urine-specific-gravity.11150/post-131716>

Oh, I just found this.
[http://www.topendsports.com/testing/tests/urine-refractometer.htm](http://www.topendsports.com/testing/tests/urine-refractometer.htm)

### Melanie Procter — 2014-06-22T10:32:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/urine-specific-gravity.11150/post-131720>

> [Jonathin said:](/goto/post?id=131714)
>
> For awhile I was checking my urine with a refractometer. That gives you a refractive index that pretty closely corresponds to the percent of dissolved solids. I've used it in agriculture for years determining the sugar content of plant sap and fruit. Anyhow, I've had a lousy BUN/creatinine ratio for a long time and when I started drink lots of water, I could monitor some changes. If I drank about 1.5 qts. of water before breakfast I could get my urine down to about 1 or slightly lower. First pee in the morning was about 4 typically. Now I more often rely on the amount of free liquid under my tongue as an indicator to remind me to have a pint or two to drink right now especially in the summer. I live in a high and dry climate so it seems doubley important.

What kind of water were you drinking?

### Jonathin — 2014-06-23T03:41:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/urine-specific-gravity.11150/post-131828>

I drink RO water when I don't have any more stored rainwater in barrels from spring storms and snow melt. I filter the rainwater through a Berkey just to remove minor floaty stuff.

# Thread: cozze bianco
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cozze-bianco.11153/>
**Subforum:** Epi-Paleo Recipes
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Josh (Paleo Osteo) — 2014-06-22T11:10:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cozze-bianco.11153/post-131723>

my father used to make this for us regularly...it is a very quick meal, one of my all time favoutites, and epi paleo friendly

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

couple lugs of best quality olive oil
1 white onion
2 cloves garlic
handful freshly torn or roughly chopped parsley
splash dry white wine
1 kg mussels
lemon, to serve
(if tolerated, splash of cream to finish)

heat the olive oil in a deep dish that has a lid
gently sautee the onion and garlic until soft
add salt and pepper, and turn up the heat
splash in some white wine and immediately add the mussels
cover, and steam for 5 minutes until all mussels are open
uncover, splash in the cream if tolerated, the parsley, and the juice of half a lemon

ladle into deep bowls and serve with a lemon wedge

### caroline — 2014-06-22T12:19:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cozze-bianco.11153/post-131733>

lovely! thanks for sharing Josh.....

### Josh (Paleo Osteo) — 2014-06-22T13:20:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cozze-bianco.11153/post-131740>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=131733)
>
> lovely! thanks for sharing Josh.....

had it tonight with local mussels, felt compelled to share!

# Thread: Fish head smoothie recepie
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fish-head-smoothie-recepie.11162/>
**Subforum:** Epi-Paleo Recipes
**Posts:** 20
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### kyrakitty — 2014-06-23T05:14:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fish-head-smoothie-recepie.11162/post-131844>

So I picked up 3 fresh sardines and I would like to make a fish head smoothie from the heads... I've searched the forum but having problems finding an actual recepie... that will taste good for a niewbie. I am guessing the fish head is left raw with nothing removed right? :/

### caroline — 2014-06-23T05:25:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fish-head-smoothie-recepie.11162/post-131849>

I just use small frozen fish that I get from a chinese market. I thaw a little and then chop up a bit and throw in my chopper thing with cold water and fresh lemon juice and fresh herbs - like corriander. Use what herbs appeal to you.

whatever takes your fancy really ....I suppose some green onions would be nice.....

I do insist on a mouthful of malbec afterwards however!

Just don't make it too big the first time ...........just enough to scull down! It really tastes okay BTW.

### kyrakitty — 2014-06-23T06:07:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fish-head-smoothie-recepie.11162/post-131851>

These sardine heads are like 2 1/2" long. They were previously frozen and slightly thawed when I purchased them yesterday. Today they were still really cold a tiny bit of ice. I don't know if I can do it. I wold prefer they had been frozen and slightly thawed like you do and smaller. Do you use the whole body and head since the fish are small or is it only the head. I have no lemon which would probably help. I may chicken out

### Andrea — 2014-06-23T07:36:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fish-head-smoothie-recepie.11162/post-131856>

The mackerels I've come across so far have about as big heads as your sardines. One of these heads is enough for me for dinner.
I cut them in a few pieces and put in my blender with a little water. I use them thawed; it's easier to get a somewhat smooth result. The only seasoning I use is a little black pepper on top - I quite like it that way.

### kyrakitty — 2014-06-23T08:25:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fish-head-smoothie-recepie.11162/post-131860>

OMG I completely chickened out! I wasted good food ugh! Next time I will do it!

### caroline — 2014-06-23T08:33:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fish-head-smoothie-recepie.11162/post-131863>

Kyra - when you use lemon and herbs it is kind of like ceviche ...and black pepper is a great idea too.
The reason to use them slightly frozen .....I think it would be totally gross to have a warm MHS!!!

just don't make it too big the first time .......I really just taste the lemon and herbs ...honest!!!!

### caroline — 2014-09-03T01:21:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fish-head-smoothie-recepie.11162/post-139726>

They are just small frozen fish ...maybe 6-8 inches.

I just take 1 out of the freezer and let it sit on a plate for a bit. And then chop it into chunks and blitz with the other stuff.

The great thing about this is ....it is still partly frozen ...so you get a nice cold drink. A MHS would suck if it wasn't ice cold me thinks!

This way you get brains, liver etc.

the smoothie is way easier... and faster too. Eating a mackerel head might be a bit hard as it is not too soft and small either....

Please share if you have tried it, how was it?

# Thread: grrrr I wanna sleep!!!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grrrr-i-wanna-sleep.11177/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 20
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### margor_j — 2014-06-24T19:07:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grrrr-i-wanna-sleep.11177/post-132047>

hi guys..
these last few days have been a joke.. ugh, insomnia like mad! and its making me mad, literally crazy! iv just been asleep for around ten minutes, and now im awake again (11pm) had myself all set up for like 10.30 lights out.. took melatonin earlier tonite. did some hard core qi kung.. which made me feel sooooo relaxed.
Iv cut back on BP coffee.. iv discovered thai green tea (by accident) BUT folks, green teas never kept me up going hard core in my head til 4.30am (friday nite)
iv had acupuncture (saturday) , iv had my neck 'cracked'... AGGGHHHH
Im also earthing, twice a day, in the park. up to 2 hours a time.. im doing eft, im doing affirmations, relaxation etc..
yes to BAB, yes to epi paleo - and yes to a few more carbs lately - nothing too sinister. also cos i feel so dam crappy.. and im post period.. so.... wtf gives?

tomorrow im back to hospital to get my test results .. cant wait to see what that shows.

oh, and i started doing cold face baths! i worked out a system! keep a bottle of water in the fridge, buy ice from 7-11, add both to the hand basin! hey presto!! hello vagus nerve!

i was able to get these chinese herbal sleepers of an acupuncture dude in KL.. (which have saved my ass during this 4 years of insomnia) but the company has stopped making them.. thats why i mention melatonin.. which i was getting sooooooooo low on dosage of, to the point of not taking it - even if i was drinking BP coffee.. yeah! see? madness.. WHY this week? parasites? sex hormones? and yes, im in teh same accommodation here in thailand - i do wonder about that to, but iv slept here before.. arrggghhhhhh :-/

heres my thought ' you dont know crazy til you cant sleep'

rant done, #insomniac

### Lahelada — 2014-06-24T20:17:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grrrr-i-wanna-sleep.11177/post-132050>

hi hi!
oh is that what melatonin does? tho.. i have been as low as like .5mg or sommit.. hmmm the 'bonus' in this situation is, im not working atm - holiday time,, so... thats one less stressor..
half the time, it feels like 'busy head' as opposed to the bashing heart that i used to have.. hmmmm cant seem to shake it this time though.. grrr thanks for your replies tho!

:-/

Oh and thinking about sun where did I stuff last years bikini? Must be in the laundry already. My,how can one pwrson have that much laundry. Taking all tgat to the laundry feels like exploitatoon.

nah, its not specific thoughts.. its hard to describe.. but right now, its nearly 1am and im just like.. AWAKE! i find i am sooo easily stimulated to - night markets, busy days etc.. its like they make me take forever to wind down.. and now, cos iv suffered at the hands of insomnia for ... must be about 4 years now, its fully entrenched in my head that i 'cant sleep'

### margor_j — 2014-06-24T20:44:42+0300

### margor_j — 2014-06-24T22:24:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grrrr-i-wanna-sleep.11177/post-132066>

yah im wondering abou parasites myself.. seriously.. well, il be picking up metametrix test this weekend..
and as for coffee, personally, BPC has been amaze balls for making life REALLY easy! but like, yesterday i had meat for breakfast (love getting on my bike in thailand and riding to a market nearby and getting fresh meat on a stick!) its hot, so i drink more water than tea , and i dont do the caffeinated BP coffee daily. def mix it up with BP cocoa and / or decaf.. its just toooo amazing to make that in minutes and be set for hours..
maybe the higher carbs iv had have done someting to - like fed any nasties.. i was feeling so shit tho, and then i got an urge for a smoothie.. and my 'higher carbs' isnt off the charts on a paleo carbometer, but, its still been more than ive eaten in a while.. hmmmm #bugger.

2.30am and HERE I AM!

# Thread: Experiments in fasting
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/experiments-in-fasting.11180/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Fitness
**Posts:** 14
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### sjoshua — 2014-06-25T02:06:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/experiments-in-fasting.11180/post-132089>

This past weekend I participated in a Cycle for Life bike ride to raise money for Cystic Fibrosis. The [ride](http://www.strava.com/routes/105147) was in Half Moon Bay, right along the ocean coast. It was a beautiful day, and the fog rolled out just in time for us to pedal. The sites were fantastic, the downhills were mad, and the climbs were... well, as fun as a climb can be

The reason I am sharing is not because I participated, but moreso how I participated. For the three days leading up to the ride, I barely ate anything (~50% of one typical day's food over the 3 day stretch, estimate). Not so much intentionally, but moreso because I didn't need nor want it. Also relevant is that I've been on vacation, which means spending my time away from technology. I spent my time bicycling around and laying in the grass and climbing trees at the local parks with some friends.

There was no point in the ride where I ever 'crashed' despite the fasted state. There was no muscle soreness during or after the ride. The first meal I ate afterwards was ~6 hours later, and it wasn't that I was hungy.. I just knew it was time to re-fuel. When your body works, it works.

Oh yea, and I started prototyping a new invention over the weekend... you can see it on my bike if you look realll close! (no spoilers yet)

Here's our team (I'm on the far left): [https://www.dropbox.com/s/oxu8z8ddetdxafn/Team\_Missy-llaneous\_Miles-2014.jpg](https://www.dropbox.com/s/oxu8z8ddetdxafn/Team_Missy-llaneous_Miles-2014.jpg)
Shenanighans on the podium with some teammates after the ride: [https://www.dropbox.com/s/c3sohs828hpq4o8/CM-JS-AM-MP.jpg](https://www.dropbox.com/s/c3sohs828hpq4o8/CM-JS-AM-MP.jpg)

### sjoshua — 2014-06-25T02:56:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/experiments-in-fasting.11180/post-132100>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=132090)
>
> nice....
> How was your weight? stay the same? How was your water intake?

I drank some water, not much though. Less than 12 oz for sure, prolly less than 8... Funny thing is, the only reason I even brought the camelbak was to carry some extra water in case the prototype needed a re-fill.
I haven't weighed myself in weeks. As my posture has been correcting and I've been consistent with my weekly pull ups, my weight has naturally shifted to around 160lbs. If I had to guess, nothing changed... I really don't sweat much aside from covered areas i.e. under my camelbak where the skin can't breathe.

> [yewwei.tan said:](/goto/post?id=132091)
>
> Thanks for sharing! Are you barefoot? (is this some way to stay grounded while on the bike?)

I am barefoot as often as possible, but wore vibrams while pedaling for a little cover on my feet in case of errors. Grounding while on a bike would be great, but I don't want to risk connecting myself to dirty power, so this is still a thought project.

> [Josh said:](/goto/post?id=132099)
>
> I am thinking it is a bar cooling system to cool your hands and the blood like the hypothermic glove???

Eating oysters lately I see

# Thread: WOW, lots of labs
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wow-lots-of-labs.11184/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Shellie — 2014-06-25T04:38:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wow-lots-of-labs.11184/post-132135>

First some background. I am 63 (almost 64) YO female, overweight at 160 #s on my 5'6 1/2" frame (ideal would be around 130 #s). I am menopausal. I have had lots of labs within the last 6 months. Since my health has been unchanged since then, I will use these values.

I just realized that I probably need to add more context so my labs make sense. My number one problem is insomnia. Most nights I get 4-5 hours of sleep. I rarely dream and can only get to sleep by using some sublingual melatonin & magnesium oil transdermally . I also sometimes use the hot-tub in the evening because the drop in body temp after the hot-tub helps to get me sleepy. My energy levels are very low as is my motivation. I have Hashimotos and have been hypothyroid for 40 years. Taking thyroid meds, either synthetic or natural or using desiccated glandulars has not helped. My adrenals are also exhausted and have been for many years. I have tried hydrocortisone treatments, glandulars, herbs and adaptogens, none of them make any difference. I feel the same whether I take or don't take medications/ supplements. I have osteopenia bordering on osteoporosis and degenerated disks at the L4/L5 region. My memory is also affected, both short and long term. Libido is dead and has been for many years. I'm a mess!!

Please see the below list for my labs.

# Thread: Hot head
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hot-head.11185/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### nicld — 2014-06-25T05:03:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hot-head.11185/post-132139>

Not sure if this means anything but the past couple of days the top of the head has been feeling really hot, very very strange. I know that I have been having to think more at work the past two day but that should not be making the head hot.....hehehehe.

Anybody have any ideas?

I even put a cold pack on the top of the head and it did feel good. I am sure that it does not help that I think it is hot here.

### caroline — 2014-06-25T05:26:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hot-head.11185/post-132142>

interested to hear thoughts on this.....

### Jonathin — 2014-06-25T06:04:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hot-head.11185/post-132152>

### nicld — 2014-06-25T16:33:12+0300

^^^^Nope. I have actually not really had a bit of downtime at work. It is just the very top of the head, very strange and no, I do not have a sunburn on the top of the head. I have done that before

Just curious if anybody else has had this.

> [nicld said:](/goto/post?id=132139)
>
> I even put a cold pack on the top of the head and it did feel good. I am sure that it does not help that I think it is hot here.

No ideas about the "hot head" Nicole, but I have been knows to put ice packs on my ears when they flare hot. It _does_ feel good.

During the summertime I also use one of those ice sheets and tuck it inside my pillowcase. Yes, it sweats a little as it melts, but I just place a hand towel on top my pillow.

# Thread: Labs back - more 'eeeek' moments and freaking out. lol
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/labs-back-more-eeeek-moments-and-freaking-out-lol.11186/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### margor_j — 2014-06-25T11:46:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/labs-back-more-eeeek-moments-and-freaking-out-lol.11186/post-132181>

hi hi
so, went back to the thai hospital today to get my labs back. man, im pleased i was sitting down. though iv had time to digest them now i guess.. SIGH, keep carrying on.. I was awake til 4am again last nite. When i stood up to leave the docs office, I was so light headed, lol.

I went out on a limb and opted for a vitamin B12 shot while i was there. it was $3. Il check with Dr TJ about that, now that iv had it done , lol. But im sick of feeling like crap, and I love here how you can pretty much ask for what you want and get it..

Dr TJ hadnt asked me to get HS-CRP but, i tacked that onto my wish list.. boy, im not sure if im happy I did or not! Here goes: (ps - as to be expected, i was classified as all 'normal' and the endo wanted to book me in to see an Nutritionist (BIG LOL!), and a gyno, once she knew I have sky high estrogen and a heavy period this month.. . more lol's. which are better than tears i guess...

1) HS-CRP 5.24 ( i know i know, its effing sky high.. i know
2) THYROID: TSH 1.730, (0.270 - 4.200) T3 110.30, (60.700 - 176.740) T4 9.19, (5.100 - 14.100) FT4 1.34, (0.930 - 1.700) FT3 2.40 (2.000 - 4.4000) erm, yeah, alarm bells on that one?
Thyroglobulin Ab 2.49 (0-4.11)
3) Dexamethasone (cortisol) 0.67
4) calg agglutinin - negative
5) IgG 1220.0 (800 - 1.700)
6) IgM hmm this is hard to read, theres something and then 14.0 , i dont know what the first bit is though, and of course i only see this now that im home  but it was declared as 'clear'.. the range is (50-370) so it cant be a two, or a 7... hmmm or a less than symbol.. dam..
7) EBV - negative
8) mycoplasma IgM - positive (but we worked out it was cos id had a head cold at the time of testing...)

i have just been looking at getting out of chiang mai for a bit - to a secluded place in phuket - easy to fly there form here. access to beach, seafood.. iv found a place on the mountain (but near the beach!) that has beds in tents!! hello! no wifi, close to earth, dark at night... il keep you guys posted. i dont like the thought that im frying myself in chiang mai, and in the meantime.. il back off fruit again, i wasnt having much, but i was feeling so lousy after my period that ..... it was what my body wanted. i DEF feel like im seeing change when i do BPC / fish / fat.. its just hard to get carbs in cos im never hungry, and im not aiming for ketosis.. hmm

thanks sweet fellow krusers, for your input and thoughts

### Shijin13 — 2014-06-25T15:37:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/labs-back-more-eeeek-moments-and-freaking-out-lol.11186/post-132197>

Your hsCRP is high b/c of the positive for the mycoplasma (infection)& b/c of the positive TAB (even though its small). I'd bet good money you've got other inflammatory signs going on.

get in the ocean, get your CT on, get your photoelectric effect and start swallowing as many oysters as possible.... to help your body heal and reverse disease

### margor_j — 2014-06-25T16:01:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/labs-back-more-eeeek-moments-and-freaking-out-lol.11186/post-132199>

hmm thanks for that feedback sister! we decided the postive TAB was cos i had a head cold at the time of test....
im currently looking for accomm near the beach - so il get out of chiang mai and go get some sun - sea - sand - seafood!! cram it in in the time i have left on my visa..
CT is a hard one, theryre not big on baths here, but! my solution was to get ice face baths happening again, so im doing that.
oysters.. ugh.. ok, il try. if thats part of my missing key, il give it a whirl. we grew up on fresh seafood in nz, i really struggle to buy stuff from supermarkets etc and restaurants as i dont know how old it is.. but theres good big organic markets here - tho i had a quick look at seafood this week, but the smell of fish made me high tail it outta that section! :-/
BUT, il swallow a handful of oysters with my smoothies or BP drinks in the mornings...

i just found an amazing looking place to stay near one of the beaches down south in thailand - close to the beach to.. yayness!! im sure im getting fried in chiang mai - im wondering if the sleep issues atm are the chiang mai factor ... im staying where i always stay and HELLO ! insomnias back.. but also, i think some of its my blue light addiction. so, its 8pm, time to start shutting down (not physiologically or biologically!!) and seeing if i can sleep, with no melatonin! im gonna stop that carry on to!

thanks again

### margor_j — 2014-06-25T16:26:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/labs-back-more-eeeek-moments-and-freaking-out-lol.11186/post-132203>

oh.. @shijin.. 'tab' - is that the thyroid antibodies youre referring to? cos i was told it was normal.. so are you saying that its a little out of whack?
thanks sister..
nite @lahleda!

# Thread: Seafood in Thailand - Inland :)
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seafood-in-thailand-inland.11193/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### margor_j — 2014-06-26T12:04:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seafood-in-thailand-inland.11193/post-132318>

SO, after getting my dismal labs back yesterday... and getting some cracking advice on here about ... electrons ! (hello oysters) i went back to Rim Ping organic store, and low and behold! Seafood! Including oysters!! I bought - shucked oysters (55 baht per punnet of about a dozen = @ $1.50 AUD), a bag of steamed mussels in the shell ($3.00 AUD), some raw salmon and some norwegian caviar..
Came home, made THE epi paleo meal to end all others ! (for me) plated it up - away i went.. (after adding a dash of fresh lime juice)
OYSTERS - practiced the 'barely chew' i had to :-/ these are big, fleshy oysters. not like the snot we get back in nz (no offence oysters!) probably scoffed about 8
SALMON : same again. Barely chewed. I got bits of 'taste' - which is an alarm bell ringer for me and seafood - or is this stuff just got taste? not like the insipid pink cuts at home..
CAVIAR - i was scraping it onto what i ate
MUSSELS : ate one and nearly gagged..  threw out the whole bag, i HATE wasting food but i know i cannot 'go there' again..

my stomachs still a bit grumbly, but it may have been the emergency bit of dark chocolate i had at the shops - i was feeling REALLY dodgy after some coffee (what was i thinking, i know i know...)

proves my point to me.. i struggle with seafood that isnt up to my expectations  its why i NEVER eat it if i go out for dinner... and those big bbq fish that i can get over the road? that i DO eat? i found out are river fish  not ocean..

im still looking at heading to southern thailand, and staying sea side.. eating seafood, and il persevere with the oysters. Now i have to deal with the fact i have a punnet and a half in the fridge - theyre definitely going to be something i struggle with after today. like left over food..  hah mental block! id be hopeless on "Fear Factor" in the eating sections, so dont ask me to do it with you! oh, plus id fail on the 'skinny bikini' front to - atm..

AND LO AND BEHOLD! i slept a LOT better last nite - secret? i read before bed, no watching desperate housewives or surfing the net.. it really was THAT easy.... and no supplemental melatonin either!


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# Thread: Develop IgE Duck Egg Allergy in 5 egg eatings?!?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/develop-ige-duck-egg-allergy-in-5-egg-eatings.11208/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### LaurenR — 2014-06-28T18:18:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/develop-ige-duck-egg-allergy-in-5-egg-eatings.11208/post-132517>

I am IgG to hen egg white and spent over a year off them.
I now eat them occasionally or if they are in something.

In order to try something new I have been eating duck eggs occasionally.
I have had them 5 times now.

1st- nothing
2nd- nothing
3rd- felt a bit off unwell
4th- 4 hours later nausea, followed be diarrhea
5th- today this happend...

warning below TMI!

Within a few minutes of eating the eggs (scrambeled with spinach) my throat starting to feel itchy.
Then my tongue feel tingly. The bigger tastebuds at the back of my tongue started to swell.
I stopped eating then took a shower as I felt a bit anxious.
Went to lie down, started to feel nausea, 10-20minutes later I developed cramps and a very strong need of vomit.

I didn't vomit but I did have to go to the toilet, 2 x BM- normal.
Went back to bed, and feel asleep for ??15mins maybe.

Woke up more cramps, painful, nausea.
Felt Hot and had tingly hands.
2 x diarrhea

Then extremely intense vomitting! ABout 10 waves/retches all in a row.

Back to bed, more painful cramps, one more diarrhea

Then back to sleep.

All of that within in 3 hours.

So...is it possible to develop an IgE allergy out of nowhere and with eating duck eggs 5 times?

I have no other IgE allergies (nettle tea gives me itchy throat immediately).

I found it all quite scary as I knew what was going on as soon as felt itchy and my tongue tingly.
Thanks

### LaurenR — 2014-06-28T18:22:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/develop-ige-duck-egg-allergy-in-5-egg-eatings.11208/post-132518>

[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/10756237](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/10756237)

# Thread: Lab results back...need help please
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lab-results-back-need-help-please.11212/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Khim — 2014-06-29T00:58:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lab-results-back-need-help-please.11212/post-132545>

Hi all,

Just got most of my lab results back and I need help. My cortisol levels are high as far as I can tell and low low DHEA. Some labs are still not back yet. But just a little history of my health...

Born 2 months premature.
Very skinny from birth to age 12, never felt hungry, only ate because it was meal time. I have often asked my friends what hunger feels like, weird.
Weight became normal with puberty but started getting cellulite at 14 years old despite being active. Also started having migraines.
Worked night and double shifts at 7-Eleven for a year after graduating high school, insomnia set in and then high blood pressure. Treated with HBP with chinese herbal concoction.
Started gaining weight at college due to stress of working and studying. Developed peptic ulcer, gained a lot of weight from taking Tagamet with milk. Lost weight after college doing karate.

Moved to Australia when our daughter was 3 yo. It was a very stressful first couple of years trying to become permanent residents and that was when my hair started turning grey, gained weight, and insomnia was the worst.
Worked night shift when our daughter started school so I can be home after school and on school holidays. I would also go to the gym after sending her to school. During this period I also developed asthma when our landlady decided to change all the windows in the whole apartment block while we were still staying there. I was put on Ventolin and after the first year and it got worse, so I decided to use alternative medicine to get better. I had started using Bragg's Apple Cider Vinegar after reading a lot of positive feedback from a health website and from that website read about home remedies for asthma and decided to try it (It took about 8 months and asthma is gone, but my lungs are still weak. I would have mild chest congestions and chronic coughing every winter).

I was sleep-deprived, moody, and after 3 years my memory suffered big time. This was when I decided to get a part-time job working school hours and letting my body heal. Went back to full time when she was old enough to walk to school after we bought our house.

Started low cab again in Oct 2013 and have been since then but only lost 3kg after 3 months. Was doing Bulletproof coffee intermittent fasting, lost 2 kegs in 2 weeks and read about Leptin Rx on the BP forum. Started Leptin Reset in April and gained 4kgs after 2 months. I don't have a lot of weight to lose but I am certainly "over-fat". Body comp is horrible, with poor skin elasticity. I used to have most fats in my hips, butt and thighs, but in the last year my upper arms are getting bigger with cellulites.

I hated most vegetables especially green leafy vegetables as they tasted extremely bitter to me, hated all meat, except for white meat chicken, hated shrimps. Loved crabs and white fish. It was until I was in college that I started loving green vegetables and shrimps. I still cannot tolerate the taste of red meat unless well marinated. Absolutely cannot tolerate salmon, tuna and fishy fish.

Here are my lab results. Any advise would be appreciated. Thanks!

Cortisol 6:30 am 46 nmol/L
Cortisol 8pm 30 nmol/L
DHEA 3.4 umol/L
Total Cholesterol 7.4 nmol/L
Hdl. 2.9 nmol/L
LDL 4.3 nmol/L
Trig. 0.4 nmol/L
LDL/ HDL ratio. 1.5
Chol/HDL ratio 2.5
IgG. Positive
IgA. Low positive
Homocysteine. 5.9 umol/L
CRP. 0.7 mg/L
Testosterone. 0.8 nmol/L
Free Testosterone. 5 pmol/L
Prolactin. 4.4 ug/L
Progesterone. 1.4 nmol/L
Estrogen. Still waiting for results
Estradiol. Still waiting for results
Free T4. 17.8 pmol/L
Free T3. 4.7 pmol/L
TSH. 2.81mIU/L
RT3. Still waiting for results
IGF1. Still waiting for results
Platelets. 204
Iron 21.2 umol/L
Transferrin. 2.54g/L
Saturation. 33%
Ferritin. 22 ug/L

# Thread: The meaning of dream subjects?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-meaning-of-dream-subjects.11244/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Lahelada — 2014-07-02T14:12:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-meaning-of-dream-subjects.11244/post-132984>

Now by that I dont mean whether if you dream of sunflowers you will wi n the lottery.
My question is inspired by dream themes that I currently experience. I dream of work meetings in the past with the right jeople mix(from the same "era"workwise) but the meeting takes place at my parental home. Or I dream about an office building where the people working there are a mix of all my previous colleagues. Today I dreamt that I should not accept my team reward lunch as my bosses teeth were hurting,the boss being one of my very early career.
So to make my question more precise. Are recurrent dream themes a reflection of brain repair / brain health state or maybe an indicator where repair is taking place?
I will add that one of the things I noticed about me is that I have a chronic lack of year memory. I can tell you all the restaurants I ate at during a holiday and in particular what I ate but I struggle to piece together what year it was.

### Lahelada — 2014-07-02T14:24:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-meaning-of-dream-subjects.11244/post-132985>

I dont seem to dream a lot about my present. No sailing dreams for example or fantasy dreams of walking through the desert with Elvis. Other people here have reported persistent oxy themes to their dreams.
So it means something that one dreams. Does the dream subject or the dream congruence also have a meaning as a sort of brain status indicator?

i wanna get some contact adhesive in clear red or yellow and cut circles to stick over downlights to protect us from blue light of a night time, as well as our friends, so we dont all have to be wearing glasses

will yellow/amber be better than red?
yellow blocks blue and allows red and green light...
will a red transparent film be as good (obv dimmer)

### Jude — 2014-07-04T15:17:16+0300

Most folk are initially surprised but by the time they leave have decided it's more relaxing and seriously considering adopting a few red bulbs into their lives...and some actually have.
