# Thread: Help create a real glossary of terms for study guides
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-create-a-real-glossary-of-terms-for-study-guides.11329/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### kyrakitty — 2014-07-15T02:07:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-create-a-real-glossary-of-terms-for-study-guides.11329/post-134369>

My next project is a "**_JK Dictionary"_** a downloadable PDF of terms in alphabetical order to refer to while reading the study guides in chronological order. I know there is already a glossary of terms thread in this part of the forum but it sucks (sorry no offense meant) and mine will be better.

Highlights include:

-   Listing the terms, without the definition, in alphabetical order on a glossary page which will then hyperlink to the definition so it will be efficient.
-   Both PDF and Word version so that if you want you can make it your own.
-   Will include any terms requested in this thread.

Why am I doing this....Try as I might I am not able to remember what most of the terms mean while reading the blogs because my head is seriously always spinning and the info flys back into the ether....I need a dictionary to accompany these study guides because when a word I have looked up countless times (which I should know due to looking it up over and over) it bums me out and I get sick of constantly having to search for it's meaning again and again so I stop reading the blog.

That way when I forget what one of the countless over my head terms means instead of feeling like a complete unteachable idiot who can't retain info... I can ignore the self abuse and open the JK Dictionary and quickly find the word so that I can push forward and continue reading vs exploding into a million depressed pieces .

You can help in one of two ways by either:

-   Posting any terms you don't understand so that other smarty pants can give us the definitions

or​

-   Post both a term along with it's meaning... for those terms that you already have a definition, keep forgetting and have added to a personal glossary.

I suspect that the JK Dictionary will be as big as it wants to be based on what terms everyone wants to have instant access to and it will be an ongoing project as new blogs and terms come up.

### JasonB — 2014-07-15T13:56:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-create-a-real-glossary-of-terms-for-study-guides.11329/post-134463>

I agree that the current glossary isn't as effective as it could be, but I think that's the result of generating an actual HTML table and pasting the code right into the forum - it may have worked in the past but I bet some background changes to this site broke it. I've taken a copy and stripped out the HTML which leaves 58 acronyms, unfortunately from that long list. Some are relevant and some are not. I'll post what I have re-alphabetized (with alpha prefixed terms grouped with the first letter of the word immediately after alpha) with the initials of people at the end (do we really need those?) with any strange acronyms from the list and then choose to include or exclude for your JK dictionary, Kyra. Here goes.

AA: Arachadonic acid
ACTH: Adrenocorticotropic hormone
ADH: Anti-diuretic hormone
AGEs: Advanced Glycation End-Products
AI: Auto-Immune
ALE: Advanced lipoxidation end-products
ALEs: Advanced Lipoxidation End-Products
ALS: Amyotrophic lateral sclerosis aka - Lou Gehrig's
AV: Aajonus Vonderplanitz
BCAA: Branched Chain Amino Acid
BCP: Bombyx acid cysteine protease
CLA: Conjugated Linoleic Acid
CO: Coconut Oil
CRH: Corticotropin-releasing hormone
C.R.O.N.: Caloric restriction with optimal nutrition
CT: Cold Thermogenesis
CW: Conventional Wisdom
ECCG: Green tea antioxidant
eNOS: endothelial Nitric Oxide Synthetase
HC: High carb, also hydrocortisone
HCG: Human Chorionic Gonadotropin (the hormone that Simeons discovered in pregnant women)
HIIT: High intensity interval training
HRV: Heart Rate Variability Test
IF: Intermittent fasting
IL6: Interleukin-6
LR: Leptin Reset
LS: Leptin Sensitive
MCT: Medium Chain Triglycerides
MDA: Malondialdehyde
ME: Myalgic encephalomyelitis
MMP9: Matrix metallopeptidase 9
a-MSH: alpha Melanocyte-stimulating hormone
NPY: Neuropeptide Y
NSAs: Nitrosamines. HCAs= Heterocyclic Amines
PAHs: Polycyclic Aromatic Hydrocarbons
PCOS: Polycystic Ovarian Syndrome
PD: Primal Diet
PPAR: Peroxisome proliferator-activated receptor
RPD: Raw Paleolithic Diet
ROS: Reactive oxygen species
RVAF: Raw Animal and Vegetable Diet
SAD: Standard American Diet
SCN: Suprachiasmatic Nucleus
TGF: transforming growth factor beta-1
a-TNF: Alpha tumor necrosis factor
VAP: Vertical Auto Profile
VDR: Vitamin D Receptor
VEGF: Vascular endothelial growth factor
VGEF: Vascular Endothelial Growth Factor
VIP: Vasoactive Intestinal Peptide

Names and other assorted acronyms:
GCB: Guy-Claude Burger
JMM: Jack's Marketing Monkeys
LC: Loren Cordain
MDA: Marks Daily Apple
SF: Sally Fallon

### PaulG — 2014-08-06T10:24:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-create-a-real-glossary-of-terms-for-study-guides.11329/post-136957>

Having a common template for each term might be a good idea to give a standard common experience to the Glossary

A programmer (cough cough Yew-wei) could write a program to use a template and 1) Extract text from Wiki, 2) Auto-generate the Search links, 3) generate the HTML/PDF.

One of the issues with downloadable pdf/word is that the content is potentially out of date as soon as you download it and you could end of with various versions flying around the internet. A wiki is a better option, a wiki that you can generate pdfs/word from would be good also, similar to the way the blog content can be transformed using the [CleanPrint](http://www.formatdynamics.com/saving-paper-trees-ink-and-money/) add-on to wordpress.

Whether WIKI is a reliable source is debatable and accuracy of content will vary from term to term. Also exposing people to the consensus view could be good or bad. If you were to follow the rabbit hole of links, sure you would gain knowledge but that knowledge \*may\* box your thinking in. Also WIKI may not be around forever.

For example...

**Autophagy**

**Wiki Definition (If available)**
[Autophahy](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Autophagy) is the basic [catabolic](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Catabolic) mechanism that involves [cell](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cell_\(biology\)) degradation of unnecessary or dysfunctional cellular components through the actions of [lysosomes](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lysosome).[\[1\]](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Autophagy#cite_note-Lin2012-1) The breakdown of cellular components promotes cellular survival during starvation by maintaining cellular energy levels.[\[1\]](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Autophagy#cite_note-Lin2012-1) Autophagy allows the degradation and recycling of cellular components.[\[1\]](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Autophagy#cite_note-Lin2012-1) During this process, targeted [cytoplasmic](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cytoplasm) constituents are isolated from the rest of the cell within a double-membraned vesicle known as an [autophagosome](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Autophagosome). The autophagosome then fuses with a lysosome and its cargo is degraded and recycled.[\[2\]](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Autophagy#cite_note-Patel2012-2) There are three different forms of autophagy that are commonly described; macroautophagy, microautophagy and chaperone-mediated autophagy.[\[3\]](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Autophagy#cite_note-Isidoro2012-3) In the context of disease, autophagy has been seen as an adaptive response to stress which promotes survival, whereas in other cases it appears to promote cell death and morbidity.[\[2\]](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Autophagy#cite_note-Patel2012-2)

**Why Important?**
Summary definition (Bullet points) of why it is significant in the JK Quantum Biology plus this community [World View](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/World_view)

**Most important Blogs and Forum Entries
(How determined??)**

# Thread: Watching your 'spins'
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/watching-your-spins.11332/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### sjoshua — 2014-07-15T04:00:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/watching-your-spins.11332/post-134385>

I've noticed a strange phenomenon lately that I'm curious if others have experienced.

The conditions that have resulted in the phenomenon are lying outside on the ground, looking up towards the sky without the sun being within the direct viewing 'window'. I saw it today, midday, with cloud cover, but have also seen it with clear blue background; more easily in the evening as the sun begins to wane.

Anywho... when I do that thing where I focus on 'nothing' - the only way I can think to describe it is the method for viewing [magic eye](http://www.magiceye.com/3dfun/stwkdisp.shtml) pictures - I can make out countless tiny white 'spots/specs' that are circling in tight, independent patterns everywhere in my view.

The first time I experienced it I wrote it off as there just being a cloud of those tiny bugs flying around that I must have been seeing, but after further review and multiple occurrences in various locations and conditions, I believe I am somehow watching some of my eye's electrons spinning - or at least a reflection of them.

Anyone else?

### nonchalant — 2014-07-15T04:37:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/watching-your-spins.11332/post-134394>

How about tiny bubbles/droplets inside your vitreous humor?
I haven't seen that, but you've got me intrigued. The lower angle of the sun sounds like a clue. Some things that are invisible with direct light can be detected with light from a different angle.

### Jonathin — 2014-07-15T22:59:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/watching-your-spins.11332/post-134509>

I've seen what you are describing for 40 yrs. Stare without focusing on to any uniform background - blue sky, monolith concrete wall, white ceiling. The degree of sharpness of the white spots/stars will vary with your energy level and state of mind. It helps to be able to be able to relax your focus on things. If you can stare at a small object, relax and easily have a double vision (see two objects instead of one) this seems to help. What all this is I am not sure. Some form of energy no doubt.

Lasts 4-5 Years.**Stage 3**: FAR-Infra-Red Life Energy Filter Lasts 1-2 Years. **Stage 4**: XstreaMn Alkalizer Filter. Lasts 12 Months.**Stage 5**: XstreaMn Carbon Filter. Lasts 12 Months, **Stage 6**: Magnetic Water Energizer

The Ellis method seems to be a special type of distillation that repeatedly heats and cools water and adding ozone.

> In greater detail: this method provides extreme heat and ozone concentration on relatively small volumes of water... only 1 1/2 cups at a time about 3 times/minute!! In any event, common sense would tell you it would be better than just passing by an ozone bulb one time! You will note that we make ozone using a special quartz bulb, not corona discharge that produces unwanted nitrides. ### PaulG — 2014-07-16T15:04:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/drink-water-with-3000-more-energy-the-hydrogen-bonding-angle-of-water-and-john-ellis.11335/post-134579>

This is an informative paper on hydrogen bonding by Martin Chaplin and how it relates to life. I think it explains why the ozone hole is bad news for Australia.

arxiv.org/pdf/0706.1355

> Life as we know it could not exist without the present balance between Na + and K + ions. The weakening of hydrogen bond strength shifting K + to become chaotropic would either cause K + ions to remain outside cells with consequences on the cell membrane potential or would cause intracellular water to be too disorganized to support present intracellular processes.

> Changes in hydrogen bond strength between water molecules alter its degree ofionization (Figure 4). Strengthened hydrogen bonding increases the (Grotthuss) rate of transfer of these ions in electrical fields but slows down their diffusion otherwise. Acid strength of biomolecular groups is determined by the competition between the biomolecules and watermolecules for the hydrogen ions. The strength by which the watermolecules hold on to the hydrogen ion depends on their hydrogen bonding strength as a distributor of the charge. Biomolecularionization, therefore,also depends on hydrogen bond strength. Since all biological processes have dependence on charge, a completely new evolutionary perspective is required if waterionization is suppressed by water hydrogen bond strengthening. At intermediate hydrogen bond strength, ionization increases, reducing the pH of neutral solutions.The acidity(pKa) of biomolecular groups, such as phosphate, also shows complex behavior with decreasing water hydrogen bond strength and often produces a pKa minimum. Here, there are opposite effects of(a) reduced dielectric, at lower hydrogen bond strength, reducing ionic separation so tending to increase the pKa and (b) increased water reactivity,also at lower hydrogen bond strength, increasing hydration effects and enabling the ionization, so tending to reduce the pKa.

I think recent electrolyte intake and how that affects electrolyte balance could also affect that.

"Electrolytes in living systems include sodium, potassium, chloride, bicarbonate, calcium, phosphate, magnesium, copper, zinc, iron, manganese, molybdenum, copper, and chromium. In terms of body functioning, six electrolytes are most important: sodium, potassium, chloride, bicarbonate, calcium, and phosphate." ([https://open.oregonstate.education/aandp/chapter/26-3-electrolyte-balance/](https://open.oregonstate.education/aandp/chapter/26-3-electrolyte-balance/))

Sometimes I drink milk before sleeping, milk has lots of potassium compared to how much other electrolytes, if I add salt to it I don't get thirsty for much water soon after and I can sleep through the night almost every time. think it's the blood sodium-potassium balance being able to be maintained using mostly what was recently eaten so making something easier for the kidneys.

The matrix water production is interesting. I've been eating ~95% animal foods when I noticed the milk with salt thing so maybe what I'm eating is affecting matrix water production enough it makes sodium-potassium balance in a snack seem like an easy fix when that wouldn't work nearly as well without the matrix water production context.

### Sue-UK — 2020-09-01T17:26:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/drink-water-with-3000-more-energy-the-hydrogen-bonding-angle-of-water-and-john-ellis.11335/post-291887>

>

I'm not saying Doris has proved anything, but the article I linked to brings up a couple of interesting questions …  Add to that is the Dr Boros interviews where the camel nonsense is unresolved … Camels may use fat to make water to cross the desert, but they drink gallons and gallons of deuterium loaded water _when the environment changes_, i.e. in this case, when they get the chance, which would also spare any remaining fat stores.

There is also the Herrera book that states that the darker the skin (i.e. more melanin), the less mitochondria. So for equatorial haplotypes at the equator, having less mitochondria implies a reduced capacity for mitochondria produced metabolic water, despite the extra need because of equatorial temperatures. Living more naturally (like the Hazda) would usually mean less body fat, and prey would be leaner. They also eat substantial amounts of deuterium loaded foods. So as we originated from the equator, I think its highly unlikely that mitochondria originally evolved to supply all, or almost all, the human body's needs for water. Haplotypes that developed to cope with higher latitudes and cold (lighter skin so less melanin, more mitochondria, more fat storage capability than the average Hazda, particularly SQ fat) might make more metabolic water from fat (and generate heat at the same time). Changing light signals and then cold being the dominant signal … Even then for humans there's fire up to certain latitude where there are still trees and/or using animal skins (melanin connection?) to "recycle" heat and light emitted from the body, practices that are sparing of using up dietary and stored fat.

### Sue-UK — 2020-09-02T11:28:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/drink-water-with-3000-more-energy-the-hydrogen-bonding-angle-of-water-and-john-ellis.11335/post-291906>

> [JanSz said:](/goto/post?id=291883)
>
> If I recall correctly, [@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) initially had positive comments toward Doris Loh, later on not so much.
> Similarly as with Dr Boros.
> \------------------
> I still think that dr Boros may be right with his claim about 2000 gallons of water being synthesized (daily when at rest) in the mitochondrial matrix (plus peroxisome).
> There is not much controversy about huge amount of ATP being produced.
> There is a tight relationship between ATP and newly synthesized water amounts.
>
> That being the case, we live while using much larger amounts of energy than usually thought of.
> That eventually must be addressed by someone.
>
> \]

My current speculation / musing  is that it's related to melanin (and to a lesser extent haemoglobin, because of the amounts involved. In Herrera's book, he talks about the amount of oxygen breathed out compared to breathed in being too much for the breath to be the only source of oxygen for the tissues).

So by splitting the water molecule using light, melanin and haemoglobin provide a source of hydrogen and oxygen, which the mitochondria can use to make ATP and metabolic water. Melanin could then split that metabolic water back into hydrogen and oxygen and off we go again ...... haemoglobin is more limited by the frequencies of light it can use, so Herrera compares this to chlorophyll, whereas melanin is more lignin like.

Haemoglobin can split water, but melanin can both split and reform it, and melanin seems to have far reaching effects beyond mitochondria. In the context of mitochondria, I suppose theoretically and mathematically 2000 gallons might be synthesised, and match the ATP calculations. I don't have a problem with that, but my concern is that if what' water is produced is deuterium depleted, it seems to make more sense to recycle it, (perhaps until the light hydrogen is "spent "?) rather than rely on it as a source of water for the body as a whole.

### Sheddie — 2020-09-02T21:07:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/drink-water-with-3000-more-energy-the-hydrogen-bonding-angle-of-water-and-john-ellis.11335/post-291933>

> [Sheddie said:](/goto/post?id=291875)
>
> My truth is I don't want to drink recycling pharmaceuticals and agricultural chemicals in the tap water in my area. Distilled worked better until I got tired of lugging in 20 gallons of single-distilled water at a time from the store. As an in-home counter-top multiple-distiller, the Ellis machine has been great. Adding back clean minerals, if and when desired, is no problem.

Addendum: JanSz--For clarity?... dragging home from the store 20 gallons of distilled water would last me between 2-3 weeks for drinking, cooking and the dogs!! Have SUV so used to 'load up' when going to the store, until home-distilling replaced store-bought. The Ellis machine was comparably faster than many home distillers I reviewed for getting about 10 gallons in about 4.5 hours, approximately once a week, unattended except for one check in the middle hours when I swap for a second 5 gal catchment container. Other distillers would take overnight to fill 1-2 gallon catchment containers... I keep bottles of plain, nothing-added, Qlarivia in the fridge to grab on the go. San Pell is my fave for the pleasure of effervescense.

Variety of waters is interesting to some, not everyone, unless maybe someone was tested with high Deuterium and doing a specific detox to correct the scores? I consumed Qlarivia for about 6 months exclusively. Never tested. Personally noticed nothing changed or different.

Clean water intake, including low Deuterium, is of course worth monitoring. In addition, I subscribe more to good fat for physical energy 'fuel' and do 'feel' it as energizing for me, a long-term Chronic Fatigue patient. I don't eat an especially high deuterium diet. Would like to see a measure the hydrogen bond angle for Deuterium-depleted water. I developed disabling autoimmune problems over 20 years ago right after surgery for back injury. They made me take a Hepatitis shot before the surgery. I'm learning, these days, about possible viral contaminants in conventional vaccines going back to the 50s...

### JanSz — 2020-09-03T14:28:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/drink-water-with-3000-more-energy-the-hydrogen-bonding-angle-of-water-and-john-ellis.11335/post-291938>

> [Sheddie said:](/goto/post?id=291936)
>
> What Lab tested waters?

[https://www.ddcenters.com/](https://www.ddcenters.com/)

[https://www.ddcenters.com/tests/](https://www.ddcenters.com/tests/)
\====================================
**D-Terminator**
Purchase a D-TerminatorTM test to determine your DFactor, how well you deplete deuterium, by measuring markers for deuterium in your tissues and biological fluids.
\====================================
**D-Air**
This test can be used to evaluate the water in the air (humidity) from your home, gym, church, workplace etc.
\===================================
**D-Food**
This test determines the deuterium levels in meats, fish, vegetables, fruits, breads, soups, stews, and food ingredients like flour, butter, herbs, seasoning, etc. as well as in supplements and protein powders. The test will provide one value that represents how much deuterium is in the food after we remove the residual moisture and liquid from it.
\======================================
**D-Water**
This straightforward test can be used to evaluate any pure water source from commercial waters sold in stores and online to ground-waters from the tap, streams, wells, rivers, lakes, and oceans to seasonal waters from rain, snow, and glaciers to water someone gives you from another country or planet. If it’s water then this is the test you want to order. Please note that if you want the total deuterium in a beverage like milk, soda, alcoholic beverages, etc., you will need to order both a test for water and a test for food since the dry ingredients like sugars or dyes in such beverages will have to also be measured.
\=====================================

# Thread: What sugars will you tolerate in store bought foods?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-sugars-will-you-tolerate-in-store-bought-foods.11340/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### JasonB — 2014-07-16T19:41:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-sugars-will-you-tolerate-in-store-bought-foods.11340/post-134598>

I'm just curious to know what sugars you'll allow into the shopping cart? I'm not talking the incidentals like in fruits/vegetables or dairy products. These are the (likely) refined sugars you find added to things.

Say, for instance, some kind of salami, sausage, or breakfast meats. I find very few that do not include flour of some kind as a filler. Then the remainder tend to say glucose or dextrose (another name for glucose - I see you trying to fool me) as the second or third ingredient - yet the nutritional info says there are zero carbohydrates per serving. You almost want to call it incidental sugar at that point. Yet, I've seen dextrose added to a protein shake and the label says zero carbohydrates and it does have a high glycemic index. That makes no sense to me.

Yes, I agree that I could be over-analyzing this point but when you're aiming for a ketogenic diet or very low carb those things do matter or do you take the nutritional label at face value even though the ingredients listed include some kind of sugar.

Yet, glucose itself is usually derived from grain starches and we know how bad those are for us.

Anyway, before I take up two pages of for and against I'll ask the rest of you, what sugars will you tolerate in store bought foods?

### cantweight — 2014-07-16T22:01:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-sugars-will-you-tolerate-in-store-bought-foods.11340/post-134607>

I dont know where you live but you need to source better food. The only sugar I really allow in store bought processed meats is fruit/fruit juice....often I find that in jerky.

Wegmans, whole foods and of course farm sourced/artisan sausage and salami should be good places to look for foods with quality ingredients.

I do buy a breakfast sausage from a local farm that has maple syrup in it....I shouldnt...but it is sooo good.

If you have none of these resources where you live look online. Dont settle for crap laden with sugar....not worth it.

### SeaHorse — 2014-07-17T00:25:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-sugars-will-you-tolerate-in-store-bought-foods.11340/post-134635>

pretty much zero…..it means doing a lot of searching for awhile and then you just stick to your sources…

### sjoshua — 2014-07-17T01:06:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-sugars-will-you-tolerate-in-store-bought-foods.11340/post-134642>

@[JasonB](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/10599/) consider that the ideal foods for you are fresh, seasonally available and locally sourced. While you are working towards optimal (vs. maintaining) it is even more important to stick to these rules. First, consider that processing of meat (or any foodstuff) is breaking it down in an unnatural way prior to your body's digestion, which means that it is not the form your body would have expected to receive it. Therefore any processed food, whether meat or otherwise, is going to be less optimal than its unprocessed counterpart. Second, if meats have other ingredients in them other than animal and you aren't buying them directly from the farmer or butcher who processed them, then it isn't the most ideal foodstuff and probably indicates it is even less likely to fit the categories of fresh, seasonal, local. Sausage is one of those foods that is going to be a bad choice much more often than a good one. Unless you can source from a reliable farm that pastures their pigs, it is likely that the fatty acid profile is abnormal and their environments and lifestyle will not have promoted the correct hormone balances. Still though, sourcing the pastured pork belly, shoulder, chops, etc are going to be a better choice than sausages.

I don't know what Rx or protocol you are currently following, but the overall premise of the epi-paleo diet is sticking to energy dense foods in order to deliver the highest electron:mass ratio. The reason for this is, the less mass you need to consume to derive the electron charge your body requires, the less mass you are adding to your body's system. We've also learned about the importance of having proper DHA levels, which allow our body to more readily 'accept' those electrons. In the same way that modern humans are electron-starved due to the unnatural environments we have created around us, the animals that we raise in CAFO or otherwise unnatural habitats are going to be much more likely to have similar electron-starved bodies, and as a result their meats will reflect a similar lesser charge vs., per se, a grass fed or pastured counterpart that was raised in an environment more similar to where it has naturally evolved.

For some, this is a bit difficult financially when you are standing at the meat cooler choosing between the $10/lb grass fed chuck roast vs the $5/lb CAFO chuck roast... both have the potential to fill your stomach with the same mass of food, sure. This is exaggerated and simplified to show effect, but consider if the grass fed one imposes a natural and more abundant source of electrons that only required you to eat 1/2 lb to obtain the same # of utilizable electrons as 1 lb of the CAFO:

1/2 lb grass fed chuck roast = all necessary electrons = $5 cost and 1/2 lb mass eaten

1 lb grass fed chuck roast = all necessary electrons = $5 cost and 1 lb mass eaten

Same cost, same # of utilizable electrons... but the mass consumed is DOUBLE with the CAFO. Which one do you think would promote adding more mass to your human 'system'...?

With these basic criteria, it makes the most sense to organize your food choices around seafood due to the high electron density since they live life in water; also their propensity for excellent DHA levels. In lieu of seafood, choose grass fed, pastured, wild, local, land animals. Or, well, actually there are probably a whole bunch of insects that would be better choices than the land animals... but we can take baby steps to that point.

@[cantweight](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1283/) don't mean to derail the thread but when I was in high school and college I worked for Wegmans, and went down for a week each to help open the Dulles and Fairfax stores... Excellent company, inside and out... curious if one of these is 'your' Wegmans?

### bionaut — 2014-07-17T01:59:58+0300

### JasonB — 2014-07-17T04:10:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-sugars-will-you-tolerate-in-store-bought-foods.11340/post-134689>

Yes, I'm looking at better sources of food. Although it's hard to resist AAA Canadian sirloin from Costco. And I am avoiding all of those processed meats with flour and sugars. It was more a comment on the state of what I see and wondering where people in this community will bend the rules just a smidge.

### sjoshua — 2014-07-17T19:20:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-sugars-will-you-tolerate-in-store-bought-foods.11340/post-134753>

> [JasonB said:](/goto/post?id=134689)
>
> Yes, I'm looking at better sources of food. Although it's hard to resist AAA Canadian sirloin from Costco. And I am avoiding all of those processed meats with flour and sugars. It was more a comment on the state of what I see and wondering where people in this community will bend the rules just a smidge.

Bending the rules doesn't make quantum sense in your typical daily life if you can fathom the larger picture of how this all ties together. I'd point you to Jack's flower pot analogy taken from: [http://jackkruse.com/guest-blog-forever-ruined-ordinary/](http://jackkruse.com/guest-blog-forever-ruined-ordinary/)

> Jack used a flower pot analogy, if you are watering a flower pot but you have too many holes on the bottom of the pot are you actually doing anything by watering? You may collect electrons but if you are losing them at a faster rate than you take them in you are still in danger to damage your health.

Don't poke more holes in your flower pot if you are trying to patch other holes at the same time... This represents the definition of counter intuitive in my book

'Exceptions to the rules' should be reserved for special occasions during which the environment and the people you surround yourself with are more important than anything else in your world at that moment.

EDIT: you mentioned Costco access, I'd suggest checking out this thread for some food choice ideas from other members here: [http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/what-staples-do-you-buy-at-costco.11038/](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/what-staples-do-you-buy-at-costco.11038/)

### JasonB — 2014-07-17T20:31:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-sugars-will-you-tolerate-in-store-bought-foods.11340/post-134758>

sjoshua. I'm not intentionally bending rules and trying to be as strict as possible within my current surroundings.
I've been watching the Costco thread and I'll see if some of the items are available. I'll be scoping the store out more thoroughly when I'm there in the next couple of days. In some ways it's harder to get the variety in Canada that you have in the U.S. - especially in the prairies.

I have to say the answers I've seen are much of what I expected, but my intentions were not to ask for permission to bend rules. A better question would have been when and why you go off of the optimal templates which sjoshua summed up well. I'll rephrase it with Tim Leary's quote "set, setting, and dosage"

Freyja it is, quite a woman...beauty, sex and death.....

> In [Norse mythology](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Norse_mythology), **Freyja** ([Old Norse](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Old_Norse) the "[Lady](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lady)") is a goddess associated with love, sexuality, beauty, fertility, gold, [seiðr](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sei%C3%B0r), war, and death. Freyja is the owner of the necklace [Brísingamen](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Br%C3%ADsingamen), rides a chariot pulled by two cats, owns the boar [Hildisvíni](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hildisv%C3%ADni), possesses a cloak of [falcon](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Falcon) feathers, and, by her husband [Óðr](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/%C3%93%C3%B0r), is the mother of two daughters, [Hnoss](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hnoss) and [Gersemi](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gersemi). Along with her brother [Freyr](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Freyr) (Old Norse the "[Lord](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lord)"), her father [Njörðr](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nj%C3%B6r%C3%B0r), and her mother ([Njörðr's sister](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sister-wife_of_Nj%C3%B6r%C3%B0r), unnamed in sources), she is a member of the [Vanir](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Vanir). Stemming from Old Norse _Freyja_, modern forms of the name include **Freya**, **Freija**, **Frejya**, **Freyia**, **Frøya**,**Frøjya**, **Freia**, and **Freja**.

[http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Freyja](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Freyja)

# Thread: is there a way to easily find a thread I've posted or one I've commented on?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-there-a-way-to-easily-find-a-thread-ive-posted-or-one-ive-commented-on.11371/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Sandra — 2014-07-22T01:44:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-there-a-way-to-easily-find-a-thread-ive-posted-or-one-ive-commented-on.11371/post-135147>

... or do I have to actually 'watch' the threads I want to keep track of, or add them to my news feed?

Thanks

### sjoshua — 2014-07-22T02:16:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-there-a-way-to-easily-find-a-thread-ive-posted-or-one-ive-commented-on.11371/post-135151>

click your username in the top right, and a menu should appear.

click 'Your Profile Page'

Once there, click the 'postings' tab

You can see everyone else's stuffs the same way, you'd just have to get to their profile page (which can be done by clicking their username, under their picture, in any thread)

Therefore the system must become very efficient at storage and utilizing this light otherwise its light will go out..............

I am thinking that Near Death Experiences could be somewhat related to what happens in a supernova but at the quantum brain level - a massive release of light and energy results in a [wormhole](http://www.edinformatics.com/math_science/solar_system/black_holes_wormholes_white_holes.htm) that connects a black hole to white hole. Time ceases to exist in the conventional sense and encounters with 'beings of light' are actually glimpses of our future selves where we have somehow managed to hack redundant design in the skin to fill it with quantum photonic energy that allows us to exist without food by transducing or modulating the different wavelenghts of radiation to produce the energy via bio superconductivity required for consciousness and life.

# Thread: Rain Soft RO Filter Feedback Please
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rain-soft-ro-filter-feedback-please.11385/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Paul S — 2014-07-24T19:17:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rain-soft-ro-filter-feedback-please.11385/post-135466>

I moved into a new home recently and DW and I are looking at water filtration and softener systems. One is the Rain Soft EC4 softener system (removes chlorine, and other contaminants as well), but they are also proposing adding the Rain Soft Ultrarefiner II Reverse Osmosis filtration system. It is mounted under the kitchen sink, and only runs RO water through the sink faucet and within the RO filtration system, it is not a whole house system. I asked about pipe damage and they indicated since it is direct mounted to a single source (the kitchen sink), it won't damage the plumbing in the home and it also removes flouride, chlorine and other contaminants. Since we currently buy bottled RO water (5 gallon jugs), this system would be a cost saver in the near term/long term.

Does anyone have any experiecne with their RO system or claims? If I recall, on this Forum (I tried searching, but could not find the old posts/threads on it), the main concern was plumbing damage in a home? Anyone with experience with their claims or would recommend/not recommend either RO system or softener?

So, this is who he is:

My name is Lloyd Morgan. I am an electronic engineer by training, a Director of the Central Brain Tumor Registry of the United States (see [www.cbtrus.org](http://www.cbtrus.org/)) a member of the international science group, the Bioelectromagnetics Society (see [www.bioelectromagnetics.org](http://www.bioelectromagnetics.org/)) and a volunteer with the National Brain Tumour Foundation (see[www.braintumor.org](http://www.braintumor.org/)). Since 1995 I have devoted myself to the study of electromagnetic fields and health problems. With such a focus in mind I have attended, and sometimes presented at, multiple science meetings each year including, among others, those of the Bioelectromagnetics Society, the Neuro-Oncology Society, the American Academy of Environmental Medicine and the Brain Tumor Epidemiology Consortium.

[http://instantteleseminar.com/?eventid=57881091](http://instantteleseminar.com/?eventid=57881091)

# Thread: Wired printer.....

**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Hope — 2014-07-29T00:37:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wired-printer.11414/post-135872>

Have any of you found any good wired printers? Or, is the only option now to do wireless.....and turn off that feature?

### prAna303 — 2014-07-29T08:38:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wired-printer.11414/post-135899>

There are tons of good ones on the second hand market. Place it in a different room than the one you sit and do your computing, especially if it is a laser based one.

### Inger — 2014-07-29T22:25:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wired-printer.11414/post-135945>

We got a new printer this winter and it had wireless built in but I shut that off and use only the wired option. It should not be hard to find one that have both options? Just be sure you turn off/deinstall the wireless option....

### LilyIncex — 2020-06-24T19:36:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wired-printer.11414/post-290499>

had the same problem a not long time ago. I think that wireless printers are so comfy. I use it every day for my work. But two months ago I wanted to buy a new one for my mum. Thanks to these guys [https://www.mrdepot.ca](https://www.mrdepot.ca/) I bought a quality printer from a famous brand. They provide a bunch of quality office products such as printers and toner cartridges. Moreover, they have free delivery, and that has saved my money and time. My mum was absolutely pleased with her gift from me! So if u still are looking for a good printer, try to check out this site for more info!

### Katie Durham — 2020-06-25T17:36:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wired-printer.11414/post-290519>

I thought WiFi on my new printer was turned off but my SmartPhone says otherwise. Worth checking occasionally what WiFi signals you can pick up from inside your home.

# Thread: tick
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tick.11418/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Kids
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### kovita — 2014-07-29T18:01:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tick.11418/post-135923>

Hi, I just removed a tick nymph from my child's foot. In my area apart of borrelia, tick born encephalitis is a risk. he is not vaccinated against encephalitis. Unfortunately the nymph was too small and after removal there was eventually nothing left to be sent to the laboratory for analysis. I am just wondering what to do having chronic lyme myself...

### Inger — 2014-07-29T22:04:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tick.11418/post-135941>

I only know about the lyme issue, that you should see turn red in a circle around the bite if it is borrelia. Then take antibiotics if you want to. But the encephalitis I do not know much about.....
i do guess it is pretty rare? I would just follow my child extra closely next days and weeks to see if he changes in any way, and if, then take him to a good doc?
I bet if he eat good and is plenty outside in the sun and grounding in the nature his body could get rid of pretty much stuff just by itself

### kovita — 2014-07-30T14:43:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tick.11418/post-136082>

Inger, he is now all days long out in the sun and in the nature with almost none emf. He has been eating consistently well for at least a year, has great vitD levels, so I hope even if there was something in the bloody tick, he would manage by himself. I started to feed him raw onions and garlic. He is a brave tiny thing and eats it without major objections. i was thinking in adding echinacea, ginseng, beta glucanes and maybe astragalus for at least a month now to bring up first line part of immune system. Good bee products would be great, but this year the bee keepers around struggle and it is very difficult to get it from the area, which i would prefare.

### Inger — 2014-07-30T14:57:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tick.11418/post-136083>

Sounds great Kovita! I bet he will be just fine! Maybe do not give him too many "herbs" because i guess children are more sensitive, even if they are natural....

If you want great honey products, order by Orkos.com. I only buy my honey products there, they are totally natural and do not feed the bees sugar in winter at all. Pretty expensive but I have not been able to find anything as good as theirs. They are in Germany. Honey is usually from Italy or Spain. They have great honeycombs too, and bee pollen too. All raw and natural
just check out their website. That is where i buy my nuts too, all raw and organic. They have great dried and fresh seaweeds too. And their fruit is divine, used to buy it when I ate it but I do not anymore, I avoid fruit. I might order papaya tho soon. I love fresh papaya eaten with seeds and skin... very healing - not much sugar either - they have all kind of organic veggies too

### kovita — 2014-07-30T15:35:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tick.11418/post-136085>

thanks, Inger, their catalog looks interesting. I see they sell durian. Gosh, how do they send it! It brings me back to my previous life in Singapore, where carrying durian in any form or package was strictly prohibited in the public transport. There were even really funny signposts all around metro and bus stations. It was my favourite fruit. There was a restaurant specialized in durian feasts exclusively, always fully booked. I may try papaya myself. I love papaya but the same as you I stopped all fruit with exceptions of some wild berries picked in the woods.


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: The color .....red....
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-color-red.11422/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 18
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### caroline — 2014-07-30T09:45:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-color-red.11422/post-136048>

What does the color red ....or any color mean to you?

Is it a power color? A confidence color? How do you feel when you wear it?

Notice Dr. K's new FB picture .... Clearly he feels powerful/confident - or at the very least - in a very good place!
And he very likely has his red shorts on.

So when we start our day ....do we reach for a particular color because of how we feel?

I had forgotten that I have owned a couple of red outfits years ago...

One night we were in our favorite restaurant in Nassau - we had been going there for years - we knew all the staff and they had always treated us with extreme generosity and care.

This night I had a lovely red outfit ....and red shoes...... And they completely forgot about us! I kept thinking how could they overlook us when I am dressed from head to toe in red???

I think this says way more about me ...and much less about them ......

Did I just fade into the woodwork? even tho I was wearing red? I had no presence regardless of what I was wearing?

### Josh (Paleo Osteo) — 2014-07-30T10:27:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-color-red.11422/post-136051>

red popped into my head yesterday regarding sunburn

do we go reflect red because we have obtained enough UV (blue) - is it therefore a signal that the cache is full?
much like how the brain appears pink because it functions on absorbing blue light?

### Inger — 2014-07-30T10:51:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-color-red.11422/post-136054>

Haha.... that is weird Audrey! Hmmm... I seriously have no clue... but i do think no color or clothes can mask our energy.........

I do like colors, and I love red too, I like different colors, like some days I want just white and another day red.... i have so many different colored dresses
i like pastel colors too, like rose....
but i think colors fit differently depending on your skin / hair tone and appearance... it is a kind of art to get good at using colors that fits you

I have discovered gray fits me very well.... what does that tell?

Maybe the red color did not fit you too well... maybe it was too strong and took over? So one could see only the red, not you? I think you are blond like me and if one were black it would be a different thing, I love when black people wear strong colors, it fits them so well!

### Sue-UK — 2014-07-30T11:10:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-color-red.11422/post-136057>

Caroline, I have a friend who helps people find what colours suit them, and how to emphasise good points etc ...she told me that if people tell me a dress looks fabulous, it's possibly the wrong dress , even if the colour choice is right ...If people notice me in the dress (think I look great, really well, healthy, glowing etc), its probably the right dress ....... when trying on clothes now I ask myself am I wearing the dress, or is the dress wearing me .....

### caroline — 2014-07-30T12:38:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-color-red.11422/post-136063>

Sue ..... I totally get that! I really notice now if I am trying things on.....sometimes it is overpowering - not that it doesn't look good - but it overtakes me and I get lost and I don't want to be lost anymore!

I would never have noticed that before for some reason...maybe I chose to hide inside my clothes? Maybe that was the preferred state of being that I wanted ...I didn't know who I was..... especially with all the crazy weight gains. I was lost.

I have a lot of black clothes - from before my renovation \[as Jack would say\] ....and I cannot wear them - I put them on and take them right back off again!

They seem to drag me down to a place I absolutely won't go anymore. They actually give me the creeps - like a death sign or symbol or an unease that isn't acceptable to me any longer.

And yet ....what could be nicer than a little black dress? Maybe I am not where I need to go yet? I need to wear the clothes - not them me. Maybe one of these days I will put on a little black dress and feel awesome ...I will really know who I am. Of course - it is going to need to be short and low cut.......

What could be better than glowing with health and vitality.....but then - does wearing something red support that?

I definitely think ...YES

### caroline — 2014-07-30T12:53:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-color-red.11422/post-136064>

There is another component to this ..... with all the C/T and sunshine and seafood etc - my skin tone has changed.

People now tell me I am so lucky because I have an olive skin tone and don't burn. This has never, ever, ever been the case!
I used to be so envious of a person with an olive skin tone.

So I have just circled back to being healthy and glowing and being aware of who I am .... more and more every day  and, of course, I want to wear red now!

Maybe we could all change our avatars .... and wear red!

and .... of course, every guy needs some red speedos ......

### yewwei.tan — 2014-07-30T13:12:50+0300

### nicld — 2014-07-30T17:22:56+0300

I am wearing red today  Now that I am getting more sun I seem to be a deeper shade of pink. I saw my mother yesterday and she commented on how I must have really had a sunburn on my chest since it appeared to be very red, I was not burned. I was wearing pink so the pink just made it look red.

I hardly every wear just black, it has to have some other color with it. I look better in warmer hues of colors and my favorite color is salmon or coral. I look really really good in that color.

### caroline — 2014-07-31T01:10:36+0300

# Thread: Oral Temperatures During CT
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oral-temperatures-during-ct.11426/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Valerie — 2014-08-01T03:25:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oral-temperatures-during-ct.11426/post-136342>

So I measured my (oral) temperature with mercury thermometer during CT for first time (after 5 months CT).
Within about 5 minutes of getting into a 12 deg C bath my temperature went from 98 deg F to 99.2 deg F
Body temperature was steady for about 30 minute. For the next 15 minute (through 45 minutes total) my temperature dropped to 98.8 deg F as the bath temperature cooled to 9 deg C.

I think this is a good sign that I have some BAT. I do feel warmer throughout the day. Just want to wear sleeveless tops. Also notice I don't shiver quite as much after CT, but that could be because it is summer.

Does anyone else have temperature values to compare with mine?

### nonchalant — 2014-08-01T05:30:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oral-temperatures-during-ct.11426/post-136348>

I did take some oral temps long ago. My temp also rose a little while after getting in the water. I think it also went back to normal after a while. Sorry I don't have anything specific!

### QuantumRadical — 2014-08-07T20:57:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oral-temperatures-during-ct.11426/post-137182>

Stay in long enough and you will get hypothermic no matter what. Even the Iceman and Jack have limits for tolerating the cold. I notice a distinct spike in generated heat coming off the top of my head within seconds of hitting the cold water. I may have to start using my nice IR thermometer and measure that phenomena.

With a bath temp starting at around 45-47 degrees, I can usually stay in the water fully submerged up to my chin for around 30-35 minutes without starting to shiver. However, I have gone as long as 50 minutes without starting to shiver (I was in the zone), and after a bit of mild shivering I usually get out. Again, the point is not to hurt yourself, but to kick off the bio-quantum-chemical reactions to illicit the right response for optimality. I have tried even colder water, in fact right at 32 degrees, and don't think after those couple of sessions that it has any more value than the 45-56 degree water temperature. Just that you get out faster.

# Thread: Anxiety and Choice
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anxiety-and-choice.11439/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Josh — 2014-08-02T18:27:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anxiety-and-choice.11439/post-136517>

Note the fMRI study methodology....

> It may not surprise you to learn that healthy, well-fed people in affluent countries are often unhappy and anxious. But it did startle Zbigniew Lipowski when he came to a full realization of this fact. He had emigrated to North America from Dublin, in 1955, and, in the mid-nineteen-sixties, was put in charge of the psychiatry practices at two Montreal hospitals, Royal Victoria and Montreal Neurological. Why, he thought, as he worked there, would so many people living in such good conditions have so much anxiety?
>
> Lipowski was born in Poland and, in 1944, took part in the Warsaw Uprising, a mass revolt against the German Army that left more than two hundred thousand civilians dead. Lipowski, masquerading as a French refugee returning to France, was one of the fortunate few who escaped. “Those two months were the most significant experience of my life,” he would later recall. “The odor of burning flesh was with us day and night. We were bombed and shelled daily, food was very scarce, and water had to be obtained at night from a well some distance away. I was so hungry as to almost hallucinate food.”
>
> North America, however, greeted him with constant abundance and leisure. As he pondered the contrast, Lipowski thought of Buridan’s ass: an apocryphal donkey that finds itself standing between two equally appealing stacks of hay. Unable to decide which to consume, it starves to death. The donkey got its name from Jean Buridan, the nominalist fourteenth-century philosopher and Catholic priest who wrote extensively about free will. Buridan posited that free will could sometimes lead to inaction: an inability to choose due to excess uncertainty and, potentially, excess choice. Buridan’s ass, in turn, became the mascot for that general principle (though no equines of any kind actually appear in Buridan’s writing). For Lipowski, this scenario helped to explain the type of anxiety that he was witnessing around him. He called it an approach-approach conflict: faced with enticing options, you find yourself unable to commit to any of them quickly. And even when you do choose, you remain anxious about the opportunities that you may have lost: maybe that other stack of hay tasted sweeter.
>
> Lipowski summarized his theory in a [paper](http://ajp.psychiatryonline.org/article.aspx?articleID=151991) published in The American Journal of Psychiatry, in 1970. “I maintain that it is specifically the overabundance of attractive alternatives, aided and abetted by an affluent and increasingly complex society,” he wrote, “that leads to conflict, frustration, unrelieved appetitive tension, more approach tendencies and more conflict—a veritable vicious cycle.” That cycle, in turn, likely had “far-reaching and probably harmful effects on the mental and physical health of affected individuals.” Lipowski concluded that the overabundance of good scenarios was the main source of the anxiety around him. It was here, in the land of plenty, he wrote, “that the fate of Buridan’s ass haunts us.”
>
> While Lipowski’s work received some immediate wide attention, it soon fell into relative obscurity. Thirty years later, the Columbia University psychologist Sheena Iyengar [revived](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/11138768) the idea of conflict created by an overabundance of choice—a concept that the Swarthmore University psychologist Barry Schwartz would then popularize and rename as the [paradox of choice](http://www.newyorker.com/magazine/2004/03/01/select-all)—though, unlike Lipowski, she focussed largely on the concept of cognitive demands. When shoppers had to choose between jams or chocolates, she found, they were more likely to make a selection when faced with six choices than when presented with twenty-four or thirty. They were also more satisfied with their ultimate selection. Too much choice, Iyengar concluded, would reduce motivation. Why exactly that would be the case, however, remained unexplored. One possibility, she hypothesized in her conclusion, was that an abundance of options may “simultaneously attract and repel choice-makers,” an emotion-based explanation not unlike the one that Lipowski had considered. Still, the potential of an underlying emotional tug-of-war remained more speculation than fact.
>
> Around the time that Iyengar and Schwartz were writing, Amitai Shenhav, now a psychologist at Princeton University, began to examine the literature on anxiety—one of the core emotions that people feel when they’re pulled in two directions. As he read, he realized that the nature of the experiences being studied was generally negative by design. “When people study anxious emotional states, they usually use negative stimuli,” he told me. “Like electric shocks: something that makes you anxious because you want to avoid it.” When experimenters looked at choice, on the other hand, they focussed more on the cognitive demands and the results of decisions, not the experience of anxiety as the choice was being made. So, inspired in part by his reading of Lipowski, Shenhav decided to investigate whether making choices from among appealing options could, in itself, provoke anxiety—and whether that anxiety was caused by the sort of emotional back-and-forth that Lipowski had speculated about. “If you think about it, that’s a much more prevalent scenario, when we have to choose between things we experience simultaneously,” he said. “I’ve had intuitions about this my entire life—from decisions like where to go to grad school to where we’re going to go for dinner tonight.”
>
> In a [series of imaging studies](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/25024178), Shenhav and Randy Buckner, a cognitive neuroscientist at Harvard, observed students making various choices. One to three days before the actual study began, Shenhav and Buckner had all of the participants evaluate more than three hundred different products, ranging from iPods and digital cameras to water bottles and T-shirts. When the experiment started, participants were put inside an fMRI scanner, shown pictures of the objects, and then asked to indicate which ones they preferred: Would they, for example, like to choose a digital camera or a camcorder? (The participants were told that, at the end of the study, they would randomly receive their object of choice from one of the trials.) Each choice was between either two similarly ranked items—both relatively low-value or both relatively high-value—or two items that were on opposite ends of the spectrum. After they had finished making all of their choices, the participants were asked to rate each decision on a series of five-point scales: How emotionally positive did it make them feel? How anxious did it make them feel? And how certain were they that they had chosen the way they had wanted to? The participants were also offered the surprise opportunity to change their choice: If they had it to do over, would they go the other way?
>
> Unsurprisingly, when people were asked to decide between something like an iPod and a bag of pretzels, they didn’t feel particularly anxious: the choice was clear and life was good. When both choices were low in value, the emotions were similarly clear-cut. No one was particularly happy, but neither were they anxious. But when multiple highly positive options were available—a digital camera and a camcorder, say—anxiety skyrocketed, just as Lipowski had predicted. The choices between those objects that they valued most highly were both the most positive and the most anxiety-filled. The more choices they had—the study was repeated with up to six items per choice—the more anxious they felt. “When you have more good choices, you don’t feel better,” Shenhav says. “You just feel more anxious.”
>
> **_The neural data that Shenhav and Buckner collected told the same thing. There was a very clear dissociation between the positivity-related and the anxiety-related regions of the brain involved in each choice. In other words, one distinct activation pattern tracked closely with positivity—the value of the good options themselves—while another tracked with the anxiety of making each choice. The activity in the anxiety-related regions, in fact, predicted how likely it was that a person would want to reverse her initial choice. The more difficult the choice was to begin with, the higher the chance of reversal. “It might actually be tracking the conflict after the choice is made,” Shenhav speculates._**
>
> Perhaps, then, what we’re really seeing is how the old fear of missing out plays out in the brain. We’re surrounded by great choices to make, great places to be, great things to do—and that’s wonderful. But when we’re made to commit to one, just think of everything that gets away. Shenhav himself refers to it as the “neural correlates of First World problems.” We know that someone else is eating that delicious ice cream that we passed up—or filling that job that we turned down.
>
> Lipowski himself, however, didn’t feel as if the relatively minor scale of the problems that wealthy people face in any way cheapened the emotions felt by his patients. **_What changes as we move from the scarcity of wartime Warsaw to the abundance of the First World isn’t the nature of the anxiety, it’s just the nature and significance of the choice itself. In one case, it seems heart-wrenching; in the other, trivial. Our brains, though, don’t make those kinds of value judgments: to them, a difficult choice is a difficult choice. And difficult choices mean anxiety._**

[http://www.newyorker.com/science/maria-konnikova/bad-good-choices?src=mp](http://www.newyorker.com/science/maria-konnikova/bad-good-choices?src=mp)

### Josh — 2014-08-02T18:29:33+0300

Will need to reflect on the correlations....comes down to Dopamine vs. Cortisol on some level and all of the stuff related to that.

### nonchalant — 2014-08-05T01:19:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anxiety-and-choice.11439/post-136763>

> [Josh said:](/goto/post?id=136517)
>
> Note the fMRI study methodology....
>
>
>
> [http://www.newyorker.com/science/maria-konnikova/bad-good-choices?src=mp](http://www.newyorker.com/science/maria-konnikova/bad-good-choices?src=mp)

I disagree with the article, Josh. The anxiety isn't caused by the choice to be made.
The anxiety is caused by the inability to make a good, fast decision. That donkey needed more energy to easily make a good decision. Like you said, dopamine and cortisol. I know what it is like to not want to make a decision. How lame is that? It's like someone who wants to be a child forever. Guess it takes electrons to be mature.

### Inger — 2014-08-05T09:36:31+0300

I agree with you Sally - you nailed it right there

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anxiety-and-choice.11439/post-137284>

> A while back I read an article about a specialised region above the frontal lobe, excuse me if I am about to make some mistakes I am not exact on this, I think it was called the anterior cingulate area. Apparently in humans, and I understand that this may not apply to animals I don't know, there is yet another area within this that in some is developed more than in others, or even not developed at all. # Thread: Rewild Yourself with Daniel Vitalis and Dave Asprey

**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### PaulG — 2014-08-03T14:11:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rewild-yourself-with-daniel-vitalis-and-dave-asprey.11442/post-136610>

Just listened to the latest Dave Asprey Podcast and found it very interesting, entertaining and thought provoking and certainly will resonate with everyone here. One of the best podcasts I've listened to..

[http://www.bulletproofexec.com/rewild-yourself-with-daniel-vitalis-podcast-141/](http://www.bulletproofexec.com/rewild-yourself-with-daniel-vitalis-podcast-141/)

> Daniel Vitalis Bio section said:
>
> I believe that in your heart resides the vigorous spirit of something wilder, something stronger, and something more brilliant than you have ever imagined. At the heart of each of your cells resides the ancient record of your DNA. It reaches back through the ages connecting you to each and every one of your ancestors, and to the wildness that existed in our species before the degenerative processes of domestication took hold.
>
> Awakening that wildness is what fuels my passion for the work that I do! It’s why I have created this website and why I’m encouraging you to “ReWild Yourself!”
>
> I’ve come to believe that all strength, all vigor, and all vitality emanates from the wildness of a thing. The further down the path of domestication we travel the more mal-adapted we become. Our health degenerates as does our will and our sense of purpose.
>
> Perhaps it’s presumptuous, but I believe you would like to be healthier, more self-sufficient, more confident and more in control. I would like to help you achieve that. I teach an approach to lifestyle that can put us in touch with our instincts and our incredible powers of adaptability. I’ll never tell you what to do, but I will always remind you of what you already know, that a wild intelligence resides in your DNA.
>
> My philosophy has helped thousands of people to rediscover their inner physician, their inner teacher, and that part of themselves that is free and independent to think, to feel, to perceive and to lead. It has worked for them and I believe it can work for you too.

# Thread: Minoxidil and hair loss
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/minoxidil-and-hair-loss.11443/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Ben1983 — 2014-08-03T18:01:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/minoxidil-and-hair-loss.11443/post-136627>

What damage can the use the hair loss product "minoxidil" do to our health? I used this product for three years, combined with laser treatments to scalp (initially) and had good results as far as some hair growing back. I stopped using this product about a year ago as I just became too lazy, but then I noticed my hairline gradually start to recede again (hair loss completed halted when I was on the product). I was going to start using the product again but a naturopathic friend of mine suggested not to as it could damage my health in one way or another...I should have asked for details but I did not hence why I'm here now. The strength of the solution I was using was 3%. Also, on that note, does anyone know of any solutions that work to stop hair loss and possibly even grow back some hair? My hair is very thick and is only receding at the front...there's no hair loss at the back of my head.

### SeaHorse — 2014-08-03T18:46:39+0300

# Thread: Digestive enzymes...strange reaction
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/digestive-enzymes-strange-reaction.11444/>
**Subforum:** Adrenal Rx and Leaky Gut Rx
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Ben1983 — 2014-08-03T18:26:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/digestive-enzymes-strange-reaction.11444/post-136628>

I have had some issues with my gut over the past year. I get minor cramps after eating or drinking things such as coffee, dairy products and eggs. I had a food intolerance test done which showed quite a number of foods on the list. I decided to try digestive enzymes for the first time as I have read that that can help. I ordered a product called "Beauty Enzymes" by a company called "Sunfood". The product was far from cheap at nearly $70 for one month supply. It is supposed to be the most potent plant-based digestive enzyme product available (apparently 7 times more potent than most enzyme products and twice as potent as the next-best formula). Anyway, the first day I tried it, I took it with my first meal of the day, along with my other supplements, and didn't really notice much other than strting to feeling very slightly agitated, but also a little more alert and aware for the whole day. I also took the product again at my second and final meal of the day. I felt like the agitation increased somewhat just prior to going to sleep, too.
The next day was somewhat more interesting. I once again took the product in the morning along with my other supplements, however, this time I felt a huge rush feeling go straight to my brain probably five minutes after I took it. I was quite drowsy when I took it (as I am most morning when I wake up), but when the rush hit my brain (and it kind of scared me but only a little) I went from that drowsy state to feeling very alert and focused....probably unlike I can recall. I also felt like things that I had been worrying about previously were now just a piece of cake....it felt awesome. However, that state of mind only lasted fro 30-45 minutes and then I started to feel agitated again as I had the day previously.....but this time I felt agitated to a greater degree. I was at work thinking about going home because I just felt so strange and I just wanted to get away from people incase something weird came out of my mouth....that's how strange I felt. Anyway, the agitation died down after several hours, but since then I haven't been game enough to try it again. It's probably hard to tell what would have caused that reaction without any labs to go by, but I'm just wondering what some possibilities may be? Oh, and if I could always have that state of mind I had for 3o minutes, my God could I achieve much more in life!

### SeaHorse — 2014-08-03T18:35:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/digestive-enzymes-strange-reaction.11444/post-136630>

Sounds like some excitatory neurotransmitter activity was triggered in your gut…..have you read the Brain Gut series of blogs? Sorry I can't be more helpful...

### Ben1983 — 2014-08-03T18:55:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/digestive-enzymes-strange-reaction.11444/post-136633>

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-best-laugh-ive-had-in-a-long-time.11452/post-136715>

I was watching an old episode of the sitcom The Detectives last night on DVD and haven't laughed so much in a long time... plus it was epi-paleo themed, lol. I managed to find it on Youtube so if anyone wants a good laugh.

"On Customs and Excise duty in Felixstowe, Bob and Dave manage to get themselves trapped in the back of a pilchard lorry -- and Bob hates pilchards. A tenuous line of reasoning brings Dave to the conclusion that they must be in Venice -- when in fact they're in Amsterdam. Unbeknownst to them, they are also in possession of a large quantity of diamonds that had been smuggled there in the lorry -- attracting the attention of the "wrongful owners""

# Thread: Low Cortisol - Major Fatigue

### [email redacted] — 2014-08-04T21:03:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-cortisol-major-fatigue.11454/post-136730>

So... I've made a lot of strides the past few years to really clean up my family's and my diet, get the toxins out of our house, and just generally move towards healthier and more natural living. Despite this, I continue to feel worse and worse and worse. The last few months it's really escalated to the point where I'm so fatigued I can hardly keep with with necessary things around the house and I'm gaining 3+ pounds a week. I'm up nearly 50 pounds (yikes!). I have lots of other weird symptoms to go along with all of this too.

Anyhow, had some labs ordered. What's your take?
Cortisol
AM 4.1 (13-24)
Noon 3.2 (5-8)
Afternoon 1.2 (4-7)
Night .4 (1-3)

Salivary Estirol 0.1 (1-4)
Testosterone 37.64 (20-60)
Progesterone 136.8 (50-400)

I also had a GI pathogen screen done. The results showed that there was moderate growth of Escherichia coli isolated.

### Martin — 2014-08-04T21:21:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/science-daily-info-source.11455/post-136734>

[http://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2014/08/140801091114.htm](http://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2014/08/140801091114.htm)

There always seems to be many articles relating to Optimal principles on this website.

I used to get a magazine, Science News, (actual _paper_ - remember?!) from a person at Georgia Tech and devoured the latest news. That's where I first saw the Chixaloub crater info which answered a question I've had since a young boy - what killed off the dinosaurs?

Another article reported on wooden cutting boards being more sanitary that the new-fangled plastic ones that grew bacteria at a faster rate. The tannins in the wood is a natural inhibitor. This started me taking a critical view of "new" things sometimes being crappier than the old.

NSH

"Elevated 5-a-reductase activity is associated with polycystic ovary syndrome and hirsutism in women, benign prostrate hypertrophy and premature baldness in men, and obesity and insulin resistance in both genders. Low 5-a-reductase activity may result in reduced conversion of testosterone to DHT and undervirilization in males."**

> Thanks so much for this advice. Could you point me to more information about the RIA method? I've never heard of it.
>
> I've been told to avoid iodine as it's (counterintuitively) going to aggravate my thyroid issue. You don't think that's the case? Thank you!!

Spend some time on U-tube listening to what dr Brownstein have to say on thyroid and iodine. Start here:
**Iodine: The Most Misunderstood Nutrient Presented by David Brownstein MD**

**..........**
**Go to esoterix.com**
**look for: test menu-->endocrinology**

**[500903](http://esoterix.com/prodserv/test_menu/test_detail.asp?center=endocrinology&testid=1120)**

**Growth Receptor Test Group II (Growth Hormone, IGF-I, IGF-II, IGFBP-3, GH Binding Protein)**

**Methodology
Double antibody RIA, Blocking RIA after acid:alcohol extraction, Blocking RIA after acid:alcohol extraction, RIA in dilute serum, Ligand-mediated binding assay
**
\=======
Esoterix is part of LabCorp
so you draw blood for this test at LabCorp locations.

////

### BTA — 2014-09-09T04:44:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-testosterone-low-oxytocin-possible-pcos-high-glucose-hypothyroid.11475/post-140357>

From what I understand (_could be wrong_) the liquid iodine you find for sale is from old oil wells - rather toxic in nature. The type the body needs is atomic nascent iodine. Only one company sells the real deal - from crystal mines more than 8,000 ft below the earth. This iodine product uses glycerine as the base not ETOH - That form is full of GMO-corn to make it. Not good. Global Healing Center - Dr Edward Group.
[http://www.globalhealingcenter.com/nascent-iodine-detoxadine.html](http://www.globalhealingcenter.com/nascent-iodine-detoxadine.html)

Regarding K+, the RDA (_recommend disease allowance lol_) is 3 grams. Just 20 minutes of walking uses up 3 grams of potassium. Which means now you need 6 grams to break even & you'll need alot more if you're taking Magnesium to offset it. The only thing I've found to get high doses is a combo of a electrolyte powder and a drink made by company called Vitamin Water - that uses RO water (good!) & high dose potassium - no calories. Look for "revive" zero by Vitamin Water.
