# Thread: Project: Quantifying Health, Performance & Longevity
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/project-quantifying-health-performance-longevity.11478/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 13
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### biohacked — 2014-08-07T17:40:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/project-quantifying-health-performance-longevity.11478/post-137154>

**_Note: Post I made on another forum, putting here in case others interested._**

For a couple of months I've been working on a project to explore and find the most useful biometrics for health, performance and longevity. These include lab tests, data from tracking devices and anything else we can track on our bodies.

I've already interviewed experts in areas such as **HRV for work out optimization**, **oxidative stress vs antioxidant impact**, **brain optimizations via methylation, methylation in general and micro-nutrients** and **mitochondria** and am lining up many others.

These recordings will be going up in a podcast named "[The Quantified Body](http://thequantifiedbody.net/)" on August 15th. From then on I'll get an episode out every week on new biomarker related topics.

You can check it out here: [http://thequantifiedbody.net](http://thequantifiedbody.net)

**My story:** Data has been extremely useful for me managing the recovery process from my own chronic health issue - life changing in fact. But I've spent a lot more money than I needed to, if I'd know what I know now about labs, proxy biomarkers etc. So the goal of this podcast is to spread the word about the most useful biomarkers, what reliably changes the markers, and the topic of leveraging quantification of our bodies to improve our lives.

The more I've dug, the more I've realized how useful biomarkers truly are is not straightforward: cost, accuracy, practicality, research study quality backing their use, benchmarks available? healthy control benchmarks? optimum performance benchmarks? stability? access for consumers? access for physicians? risks of data corruption e.g. some blood labs have this issue if treated wrongly... etc. etc..

I have stacks of topics I'm interested in exploring in this area and people I'm interested in getting on the show because I think they have important things to say on the topic.

But I'd love to know **what you are interested in hearing about on biomarkers and their use** - I'd like this to become a really useful resource for you, for biohackers.. for citizen scientists etc who do a lot of this stuff already, but probably have questions and ideas about areas you'd like to know more about.

I've put up a 2 Question survey here for you to add topics and people for me to put on the list to chase down and explore. Literally 30 seconds to fill in.

[http://thequantified...ics-survey.html](http://thequantifiedbody.net/topics-survey.html)

I'll post a summary of the survey results in this thread once I've got a decent sample - as I think that could already be useful.

Also welcome any other ideas you have about this project. It's a learning curve for me, but already been very rewarding just with the first 4 episodes recorded - I'm sure you're going to get a lot out of them - especially the practical details.

### QuantumRadical — 2014-08-07T21:02:23+0300

### nonchalant — 2014-08-08T03:28:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/project-quantifying-health-performance-longevity.11478/post-137234>

My most important biomarker is EMF sensitivity. I have had a tickle in my throat (when I was taking iodine) around EMF, and also tinnitus, currently, as well as feeling a slight trickle of electricity around some electronics. Someone else got a rapid heartbeat, and some get a heat sensation on the skin.

Other biomarkers for me are tolerance of heat and cold, hunger, eyesight (blurriness), hearing acuity, pain tolerance, condition of skin. Someone else, if they get a cut, check their blood color and viscosity. Jack checks his night vision. For thyroid issues, the outer third of the eyebrow is an indication, as well as the fingernail lunulas.

### biohacked — 2014-08-08T07:22:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/project-quantifying-health-performance-longevity.11478/post-137265>

Thanks for the positive energy @[PaulG](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1838/) - I'm looking forward to launching it next Friday.

The interviews have been great so far and I feel it will get much better as I find my groove with it.

My story in brief is CFS/ Biotoxin Illness - if you're interested in it specifically I've written about it and put video of my presentation at last year's Quantified Self here:
[http://biohacked.net/treatment-strategy-for-me-chronic-fatigue-syndrome/](http://biohacked.net/treatment-strategy-for-me-chronic-fatigue-syndrome/)

### biohacked — 2014-08-08T07:26:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/project-quantifying-health-performance-longevity.11478/post-137266>

@[nonchalant](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/191/) I would describe those as symptoms and your health condition, rather than biomarkers.

I'll explain.

Biomarkers are specific quantified metrics like lab tests and measurements through tracking devices. The interest in biomarkers and quantification is that it is an objective look at the situation, whereas using symptoms tends to be more subjective. Symptoms are still useful but not as reliable for taking decisions and judging your status (health condition, performance etc.).

Hope that's helpful for you.

### biohacked — 2014-08-13T16:11:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/project-quantifying-health-performance-longevity.11478/post-137748>

As promised I'm posting the results of the survey below - in bold are the interviews that have already been done and will be published as episodes the first few weeks.

If any of these topics are of particular interest to you, keep the feedback coming and I'll put more effort into covering the areas most in demand. The interviews so far have been much better than I'd even hoped for - I'm truly humbled by the sharing of outstanding practical information on this topic and how engaged the guests have been.

**What Topics Would You Like to See Discussed on the Show?**

Tracking devices and their value/accuracy
**Best HRV monitor devices \[1st Episode covers this topic, coming up this weekend\]**
Blood tests - measuring and improving
**Increasing mitochondria function \[One of first episodes covers this, more to come in 2nd month\]**
Cognitive performance
**Methylation \[Several of first episodes cover this\]**
Detoxification
Athletic performance
Improving dopamine release in regards to athletic performanceOxygen Saturation Testing
Electrical muscle stimulation - measuring strengthening and healing
Brain fog/ anxiety
Nootropics
NeurofeedbackEffects of WIFI in schools - can it be quantified?
Sleep related Biomarkers and biohacking
**How accurate are lab tests and how are reference ranges determined and how have they changed in the past 50 years \[is an ongoing theme we cover in each episode in relation to biomarkers\]**
Use of natural medicine
**What biomarkers are there for various diseases such as Cancer? \[covered in part in episode on oxidative stress markers\]**
What biomarkers are there for various injuries such as head trauma?
Biomarkers for depression and adhd

**Who Would You Like Us to Get on the Show? And What Topics Would You Like Them to Talk About?**

**Dr. Ben Lynch (Methylation, Epigenetics) - \[already interviewed, in upcoming episode\]
William J Walsh (Methylation) - \[already interviewed, in upcoming episode\]**
Dr Amy Yasko (Methylation, Lyme, Genetics, Epigenetics, Helicobacter Pylori)
Chris Kresser (health in general, diet)
Dr. Jeffrey Bland
Steve Maxwell (Ayurvedic medicine)
Round table discussion - Ben Greenfield and Dave Asprey OR Dave Asprey and Jack Kruse \[3 way informal conversation of biomarkers\]

# Thread: So long for now….
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-long-for-now%E2%80%A6.11479/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 13
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### SeaHorse — 2014-08-07T22:25:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-long-for-now%E2%80%A6.11479/post-137194>

I just finished rereading Jack's post for newbies "Why newbies are important…." and felt it was time to put into action something I've been considering for a long time….redirecting my energy away from the computer and the forum. It has played such a pivotal role for me these past three years and now it's come down to the crux….I'm addicted to the site and keeping up with all the new information offered, life stories, Jack's blogs, hilarious exchanges etc. etc. It's all so amazing and has changed how I think and act profoundly and yet I sense very strongly that how much time I spend here is becoming a detriment. The compulsion to read everything has been feeling like the chemistry of addiction and that's something I know well and want to address. It is also why I'm making this a public declaration…you know…12 steps and all that.

If I am honest and look at my environment what I see is a number of things that I can still change and improve. I don't need to be in front of a computer for my job and yet I'm here in front of the screen for hours at a time. I'm heading back to the basic principals that we've all been presented with. More time in nature, less emf, lots of sun while it lasts, good water and as much cold as I can imagine. I have the opportunity to have these things right in my home and I don't want to miss it.

So, without getting sappy, I'm sending all of you a hug and an enormous amount of gratitude. JK, the biggest electron collector and donor of them all….thank you! This is my N=1 for the next few months…..I'll be keeping up with blogs and webinars, but aside from that I'm going to be living my life "in the flesh," ….let the experiment begin!!

xo Sarah

### nonchalant — 2014-08-07T22:49:51+0300

Be well!

I will miss your insights, Sarah.... But I completely understand. Still, it would be great if you manage to sneak in from time to time with a few updates to let us know how you are doing.... Good luck!!!

### caroline — 2014-08-08T02:01:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-long-for-now%E2%80%A6.11479/post-137214>

Jack has said we can do too much reading ....way better to be living life in the flesh!!

### Martin — 2014-08-08T18:20:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-long-for-now%E2%80%A6.11479/post-137299>

Well said, Sarah.

It's the dilemma I think we all seem to deal with in many ways - The Kraken included! I absolutely LOVE to learn about all this and have found a "home" on the web here. BUT! If we are to improve our lot in life, we need to embrace the wild side more and more. I've been doing that a lot lately. This place has given me the tools. materials, and more importantly, the CREDIBILITY of Dr. Jack Kruse (afore mentioned "Kraken" guy) and his insights and innovations that are following the Laws of Nature while adding in a few human strategies to adjust to our changing planet.

I know I'm addicted to learning things. I have always read, looked, listened, asked, about the Natural World. When the computer came along - boom! The Biggest Brain in the World was at my fingertips - literally. Addictive!!!

Seahorse, you contribute so much to the rest of us. Let us know how're you're doing.

NSH

### Jude — 2014-08-09T02:56:22+0300

All the best

**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### caroline — 2014-08-10T10:37:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-tilted-quilt-random-musings-4.11488/post-137465>

I love when Dr. Kruse does these ...not as much brain pain!

It is great to go back and read old blogs and realise that my comprehension is better!

I love this quote from CT#6 ..... "you must live congruent to your biology!

Do you have a favorite quote? one that brings it home for you?

I am constantly amazed to realise that Dr. K. did his quilt document so many years ago.... and he has been patiently and surely and with the utmost focus dragging us all forward .....

talk about heavy lifting!

### caroline — 2014-08-10T11:47:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-tilted-quilt-random-musings-4.11488/post-137467>

Dr. Kruse asks us in this blog ..... How discerning are we when "something popular" could be the wrong thing for us?

I am wondering if I can look backward and see a defining moment for me ....... I have to think about this....

Can you see a defining moment when you realised that often things aren't as they seem ...no matter how popular?

Why did I buy every diet book ever printed ..... and think this is going to be the one?

How did all of us who have been here from the beginning and lots and lots of newbies too..... know instinctively that this all just made so much sense?

This must mean that we are discerning ... and given a choice.... we can make good decisions.

To be sure .... it has been a struggle for all ......but we are learning to follow the science and we are learning that Mother Nature knows best and she is the ultimate task master.

In some quarters we are seen as lambs without a mind of our own .......that couldn't be farther from the truth.

Thank goodness that Dr. K. has never given up on us!

''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''

I am freezing cold all the time, especially my feet, but also my core, and in the winter I just can’t get warm. I am not exaggerating — I am cold beyond cold (but don’t have Reynauds specific symptoms).

Ask doctor to prescribe you natural thyroid hormone (NTH) from pigs.
You may also buy it from e-bay. Thyroid-S (they are one grain pills)
Try 1-3 grains. Start with one pill, observe your self for a week, then eventually increase to 2 pills, and so on. Do not use other thyroid hormones (T4 or T3) while taking NTH.

Get body thermometer that is liquid based. Ideal would be mercury.
Measure under arm, hold for 10 minutes.
Ideal temperature is 36.6C=97.88F

Measuring thyroid get:
Total T4
Free T4
TotalT3
FreeT3
ReverseT3
TSH

All above values are important to know.
\---
take
2/day-->Super Selenium Complex, 200 mcg 100 capsules (lef.org)
after you took selenium for a month, take

1 dropperfull/day=48mg/day

[http://www.amazon.com/Lugols-Iodine-Solution-oz-Twin/dp/B001RRH814/ref=sr\_1\_1?s=hpc&ie=UTF8&qid=1408803496&sr=1-1&keywords=Lugol's Iodine](http://www.amazon.com/Lugols-Iodine-Solution-oz-Twin/dp/B001RRH814/ref=sr_1_1?s=hpc&ie=UTF8&qid=1408803496&sr=1-1&keywords=Lugol%27s+Iodine)

Lugol's Iodine Solution(2 oz.) Twin Pack(2 bot.)

If you would take different product, remember that it is about (iodine + potassium iodide), not only about iodine.
If you would have any adverse reactions stop immediately.
You would have to go thru detoxification process first. (rare).

Iodine is halide, so are bromine, chlorine, fluoride
Minimize exposure to harmful halides.
watch this video:
Iodine: The Most Misunderstood Nutrient Presented by David Brownstein MD

\--------------------------------------------------
Every time I increase my water intake to increase hydration, it seems that all I do is pee it out. Really, the amount I have to urinate makes it very hard to hydrate.

You may be low on vasopressin, ADH antidiuretic hormone.
Ask your doc to prescribe to you nasal spray or pills for trial.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/feedback-about-labs.11542/post-138395>

Hi there, I would really appreciate some feedback about my labs.
I don't have much money for good labs and have a super high deductible. Been working with a doctor on my plan for about 8 months. He dx with hypothyroid and low testosterone. He put me on bio identical testosterone but it did nothing so I stopped it. I am still on 130 grains of nature thyroid but don't see much change there. I've been looking over my labs and the numbers seem strange to me. Maybe there are fine but I just want to be sure I'm getting the right treatment. I tried iodine supplementation but has a terrible detox reaction so stopped it. I would try again though with the right supplementation support... Which I did not do thoroughly enough. I am supplementing with selenium now. Hoping to gather some money together good labs soon! There are 3 sets of labs spanning over 6 months.

Symptoms:
Up early.. Cortisol issues
Mood dysregulation
Anxiety
Digestive/ leaky gut stuff
Chronic fatigue
No sex drive
Hard to lose weight although at an average weight (5'6, 145)
Awful pms lasting 2-3 weeks. Cycle has gone from 30 days to 46 days over 8 month span

Labs 12/11/13:
Ferritin 13
Testosterone 37
DHEA 316.4
Free t3 2.52
Free t4 0.62
Tsh 1.77

Labs: 3/5/14:
No DHEA numbers
No ferritin numbers
Testosterone 35
T3 2.44
T4 0.72
Tsh 0.72

Labs 6/8/14
DHEA 205.1
Testosterone 23
T3 3.04
T4 1.14
TSH 0.19

I really appreciate any help!
Thanks
Tara

### nonchalant — 2014-08-21T02:59:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/feedback-about-labs.11542/post-138420>

Hi Tara. Not sure about your labs, but I also had problems with iodine. I finally put 1 drop of 2% in a water bottle, and took only a tablespoon each day, and also drank about a half gallon of water within a couple hours of the iodine. Finally I could tolerate it. A test showed that I was extremely deficient in iodine.

Eventually, over a year or two, I was able to take a couple drops/day. But now I don't supplement any more. I just eat seafood daily.

### JanSz — 2014-08-23T18:51:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/feedback-about-labs.11542/post-138715>

Until you figure out how to get better labs, work on
selenium supplementation and Lugol's solution as form of iodine+potassium iodide supplementation.

If you are sure that you had iodine detox problem that likely indicate that you are very high on the other halides.

You must stop taking in new and detoxify those that you already have.

But possibly you were too low on selenium.
2/day-->Super Selenium Complex, 200 mcg 100 capsules (lef.org)

Take selenium for a month, try Lugol's again.

If it happens again, detoxify.

I have noted that there is Detoxamin back available for purchase.
[http://www.detoxamin.ca/index.php?option=com\_content&task=view&id=1&Itemid=2](http://www.detoxamin.ca/index.php?option=com_content&task=view&id=1&Itemid=2)

Few years back I used it myself, highly efficient.
You may want yo get 2-3 jars at once (this may be short supply).

Other way of getting it is thru doctor and compounding pharmacy.

After a month on Detoxamin suppositories, try Lugol's again.

Repeat another 2-3 times if need.

Good iodine/iodide, actually super excess of iodine/iodide is basic need.

In the meant time work also on:
Circadian Rhythm
and
Vit D levels (you want to be at 100 or more)

### Hope — 2014-08-22T04:09:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/darkening-windows-and-pretty.11550/post-138493>

Hi all,

do any of you have suggestions for how to darken windows to make them not let light through, and in a pretty way? I have to still be able to open blinds/curtains so am limited. I used to have liners before curtains but had to put towels on the top.......to keep light from sneaking through there. But I'm kinda sick of that look.
SO I thought I'd ask how anyone here does theirs.....oh and I'd Love to see a pic. But even a quick description would be so appreciated.

My brain feels tired.. ....from all the organizing, cleaning, unpacking, figuring out what to put where, LOL

### Lahelada — 2014-08-22T04:20:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/darkening-windows-and-pretty.11550/post-138494>

If the blind top offends you, you could fashion it a disguise . A box, a swag or a wooden pelmet,whatever fits with the rest of the decor.

I have thought about replacing the current super light blinds with room darkening ones - but they let a little light through at the sides. # Thread: Duke University Bioelectricity: A Quantitative Approach free course: Aug 25th
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/duke-university-bioelectricity-a-quantitative-approach-free-course-aug-25th.11553/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Penny — 2014-08-22T06:19:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/duke-university-bioelectricity-a-quantitative-approach-free-course-aug-25th.11553/post-138515>

Uh, I about fell over dead when I saw Duke University offering a free course in \*bioelectricity\* through Coursera... Wow, I sure know how to have fun....

In this class you will learn how to think about electrically active tissue in terms of individual mechanisms, and you will learn to analyze the mechanisms quantitatively as well as describe them qualitatively. The course uses many of the same examples used by Hodgkin and Huxley, who won the Nobel Prize for their experimental unraveling of the mechanisms of the nerve axon of the giant squid, and their creation of a mathematical model of membranes and propagation to understand its function. That work has been the foundational element of most subsequent understandings of electrically active tissue, whether in nerves, the brain or in muscle, including the heart.

In this course, topics include:

1.  The electrical charging of active membranes from the creation and use of differences in ionic concentrations across the membrane.
2.  The stimulation of the membrane, both naturally and from engineered sources.
3.  The creation of action potentials by the membrane in response to stimulation.
4.  The chain reaction of membrane responses, with each small region or cell initiating an action potential in adjacent ones.
5.  The observation of associated electrical currents in terms of extracellular wave voltages they create, the basis of clinical measurements such as the electrocardiogram.

[https://www.coursera.org/course/bioelectricity](https://www.coursera.org/course/bioelectricity)

### Penny — 2014-08-22T18:17:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/duke-university-bioelectricity-a-quantitative-approach-free-course-aug-25th.11553/post-138573>

### nonchalant — 2014-08-22T19:11:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/duke-university-bioelectricity-a-quantitative-approach-free-course-aug-25th.11553/post-138577>

Aw, thanks Penny. I was in a quantum physics class, and chatting with one guy, who was excited that he thought eyesight had quantum elements, if he could just figure out the machine involved. I mentioned water, and he seemed to go into shock, as far as I could tell from a distance. I later gave him some links to Becker and Kruse (this was before Jack's vision blog- he had just gotten to quantum bone and such), and my friend said he had to think things over, and that's the last I heard from him.

That physics class was very time consuming, and I soon fell behind and stopped participating. Actually, the first lecture or two were the best, in my opinion, because they expressed the broad ideas. But then they made certain assumptions about what was worth pursuing, or they explained away certain aspects and made them "non issues". Later classes went deep into the details of the aspects that were 'important', and I lost interest (and I fell hopelessly behind.)

I wanted to spend my time on Jack's website instead. But I kept participating on the Coursera forum. The physics students were more open/inquisitive than I experienced with the biology or genetics classes I took. It was fun, discussing things with them, because they knew far more than I did about physics, but I had some fascinating knowledge that they hadn't encountered before. I'd say something offhand, and someone would want more info, and I had to scramble to find a cite or two to back it up.  I felt like Dr. K, offering one-liners here and there. Some were ignored, some not.

I'd like to do another course, some day.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/duke-university-bioelectricity-a-quantitative-approach-free-course-aug-25th.11553/post-138609>

**Posts:** 3

### Da-mo — 2014-08-22T17:52:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/%E2%80%98bad-fat%E2%80%99-may-be-good-for-cancer-patients.11558/post-138564>

[http://news.sciencemag.org/biology/2014/07/bad-fat-may-be-good-cancer-patients](http://news.sciencemag.org/biology/2014/07/bad-fat-may-be-good-cancer-patients)

Conversion of WAT to BAT linked to cachexia . . . is this just the body devouring itself to fuel cell immortality?

Moved to other thread . . . [http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/things-i-saw-in-the-news-today.11246/page-6](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/things-i-saw-in-the-news-today.11246/page-6)

### nonchalant — 2014-08-22T18:10:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/%E2%80%98bad-fat%E2%80%99-may-be-good-for-cancer-patients.11558/post-138571>

Well, Dr. K said that Warburg's metabolism wasn't the whole story.
Interesting, Da-mo. Whole lot more energy in fat than in fermenting carbs.

### Grmoline — 2014-08-26T22:23:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/%E2%80%98bad-fat%E2%80%99-may-be-good-for-cancer-patients.11558/post-138966>

I don't understand the link to cancer. The Warburg Effect kicks in with cancer cells due to the disruption in mitochondrial function that blocks the cell's ability to derive energy from the ETC. It can only use glucose as fuel, which is presumably why going ketogenic helps. Fat burning liberates ketones, which healthy cells can use as fuel, but not cancer cells.

# Thread: Chinese Healthcare Woes...Will They Become Ours?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chinese-healthcare-woes-will-they-become-ours.11566/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Josh — 2014-08-23T21:03:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chinese-healthcare-woes-will-they-become-ours.11566/post-138721>

> Violence against doctors in China has become a familiar occurrence. In September, 2011, a calligrapher in Beijing, dissatisfied with his throat-cancer treatment, stabbed a doctor seventeen times. In May, 2012, a woman attacked a young nurse in Nanjing with a knife because of complications from an operation performed sixteen years earlier. In a two-week period this February, angry patients paralyzed a nurse in Nanjing, cut the throat of a doctor in Hebei, and beat a Heilongjiang doctor to death with a lead pipe. A survey by the China Hospital Management Association found that violence against medical personnel rose an average of twenty-three per cent each year between 2002 and 2012. By then, Chinese hospitals were reporting an average of twenty-seven attacks a year, per hospital.

Here is where it gets interesting....

> If the official channels yield nothing, patients will often stage protests. In 2013, a thirty-four-year-old mother alleging malpractice after a Cesarean section rallied a dozen friends and relatives to storm Beijing’s No. 6 Hospital. She ended up winning about ten thousand dollars in compensation. In May this year, in Kunming, a man whose wife and baby had died during a Cesarean section climbed onto the roof of the hospital carrying his other child in his arms. He threatened to jump while a crowd of relatives below brought traffic to a standstill. When a patient dies and family members blame the doctors, they sometimes lay the corpse in front of the hospital entrance until they are paid off.
>
> In Beijing, I met Benjamin Liebman, a professor at Columbia Law School, who has published a study on “malpractice mobs” in China. He told me that protests consistently extract more money from hospitals than legal proceedings do. Family members can even hire professional protesters. One report in Shenzhen mentioned an average price of fifty yuan a day for the service of a protester. The radiologist in Shanghai told me, “If your mother dies in the hospital, there will be an agency that comes to you and says, ‘We can help you. We can have twenty guys who can come to the hospital, blackmail them, and share fifty per cent of the profits.’ They’re very professional.”
>
> Surprisingly, in a country where even a small public gathering can spark a government response, malpractice mobs often proceed without intervention. It’s possible that the authorities see them as justified or simply as an efficient method for negotiating compensation. Some scholars even argue that the Chinese government tacitly welcomes such protests, considering them to be a relatively harmless outlet for discontent and an early-warning signal of more serious social unrest—a phenomenon that the Berkeley political scientist Peter Lorentzen, in a study of authoritarian regimes, has called “regularizing rioting.” Liebman told me, “Protest becomes the mechanism for providing social security and for distinguishing people who need help.” The problem is, he said, “everyone knows the government has funds to pay people who petition or protest, and that incentivizes more protest.”

[http://www.newyorker.com/magazine/2014/08/25/under-the-knife?src=mp](http://www.newyorker.com/magazine/2014/08/25/under-the-knife?src=mp)

# Thread: I Bet This is EMF/Protein Folding Dependent -Spinal Morphology Stuff
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-bet-this-is-emf-protein-folding-dependent-spinal-morphology-stuff.11580/>
**Subforum:** Educating Doctors
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Josh — 2014-08-25T20:27:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-bet-this-is-emf-protein-folding-dependent-spinal-morphology-stuff.11580/post-138862>

> Effects of N-cadherin disruption on spine morphological dynamics
>
> Structural changes at synapses are thought to be a key mechanism for the encoding of memories in the brain. Recent studies have shown that changes in the dynamic behavior of dendritic spines accompany bidirectional changes in synaptic plasticity, and that the disruption of structural constraints at synapses may play a mechanistic role in spine plasticity. While the prolonged disruption of N-cadherin, a key synaptic adhesion molecule, has been shown to alter spine morphology, little is known about the short-term regulation of spine morphological dynamics by N-cadherin. With time-lapse, confocal imaging in cultured hippocampal neurons, we examined the progression of structural changes in spines following an acute treatment with AHAVD, a peptide known to interfere with the function of N-cadherin. We characterized fast and slow timescale spine dynamics (minutes and hours, respectively) in the same population of
> spines. We show that N-cadherin disruption leads to enhanced spine motility and reduced length, followed by spine loss. The structural effects are accompanied by a loss of functional connectivity. Further, we demonstrate that early structural changes induced by AHAVD treatment, namely enhanced motility and reduced length, are indicators for later spine fate, i.e., spines with the former changes are more
> likely to be subsequently lost. Our results thus reveal the short-term regulation of synaptic structure by N-cadherin and suggest that some forms of morphological dynamics may be potential readouts for subsequent, stimulus-induced rewiring in neuronal networks.

[http://journal.frontiersin.org/Journal/10.3389/neuro.03.001.2007/abstract](http://journal.frontiersin.org/Journal/10.3389/neuro.03.001.2007/abstract)

# Thread: Best way to measure temp of water

**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Tms — 2014-08-26T17:19:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/best-way-to-measure-temp-of-water.11589/post-138947>

Hi! I'm just trying out face dunking. Definitely an invigorating experience. But Im kinda flying blind because my thermometer will not measure water temp, only my temp. Which one do I do get to measure water temp?
Thanks!
Tara

### nicld — 2014-08-26T17:27:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/best-way-to-measure-temp-of-water.11589/post-138949>

I use a basic meat thermometer from Walmart. Some have a pool thermometer too.

[http://www.amazon.com/Weber-6492-Or...d=1409063185&sr=8-1&keywords=meat+thermometer](http://www.amazon.com/Weber-6492-Original-Instant-Read-Thermometer/dp/B009IH0BZ0/ref=sr_1_1?ie=UTF8&qid=1409063185&sr=8-1&keywords=meat+thermometer)

### nonchalant — 2014-08-26T18:11:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/best-way-to-measure-temp-of-water.11589/post-138953>

Tara, so glad you tried it! It is scary, the first time, to actually put your face in that water, especially when your skin gets close enough to feel the cold air above it!  But it's surprising how easily the face can handle the cold. Just think, your body is already starting to make some changes to prepare for colder temps that may be coming.

I'd just put a few trays of ice in the bowl and let them melt about half way. But I'm sure you're curious about the temp. I bought a laser thermometer similar to this: [http://www.amazon.com/Nubee®-Temper...ometer/dp/B00CVHIJDK/ref=cm\_cr\_pr\_product\_top](http://www.amazon.com/Nubee%C2%AE-Temperature-Non-contact-Infrared-Thermometer/dp/B00CVHIJDK/ref=cm_cr_pr_product_top)

But any food thermometer that gets down low enough in temp will work.

>

### Penny — 2014-08-27T04:20:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-92-year-old-mother-in-law-just-had-a-stroke-there-was-a-clot-in-her-brain.11595/post-138991>

They tried dissolving it with DMSO, but it was too big, so they just removed it surgically - her left side is still wonky but better than before they took out the clot and her speech is slurred - I have been looking for a blog on this site that talked about strokes - I thought there was one? She is undoubtedly MTHFR 1298 and maybe 03 - has had a couple miscarriages - they will remove the rest of the clot with DMSO and told us the next 24 hours will be critical - I read in the Earthing book how that is helpful, but probably would do nothing while whe is in the hospital except maybe donate electrons by holding her hand - assuming I had any at this point...  So, if anyone has any ideas that might help her, I am all ears - or even where the stroke blog is ?
thanks!

### Penny — 2014-08-27T06:49:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-92-year-old-mother-in-law-just-had-a-stroke-there-was-a-clot-in-her-brain.11595/post-138998>

Thanks Caroline - that was a great blog - it at least answered a question I had about blue light and being on the computer all day - it seemed to me that you are damned if you look at the screen without the glasses because you're getting too much blue light and damned if you put on blue blockers because you screw up the photoperiod/circadean clock... I of course am totally happy when I'm outside and not on a computer but alas, my children are addicted - mother-in-law has all the modern death traps - a brand new big screen tv that is always on, the cordless phone with the base station 5 feet from the chair she is always in, the cell phone next to her - she had her cataracts done recently also - it's funny, well, not funny really, I was just reading this guy - again - Gestalt Genova from the phoenix rising forum who had been "Accutaned" at 15 and developed fibro and fatigue and all that stuff and he said he got better using glutamine for his leaky gut which was causing his immune system to go nuts every time he ate anything - but then he took Indole-3-carbinol and DIM because it helped his estrogen issues because he had the COMT and a couple of cyp SNPs which caused him to not break down estrogen - he also took d-glucarate - I thought he probably had missed the EMF boat like so many of them do, but didn't know the mechanism - and let's face it, I only could fathom 20% of CPC 6 -

I was thinking my mother-in-law could be more affected by the stuff in EMF 4 but it all ties in - she is deficient in electrons, hates the sun, and is surrounded by EMF and blue light - funny, I used to think that everyone's adrenals were blown that couldn't deal with the sun, but now I realize - I think it's true anyway - that it's a vitamin A story just like that second hormone 101 blog Dr. K wrote - which totally cracked me up

Well, there is a silver lining in the cloud - my sister-in-law does Reiki...

I just found a site that blocks blue light from electronic devices - some kind of filter for the device:
[https://www.lowbluelights.com/detail.asp?id=116](https://www.lowbluelights.com/detail.asp?id=116)

### caroline — 2014-08-27T08:38:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-92-year-old-mother-in-law-just-had-a-stroke-there-was-a-clot-in-her-brain.11595/post-139011>

I am pretty sure I am getting more and more confused with each passing day ...and each passing blog!

I absolutely get the big picture .....but I am floundering in the details lately.

Dr.K. did talk about ICU and all the bright lites and equipement etc. He said in his training one of the doctors would always insist on limiting the lighting etc. for the babies born early.

Could you limit the lighting and hug her a lot? ...... I suppose just rubbing her back or her feet or holding her hand would be huge what about soft music? my choice would be Pink Floyd...lol

and then there is what JK told us ....if your redox is good ...you can cope with what our modern world is dishing out!

I seem to think if we are really thinking and doing what we can ...and then thinking some more ....and always question everything......we may stand a chance....

### caroline — 2014-08-27T08:39:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-92-year-old-mother-in-law-just-had-a-stroke-there-was-a-clot-in-her-brain.11595/post-139012>

what about eye shades? and a MHS???

### caroline — 2014-08-27T08:45:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-92-year-old-mother-in-law-just-had-a-stroke-there-was-a-clot-in-her-brain.11595/post-139014>

I wish we could see a pic of you ...... I like bunnies ....but I like people much better......

I may never shut up here .... sorry!

Do you remember when Dr. K's FIL was so sick? He felt pretty terrible that he couldn't do much - and his FIL had never listened to him anyway. Even tho Jack has told me a million times that not everyone makes it ...it still hurts when it is personal. I still have to re visit that conversation with him.

Even tho we love some one so much ..... we really can only help ourselves.

Is you DH on board with all this?

### Penny — 2014-08-27T17:05:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-92-year-old-mother-in-law-just-had-a-stroke-there-was-a-clot-in-her-brain.11595/post-139041>

DH thinks Jack is nuts - because DH read one of the early EMF blogs - not sure which one - but DH is quite well versed in physics/electronics/chemistry - in fact, DH thinks he has solved the universal field theory - weirdly, I think he could actually wrap his head around this stuff with aplomb, but alas, you know how when you're stuck in your paradigm it is tough to think outside that box... I told another engineer last weekend when we had a party on top of a mountain about one of those electricsense interviews where the engineer got brain cancer and asked the brain surgeon why and the brain surgeon said it might be EMF - so he traced it down to his alarm clock at 12 milligaus that had been sitting 6 inches from his head for years... now he has a site and is devoted to spreading the message - but I bet if you had told him prior to that experience that it cause him problems, he would have also thought you were nuts...

so the engineer I was talking to started thinking about how he had this alarm clock by his head for the past 40 years - from a child really... and he wanted more information - so you have to chip away at people - my constant bitchfest about the blue blockers and the computer are even making headway as my oldest son told me his eyes were hurting and so he put them on -

Their friends are even asking me questions now - like why they have constant headaches and their hormones are all screwed up - 2 years ago they would roll their eyes and walk away -

I think it's the best we can hope for really -

We went to visit her last night - she looked next to perfect - a bit of a slight slur on the left side, but I could understand perfectly what she was saying - in fact, she was more lucid than we were - and was doing leg lifts on the bed - now, getting my sister-in-law to tell the doctor "hey, you might want to check for MTHFR 1298" is proving to be quite a challenge... and I thought I was a control freak... oh wait, I AM a control freak...

Wow, the EMF in the room was insane... it wasn't even ICU... there is a computer 5 feet from the bed, some electronic thingy that I think causes her legs to be compressed automatically at the foot of the bed, a whole rack of outlets behind her in case of emergencies - I held her hand for awhile in the fond hopes of making a dent, what else can anyone do?

# Thread: What exactly is the Fournier Effect?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-exactly-is-the-fournier-effect.11598/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Sammy — 2014-08-27T07:34:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-exactly-is-the-fournier-effect.11598/post-139003>

Hi all,
I was hoping whether someone can explain the Fournier Effect. I did not find any information about the Fournier effect by google search. I have read here and there in the forum and from what I gather it has something to do with using both cold and heat together. Also a bit confused as it seemed some were using the heat and cold simultaneously(e.g. CT with some parts of the body kept warm), while others were using cold and heat sequentially(e.g. cold showers followed by warm showers)

Would also love to hear how you all utilise the Fournier Effect as well.

Thanks :3

### prAna303 — 2014-08-27T08:17:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-exactly-is-the-fournier-effect.11598/post-139007>

Go search for: photothermal deflection spectroscopy

Also read this book, have many great details for us humans...
[http://books.google.se/books?id=eVt...=0CFIQ6AEwBw#v=snippet&q=photothermal&f=false](http://books.google.se/books?id=eVtPPpvJDOUC&pg=PA111&lpg=PA111&dq=fournier+semiconductors&source=bl&ots=q6D9kP5laU&sig=ZnreAezwaD29AkS2asaqusMVqSM&hl=sv&sa=X&ei=7mf9U4SoDInUaqO6gtAM&ved=0CFIQ6AEwBw#v=snippet&q=photothermal&f=false)

[http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Photothermal\_spectroscopy](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Photothermal_spectroscopy)

### yewwei.tan — 2014-08-27T09:57:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-exactly-is-the-fournier-effect.11598/post-139020>

First off, basic principles:

-   infrared heat from warm treatment of a region expands the EZ of water in the cells that are heated
-   cooling water increases the EZ of the water, as well as increases electron flow in that water (more O2)

Obviously you can't both heat and cool a single cell at the same time. But you can do one after the other, which would theoretically increase energy in the cell via 2 different mechanisms, with the net effect being more positive.

A key point is that energy from the cold can be "transmitted along the body's topological insulator network" to other regions of the body that aren't actually being cooled, while energy from heat is restricted to the EZ expansion of water in the regions that are heated.

By that logic, you should be applying warmth to the region that you want maximal increase in energy, since it's getting the EZ increase from the heat, while simultaneously having higher energy water flow to it from the regions of the body that are being cooled. Hot+Cold can thus be said to be superior to Hot-then-Cold for this reason.

Example: for PCOS, Dr Kruse has recommended putting the lower body in warm water and then wearing an ice vest, thereby helping the ovaries with increased energetic flux.

Sidenote: the ultimate fournier effect therapy would probably be full immersion in cold water, while being bombarded from all directions by red light lasers which expand cell water EZ a lot without much change in temperature at all (less than 1 degree Celsius change according to Pollack). Add a Schumann resonator for even better effect

### Sammy — 2014-08-28T12:28:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-exactly-is-the-fournier-effect.11598/post-139134>

Thanks for the explanation Yew Wei, I was experimenting having a warm shower after CT. Not sure if it did anything though. I don't have the facilities to do hot+cold simultaneously so I can't really compare.

PrAna(lovely name by the way), I read the link but I can't say I understand

### Lahelada — 2016-06-21T15:11:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-exactly-is-the-fournier-effect.11598/post-197007>

> [LouiseAngela said:](/goto/post?id=197004)
>
> I thought Jack doesn't like Schumann resonators?

You are correct. Bear in mind though that the post you are reading is from 2014. We have come a long way since then.

A quote : " Colder temperatures increase the strength of a magnetic field. Why? Cold can have an effect on magnetism, because cold can have an effect of how fast or slow electrons move. This links the sun’s power to the magnetic field of the Earth. It also links the **_Schumann resonance_** strength to the sun. When the sun is shining the Schumann frequency is strongest. When the sun is shining the Earth’s magnetic field is weakest. This implies that the Schumann resonance strength varies inversely with the magnetic field’s strength. _**At night time,**_ **_the magnetic field is stronger on Earth._ So when you use a fake generator better be well thought out."**

### Lahelada — 2018-10-12T15:27:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-exactly-is-the-fournier-effect.11598/post-252961>

> [axel brandt said:](/goto/post?id=252731)
>
> . That means that grounding is better done at night?

Yes but no.Light and dark initiate different programs in our cells. There is no choice. Both "programs" are necessary for optimal function.
Basically you are missing sunlight as the cathode ray in your thinking. Earth is the anode. Any anode hit by a cathode ray causes electrons to be liberated.This free electron flow ceases at night.

That is presuming that you are thinking about grounding for catching electrons from above. But **y**ou are meant to do other things at night.
Grounded sleeping has benefits,though. Your sleep is "deeper" as one would say in common parlance. We would have slept on the ground in an increased magnetic field "listening" to the Schuhmann frequency . The Schuhmann frequency exists in every neural system.Alpha waves take over our "timing" while the SCN is uncoupled from photic input. The geomagnetic pulsations of the Earth are phase matched to the human brain and shared to the brain wirelessly. While we sleep the direct current in neurons changes signal direction compared to when we are awake.The DC current in the interfascial water layer changes it polarity. Magnetic fields however still re energise our protein polymers. Melatonin is necessary for this. Absence of light/absence of new electron input is necessary for melatonin to be secreted. Melatonin offsets the effect daylight had on us. Good sleep maintains myelin,which regenerates tissues in autophagy. etc. If nnemf are present little or none of this happens. "Quantum sleep" blog is a way better explanation though than what I have just written.

Side note: If ever I cannot sleep a quick stroll across a lawn,dragging my bare feet makes me sleepy, providing I am not accidentally hit by light on my way back to my bed.

### Huck — 2014-08-29T05:06:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/whats-this-called.11613/post-139208>

My doctor is learning a new diagnosis technique and offered me a free analysis since she needs people to practice on. I can't remember what she called it and I would like to do some research on the technique.

I laid on a table and she stood next to me. She placed specific substances on the table above my head. The she touched certain points on my head, chest, and body. I noticed they were the same as some of the points used in "tapping" and acupuncture. Next, and this seemed rather strange, she raised her arms and slowly swept them over my body, about 10" from actually touching me.

She compared this to a similar technique where you hold your arms out and do similar analysis. She said the difference was that with the arms out is active and laying down is passive.

Does anyone have the slightest idea what I am trying to describe and know what it is called? I believe it is muscle something.

I have been seeing her for about 5 years and trust her. Appointments are typically about 45 minutes long. I think my initial appointment was about 2 hours long.

> She is Board Certified by the American Board of Clinical Metal Toxicology, the American Board of Emergency Medicine, and the American Board of Internal Medicine.

The test indicated I had a problem with my teeth and sinuses. When she placed substances in the field (electromagnetically), the indication was I needed substances that are used for detox of heavy metals. I have a mouth full of amalgams that were put in when I was a young child (under 12). I also have 1 root canal that was redone about 3 years ago. If the amalgams are leaking mercury, that might explain a lot.

### Josh — 2014-08-29T20:47:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/whats-this-called.11613/post-139270>

> [Huck said:](/goto/post?id=139262)
>
> Thanks Josh. I may have to call my doc and ask her who she is studying under.
>
> What's a good test for heavy metals?

You and your doctor need to understand this together. That is what the resources are for. If you are comfortable with her approach, that is your decision. Dr. K's consistent clarification on metals is that they are a symptom rather than a cause. To not understand how you are no longer able to detoxify metals and how to resolve that issue, makes direct detoxification of metals risky and gives you a poor prognosis in the longer term. It easy to find evidence of metals in most people's systems through muscle testing, hair analysis, challenge urine tests, etc.. Figuring out what this means for the individual and whether to start detoxification is a more involved exercise. Dr. Tim Jackson has spoken at length about this in his Heal Your Hormones Series and elsewhere. He is a student of Dr. Stewart who learned a lot from Dr. Yasko and others. Without good context and supporting data, muscle testing and other resonant methods are the equivalent of a "blind man putting his hand on an elephant".....

We discussed it as it relates to APOE. I have APOE e3/e4 (or e4/e3). This puts me at high risk for Alzheimers and also indicates I have trouble flushing heavy metals. That's if I understood her correctly, which I think I do as the research I have done seems to back this up.

At 65, I am more interested in feeling good than long term improvement. 10 years of feeling good outweighs 20 years of slow improvement.

I've been fighting brain fog and fatigue too long as it is. I have trouble understanding most of what Dr Kruse writes because my short term memory doesn't allow me to keep very much info in short term storage. He packs so much info into 1 sentence, I can't even tie 2 or 3 sentences together. I bet there are lots of people having this same problem. I don't have a strong science background; but am intelligent enough to learn, except I can't remember enough of what I read and learn.

I read what you wrote the same way. It is like a riddle that everybody but me gets.

I take 100 mg of zinc a day and still test low.
I take 2 g of magnesium a day and still test low.
I eat 2 Brazil nuts a day and still test low for selenium.

My fuzzy brain thinks this might be because something else is binding to the receptacles and the zinc, magnesium, and selenium just get flushed out. Isn't that a classic symptom of heavy metal toxicity?

### jenaf — 2014-08-29T17:02:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-of-hr-monitors.11617/post-139249>

has anyone an idea of how much EMF is emited off of one? I've been asked to wear one as I implement some low level activity each morning (wanting to keep my HR at 70% so as not to aggrevate any breathing problems) but i'm not sure if I want to wear one everyday.
Any feedback?

### prAna303 — 2014-08-29T18:27:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-of-hr-monitors.11617/post-139254>

> [jenaf said:](/goto/post?id=139249)
>
> has anyone an idea of how much EMF is emited off of one? I've been asked to wear one as I implement some low level activity each morning (wanting to keep my HR at 70% so as not to aggrevate any breathing problems) but i'm not sure if I want to wear one everyday.
> Any feedback?

My first concern is about what it is made of, semiconductors.

Perhaps the EMF is low but you do really bad things to yourself with the pulseband and watch. You will in an instant add on to our constant stealing syndrome, in easy words... It is like having a vampire sucking energy from you all the time, slowly but with great effect.

I would not do that to myself.

### Shijin13 — 2014-08-29T18:29:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-of-hr-monitors.11617/post-139255>

> [prAna303 said:](/goto/post?id=139254)

When I used them - the batter never lasted longer than 2 work outs.... I stopped using them...now I know why the battery life sucked

### sjoshua — 2014-08-29T22:02:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-of-hr-monitors.11617/post-139276>

[https://engineering.stanford.edu/ne...rst-computer-using-carbon-nanotube-technology](https://engineering.stanford.edu/news/stanford-engineers-build-first-computer-using-carbon-nanotube-technology) maybe the next gen ones will be better at sharing

Things like this I would consider based on a cost/benefit...
Will wearing a HRM \[or any silicon-based electronic device\] be optimal/healthy to your body? I believe no. Especially around the heart...

Does the information gathered from doing so in an experimental design outweigh the potential negative effects? It could, you'd have to answer this for yourself. Wearing a device indefinitely vs. under controlled conditions for a small window of time over a short period of days is quite different.

Out of the box question: Could there be better ways of acquiring comparable data without a chest strap HRM?
I would say probably:
Finger pulse oximeters: [http://www.amazon.com/Finger-Pulse-Oximeter-SM-110-Carry-Wrist/dp/B003TJH3LI](http://www.amazon.com/Finger-Pulse-Oximeter-SM-110-Carry-Wrist/dp/B003TJH3LI)
There are a few wrist/band HRMs that could work as well, but larger investment: [https://www.google.com/search?q=wrist+HRM&source=univ&tbm=shop](https://www.google.com/search?q=wrist+HRM&source=univ&tbm=shop) (The Mio Alpha gets pretty good accuracy ratings from a lot of reviews, I'd looked into getting one in my past when I thought it would be fun)

# Thread: Raw bone broths.....!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/raw-bone-broths.11627/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Inger — 2014-08-31T12:31:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/raw-bone-broths.11627/post-139463>

Well, I think this is fascinating. I am going to try it as soon as i get some grassfed bones in my hands!
I am sure it must be way better than cooked!? Just full of great bacteria too - so good for us

[http://www.rawpaleodietforum.com/general-discussion/bone-broths-best-prepared-raw-not-cooked/](http://www.rawpaleodietforum.com/general-discussion/bone-broths-best-prepared-raw-not-cooked/)

Eric posts how he makes raw bone broths;

> On the topic of raw bone broths, I've been experimenting for the past year with making bone broth using fermentation. Basically I start as if I'm making some sort of fermented beverage, kvass-style. I use half-gallon mason jars, cut or grate 1-2 cups of nutrient-dense, starchy vegetable - beets, carrots, burdock root or some other root, or red cabbage - and put it in the jar. Next add a decently sized chunk of bone, ideally with connective tissue, fat and even some shreds of meat left on. Fill the jar with water, add a few pinches of unrefined sea salt (perhaps 1/4 teaspoon) and swish the mixture to mix everything nicely. Then let it sit at room temperature for a week or two, or three.
>
> The vegetables will start fermenting, releasing lactic acid along with all sorts of other beneficial things. The acid will help to draw alkaline minerals like calcium and magnesium out of the bone, and bacteria will start eating the physical components of the bone, liberating them into the solution as parts of the bacteria floating about. When the fermentation has used up most of the sugars in the vegetables, the broth is ready to drink (if you let it continue fermenting, other bacteria will start turning the lactic acid into acetic acid, aka vinegar).
>
> I've noticed that when I do this, much of the fat melts off the bones and forms droplets on top of the liquid, much like when you make cooked broth. I've also noticed that most of the soft tissue on the bones is either gone or very mushy by the time the bone is removed, suggesting some serious bacterial activity there. I've finally noticed that the bone is noticeably more porous at the end than when I put it in, suggesting some of the proteins that make it up were eaten by bacteria or dissolved by the acid and entered the solution. At any rate, this seems like a workable way to make bon

I suggest to real Eric's replays, he is a smart guy..... have been following him for years and he is real - pretty hard core  goes for the truth

### Lorenzo — 2014-09-01T04:50:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/raw-bone-broths.11627/post-139511>

Hey Inger,

I started searching for raw bone broth today & these were the only forums I could find on it, I also signed up for the raw paleo forum & posted the same reply there.

> I added about 800g/1.8lbs pork neck bones & 225g/0.5lbs of beef liver, some collard greens, mushrooms, onion, garlic, 1/2-3/4 tsp of salt & enough water to cover everything.
>
> My fast will probably end in 16 days, it would be ideal to have this ferment until then so I could start with the broth 1st few days, then move on to the flesh.
>
> I've eaten high meat before, but leaving this combo in water worries me since I've never done it before or seen someone eat it.....If I don't get a reassuring response or hopefully some pics I'm going to put it in a double boiler on a small burner & extract (not cook) tomorrow for a few hours then freeze until the fast is over.
>
> Do these pics/ingredients look like I have the right setup ?

# Thread: suboptimal growth hormone levels in children
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/suboptimal-growth-hormone-levels-in-children.11643/>
**Subforum:** Biohacking 101
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### kovita — 2014-09-02T20:50:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/suboptimal-growth-hormone-levels-in-children.11643/post-139698>

Friend's son was just diagnosed with low growth hormone levels. He is five and he is short for his age, taht is why they ran the test. Other hormones appear in range. I was womdering whether DHEA supplementation could be an option for a child. I think low carb diet is a mus.

### Shijin13 — 2014-09-02T20:56:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/suboptimal-growth-hormone-levels-in-children.11643/post-139700>

I looked into this when we first started dealing with chao's growth issues.

DHEA is naturally low in children, and begings to rise with the onset of puberty. high DHEA in children at a young age can lead to early puberty age 7/8 or 9, as well as other factors

GH -issues - the doc should be looking at the HPA. before starting anything I'd look at exposure to artificial light and its impact on hormones (go back and read CT7 for a refresher on when/how GH is released) IIRC its released 2x in a 24hr cycle - one is contingent upon melatonin release, and the other is contingent upon cortisol/dopamine release

I'd then start focusing on strict circadian cycles, as well as redox potential, and I'd add ice baths into the game.

### kovita — 2014-09-02T21:01:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/suboptimal-growth-hormone-levels-in-children.11643/post-139702>

> [Shijin13 said:](/goto/post?id=139700)
>
> I looked into this when we first started dealing with chao's growth issues.
>
> DHEA is naturally low in children, and begings to rise with the onset of puberty. high DHEA in children at a young age can lead to early puberty age 7/8 or 9, as well as other factors
>
> GH -issues - the doc should be looking at the HPA. before starting anything I'd look at exposure to artificial light and its impact on hormones (go back and read CT7 for a refresher on when/how GH is released) IIRC its released 2x in a 24hr cycle - one is contingent upon melatonin release, and the other is contingent upon cortisol/dopamine release
>
> I'd then start focusing on strict circadian cycles, as well as redox potential, and I'd add ice baths into the game.

thank you, Shijin, that is pretty much what I already told her. We are back at the universal recipe!

### Tms — 2014-09-03T23:03:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-survey.11649/post-139847>

Hi there
So I just had an EMF survey done by the power company.
My home measures btw 4.2-5.
She said it's because I'm so close to a 66,000 volt power line that allows electricity to travel from city to city.
She said that I am at the low end of above average.
I would appreciate any feedback/thoughts about the results and what it means for my health.
Thanks
Tara

### Shijin13 — 2014-09-03T23:09:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-survey.11649/post-139848>

MOVE!

you want your home & surrounding land to measure .05 milligauss or less...

my previous residence - the lowest reading I got in the house was 2.0; 1.5 was the lowest reading out side. The highest reading was coming from my dumb meter which topped out at over 800 milligauss.

wiring in the house was kicking out at over 200 miligauss

the house/land we moved too the lowest reading we got was .01 miligauss outside. the highest reading outside was .05 miligauss
in the house the lowest reading was .02 and the highest was 15miligauss - which we discovered was an amp and scrubber for an old hi-fi system - which when unplugged dropped that reading down to .04 miligauss

we've been in the house for over a month. the daily drive is worth every minute sitting in traffic

### caroline — 2014-09-04T02:13:28+0300

### Martin — 2014-09-06T17:40:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-survey.11649/post-140124>

4 to 5 mG is _bad_ news. And the company said it was on the low end of above-average!
Alpha UHS= great meter. I considered the Alpha, but settled on the MSI-95 meter, about$200. It’s top-rated among meters that cost thousands. Basically, gauss meters are a sensitive multi-meters, like this METEX here, and this company engineered a Hall sensor (black box thing) that is tuned to read magnetic fields. You move the remote sensor around the object you’re reading. It's a single-axis meter meaning you have to orient the probe around to get the highest reading, but that's why I got it. You can really get an accurate reading with a single axis meters while the triple-axis are averaged. Having said that, that "average" is perfectly suited for our quest to see what's around us.

NSH

# Thread: Boosting Superoxide Dismutase
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/boosting-superoxide-dismutase.11651/>
**Subforum:** Biohacking 101
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### persistence — 2014-09-04T23:00:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/boosting-superoxide-dismutase.11651/post-139938>

Has anyone seen credible research on ways to boost superoxide dismutase (SOD) levels, or to increase utilization of existing SOD? SOD is a critical antioxidant that fights against lipid peroxidation.

There are many over the counter supplements that claim to boost SOD, but I don't find research to support their claims, and many people say SOD will not survive the acids of the stomach.

### persistence — 2014-09-05T08:59:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/boosting-superoxide-dismutase.11651/post-139979>

One source of SOD I have read about is a manganese form that is taken sublingually. I have no evidence it absorbs better but would be interested in any references or opinions about it if anyone has those.

### cantweight — 2014-09-05T16:32:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/boosting-superoxide-dismutase.11651/post-139993>

Jack has discussed how this happens in the body, (I believe in some detail) but I couldnt tell you what blog/blogs it was....

### Grmoline — 2014-09-05T17:57:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/boosting-superoxide-dismutase.11651/post-140003>

There's a topical cream that combines glutathione and SOD, called Oxicell, made by Apex Energetics.

### persistence — 2014-09-11T06:38:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/boosting-superoxide-dismutase.11651/post-140622>

> [Grmoline said:](/goto/post?id=140003)
>
> There's a topical cream that combines glutathione and SOD, called Oxicell, made by Apex Energetics.

Is there any evidence that a topical formula like that would get substantial systemic absorption?

That particular product has alpha lipoic acid, which I am avoiding in the short term because I may have mercury toxicity. Mercury has come up high in urine, blood, and hair tests for me. At the time I developed my symptoms I started taking high doses of alpha lipoic acid, and it turns out that ALA is a very effect heavy metal chelator, but unfortunately it can move mercury into the brain and into the intracellular environment. So I may have ended up moving mercury that was in my tissues into places where it could do real damage. When and how you use ALA in a chelation program is tricky. Based on my research I am going to use Dr Andrew Cutler's ALA protocols, and until I get to the stage of that program that calls for ALA I need to avoid it.

Glutathione is a thiol and I'm avoiding that for now because thiols have an affiinity for mercury and can move it around in the body. I may do liposomal glutathione at some point since that has better penetration into the intracellular environment and tends to move the mercury from intracellular to extracellular environment and then to blood. Until I have amalgams removed, I am on a low-sulfur-food and low-thiol diet, trying to minimize how much mercury I mobilize.

### Marc Lacasse — 2018-03-21T20:19:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/boosting-superoxide-dismutase.11651/post-237677>

coffee enemas are reported to increase SOD?

# Thread: Grounding & Dirty Electricity
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-dirty-electricity.11668/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### BTA — 2014-09-07T05:48:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-dirty-electricity.11668/post-140173>

The US is the only country that allows electricity to be released back into the Earth.
About 70% of the electricity delivered from the substation , returns there via the ground. Thus, the ground in the US is loaded with "dirty electricity". It would seem dangerous to put your feet on the ground that's loaded with dangerous EMF from the power grid releasing energy back into the Earth?? Cows have been found to produce less milk and die early from touching ground loaded with dirty electricity. The Sine Wave is interupted by the dirty electricity. This leads to disease as the body can't repair it's self as well.

**Dr Sam Milham, MD**

Dr. Milham's groundbreaking research demonstrates a strong correlation between the rise of the so-called 'diseases of civilization' and the historical spread of electrification.

### Da-mo — 2014-10-28T23:02:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-dirty-electricity.11668/post-145457>

I don't think its the only country. Here in NZ the grid is a MENS system (Multiple Earth Neutral System). So long as the distribution system is balanced across the three phases there should be little residual current flow through the earthed neutral as the 3 phases sum to zero.

The earthed neutral phase is really only there to allow for fault currents to activate protection systems. These neutrals are usually connected to earth at the Star connections of transformers or generators where the three phases are connected to each other - thus cancelling out.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-faced-with-extreme-anxiety-or-stress-from-life.11674/post-140298>

JK..................................

When faced with extreme anxiety or stress from life my best advice to help you through it? Stand in the ocean when it is 50-55 degrees with your feet firmly planted in the sand.........you will ground yourself to improve your autonomic nervous system faster than anything that I know of. It is amazing. Once done, then walk on the beaches edge with feet in water. I have told many an educational consultant this......I do it every time I am near a body of salt water. It combines, CT, earthing, and Mg floatation to lower HS crp and improve LR and mitochondrial function in nanoseconds.

[http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.ph...-life-say-something-about-it-them.4860/page-4](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/the-love-of-your-life-say-something-about-it-them.4860/page-4)

But beach deprived girls what can we do??
I suggest a metal bowl outside with epsom salt water with bird sand and little pebbles placed on the ground in the sun,a thermometer and a brush for some piezo electric entertainment. Dunk ,brush, don 't rinse but repeat. Maybe we could even add a drop of lugols? Any element I have forgotten?

What I was thinking is how do we twice a year people get to emulate these effects as good as we can.
Metal bowl conducts grounding energy.
Water plus Epsom salts (plus lugols?) emulates sea water.
Pebbles stimulate piezo electric effect heightened by brush? Maybe a walk over wet grass and then back into the footbath would be better?

# Thread: Grounding in a basement
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-in-a-basement.11677/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### nicld — 2014-09-08T20:02:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-in-a-basement.11677/post-140311>

Winter will be here before we know it and there will be times it will be too dangerous to sit outside under my giant oak tree. I know that it was said that one can get grounded through concrete. We have an old basically root cellar for a basement (still have the remnant of the stairs to the outside down there) that I will be working on getting the peeling paint off of the floors and having pure concrete. I doubt if there was a vapor barrier put underneath since it the walls are 2 feet thick and old.

_**Can I still get some benefit from sitting on this in the winter?**_

I know that I am going to greatly miss my grounding time but it is hard to get out there with 3 feet of snow and -20F windchills. I am even thinking of getting a grounding rod and using that in the basement since we do have a little open concrete that exposes dirt that I can “plug” into. **_Thoughts??_**

### Jonathin — 2014-09-08T20:18:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-in-a-basement.11677/post-140313>

If you are thinking about doing some reconstruction, I would consider putting down ferromagnetic rock such as basalt into the soil. I have also heard of using magnetite worked into the soil. Even compacted AB (gravel and fines) might add the magnetic strength. You might not consider concrete a necessity if you did that.

### nicld — 2014-09-08T20:29:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-in-a-basement.11677/post-140316>

No, no reconstruction in the basement, just scrapping off old paint. Next house maybe, but not spending the money in this one.

### caroline — 2014-09-09T02:16:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-in-a-basement.11677/post-140343>

Josh I think has used magnetite ...I think I remember that right.....

### cinnamon — 2014-09-09T02:43:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-in-a-basement.11677/post-140347>

Based on my own n=1, yes, I think you might find grounding in the basement works. Probably not as well as being barefoot in your garden. However, when the temps are well below freezing, ice is everywhere, and you are risking frostbite, it could be worth a try!

When we first started talking about grounding on the forum it was the middle of winter here (January or February?) I started spending a half hour or so each day in my basement with my bare feet on the concrete floor. It seemed a bit unlikely to be useful to my brain at the time (I'm sure I was low on DHA!) But, strangely enough, I noticed some improvements in body composition almost immediately. In particular, my butt seemed firmer. And who wouldn't want that? LOL

Now I need to mention that my basement is old - the house was built more than 100 years ago. So, chances are excellent that there is no vapor barrier.

### nicld — 2014-09-09T15:51:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-in-a-basement.11677/post-140386>

> [cinnamon said:](/goto/post?id=140347)
>
> Based on my own n=1, yes, I think you might find grounding in the basement works. Probably not as well as being barefoot in your garden. However, when the temps are well below freezing, ice is everywhere, and you are risking frostbite, it could be worth a try!
>
> When we first started talking about grounding on the forum it was the middle of winter here (January or February?) I started spending a half hour or so each day in my basement with my bare feet on the concrete floor. It seemed a bit unlikely to be useful to my brain at the time (I'm sure I was low on DHA!) But, strangely enough, I noticed some improvements in body composition almost immediately. In particular, my butt seemed firmer. And who wouldn't want that? LOL
>
> Now I need to mention that my basement is old - the house was built more than 100 years ago. So, chances are excellent that there is no vapor barrier.

We have an old old basement too. I think that we saw that the first easement was put on the property in 1929.

I will try it, can't hurt. I agree, not as good as sitting outside but that is going to not be an option soon

Thanks everyone, now to get cleaning the basement again.

# Thread: High-Fives are the new hugs.....

**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 13
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Tanya — 2014-09-09T23:37:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/high-fives-are-the-new-hugs.11685/post-140423>

[http://www.dailynewsen.com/politics...gs-in-litigious-and-scary-times-h2604722.html](http://www.dailynewsen.com/politics/fist-bumps-are-the-new-hugs-in-litigious-and-scary-times-h2604722.html)

### cantweight — 2014-09-09T23:54:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/high-fives-are-the-new-hugs.11685/post-140425>

Oh that just makes me sad. My sons 3rd grade teacher hugged and kissed those kids everyday and at first I was shocked no one opposed it....seems everyone opposes affection these days when we need it most.

My son looked forward to Valentines Day like never before because she would put on red lipstick and leave a "kiss" on all the kids cheeks. I happened to be in the school that day and saw a line outside her door of her former students waiting for their "kiss".

It was awesome.

### nonchalant — 2014-09-10T00:04:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/high-fives-are-the-new-hugs.11685/post-140428>

In a quantum sense, it is more than sad. This "hands-off" policy is a form of neglect.

### Brother John — 2014-09-10T00:31:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/high-fives-are-the-new-hugs.11685/post-140430>

Tanya,
Yeah. The road to hell is paved with good intentions. Etiquette for hugging is a slowly developing thing even for adults. What I think would be just way better is just to define what a hug is. I E hands are here and here but not down there or there. But no that's too simple...
I have an incredible book called "Hug Therapy" and it is mostly about different types of hugs and the models are comical drawings of Bears hugging. Like polar bears. It was written by Kathleen Keating. It's a little thin book and on a 10 scale it's a 10. I just checked and it's available in print or kindle and it's cheap 8bucks for print and 7 for kindle. Sometimes I look at the pictures when there's no one around to hug. (Don't tell people I do that.) Here is a quote: The requirements for being a Hug Therapist and being a client are the same. Just being.
Here is the amazon link:[http://www.amazon.com/Hug-Therapy-B...F8&qid=1410297836&sr=1-1&keywords=hug+therapy](http://www.amazon.com/Hug-Therapy-Book-Kathleen-Keating/dp/1568380941/ref=sr_sp-atf_image_1_1?s=books&ie=UTF8&qid=1410297836&sr=1-1&keywords=hug+therapy)
Brother John
