# Thread: Fixing Aerobic Metabolism of Fats
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fixing-aerobic-metabolism-of-fats.11694/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### persistence — 2014-09-11T06:49:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fixing-aerobic-metabolism-of-fats.11694/post-140624>

Based on Metametrix ION Panel, and based on other symptoms I have, I apparently do not metabolize fats efficiently for aerobic metabolism. The ION panel has several markers for carnitine deficiency that suggest I am very low on carnitine. My adipate measures at 25.0 mcg/mg creatinine where the 95% interval tops out at 11.1. My suberate measures 6.4 mcg/mg creatinine where the 95% interval tops out at 4.6. High values on those two metabolites suggest carnitine deficiency.

I tried to address this by Acetyl-L-Carnitine, 500 mg, three or four times a day. This does not appear to be substantially improving my ability to metabolize fats. On a high fat diet I am basically stuck in glycolysis and get heavy acid accumulation on low amounts of exercise (even very mild walking). Is my dose appropriate?

How does one "debug" aerobic metabolism of fats? Is there any clinical path I can follow to get more information on what specifically is broken? I would really like to get this working.

### Josh — 2014-10-18T03:14:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tensegrity-4-comments.11698/post-144144>

> The effect of magnetic field on the dynamic of microspheres movement
> around a Nafion tube in Water
> Alireza A. Moayed1
> , Prof. Gerald H. Pollack1
> 1University of Washington, Seattle, US
> Abstract:
> It has been observed that the exclusion zone (EZ) around a Nafion tube or sheet, locally extends
> in length in the shape of fingers. In the experiments that have been conducted to study the effect
> of magnetic fields (MF) on EZ, it was observed that this finger orient itself toward the MF source.
> To study this phenomenon, a thin cross section of Nafion tube was placed at the bottom of a petri
> dish and filled with 1 micron microspheres and deionized water mixture. The petri dish then is
> sealed to prevent evaporation. After 30 minutes, usually a finger forms. Then a permanent magnet
> or an AC magnet is placed close to the petri dish. After 10 minutes exposure to MF, the finger
> orients itself toward the MF source location. Removal of the MF source, causes the finger changes
> its direction. It has been hypothesize that the appearance of fingers without magnetic field in the
> beginning of the experiment is related to Rayleigh–Bénard convection cells. Movement of
> microspheres inside the water is determined by Rayleigh–Bénard convection currents. When they
> move around EZ of the Nafion tube, they drag EZ with them and form a finger like shape. Since
> EZ has gel like properties, the shape of the finger after formation is conserved. The reorientation
> of these fingers due to AC MF could be related to two factors, the generated heat in water due to
> AC MF that creates a new convection current in water and the effect of electric field generated by
> AC MF on charged microspheres. The effect of permanent MF on the reorientation of finger is
> weaker than the effect of AC MF. However, the reorientation effect is still present. The effect of
> permanent magnet on finger could be related to several factors, the effect of MF on movement of
> charged microspheres, and the effect of MF on water which magnetizes the water and changes
> physical properties of water. Since the magnetization of water close to MF source is stronger than
> further points, it could generate a gradient of some force to be determined. In conclusion, it has
> been shown that AC and permanent MF can affect the movement of microspheres around EZ. The
> importance of this finding could be resulted in better water waste filtration, and better
> understanding of the effect of magnetic field on water and on biological systems.

[http://www.waterconf.org/participants-materials/posters/](http://www.waterconf.org/participants-materials/posters/)

### Darleen — 2014-09-11T21:35:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hormones-linked-to-depression-and-anxiety.11699/post-140659>

Hi All, I have just started reading Dr.K's book. He states that hormonal depletion is linked to things such as depression and OCD, that after 30 years of age hormones naturally decline especially on a SAD. He believes it is not genetic. My question is if this is correct, how would a child have anxiety, depression. Could it be that a child can have decreased hormones? If not genetics, then does the mothers state of hormones possibly determine the child's?
Thank you for any input. I've had anxiety and depression since at least 8 years old, (as far back as I can remember) I do not feel like anxiety meds are the answer.
Cheers to good health!

### nonchalant — 2014-09-11T22:37:00+0300

Hormones are energy, Darleen. A child could have decreased hormones if the mother also was energy depleted.
Anxiety meds are also energy, but if the body cannot direct that energy, chaos can ensue.

# Thread: Is there a way to measure the burning of stored body fat in isolation? (Lipolysis)
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-there-a-way-to-measure-the-burning-of-stored-body-fat-in-isolation-lipolysis.11702/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ColinGorham — 2014-09-12T07:56:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-there-a-way-to-measure-the-burning-of-stored-body-fat-in-isolation-lipolysis.11702/post-140712>

Question: Is there a way to measure the burning of stored body fat in isolation? (Lipolysis) In other words, how can you differentiate between fatty acids emptying out of long term storage within the fat cell vs the burning of recently ingested fatty acids that have not yet been stored within the fat cell?

I currently use a Breath Ketone Analyzer called the Ketonix Sport to measure Acetone as a Ketosis/ Lipolysis proxy. A measure of Ketosis, whether, Blood beta hydroxybutyrate, Urine Ketone Bodies, or Acetone in the Breadth just means the body has toggled from glucose/ glycogen to fat (both ingested & stored) as a primary fuel of metabolism. My self-experimentation, for instance, has shown me that after 1) CT, & 2) Vigorous or Prolonged exercise sessions ketone levels fall. (likely cause the body is burning rather than excreting ketones) I often wonder if we should be measuring something in addition to ketones. It would be great if we could measure other markers instantly. (For instance: RQ (Respiratory Quotient), Insulin, Glucagon, Insulin/ Hormone Sensitive Lipase, Cortisol, Adiponectin, or some other marker or proxy for the burning of stored body fat. The terms beta oxidation and lipolysis come to mind.)

# Thread: Examples of Scaling the Microcosm to the Macrocosm
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/examples-of-scaling-the-microcosm-to-the-macrocosm.11703/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### PaulG — 2014-09-12T13:07:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/examples-of-scaling-the-microcosm-to-the-macrocosm.11703/post-140724>

Can we scale the [evolution of the cell](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Evolution_of_cells) to the [evolution of the universe](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chronology_of_the_universe)?

In archaeology there are [transitional fossils](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Transitional_fossil) (A **transitional fossil** is any [fossilized](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fossil) remains of a life form that exhibits traits common to both an ancestral group and its derived descendant group.[\[1\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Transitional_fossil#cite_note-Herron-1) ) that lends considerable evidence to Darwin's Theory of Evolution. Of course, fossilized cells are not possible or [are they??](http://www.ox.ac.uk/media/news_stories/2011/110822.html) but I am wondering if we can infer support for the Ling's [Association Induction Hypothesis](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Association_induction_hypothesis) by looking for [homologies](http://evolutionwiki.org/wiki/Homology) in the cell physiology of other non-human species (plants, lower order animals. bacteria)

Nature and Evolution is very efficient at minimizing waste by utilizing patterns such as the golden mean and fractals that scale from [microcosm to macrocosm](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Macrocosm_and_microcosm). This is best summed up by the maxim of "As above, so below" of [hermeticism](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hermeticism)

If the Association Induction Hypothesis is true then similar mechanisms must be employed by nature at a macroscopic [scale](http://mabloom77.blogspot.com.au/2013/03/humans-scaling-universe.html). What are they? The cell is a cavity and JK talks about cavities in [Tensigrity 4](http://jackkruse.com/tensegrity-4-magnetism-electrons-sleep)
**

> Jack Kruse said:
>
> A water filled cavity is a great place to generate spherical standing waves which have identical wavelengths. We think of waves as moving through CSF water, but these waves stand still in water. The crest or amplitude of the wave still has the energy within this standing wave. This mode of action is how a guitar or violin make sounds from the standing waves on their strings above their cavity of air in the body of the instrument. Your brain works on the same physical properties. It is time for you to realize it.

> Jack Kruse said:
>
> Life was quite smart tapping the potential of water and the Earth’s magnetic field for electrons in this fashion. This points out why the hydrogen bonding network of water is critical in understanding magnetism. Since water is [naturally paramagnetic](http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=GDR3gS5ddh4), it changes its hydrogen bonding network based upon the magnetic field it is found within. This means that our mitochondria alone, can alter the water chemistry around themselves, because mitochondria make their own magnetic fields when there ATPase rotates in the innermitochondrial membrane. These membranes also have the largest electric fields in membranes [as I laid out here](http://jackkruse.com/ee-6-quantum-cell-theory-life-collective-phenomena/). This is due to the action of electric currents and the reduction of oxygen at its terminal step of electron chain transport. Nothing in the universe has the ability to transfer energy as well as water does or this easily to create complexity by increasing electron density within its structure. This helps explain why water makes up 71 percent of the earth surface and most of our cell’s insides as well. Coincidence? Doubtful.

See also**
[**Obcells as Proto-Organisms: Membrane Heredity, Lithophosphorylation, and**
**the Origins of the Genetic Code, the First Cells, and Photosynthesis**](http://www2.bgsu.edu/departments/chem/faculty/leontis/chem447/PDF_files/Cavalier_Smith_2001.pdf)

**[The origin of cells: a symbiosis between genes, catalysts, and membranes](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/3454290).**

**[Why Are Cells Powered by Proton Gradients?](http://www.nature.com/scitable/topicpage/why-are-cells-powered-by-proton-gradients-14373960)**

# Thread: Mobile Phone Companies Facing Lawsuits
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mobile-phone-companies-facing-lawsuits.11710/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Josh (Paleo Osteo) — 2014-09-14T00:46:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mobile-phone-companies-facing-lawsuits.11710/post-140892>

[http://stopsmartmeters.com.au/2014/...-biggest-public-health-cover-up-of-our-times/](http://stopsmartmeters.com.au/2014/09/13/mobile-firms-face-billion-dollar-lawsuits-will-this-mark-the-end-of-the-biggest-public-health-cover-up-of-our-times/)

[This story starts in Italy in 2012 with Italy’s supreme court upholding a ruling that said there was a link between a business executive’s brain tumour and his heavy mobile phone usage. The court ruled that scientific evidence in support of the Italian man’s claim was reliable and based on research that was independent and “unlike some others, was not co-financed by the same companies that produce mobile telephones.”

Fast forward to 2014 and move to the United States, where some of the world’s largest mobile phone companies are facing billion-dollar lawsuits brought by brain tumour victims. The biggest ever legal assault on the industry will seek to prove that mobile phone handsets can cause cancer and that FIRMS MAY HAVE DELIBERATELY COVERED UP CRUCIAL MEDICAL INFORMATION.

If the American litigation is successful, mobile phone companies will face crippling bills for compensation. What will this mean for Australian mobile carriers? How will this affect the legitimacy of the Victorian power distributors’ decision to use wireless technologies for their smart meters?

As investors digest the news, billions have already been wiped off the share values of some international wireless industry giants.

The pressure is now firmly on and it may well lead to the bubble of secrecy bursting and the truth FINALLY leaking out that wireless technologies are not so safe after all. It will be interesting to watch how the wireless industry’s spin doctors attempt to down play this announcement.](http://stopsmartmeters.com.au/2014/09/13/mobile-firms-face-billion-dollar-lawsuits-will-this-mark-the-end-of-the-biggest-public-health-cover-up-of-our-times/)

### caroline — 2014-09-14T01:33:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mobile-phone-companies-facing-lawsuits.11710/post-140900>

Thanks Josh....

the writing has been on the wall for a long time now.

# Thread: Short video of bacteria, biofilms & quorum sensing
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/short-video-of-bacteria-biofilms-quorum-sensing.11714/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jonathin — 2014-09-14T17:39:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/short-video-of-bacteria-biofilms-quorum-sensing.11714/post-140959>

This animation illustrates how bacteria form a biofilm. A biofilm - more commonly know as "slime" - is a surface-attached community of microorganisms. Formation of a biofilm involves a series of bacterial processes, including bacterial signalling and quorum sensing, production of pili and secretion of an extracellular polymer matrix.

### Martha Ray — 2019-11-08T03:00:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/short-video-of-bacteria-biofilms-quorum-sensing.11714/post-281994>

I have studied Candida in the past to no conclusions. I tried a candida diet for a short while but it was so hard I was unable to stick with it. I took a candida product called _Candex_ for a good long while and felt that it helped me but with time I lost interest in fighting candida.

I was just re listened to some videos by Dr. Thomas Janossy one from a Summit I listened to and another presentation he did on _You Tube_ titled, _Our Toxic World_. A little hard to follow due to his accent. I think he is Hungarian but he is from Canada, seems to be known for his detox protocol especially in Australia and with those using detoxing to treat Autism.
So not sure how Fluoride, aluminum, mercury and other heavy metals mesh with Optimal Health.
Since he mentioned Biofilms ....I googled Biofilms Jack Kruse and this post came up.
Dr. Janossy mentioned Biofilms and that reminded me of a product by _Food Research_ a 100% food product called _**Biofilm Detox.**_ It looks effective for parasites as well. [https://www.buyfoodresearch.com/products/biofilm-detox?\_pos=1&\_sid=34a3ab91f&\_ss=r](https://www.buyfoodresearch.com/products/biofilm-detox?_pos=1&_sid=34a3ab91f&_ss=r) I have studied parasites and heavy metals as well to no conclusions ie no idea if these are an issue for me and no idea how to use a protocol for them.
I am not in favor of synthetic supplements but am interested in herbs and food vs drugs for medicine. Not competing with what Light, Water and Magnetism accomplish but to support the body against toxins while not getting in the way of what the body does best. I am a newbie to the work of Dr. Jack Kruse and this OHF and still finding my way meshing what I am learning here with what I have wondered about before....

Three issues I have never specifically addressed with my health are possible issues with parasites, heavy metals and candida. I am sensitive to some foods, most all drugs and chemicals and EMF. Detoxing has not been a priority for me.

How to test to find out if Biofilm busting and Detox are needful ? And then, if needful, how to choose a protocol to treat?

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fuchs-dystrophy.11716/post-141021>

(I'm not sure where to post this, please move if it is in the wrong area.)

I'm interested in how the eyes may indicate (inflammatory) issues in the brain and liver. My understanding is that Fuch's Dystrophy is relatively rare, currently. But, given metabolic syndrome and an increase in inflammation, shouldn't we be seeing a dramatic increase of these sorts of eye problems, specifically due to blue light toxicity and computer use in youth?

If so, is mitigating blue light only part of the issue and liver toxicity and brain inflammation need to be addressed as well?

Or is this such a specifically genetic issue, one would have to address specific pathways?

(I have a student in her late 20s who has been diagnosed. She shows all the markers for long term inflammation and potentially some genetic markers. I'd hate for her to be blind in 10 years!)

Marie

### Marie — 2014-09-15T05:54:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fuchs-dystrophy.11716/post-141037>

Never mind.

It looks like blue blockers, glutathione, B-Complex, and saline drops are the way to go...

It does sound like a sign of the times...

It is very frustrating to look out into a sea of students and see how they are already falling apart.

Marie

Progesterone: 4nmol/L (no range)

**"5. Why does the classic paleo diet make your LDL rise so often?**
The answer is simple. Dehydration is favored when you eat lots of protein and starchy carbs. A large amount of protein and starchy carbs draws water and even will make your temperature rise. For every degree your temperature increases your basal metabolic rate increase 13%. This increase in temperature, causes water dehydration in your carbon nanotubes. If you add back water that is fluoridated you will not improve. The water should be spring, mineral, or treated with reverse osmosis or you will have severe low carb flu present. ironically this is what the paleosphere calls it. I do not."
My BUN/creatinine is down from 35 so its coming down. If my progesterone is bad then I'm gonna get salivary ASI/melatonin done.

### prAna303 — 2014-09-19T11:19:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/steve-jobs.11725/post-141387>

> [NeilBB said:](/goto/post?id=141266)

Consider this...

If i as a simple person who has worked in the business before knew it, guess he also knew it big time. Early on in this business i worked with semiconductors, mostly on the issue of ESD, also known as: Electrostatic discharge. Have i seen spooky actions? Yes, many times, both in lab and real world. Many of the companies i meet up with had big problems and still they have. When you read between the lines of all labs done back then you should be able to do the math.

Why did he go to the extreme? Just as simple as any other person would when you feel sick or ill... When answers do not apply to the issue you seek more and more, no matter what. Living and looking back at his history he found help in all the places he felt was untested, was it right? Probably not.

I would say that he did a Michael Jackson but he did not act the same way as he did.

Many of us who worked in the quite early years of semiconducting knows what would come to us, we did not just guess how big it should be.

### mafixit — 2014-09-17T22:30:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-new-hormone-labs.11727/post-141282>

I am peri-menopausal. Finally got my labs back this week and got a program adjustment from Dzugan. The have reduced my Tri-Est and increased my Progesterone, DHEA and Pregnenalone. Hopefully it will help with this head pain.

He has me on a magnesium, valerian root, progesterone and DHEA cocktail when I feel my headache coming on (which is every day). So far it has not given me relief.

Based on these labs they have increased my progesterone, decreased my estrogen, increased my testosterone and bumped my pregnenalone.

I might add I am on Tizanadine 4mg per night as a preventative for headaches (not working yet) and .125mg of Xanax each night to help me sleep. I still wake up several times during the night. I go to bed and wake up at the same time each day, regardless of the day.

Hormone labs read as such:

My cycle was at Day 55 when I had these done:

Progesterone: 0.7 ng/ml
Estrogens, Total: 412 pg/ml
C-Reactive Protein: 1.1 mg/L
Seratonin, Serum: 128 ng/ml
Testosterone, Serum: 25 ng/dL
Pregnenalone, MS: 54 ng/dL
DHEA-Sulfate: 88.4 ug/dL
Cortisol: 11.5 ug/dL
Vitamin D3 25-Hydroxy: 32 ng/mL

# Thread: Brain Responses to Food and Weight Loss - Research Review
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brain-responses-to-food-and-weight-loss-research-review.11731/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ssj3 — 2014-09-18T11:29:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brain-responses-to-food-and-weight-loss-research-review.11731/post-141327>

_The Study: Brain responses to food and weight loss, by Behary and Miras, in Experimental Physiology, 2014_
[http://www.researchgate.net/publication/265514402\_Brain\_responses\_to\_food\_and\_weight\_loss](http://www.researchgate.net/publication/265514402_Brain_responses_to_food_and_weight_loss)

**Review by Chris Beardsley and Bret Contreras**

**What's the background?**
Studying brain responses to food is challenging. However, functional magnetic resonance imaging (fMRI) and positron emission tomography (PET) are tools that have been recently developed for appetite research. FMRI provides information on brain activity by detecting changes in blood flow. PET uses radioactive tracers that detect surrogate markers of neuronal activation, including cerebral blood flow and glucose uptake, and also provide information on neurotransmission and neuro-receptor availability.
**What did the reviewers find?
_In healthy people_**
The reviewers explored the findings of the new fMRI and PET studies regarding brain responses to food in healthy people. From fMRI studies, they observed that in healthy adults, certain parts of the brain are activated preferentially in response to high-calorie food compared to low-calorie food cues. They also noted that increases in brain activation when switching from the fed to the fasted state measured using fMRI correlate with the increase in appeal bias towards high-calorie food. From PET studies, they observed that the release of dopamine in the brain was correlated with the subjective pleasantness of food.
**_In obese people_**
The reviewers explored the findings of the new fMRI and PET studies into the differences in brain responses to food in healthy and obese people. From PET studies, they observed that the activation of the hypothalamus in response to food is more reduced in healthy individuals than in the obese, indicating that a meal is less satiating for the obese. Similarly, many fMRI studies have found greater anticipatory responses in the brain to visual food cues in obese subjects than in healthy individuals. However, they also noted conflicting findings.
**_During weight loss_**
The reviewers observed that although few neuroimaging studies have examined periods of weight loss. However, one study found that an 8-week, low-calorie diet was associated with higher activation of brain reward systems in response to food pictures. Another trial exploring a lifestyle intervention observed higher activation in brain regions involved in food cue processing compared to a control group. Increased reward, perception and preoccupation with food could lead to reduced weight loss and even weight regain.
**What are the practical implications?**
The human brain responds to the rewarding value of food and promotes the consumption of energy-dense food. Also, diets lead to compensatory brain activation, which may reduce their efficacy. This implies that the psychological aspects of dieting should not be neglected when devising lifestyle interventions for weight loss.

# Thread: Circadian Success!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/circadian-success.11753/>
**Subforum:** Success Stories
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### nonchalant — 2014-09-22T19:52:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/circadian-success.11753/post-141625>

Ya'll are going to have to call me Dr. Non now. I fixed DH's rash!

Saturday evening he broke out in an extensive, blotchy rash over most of his body, especially in the less tanned areas like the back, insides of the arms and legs, etc. He got some relief that night from an OTC hydrocortisone ointment, but Sunday morning at 3am he was very uncomfortable. He didn't awaken me, poor thing. That morning I asked him to come outside and watch the sun rise with me. He stayed out in the sun for about 20 minutes in shorts, with bare feet and chest. He said the itchiness was getting worse, and I saw that the rashes were more red. He said even his head was itchy. But then in a few minutes he started feeling some relief, which rapidly took all of the itchiness away.

We came inside, because it was getting hot. But when his coffee was ready, he went back outside to sit in the shade of a tree. He asked why the sun had this effect, and I explained about the cortisol cycle and sunshine. I told him early sunshine would make cortisol peak earlier than it would otherwise. He was interested and listened closely. Maybe his wife isn't just a nut after all?

So I had the success of getting DH interested in the circadian rhythm! But wait, there's more!

Sunday evening his hives reappeared, though they weren't itchy yet. We drove home, so that we could visit the doctor in the morning. That night DH asked about putting some of the cream on as a preventive measure, but I convinced him to hold off. This morning, I asked him how he felt, and he said he was fine. Um... I was a bit surprised. He had a few of the blotches, but nothing like the evening before. And he slept through the night with no itching. He went outside to see the rising sun again with me.

We were both quite surprised. A while ago DH had a similar, though not as severe a rash, and it was a slowly progressing nightly ordeal for weeks until we went to the doc and got an Rx for a course of Prednisone tablets.

We both feel great now! Thanks Dr. K!

### Inger — 2014-09-22T20:18:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/circadian-success.11753/post-141629>

Yay......... very cool!

Congratulations Doctor Sally - nothing better than when we are able to helps our loved ones

### nonchalant — 2014-09-22T21:59:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/circadian-success.11753/post-141635>

Thanks! Nonchalant takes a bow.

I was wishing I had internet access, so I could ask about it on the forum. But then I realized I already knew the answer, I just had to figure out which protocol was key. That, and the fact that the rash was practically screaming "circadian rhythm" if you listened closely.

I told him that a less inflammatory diet would help, but he heard "no sweet tea" among the list and that was that... Oh well, one step at a time.

As to what caused the rashes, I think it was niacin. The previous rash was confusing, because poison ivy and travel/hotel stays were involved. I removed several supplements from his pillbox, but it didn't help. After he got the Rx, and a week passed with no issues, I gave him a tiny dose of 100 mg niacin on mondays only. The second rash occurred almost a week after the second such dose. And like I said, the second rash was more widespread and fast acting than the one before. A bit scary, actually.

I think his redox is low, and he cannot tolerate the niacin. And with his level of inflammation, perhaps one of those cytokine storm thingies was happening, despite the removal of the cause. We were away from the city, but you can't fix all the effects of a bad environment with a quick trip to the country. CT was also out, though he slowly (over several years) is tolerating more and more cold.

### fitness@home — 2014-09-22T23:08:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/circadian-success.11753/post-141640>

Congrats on helping him with the rash Dr. Non

The next thing you know, you'll be posting a picture on the CT thread of the both of you CTing oustside in matching water troughs!

> I told him that a less inflammatory diet would help, but he heard "no sweet tea" among the list and that was that... Oh well, one step at a time.

^^ That sounds like someone I know too...

# Thread: Magnetico + Faraday
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnetico-faraday.11758/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 26
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Inger — 2014-09-23T12:48:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnetico-faraday.11758/post-141715>

I have been thinking about buying a faraday canopy for my bed, as I can get 1 almost full and 2 very weak signals from neighbors into my bedroom. (I might talk to them about it and ask them switch it off in the night, but I need to go slow i feel...) I sleep great, but I still figure it cannot be good. I have a faraday canopy that do not filter magnetic radiation in my thoughts. Swiss daylite.

Nothing under my floor, just stone and earth... So it would not be an issue with me sleeping on a magnetico? Anyone have read/heard anything about this combination, or are using it yourself?

### Lahelada — 2015-01-27T18:16:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnetico-faraday.11758/post-154003>

This seems to be an answer that makes sense and is non geek accessible,for someone like me, like..

"At most frequencies, aluminum --\*especially\* the thin stuff, isn't all that great at absorbing EMI, and if you need to keep the RF from \*escaping\* from your design, you'll find it tends to bounce the RF around until it finds a seam somewhere else to escape. On the EMC side, if you're trying to protect your gear from inbound EMI, it can serve better in that fashion. On the other hand, steel will do a better.."
Full text here.

[https://answers.yahoo.com/question/?qid=20070413162340AAGdJCy](https://answers.yahoo.com/question/?qid=20070413162340AAGdJCy)

DHEA-S

Vitamin D
25-Hydroxy D3: 61.0 (no reference range listed)
25-Hydroxy D2: <5.0 (no reference range listed)
25-Hydroxy D Total: 61.0 (25.0 - 80.0 ng/mL)

Comprehensive Metabolic Panel
Calcium: 9.72 (8.00 - 10.50 mg/dL)
Alkaline Phosphatase: 54 (0 - 120 U/L)
Bilirubin, Total: 0.6 (0.2 - 1.2 mg/dL)
Protein, Total: 6.6 (6.0 - 8.0 g/dL)
Albumin: 4.5 (3.6 - 5.0 g/dL)
BUN: 11 (6 - 23 mg/dL)
Creatinine, Serum: 1.07 (0.60 - 1.40 mg/dL)

AST: 19 (10 - 40 U/L)
ALT: 20 (2 - 45 U/L)

Chloride: 102 (96 - 110 mEq/L)
CO2, Total: 26.0 (20.0 - 30.0 mmol/L)
Anion Gap: 11 (2 - 16)

Lipid Panel
HDL Cholesterol: 55 (>39 mg/dL)
Total Cholesterol: 394 (<200 mg/dL)
Triglycerides: 217 (<150 mg/dL)
Cholesterol:HDL Ratio: 7.2 (<5.1)
LDL Cholesterol, Calculated: 296 (<130 mg/dL)

EGFR

### BTA — 2014-09-25T18:49:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-bees-are-dying.11773/post-141936>

Research shows why bees are dying at an alarming rate - nothing to do with EMF or magnetic shift ect....
neonicotinoids are to blame - a very nasty insecticide. Similar to the nicotine found in cigarettes. This chemical was suppose to be a safer alternative to the good ol' days when it was still legal to spray with organophosphate based insecticides. Earth has way too many people to support all of us - the over population has lead to the use of more and more deadly chemicals to force crops to grow at high yield levels. Forcing Mother Nature to do something she wasn't designed to do will have a negative fall out for all of us.

The link does contain references to the research.

[http://www.collective-evolution.com...udy-proves-why-the-bees-are-all-disappearing/](http://www.collective-evolution.com/2014/05/15/new-harvard-study-proves-why-the-bees-are-all-disappearing/)

Yes, pesticides harm them. But like humans, they suffer more readily when they are damaged/distorted by nnEMF's and are less able to adapt. Both/And, not just one nor the other.

NSH\* has spoken.

_("normal sized head" - forum humor)_

From a less metaphysical perspective, these pesticides are nicotine analogues. Dr. Kruse explained that in a person with a good redox, nicotine can do good things. In a person with a poor redox, "all hell brake loose". A bee exposed to EMF would have a challenged redox and would thus be highly susceptible to nicotine analogues causing "all hell brake loose". The Bee's no doubt have a threshold for being able to maintain metastability and function, including directional/magnetic sense, when this is exceeded "all hell brake loose"........ **_Interestingly, the history of these pesticides and their use almost exactly mirrors the development of the cellphone and other wireless technology as it has rolled out over the planet._** This means that there was an ever increasing EMF stress to go along with the persistent use of these. It suggests that one might study the stress from nnEMF in conjunction with exposure and see where the threshold was exceeded where the bees began to crash. One could see whether it coincided with a significant increase in either factor and or whether it it was first in areas with higher levels of EMF like America. One could go on from there and begin to look at parallels in humans persistently exposed to nicotine and nnEMF such as comparing smokers in areas with early nnEMF technology penetration versus smokers where it came late or even not at all. Just some random thoughts on what now seems obvious to me.

>
> Isn't it interesting that all of the environmental groups avoid certain issues that might offend their donors and threaten their popularity? Isn't it interesting that researchers do the same thing? Isn't it interesting that we are conditioned to trust this stuff? Isn't it interesting that once we have invested in a place in this system, it is hard to realize we have bought into the horror? Until we encounter the axiomatic foundational concepts currently modeled somewhat in quantum physics and rigorously apply them to our modern lives, we are little more than the energy sources for the robots modeled in the Matrix movies.

A good example of this is that the livestock industry is responsible for more greenhouse gases than the energy industry. The Sierra Club nonetheless has been hellbent on the coal industry for years with little mention of livestock....cows, pigs and lambs are so cute and coal is so dirty....

### Josh — 2014-09-25T23:10:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-bees-are-dying.11773/post-141952>

Not sure if this is the right way to go.....

[http://ecowatch.com/2014/07/23/citizen-scientists-track-bee-health-colony-collapse-disorder/](http://ecowatch.com/2014/07/23/citizen-scientists-track-bee-health-colony-collapse-disorder/)

> Individuals can be part of the solution—and the fun—by keeping bees themselves. To construct the hives, keepers don’t need glue or screws, only a 4 foot by 8 foot piece of plywood and a CNC router. Those who aren’t quite as tech savvy with computer controlled saws can buy a prefabricated kit. There are two models: the smaller Colorado Top Bar (pictured) and the multi-tiered Barcelona Warre. They are easily shipped due to their flat design and packaging.
>
> These “smart hives” will be connected to [Smart Citizen](http://www.smartcitizen.me/) through a piece of hardware installed within the hives. Users can view data online as well as through a mobile app. Information such as CO levels, humidity, bee count, noise intensity and more will be sent via Wi-Fi. An upcoming feature will notify beekeepers when there is something amiss within their hive.

### av8r — 2014-09-26T01:05:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-bees-are-dying.11773/post-141961>

At 1:10 he talks about migratory birds flying into radio towers. From 1990 to 1995 I worked as an RF engineer for a public TV station. Every fall during migratory season it was common to come to work in the morning and find dead birds scattered all around the base of the transmitter tower, sometimes the count was in excess of 20 birds. It was always a sad sight to see.

### Jonathin — 2014-10-12T22:27:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-bees-are-dying.11773/post-143620>

I have bees and many people in our area are beekeepers as well. Sudden bee death is a complicated issue. Some issues not raised is that all honey bees are not the same genetically. They have deliberately messed with for decades by researchers and "developers".
What are their conditions of existence? Commercial hives often get sugar water in the spring, numerous chemicals deliberate and collateral, foraging on poor quality plants, questionable water, etc. EMFs are no doubt an issue, too. I have seen hives go down quickly in the spring that had plenty of their own honey stored in the hive and massively productive the year before. It is sad and frustrating. I don't care about the honey these days but their activity on flowering plants is amazing and no doubt beneficial in ways we don't understand fully.

Has anyone tried those airtube earphones that are supposedly much, much lower in emf? I bought some the other day but it seemed like I had a headache after using them....perhaps it was a headache from using the iphone itself which the earphones were attached to. However, the iphone was in my pants pocket so I'm not certain it was from iphone. On the topic of devices that are supposedly lower in emf, I have found many of them to be a real hit and miss kind of situation. I have purchased things you plug into walls to reduce emf exposure which made me feel terrible, other items you just place around the house but don't plug into the power socket which also made me feel worse. I wear a pendant which supposedly reduces emf exposure and that is the only thing designed to reduce that doesn't seem to make me feel like crap.

I know what i must do... And for you? Dig deep and question all and most yourself.

Page 1:

IFA, B Burgdorferi G/M/A >80

Interpretation:
<40 Negative
40 Equivocal
\= or >80 Positive

Page 2:

LYME IgM Western Blot

Igenex IGM Result Negative
CDC/NYS Result Negative

18 kDa, -
\*\* 23-25 kDa -
28 kDa -
30 kDa -
\*\*31 kDa -
\*\*34 kDa -
\*\*39 kDa -
\*\*41 kDa IND
45 kDa -
58 kDa -
66 kDa -
\*88-93 kDa -

Page 3:

Multiplex B. Burgdorferi

Negative - Genomic: B burgdorferi DNA not detected
Plasmid: B burgdorferi DNA not detected

Positive - Genomic: B burgdorferi DNA not detected
Plasmid: B burgdorferi DNA not detected

Genomic: B burgdorferi Negative
Plasmid: B burgdorferi Negative

Page 4:

Lyme IgG Western Blot

Igenex IGG Result - Negative
CDC/NYS Result - Negative

By Igenex criteria, IgG Western blot is considered positive if two or more of the double starred bands below are present from either Group 1 (23-25, 31, 34, 39 and 83-93 kDa) or Group 2 (23-25, 34, 39, 41 and 83-93 kDa).

Presence only one double starred band or IND double starred bands may indicate clinical significance.

18 kDa, -
\*\* 23-25 kDa -
28 kDa -
30 kDa -
\*\*31 kDa -
\*\*34 kDa -
\*\*39 kDa IND
\*\*41 kDa +
45 kDa -
58 kDa +++
66 kDa -
\*88-93 kDa -

Hi, do any of you know how to read an MRI to assess redox potential and read how much pos/neg charge there is, based on the amazing Redox Rx on Jack's blog? I realize this section is written for MD's....but I can't help trying to figure it out.

**The first one is an MRI**. MRIs are just like a spectroscopy exam of a star. It tells us precisely how much positive and negative charge are within tissues. MRIs of the brain and spine is the most accurate measure of redox potential I have, and it gives me a clue how bad off someone really is.

### Lauralei — 2014-09-26T22:11:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pulmonary-embolism.11782/post-141997>

Good afternoon, everyone.

Any suggestions would be helpful concerning the following:

I developed a pulmonary embolism, hospitalized (Aug. 25 - 29, 2014) with a blood oxygen level of 70, after a surgical procedure. I am 62, with a double C677T MTHFR defect. Currently taking the blood thinner Xarelto 15 mg daily; no other prescription meds. Taking CoQ-10 and adrenal supplement, plus multi-vitamin, multi-mineral.

Short of breath, 25% heart rate increase when resting, up to 50%+ when moving around (slowly). No energy, no stamina, very poor appetite (losing weight). Low grade fever every evening for about 1 hr.

Last week had an appointment with an alternative MD.

Anyone on the forum had any experience with this condition? The inactivity is causing depression & crankiness!

Thanks for any suggestions!

Explore natural darkness... let your body finally rest and heal
I bet you will get better fast....
If you are sick, Nature is what you need. Everyone needs her actually. But she will heal you better than anything else. Look for wild foods... they have huge healing properties

You will soon be cranky no more, but instead filled with peace and joy

And put up a pic.... please... Jack likes to see who he speaks to.. and I like it too

this all has a huge impact on your health.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/running-a-highly-profitable-business-without-technology.11785/post-142006>

Summer camps.... that are CEO strategy camps in the Winter.
BUY, HOLD strategy.
There is a bubble in real estate about 5 years from now. This is due to the post recession teens who will finally be buying into a mortgage.
Interest rates are ridiculously low.. Buy what you can develop.

What fullfills you? what puts a smile on your dial? What makes you want to get out off bed in the morning????

### caroline — 2014-09-27T09:39:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/running-a-highly-profitable-business-without-technology.11785/post-142022>

You are only young ..... travel and see the world ....and be inspired!

go to a different culture ...live with strangers...get out of your comfort zone!

# Thread: How to tell when it is safe to start eating ketogenic
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-tell-when-it-is-safe-to-start-eating-ketogenic.11787/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Ben1983 — 2014-09-27T19:13:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-tell-when-it-is-safe-to-start-eating-ketogenic.11787/post-142036>

How do I know when it is safe to start eating to go into ketosgenically? Can I determine this without getting tests done? I'm just cautious as I don't want to have any major energy crashes by starting it at a time when I may not be ready for it. Also, do you recommend having a carb refill day once a week like dave asprey suggests? Is it recommended to be in ketosis all year round or just in winter....because doesn't jack recommend eating some more carbs in the warmer months which I assuming would take us out of ketosis?

### prAna303 — 2014-09-27T19:32:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-tell-when-it-is-safe-to-start-eating-ketogenic.11787/post-142037>

I keep it very simple and i also have the big advantage of spending most of my time in the elements, nature.

Living in Sweden is simple because we have two big seasons with small transitions between them...

Fall - Mid september until mid november, here i start to go away from carbs, i follow the signals from nature which says, they get rotten and bad.

Winter - Mid november until late march, not a carb in sight, if they are there, snow is hiding them and they would taste like shit. Here i go with fish, seafoods, game and fats, mostly butter or lard or animal fats.

Spring - Early april until late may, not that many carbs, just some leafs here and there, things start to grow.

Summer - June until mid september, here i act like the great bear. Berries everywhere, mushrooms and other stuff.

Now this is for ME and i do like this because i live like i do... If you are in a city, sleep in a city, work in a city and get entertained in the city i would perhaps go with fats and proteins most of the time.

Now i do not like words we put on stuff, same with ketogenic, we get fucked up about terms instead of thinking what is most important for me, right here and right now.

Doing these carb loadings feel for me like mumbo jumbo, if you can follow nature you see that is is quite impossible to do in that state. Just because we can buy it does not mean we should do it....

Also, i tend to do this really strict because i love to be in sync with nature and its elements, why? I do most of my passions there and with that boost of energy i get i can survive a little better in modern world.

### caroline — 2014-09-28T00:10:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-tell-when-it-is-safe-to-start-eating-ketogenic.11787/post-142049>

Ben .... please remember that dr. K. is a clinician ....... Dave Asprey is not...

Maybe start re reading the blogs .... you have missed a lot!

do you have Jack's book?

### Ben1983 — 2014-09-28T03:54:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-tell-when-it-is-safe-to-start-eating-ketogenic.11787/post-142056>

Yes, I bought his book when it first came out but much of it went over my head. I feel like I would understand much more though if I read it a second time so I'll have to go and do that,

### Ben1983 — 2014-09-28T12:56:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-tell-when-it-is-safe-to-start-eating-ketogenic.11787/post-142078>

And yes, I need to SCHEDULE time to read the blogs or I will never have time for it.

# Thread: Hot bath after CT

**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Ben1983 — 2014-09-29T03:58:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hot-bath-after-ct.11793/post-142110>

Does having a hot bath immediately after CT negate the effects of CT? I can't really function very well after getting out of the ice bath and don't have a lot of free time to wait around to warm up before I go to work which is why I've been warming up I'm hot bath.

### Ben1983 — 2014-09-29T12:28:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hot-bath-after-ct.11793/post-142144>

It was a hot day today in Sydney (for spring) so I thought I'd test warming myself up in the sun instead of having a hot bath immediately after. I say outside for 15 as that was all the spare time I had left and I then had to go and prepare my meals for work....the kitchen is downstair and our downstairs stays very cool.....so when I was downstairs in the cool I felt very cold again (but it felt awesome outside in the sun)....I was probably only cooking for about 15 minutes and then I just realised that I wasn't cold anymore....so it actually didn't take me too long to warm up.....not sure how well that would work on a colder day though.

### Lahelada — 2014-09-29T12:30:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hot-bath-after-ct.11793/post-142146>

I found that I warm up quicker if I dont put on clothes after CT for as long as possible.

### Ben1983 — 2014-09-29T12:31:43+0300

# Thread: symbiosis of Malick and Lynn Margulis
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/symbiosis-of-malick-and-lynn-margulis.11794/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### JoeBranca — 2014-09-29T06:27:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/symbiosis-of-malick-and-lynn-margulis.11794/post-142124>

fascinating information on a way to further Margulis' passion, and how it ties into a film that has resonatned more for me than any other.

[http://www.huffingtonpost.com/nathan-currier/evolution-helps-those-who\_b\_5817298.html](http://www.huffingtonpost.com/nathan-currier/evolution-helps-those-who_b_5817298.html)

> Right now you have a chance to help the late evolutionary biologist [Lynn Margulis](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lynn_Margulis), one of the most important scientists the United States has ever produced, help us all a little bit. If you enjoyed [Terrence Malick](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Terrence_Malick)'s film [_The Tree of Life_](http://www.imdb.com/title/tt0478304/), a bit of gossip is that Lynn offered her expertise in pre-Cambrian biology as a consultant for the movie, in return for help from Malick in rescuing a series of films in developmental biology, considered by Lynn and others to be a treasure of science but now fading and in dire need of digitization.
>
> Malick and his assistant Nick Gonda did indeed help, but this only covered about half of the full series, and there is [currently a Kickstarter campaign](https://www.kickstarter.com/projects/540514942/unobtainium-digitizing-the-reel-life-films-of-lynn), begun by some assistants and friends of Lynn Margulis, to help save the rest, such that all the films will be preserved at the Library of Congress, as well as online for your enjoyment and edification. (The 38 films Malick helped Margulis with are now preserved at the Library of Congress.)

# Thread: Okay, here's a weird question...
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/okay-heres-a-weird-question.11797/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Hansen Kenimer — 2014-09-29T17:47:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/okay-heres-a-weird-question.11797/post-142159>

Is it possible to improve your redox potential so much that YOU affect electronics instead of the other way around? Yesterday on two occasions I walked into the room where my daughter was watching TV (satellite) and the picture pixels went funny for a moment...perfectly clear day, TV only a few months old, and she'd been watching for a while. It's never happened before, it didn't happen that day prior to those moments, and it didn't happen again afterwards. And this morning driving to work I had my phone plugged in to the mp3 jack and there's this one song that touches me to the core, making me very emotional, that was I singing along with, and it kept stopping. Not pausing like it was buffering (it's all downloaded on my phone), but I literally had to keep pressing the play button to get it to keep playing. Finally I got so frustrated I let it loop through the playlist, and never had another issue.

Just WEIRD. But two days ago I entered into a relationship with "aura man" with whom I have this amazing energy connection (some of you will remember the story from another thread from a few weeks ago) and have been feeling pretty through the roof. Okay, I'm levitating. ;-)

Am I crazy?

### kovita — 2014-09-29T19:14:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/okay-heres-a-weird-question.11797/post-142162>

my grandmother is regulary discharging AAA type batteries anywhere around her. She has some special gifts and skills i must say. So I think perfectly possible ;-)

### Hansen Kenimer — 2014-09-29T19:36:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/okay-heres-a-weird-question.11797/post-142168>

### Josh — 2014-09-29T19:43:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/okay-heres-a-weird-question.11797/post-142169>

### bionaut — 2014-09-29T20:26:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/okay-heres-a-weird-question.11797/post-142171>

I would think that a persons cns would have a major electrical deficit to be able to strip electrons from atoms with higher atomic masses from physical contact with a device.

Your heart and head emit a pretty big magnetic field, perhaps that had something to do with it.

### kovita — 2014-09-29T20:33:59+0300

she can find places where the bones have been broken or damaged without touching, she can find and relieve pains without touching and she can sense what I am doing and where I am (this seems to be working with me much better than with other people). I have never tried to find an explanation but landing on this page and it's scientific facts and having eyes and mind opened to see and feel things that are on the verge of paramormality opened a lot of possible explanations.

I test new systems for my companies IT dept - b/c I crash systems in ways no one else ever does. and those crashes find bugs they never knew were in the programs. Last week we were preparing for a briefing - and I like to think and walk - so was walking around my co-workers office - as I got over to his work station suddenly his keyborad, and mouse stopped working, as soon as I'd walk away it would start working again. I had just consumed a massive amt of oysters... This is why I use a mac at home - rarely crashes - bu then the down side is its probably sucking more electrons than my work PC.

# Thread: Where to get oysters?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-to-get-oysters.11799/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### lakers — 2014-09-30T00:47:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-to-get-oysters.11799/post-142196>

I live in socal, about an hour from the beach. LA County to be specific.

Any recommendations on where to buy? Are canned oysters ( for example, from Trader Joes ) frowned upon? Or are they better than nothing, or are they not recommended?

### fitness@home — 2014-09-30T01:43:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-to-get-oysters.11799/post-142203>

Try a local seafood shop or Oriental market. I also frequent local oyster bars and Mexican seafood restaurants.

The Mexican seafood place near where I live offers a dozen oysters for $6.99 at lunchtime, Monday thru Friday.

I also used to buy them frozen at Costco.

### JoeBranca — 2014-09-30T22:38:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-to-get-oysters.11799/post-142304>

The canned are still good, but you can get live oysters probably witout too much hassle, probably more in L.A. but these are spots I know of:
_
\- Zion Market_ (there's one near me in Orange County) have good sized live pacific oysters for .99c each. There are Zion markets in Long Beach and L.A., check them out.

\- Also _HMart_ (another Korean market) often have live oysters and live mussels.

\- The _99 Ranch_ markets often have live oysters too but I seem to get a higher amount of less-than-fresh ones from them, so I stick to Zion.

\- If you don't mind spending more for some more variety of oysters, check out _Santa Monica Seafood Co._ in Santa Monica.

\- the _Redondo Pier fish market_ in San Pedro have all kinds of live and local seafood. Haven't been there for a while so I don't recall their prices.

\- Whole Foods usually have one or two kinds of live oysters

\- some farmer's markets have seafood booths, check them out and if there is one with oysters ask the vendor if they cut a deal for quantity.

When you get live oysters, obviously make sure the shell is completely closed, and also make sure to knock each oyster against a hard surface or with a shucker (or have the employee do it if they're getting the oysters for you), listen for it having a hollow sound or solid sound. If it's hollow, put it back in the bin.

Good luck!


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: Everything Mae-Wan Ho
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/everything-mae-wan-ho.11800/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### PaulG — 2014-09-30T03:31:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/everything-mae-wan-ho.11800/post-142209>

**Biography**
[Mae-Wan Ho](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mae-Wan_Ho) (b. 12 November 1941, [Hong Kong](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hong_Kong); [UK](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/United_Kingdom) citizen) is a [geneticist](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Genetics)[\[1\]](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mae-Wan_Ho#cite_note-1)[\[2\]](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mae-Wan_Ho#cite_note-Independent_Science_Panel-2)[\[3\]](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mae-Wan_Ho#cite_note-Public_Hearing-3) known for her critical views on [genetic engineering](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Genetic_engineering) and [neo-Darwinism](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Neo-Darwinism).[\[4\]](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mae-Wan_Ho#cite_note-Dr._Mae-Wan_Ho_bio-4)[\[5\]](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mae-Wan_Ho#cite_note-5) Ho has authored or co-authored a number of publications, including 10 books, such as [_The Rainbow and the Worm, the Physics of Organisms_ (1993, 1998)](http://www.amazon.com/The-Rainbow-Worm-Physics-Organisms/dp/9812832602), _Genetic Engineering: Dream or Nightmare?_ (1998, 1999), _Living with the Fluid Genome_ (2003) and [Living Rainbow H20](http://www.amazon.com/Living-Rainbow-H2O-Mae-Wan-Ho/dp/9814390895/ref=la_B001JOI1UW_1_1?s=books&ie=UTF8&qid=1412034566&sr=1-1).

[**Books by**](http://www.amazon.com/Mae-Wan-Ho/e/B001JOI1UW/ref=dp_byline_cont_book_1)

**[Main Website (http://www.i-sis.org.uk/)](http://www.i-sis.org.uk)**

[**CV**](http://www.i-sis.org.uk/MWHcv.php)

**Recent Articles**
[Illuminating Water and Life (](http://www.mdpi.com/1099-4300/16/9/4874)2014)
[Story of Phi (6 Parts)](http://www.i-sis.org.uk/The_Story_of_Phi-Part_1.php) (2014)
[Colours of Water (A Festival of art, science and music inspired by water)](http://www.i-sis.org.uk/coloursofwater/) (2013)
[Quantum Coherent Water and Life](http://www.i-sis.org.uk/Quantum_Coherent_Water_Life.php) (2011)

[**List of Articles relating to water in the organism**](http://www.i-sis.org.uk/SO_water.php)

[**YouTube Videos**](https://www.youtube.com/results?search_query=Mae-Wan+Ho)

Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

### Josh (Paleo Osteo) — 2014-09-30T03:34:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/everything-mae-wan-ho.11800/post-142210>

couple good audios

[http://thegarynullshow.podbean.com/e/the-gary-null-show-dr-mae-wan-ho-102912/](http://thegarynullshow.podbean.com/e/the-gary-null-show-dr-mae-wan-ho-102912/)

[http://www.futureprimitive.org/2010/09/mae-wan-ho-radical-democracy-of-organisms/](http://www.futureprimitive.org/2010/09/mae-wan-ho-radical-democracy-of-organisms/)

### smart cat — 2015-05-12T15:00:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/everything-mae-wan-ho.11800/post-163952>

Commun Integr Biol. 2013 Mar 1; 6(2): e23149.
Published online 2013 Mar 1. doi: [10.4161/cib.23149](http://dx.doi.org/10.4161%2Fcib.23149)
PMCID: PMC3609844

**The rainbow and the worm**
Establishing a new physics of life

[Tam Hunt](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/?term=Hunt%20T%5Bauth%5D)\*
University of California Santa Barbara
What is life? Many have asked this question, and no definitive answer is yet widely accepted. (...) There is, however, an intriguing new set of ideas that have been developed by Mae-Wan Ho, a biophysicist and science activist (as she calls herself) based in London. Ho’s basic assertion is that life exists on a spectrum and is at its root organized, quantum coherent energy. Ho’s work attempts to bridge the gap between physics and biology by recognizing that there is no real gap at all—just a gap in current methods and habits of thinking.

I had the pleasure of interviewing Mae-Wan via email about her books, _The Rainbow and the Worm: The Physics of Organisms_ (now in its third edition) and _Living Rainbow H2O_, her newest work about the startling properties of water and its relation to life as we know it.

[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3609844/](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3609844/)

# Thread: Cognitive Enhancement versus Cognitive Destruction?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cognitive-enhancement-versus-cognitive-destruction.11819/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Josh — 2014-10-02T17:53:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cognitive-enhancement-versus-cognitive-destruction.11819/post-142563>

[http://www.newyorker.com/magazine/2009/04/27/brain-gain?src=mp](http://www.newyorker.com/magazine/2009/04/27/brain-gain?src=mp)

> Still, even if you acknowledge that cosmetic neurology is here to stay, there is something dispiriting about the way the drugs are used—the kind of aspirations they open up, or don’t. Jonathan Eisen, an evolutionary biologist at U.C. Davis, is skeptical of what he mockingly calls “brain doping.” During a recent conversation, he spoke about colleagues who take neuroenhancers in order to grind out grant proposals. “It’s weird to me that people are taking these drugs to write grants,” he said. “I mean, if you came up with some really interesting paper that was _spurred_ by taking some really interesting drug—magic mushrooms or something—that would make more sense to me. In the end, you’re only as good as the ideas you’ve come up with.”
>
> But it’s not the mind-expanding sixties anymore. Every era, it seems, has its own defining drug. Neuroenhancers are perfectly suited for the anxiety of white-collar competition in a floundering economy. And they have a synergistic relationship with our multiplying digital technologies: the more gadgets we own, the more distracted we become, and the more we need help in order to focus. The experience that neuroenhancement offers is not, for the most part, about opening the doors of perception, or about breaking the bonds of the self, or about experiencing a surge of genius. It’s about squeezing out an extra few hours to finish those sales figures when you’d really rather collapse into bed; getting a B instead of a B-minus on the final exam in a lecture class where you spent half your time texting; cramming for the G.R.E.s at night, because the information-industry job you got after college turned out to be deadening. Neuroenhancers don’t offer freedom. Rather, they facilitate a pinched, unromantic, grindingly efficient form of productivity.
>
> This winter, I spoke again with Alex, the Harvard graduate, and found that, after a break of several months, he had gone back to taking Adderall—a small dose every day. He felt that he was learning to use the drug in a more “disciplined” manner. Now, he said, it was less about staying up late to finish work he should have done earlier, and more “about staying focussed on work, which makes me want to work longer hours.” What employer would object to that? ♦

### Jonathin — 2014-10-02T18:10:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cognitive-enhancement-versus-cognitive-destruction.11819/post-142564>

Seems like 'we' are cleaving our self into more pieces rather than fusing who we are into a unitary whole.

# Thread: The Air You Breath

**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### BTA — 2014-10-03T05:38:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-air-you-breath.11826/post-142657>

Sure non-native EMF is of concern but what about the air you breath? You have to breath & what's in it is very toxic. You can't escape the air  gasp....

**"What in the World are They Spraying?"** a groundbreaking documentary Co-Produced by journalist and political activist Michael J. Murphy. By now everyone has seen crisscrossing streaks of white clouds trailing behind jet aircraft, stretching from horizon to horizon, eventually turning the sky into a murky haze. Our innate intelligence tells us these are not mere vapor trails from jet engines, but no one yet has probed the questions: who is doing this and why.

We are being sprayed with toxic substances without our consent and, to add insult to injury, they are lying to us about it.

I'm interested in what it tells me about the kinds of things we're interested in within the paradigm we're working in on this site.

I had a bunch of labs done a few weeks ago, which I'll list separately. But I'm wondering if anyone has some experience with RBC Fatty Acid profiles and can assist with this one. Values are reported in % of total. To do an O6:O3 ratio, this lab uses just ARA:EPA rather than total n-6:total-3. I don't know if that is the norm, and it seems a bit odd given the importance of DHA. Here they are:

14:0 (myristic) 0.23%

15:0 0.18

16:0 (palmitic) 21.46

18:0 (stearic) 15.70

20:0 (arachidic) 0.28

22:0 (behenic) 0.70

24:0 (lignoceric) 1.96

**Total Saturated 40.51%**

16:1n-9 0.14

16:1n-7 (palmitoleic) 0.26

18:1n-9 (oleic) 12.21

18:1n-7 (vaccenic) 1.62

20:1n-9 (eicosenoic) 0.25

22:1n-9 (erucic) 0.06

24:1n-9 (nervonic) 2.80

**Total monounsaturated 17.34%**

18:2n-6 (linoleic) 6.96

18:3n-6 (GLA) <LOQ

20:2n-6 0.26

20:3n-6 (di-homo-g-linolenic) 0.83

20:4n-6 (arachadonic) 15.01

22:4n-6 (adrenic) 2.16

22:5n-6 (docosapentaenoic) 0.50

**Total n-6 PUFA 25.73%**

18:3n-3 (a-linolenic) <LOQ

20:4n-3 <LOQ

20:5n-3 (EPA) 1.67

22:5n-3 (DPA) 2.60

22:6n3 (DHA) 5.47

**Total n-3 PUFA 9.74%**

16:0DMA 1.94

18:0DMA 3.63

18:1DMA 1.10

**Total Dimethyl acetals 6.67%
**
Thanks for any assistance!

══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### BTA — 2014-10-05T03:01:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cancer-coverup.11837/post-142845>

Sloan Kettering covered up a cure for cancer derived from apricots in the 1970's.
Dr Moss has come forward with proof.

Dr. Ralph W. Moss was Assistant Director of Public Affairs at Memorial Sloan-Kettering Cancer Center in New York City when he unveiled a cover-up of positive tests with America’s most controversial anticancer agent, laetrile. He was ordered by Memorial Sloan-Kettering Cancer Center officials to falsify reports. He refused. Instead, he organized an underground employee group called Second Opinion to oppose this cover-up.

[http://www.secondopinionfilm.com/product/second-opinion-laetrile-at-sloan-kettering-dvd/](http://www.secondopinionfilm.com/product/second-opinion-laetrile-at-sloan-kettering-dvd/)

# Thread: Schnitzler's Syndrome?  Help Please - anyone.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/schnitzlers-syndrome-help-please-anyone.11848/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### JNV — 2014-10-06T20:08:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/schnitzlers-syndrome-help-please-anyone.11848/post-142997>

I realize this may not be the appropriate place to post this, but I'm losing options of where to turn.

Our 16 year old daughter was recently diagnosed with symptoms similar to Schnitzler's Syndrome and Muckle Wells Syndrome.

For the last 2.5 years, she has joint pain and chronic urticaria.

She was prescribed Anakinra (Kineret) shots daily since May. I want to say 100 mg (this is a very expensive shot, costing $3000k per month for 100 mg). Although her joint pain has been effectively eliminated, it has all but lost its effectiveness with regard to chronic urticaria, although it is slightly reduced. Her skin feels like she has shingles and she is in pain and can't sleep. Moreover, her lymph nodes are quite swollen all over her body. The docs want to do a biopsy to rule out cancer. They also said we can increase her dosage of Kineret to 100 mg twice daily. We have done so, but with no more success than the 100 mg.

She also does not respond to any antihistamines, such as Doxapine, Levocitrazine, Hydroxazine, Zirtec, and Zantac.

Gene tests came back negative for both Muckle Wells and Schnitzler's. However, the doctors think the test could be a false negative.

We need help and don't know where to turn given the limitation of the meds. Any advice? Thoughts? We are doing everything we can to help her with her dietary needs by going the EpiPaleo route.

# Thread: Scary look back...2002
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/scary-look-back-2002.11852/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Josh — 2014-10-07T00:03:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/scary-look-back-2002.11852/post-143050>

[http://www.irsst.qc.ca/media/documents/PubIRSST/r-301.pdf](http://www.irsst.qc.ca/media/documents/PubIRSST/r-301.pdf)

> In conclusion, although the studies of Dowson et al.(1988), Perry et al. (1989) and Bonhomme-
> Faivre et al. (1998) might be considered to be particularly troubled by various difficulties and,
> therefore, should be given less weight, the same cannot be said of the Poole et al. (1993) study, in
> which an increased risk was found. Since, however, the remaining five valid studies (Broadbent
> et al., 1985; Savitz et al., 1994; McMahan et al., 1994; Beale et al., 1997; Verkasalo et al., 1997)
> are mainly null, there is only weak evidence supporting an hypothesis of an increase in depressive
> symptoms occurring as a result of exposure to electromagnetic fields. If one includes suicide as
> an extreme response to depression, then the conclusion is similar. The present situation is,
> therefore, that the larger proportion of acceptable studies does not show a relation of exposure to
> electromagnetic fields with the health effect in question. **_Finally, the current status of research on
> plausible biological mechanisms, including a role for melantonin, does not provide strong support for the hypothesis that exposure to extremely low frequency electromagnetic fields is a risk factor in the development of depressive symptoms._**

# Thread: What time of the day do you work out?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-time-of-the-day-do-you-work-out.11866/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Fitness
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### K123 — 2014-10-09T13:03:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-time-of-the-day-do-you-work-out.11866/post-143304>

Some places Dr.K recommends best time for activity as 1-4pm and some place else best time to work out as 4-6pm. I am trying to do strength training, HIIT and CT all twice a week with Sunday being the day of rest. Can you share what time you typically work out and what time have you found to be the best if you have tried various timings?
I work out with pretty heavy weights like Dr.McGuff suggests in Body by science and do HIIT for 25 mins with 2 mins walks (3mi/hr) and 30 sec sprints at 8mi/hr speed. Quite a bit overweight (55lbs) but I am able to do these without much trouble...can finish and not be sore the next day. Would like to hear if you have any insights into what I am doing vs what I could be benefiting more from. Thank you!!

### Danco3636 — 2014-10-09T19:35:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-time-of-the-day-do-you-work-out.11866/post-143335>

For me all day is a workout (fun-in). I always get up in the morning with some activity, mobility, stretching or tree climbing fun.
Most of my lifting is around 10-11:00 fasted- three days a week- minamist- high intensity. Large meal sometime post between 12-2:00, largest meal of the day.
I may do some higher intensity stuff around 4-6 just before I have my last meal around 7:00 light protein/ fat meal.
My cardio is always done fasted before noon.
But I incorporate movement all day even if it is a quick hand stand, tree hang, walk, mobility, etc.
I do what feels right.

I am sure when you workout plays into:
Your context and lifestyle matters and dictates.

### shah78 — 2014-10-09T20:35:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-time-of-the-day-do-you-work-out.11866/post-143342>

Welcome to the forum. Why do you do 25 min. of HIIT AFTER a BBS workout.? You've incorporated two HIT workouts in one day. Thus you're be definition overtraining and at the same time doin gso much quantity of work that you not really doing BBS either. Lying down,or reading forums is post BBS activity, not getting on a tread mill and cranking out 25 more minnutes of HIT. JUst my two cents.

Thus you're be definition overtraining and at the same time doin gso much quantity of work that you not really doing BBS either. Lying down,or reading forums is post BBS activity, not getting on a tread mill and cranking out 25 more minnutes of HIT. JUst my two cents.

Hi Shah. I did BBS alone for a while but it has been really slow going and especially since I am home all day life becomes too sedentary living in a tiny apartment in London. So I am trying to do BBS Mondays and Thursdays, 25 mins HIIT Tuesdays and Fridays and CT on Wednesdays and Saturdays. Just annoying that I have to lose about 55lbs to be in the ideal weight range for my height and BBS once a week is just not cutting it. Especially women over 30 lose muscle mass fast and I even read McGuff saying that women can probably lift twice a week. I'm just beginning to test this plan so I will see in a week or two how Im doing with over all inflammation and weight loss I thought  Brilliant plan or not is yet to be seen

### K123 — 2014-10-10T00:00:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-time-of-the-day-do-you-work-out.11866/post-143353>

> [Danco3636 said:](/goto/post?id=143335)
>
> For me all day is a workout (fun-in). I always get up in the morning with some activity, mobility, stretching or tree climbing fun.
> Most of my lifting is around 10-11:00 fasted- three days a week- minamist- high intensity. Large meal sometime post between 12-2:00, largest meal of the day.
> I may do some higher intensity stuff around 4-6 just before I have my last meal around 7:00 light protein/ fat meal.
> My cardio is always done fasted before noon.
> But I incorporate movement all day even if it is a quick hand stand, tree hang, walk, mobility, etc.
> I do what feels right.
>
> I am sure when you workout plays into:
> Your context and lifestyle matters and dictates.

You seem like you are really having fun with your body  Living in London it is still a dream for us to move someplace where we can enjoy the outdoors without first trekking it on the tube for 45mins! So you dont see any difference in your sleep patterns if you workout anytime of the day? I thought the idea is to avoid morning work outs to stop the body producing even more cortisol that what we already have in the morning.

### oceanwild — 2015-07-27T10:41:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-time-of-the-day-do-you-work-out.11866/post-173197>

well/ on weekdays, I work out for 2 times at night. on weekend I go play outdoor sports 1 time. so mostly at night time for me to do the work out. after that, sometimes I take a massage, It is very helpful adding the [Cinnamon oil](http://www.best-dietary-supplements.com/) into the essence oil couple drops. great release on pressures.
