# Thread: Wow, now they can nuke us even more...
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wow-now-they-can-nuke-us-even-more.12007/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Penny — 2014-10-28T02:02:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wow-now-they-can-nuke-us-even-more.12007/post-145364>

[http://hardware.slashdot.org/story/14/10/27/1531222/haier-plans-to-embed-are](http://hardware.slashdot.org/story/14/10/27/1531222/haier-plans-to-embed-are)

Haier [has signed a development agreement with Energous](http://www.computerworld.com/article/2838591/your-next-fridge-could-someday-charge-all-your-mobile-devices.html), a maker of the WattUp wireless charging router. Haier plans incorporate the technology in appliances allowing enabled mobile devices and wearables to take a charge at up 15 feet away. The white goods maker is expected to come out with the enabled appliances in the next 14 months or so. The WattUp router uses radio frequency (RF) transmissions to send up to 4 watts of power in a 15-ft. radius. Within 5 feet of a WattUp wireless router, a mobile device can be charged at the same rate as if it were plugged into a wall socket, but as the distance increase the charging capability dissipates. For example, at a range of 5-to-10 feet, charging capability drops to 2 watts per device and at 10-to-15 feet, the router puts out 1 watt per device (4 watts total). Pleasanton, Calif.-based Energous raised nearly $25 million when it went public earlier this year. Its chief marketing officer said the company has joint development agreements in the works with battery makers, smartphone sleeve and wearable device manufacturers. Haier hasn't disclosed what products it plans to enable with wireless charging.

> [BTA said:](/goto/post?id=145514)
>
> That's why I had HLA in the list - big reason why you can't

Yes, HLA folks are at a disadvantage. Sure, one has the markers, but what conditions finally pushed us over the edge and we can't take mold any more?

Having a SNP or a genetic weakness doesn't necessarily mean you'll get sick, but we know that. But if it gets expressed through one's environment, you may believe that you have no way out from the mess. From the information here on Optimal, I believe I have the best chance to bring balance back to my health.

I've been around mold all my life. We all have been. I'm endeavoring to change my conditions to epigenetically repair those changes that occurred. I know that exposure to mold, chemicals, perfumes, etc., can be a problem, so I avoid them. Then I go about changing my environment through less nn-EMF. Straying from natural human rhythms, cycles, foods, etc., has brought those genetic weaknesses into play.

It wasn't the mold, it was my decreased ability to deal with that shit. I brought it about by staying up late watching TV, working around electric machines, lousy diet, and stressing out over that girl I didn't have the nerve to ask out. I changed epigenitically until I crashed. The mold was always there happily living it's life, I'm the one who became susceptible.

Over the course of my life, my body wore down and I became more sensitive due to it's innate ability to adapt to the conditions of existence. I believe I can change it back to where I'm good around mold, chemicals, etc. Those triggers are everywhere. No sense in running when one has a Nature-backed plan to become a balanced human.

And one of those girls finally went on a date with me and the rest is a wonderful history.

NSH

### BTA — 2014-10-29T17:48:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fibromyalgia-infographic.12012/post-145544>

so you're saying SNP don't matter which means you can cure a child with Down's Syndrome? Interesting.

If you've got a genetic defect it does matter. Anything from KPU to HLA-DR.

### NeilBB — 2014-10-29T17:54:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fibromyalgia-infographic.12012/post-145545>

> [BTA said:](/goto/post?id=145544)
>
> so you're saying SNP don't matter which means you can cure a child with Down's Syndrome? Interesting.

Suggesting that a SNP and an entire chromosome duplication (Down's is trisomy-21, a whole extra chromosome) are equivalent is a bit like saying a BB-gun and nuclear bomb are the same thing...

### BTA — 2014-10-29T18:10:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fibromyalgia-infographic.12012/post-145546>

HLA-DR are a set of genes. If you're missing the set as outlined by Dr Shoemaker it is a big deal. These genes tell your body to make antibodies to fight neurotoxins. Without them, your body doesn’t recognize these toxins as being foreign invaders. If you lack the genes needed to clear biotoxins, and you’re exposed to harmful molds and fungi, you’ll get sick and stay sick.

### BTA — 2014-10-29T18:25:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fibromyalgia-infographic.12012/post-145547>

KPU is another genetic defect that you must use supplements for life. There's more than 50 genetic defects that have a negative effect on health despite what you eat, environment, ect...

### kovita — 2014-10-29T18:33:43+0200

> [Josh (Paleo Osteo) said:](/goto/post?id=145398)

it is nice and it will spare time and effort explicating complicated things. The problem is still the same though, unless is someone willing to do a lot of changes, sometimes extreme changes, and take it as a lifestyle and be as patient as one can possibly imagine, knowing how it happened does not mean a thing ;-) That is exactly where I found most of the people I tried to convince for active fight. Jack calls it "the comfort zone". For myself and my lyme derived fybromyalgia I can say I am improving across your diagram and this makes me hoping to welcome pain free life anytime soon! It will definitely be very usefull to have diagrams like this for many common conditions, maybe a bit more laic, so anyone can better understand how it happened and where it must change for coming back to health.

### Martin — 2014-10-29T20:58:44+0200

pretty good but you are missing the one of the physical components of the issue which is cervical spine stenosis, cord tethering, and Arnold Chiari malformation. I have treated a few hundred over the years.

I would also include SNPs especially in the serotonin melatonin pathway, gastric permeability, and improper GABA and sleep cycles.

### AJT — 2014-11-06T16:36:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fibromyalgia-infographic.12012/post-146329>

So what you do for a fibro patient?

Many of these patients are triggered by an emotional or physical trauma. Prior to that the patient has a "normal" life. I have seen fibro cases emerge after a simple rear end traffic collision.

I think some of these approaches become way to reductionist, and too complicated to follow for a person who cant sleep, has trouble digesting, and is too depressed to alter the course of their life.

For many the defective genes, molds or toxin exposure are in place before the trauma happens, but then epigenetic switch or stressor is thrown and suddenly those things that were being kept in check are now overwhelming.

How do you make them whole again?

### Martin — 2014-11-19T22:42:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fibromyalgia-infographic.12012/post-147852>

1.  Over five years ago when I started investigating why I had ME/CFS/CS that came on suddenly after an infections illness, you could have tattooed the exchange below onto my eyelids and I'd still wouldn't have had much of clue whether this explanation made sense in my life. But a science guy came along and started teaching me how the universe works and when I read this, dots start going ding dong!
2.  Mixed metaphors aside, this fills in the gaps that I've NOT been able to find anywhere else. Those "mysterious" maladies are becoming easier to understand and the way out of them is becoming simpler and clearer. _(bolds are mostly mine)_
3.

4.  **DAN:** Jack can you explain what is going on with Fibromyalgia? I saw in the comments that you say its a tough fix, but it’s possible. I'm sure it has to do with water chemistry and dysfunction of the autonomic nervous system. Because it causes such temperature deregulation, is CT something still to consider to regenerate the DC current and restore the flow of electrons? Thanks, Jack.

    **KRUSE:** Dan if you read the last few blogs you will see the reason a system goes south is because **there is not enough electrons present** within the cell membranes or the mitochondria of that system. **Fibromyalgia is a loss of electrons in the muscles and collagen fascia of the entire body.** It **can be a local loss in certain muscles**, too. With this issue, the goal is not to avoid foods, but to fix the structure of the gut by replacing the electrons lost. Dan let me be clear: It is not about the offending agent or the organ affected: **It is about the loss of electrons in muscles and collagen that causes a loss of organizational structure and function in those cells**. That is why muscles and fascia don't work to distribute energy throughout the body through the cytoarchitecture.
5.  So let’s consider an analogy for you to consider: if you cannot deliver an electric current in your house, does anything plugged into it work? Is it the muscle pain or fatigue important as the cause or the real problem today? No. You know muscles and fascia are designed to work optimally in us on while on bare Earth. So what is the issue? Is it the lack of electrons that leads to the poor functioning of this power grid system? Yes, it is. Muscles and collagen deliver electrons well when the system is organized as it’s designed to do. What captures electrons best in cell membranes and mitochondria? **DHA is the only thing in evolutionary history that can do it well.** What should you consider doing? Adding DHA to your system to do what? Capture and assimilate them over your broken network. It will take 2-3 yrs to repair the damage. **Oxidation causes you to lose DHA in that system.** Not eating enough DHA to replace it causes the system to slowly decline in function.
6.  **The loss of electrons is what causes these cells not to perform.**
7.  OK, Dan, what are muscles made of? Water and collagen. You need to read Pollack's other book about muscle...... and his latest book about water. So how does DHA, muscle, and water all join to make sense of this? Sunlight. Sunlight acts like an electron or a photon. Photons or electrons increase the crosslinks in collagen to allow you to transmit electrons over its backbone. The more crosslinks, the better. **If you have FM, you have very few cross links. You have fewer electrons. Your cell membranes and mitochondria are not sticky to electrons.**
8.  Water and DHA are directly tied to the photoelectric effect: Life catches the electron with DHA. It is common knowledge that the ultimate source of all our energy and negative entropy is the radiation of the sun. When a photon interacts with a material particle on our globe, it lifts one electron from an electron pair to a higher level. This is how the photoelectric effect works. This excited state, as a rule, has but a short lifetime and the electron drops back within 10–7 to 10–8 seconds to the ground state giving off its excess energy in one way or another. Life has learned to catch the electron in the excited state, uncouple it from its partner and let it drop back to the ground state through its biological machinery utilizing its excess energy for life processes'. Instead, life depends on catching an excited electron quite precisely (DHA) — by means of specific light-absorbing pigments connected to DHA — and then tapping off it’s energy as it falls back towards the ground state.
9.  Life uses the highest grade of energy, the packet or quantum size, of which is sufficient to cause the specific motion of electrons in the outer orbital of molecules in the neural cell membranes. It’s on account of this that living systems can populate their high energy levels without heating up the body excessively.
10.  Energy is **trapped directly** at the _electronic level_ by the electrons in DHA. It is stored as vibrational and electronic bond energies in DHA, as well as the proteins DHA has selected for in neuronal cell membranes.
11.  Energy is also **stored in the structure of the system. This means your muscles and collagen cannot store them because the structure of the system is BAD!!!!**
12.  **Where does the electrons or DC current begin in humans? It is not the muscles. It is the CSF around the brain.** You need to collect the photons of the sun using the collagen in your skin which is under the power of Vitamin D. **Skin cells need DHA in its cell membrane to capture the photon power** of the sun to transfer it to the electrons in the pi electron cloud of DHA in skin. The electrons then give off photons by falling back to its ground state through the collagen network. This is the fascia around muscles. This is bad in YOU!!!! So understand specifically, the collagen network cannot transfer electrons that are energized by the photons of the sun in FM patients. **This is why they all have shitty Vitamin D levels.**
13.  These electrons release that energy to the water and collagen in fascia which travels in your ligaments to the dura and it electrifies the water, called Cerebral Spinal Fluid, in your brain. Here the energy is transferred from fascia to water. This energy is transformed by re-energizing electrons in CSF to power them up from the energy they captured from the sun’s light. So CSF electrons are energized by sunlight. **The sun's power got there by traveling over your muscle/ skeletal system.**

15.  So if your M/S is broken, can your CSF get that energy? NO. This is why energy levels suck in FM. The sun's photoelectric power allows light to be transferred in different forms to power up the electron and give it’s energy to CSF to give it to the brain and then to fall back to its ground state. Energy is also stored in piezoelectric collagen everywhere in the cells. **You cannot do this anywhere DHA is missing.** This is found in energy gradients, fields and flow patterns, compartments, organelles, cells and tissues. In turn, all this enables organisms to mobilize their energies coherently and hence make available the entire spectrum of stored energies for work, most efficiently and rapidly, whenever and wherever energy is required. Energy is moved from the electronic storage to any type of energy needed based upon the tissue system’s design. All forms of energies, however, have to abide by mass equivalence because it is a universal law.

This is why you are broken, and why it is so hard to fix FM patients.

good to have it summarize here,Martin. I have been following the epipaleo lifestyle for over a year now. Fibromyalgia is gone, seems to be gone. Sometimes I would get a feeling it is coming back here or there, but this happens less and less often and the only with any muscular remains left deltoid muscle. The joints are not fixed tough. I know the
ains come from the structures arpund the joints not the joints by itself, but the intensity and the feeling changed. it is more a background subtle pain and unless "I tease the beasts" it usually stays this way. There are some pain free days clustering, that is good as well. Temperature issue is fixed. I know the ise is thin for the moment so I would consider the 2-3 years as a reasonable estimation. I think most FM have internal stressor as a major factor involved, that is why it is so difficult and so slow to overtake them.

Should I be careful with that? Is it a good idea?

### BTA — 2014-10-28T18:01:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/drinking-regularly-of-copper-cup.12013/post-145428>

Far more effective is **radiation hormesis** - it's been around for over 100 years. Germans used it in everything. A small "_hot_" rock placed in a pitcher of RO Water for a few hours will have amazing impact on your health. Read more on radiation hormesis [www.nighthawkminerals.com](http://www.nighthawkminerals.com)

Its called Crystal Energy, the product description is below. The guy who makes it seems a little like a quack but maybe its the real deal.

"Crystal Energy is the first product Dr. Patrick Flanagan invented after studying Hunza water. It consists of billions of spherical nanoparticles of silica that have the highest zeta potential ever measured. When added to water Crystal Energy instantly **turns water into a liquid crystal and more closely matches water surrounding the cells in the body.** Our studies have shown that Crystal Energy helps to increase the flow of water and nutrients across cell membranes 2.54 times faster than ordinary water, increasing hydration of cells. See our report in the published studies section of this website."

Any thoughts?

# Thread: Recipe for Sea Water
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/recipe-for-sea-water.12039/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### fitness@home — 2014-10-31T19:08:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/recipe-for-sea-water.12039/post-145804>

What do you think? Comments?

For those of you that have a dedicated outdoor CT tub - would using this help keep the water clean longer? _Hint, hint Nonchalant. Want to be the tester?_

(Planning, planning, planning...)

**The Recipes for Sea Water**

**If you want to fill your bathtub** (40 gallons or 150 liters of water, your bathtub may vary), add
13 cups (3.12 liters) of salt,
4 cups (0.96 liters) of epsom salt,
1/3 cup (80 milliliters) of salt substitute,
and 5 teaspoons (25 milliliters) of baking soda.

**For a 5-gallon** (19 liters) batch of homemade sea water, add
1 and 2/3 cups (0.4 liters) of salt,
1/2 cup (120 milliliters) of epsom salt,
2 and 1/4 teaspoons (11 milliliters) of salt substitute,
and 2/3 teaspoons (3.3 milliliters) of baking soda.

**If you’d like to make a pint** (0.48 liters), add
2 teaspoons (9 milliliters) of salt,
2/3 teaspoons (3.3 milliliters) of epsom salt,
1/20 teaspoons (0.25 milliliters) of salt substitute,
and 1/62 teaspoons (0.08 milliliters) of baking soda.

Since the measurements in the pint version are ridiculously small, it would probably be easier to make larger batches of the stuff, and use a pint at a time. In general, and regardless of the measuring system or total amount required, you need
6,000 parts tap water,
125 parts salt,
38 parts epsom salt,
3 parts salt substitute,
1 part baking soda.

If you’d like, you can mix up large amounts of the dry ingredients, in the above proportions, and then scoop out 167 parts of the mixture to every six thousand parts water. For every gallon of water, you’d add a little more than 7 tablespoons of the dry mix (for every liter, add 28 milliliters of the mix). For the pint-sized mixture, add a shade more than 2 and a half teaspoons of dry ingredients.

Once a reliable resource on the ingredients of the Dead Sea can be found, “recipes” for that will be published here, as well.

[http://www.psorsite.com/docs/homeseawater.html](http://www.psorsite.com/docs/homeseawater.html)

# Thread: Body Temperature Regulation - Please Help
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/body-temperature-regulation-please-help.12045/>

**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### rogerb02 — 2014-11-02T00:04:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/body-temperature-regulation-please-help.12045/post-145893>

Hi, my name is Roger, and I have an issue going on with my autonomic nervous system. I have had Lyme disease since 2010, and from what I understand the Lyme disease gives off a neurotoxin as it is killed which works to destabilize the balance of the Hypothalamus-Pituitary-Adrenal Axis. I have started to be much worse in the last month when I killed off some Lyme bacteria and it impacted my body very much.

My body temperature regulation is the most alarming of my autonomic nervous system issues. I have to keep the air conditioning on in the house and a couple fans on me. When it gets above 60 degrees, my mucous membranes dry out and my eyes burn very much. My feet have developed rashes and blisters (looks like eczema). My heart also beats extremely fast when I am in temperatures above 60 degrees.

When my body temperature is measured via a thermometer, it is around 97-98. I have been told by one practitioner it is as if my body is stuck in a perpetual hot flash. My other symptoms include head pressure on top of the head, dryness of mucous membranes, brain fog, exhaustion, tachycardia.

My body is out of balance, and I have recently started taking Maca Powder in order to help the hormones balance out naturally. Are there other supplements that work on the HPA axis?

Is this related to testosterone in the body?

Thank you for your thoughts!

I live in Chester, VA. I live with my parents - I am basically house-ridden because of the temperature difficulties. Lyme disease also causes Postural Orthostatic Tachycardia syndrome, which I have.

# Thread: Green Tea Chicken Soup
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/green-tea-chicken-soup.12051/>
**Subforum:** Epi-Paleo Recipes
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### fitness@home — 2014-11-03T02:04:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/green-tea-chicken-soup.12051/post-145985>

Started with someone elses recipe and made it my own by tweaking it a bit.

Ingredients:
\- 3 green tea bags
\- 5C water
\- 1 sliced lemongrass stalk
\- 1 cup shelled edamame
\- 1 oz dried mushroom, stir fry style (My tweak. Recipe called for 1C sliced shitake)
\- 1 lb cooked shredded chicken
\- 1.5C chicken broth
\- 1 TBL duck fat (recipe called for sesame oil)
\- 2 sliced green onions
\- cilantro for garnish

Directions:
Heat 5 cups of water until boiling. Add teas bags, lemongrass and steep for 10 minutes. Remove tea bags and simmer. Add edamame and salt & pepper to taste.

I combined the dried mushrooms with 1.5C warm chicken broth and let them soak for 10 minutes; then added broth with mushrooms to the soup. Add chicken and green onion then simmer for a few minutes. The final touch was adding duck fat.

Picture doesn't do it justice...

5th Phase Of Water

He''s been researching water & quantum biology for more than 3o years. # Thread: Interesting words from Mr. Eisenstein
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/interesting-words-from-mr-eisenstein.12056/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Martin — 2014-11-03T16:48:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/interesting-words-from-mr-eisenstein.12056/post-146034>

Interesting article on Pollack's book...
[http://charleseisenstein.net/the-waters-of-heterodoxy-g-pollacks-the-fourth-phase-of-water/](http://charleseisenstein.net/the-waters-of-heterodoxy-g-pollacks-the-fourth-phase-of-water/)

From his website: [http://charleseisenstein.net/about-charles/](http://charleseisenstein.net/about-charles/)
"In addition to writing books and essays, I have been doing a tremendous amount of public speaking. Sometimes, especially when I am tired from traveling, I wonder if the world needs yet another man performing “speaker up in front of an audience.” Why not stay at home and use technology? But I find that something happens in person that is irreplaceable. For one thing, at live events I can see the expressions on people’s faces, and I respond to that in real-time, engaging in a sort of dialog. Secondly, my speaking is not just about conveying ideas. Something else rides the vehicle of the words, even if the topic is something mundane like steady-state economics. I sometimes describe my experience as a speaker as being plugged into a field that is not my creation, but is generated by the audience and by something beyond the audience. Besides, I find that my thinking stagnates and my heart atrophies when I am in front of my computer too much. I need to interact with real people, face to face. That’s why I travel and speak."

# Thread: Sleep time in short light cycles
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleep-time-in-short-light-cycles.12057/>

### Butters — 2014-11-03T18:25:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleep-time-in-short-light-cycles.12057/post-146037>

I have a question:

At my destination sun sets at 4.30pm now and rises at 7.30. So I have relatively long night hours.
Jack is talking about 7,5-8,5h of sleep is best and too long isn't good. Does it change with the winter season/is it ok to sleep more in winter season?

### kovita — 2014-11-03T19:28:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleep-time-in-short-light-cycles.12057/post-146042>

i do sleep more in winter and it comes naturally. As the days shorten I find myslef falling asleep as soona s 9-10pm and waking up around 6am. It means I still wake up to the night, so usually i do some yoga or stretching or just meditate while waiting for the sunrise. Do what your body wants as long as it is in harmony with the season (which from my experience comes naturally as soon as you embrace nature and start to listen to the doctor in your head) ;-)

### nicld — 2014-11-03T19:46:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleep-time-in-short-light-cycles.12057/post-146045>

I sleep longer in the winter too. As soon as I get home now the blue blockers go on and by 8-9 I need to crawl into bed for sleep. Weekends in the winter I sleep a bit later too and wait for the sun to rise to wake me (not a luxury during the week).

### Danco3636 — 2014-11-03T22:39:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleep-time-in-short-light-cycles.12057/post-146052>

Last nights sleep was just over 9 hours.... one of the longer ones of the year.... Keep the bed room cold keeps me in bed a bit longer

# Thread: Sexual Organs and Hormones
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sexual-organs-and-hormones.12066/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### badger — 2014-11-05T15:46:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sexual-organs-and-hormones.12066/post-146206>

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

Biologists have evidenced through many years of observation and direct experience that the sexual glands are not closed capsules, since they excrete and **increte** hormones. The hormones of secretion are called “conserving,” because they perpetuate the species. The hormones of incretion are called “vitalizing,” because they vitalize the human organism. This process of hormonal incretion is **[transmutation](http://sacred-sex.org/methodology/getting-started/126-what-is-transmutation.html)**; it is Alchemy: the transformation of one type of energy-matter into another type of energy-matter.
The root of the word **hormone** comes from the Greek \[ὁρμή - "impetus"\] and means "that which sets in motion," "impel, urge on," "onset, impulse."

The astonishing vital power of the sexual hormones is evident. Therefore, to save them, to learn to **increte** them, to make them return inwardly and upwardly with the sane purpose of enriching life is not a crime.

It has been completely proven that the sexual hormonal incretion intensifies the hormonal production of all the **endocrine** glands. The sanguineous torrent \[blood stream\] conduces sexual hormones, it transports them, puts them in contact with all of those glandular micro-laboratories.

The gonads of women are the ovaries, the breasts and the uterus. Those of males are the testicles, the phallus and the prostate **gland**. Such reproductive glands basically turn out to be wonderful sexual micro-laboratories. It is unquestionable that these glands have a double function, since they have external and internal secretion.

Although it is true that the ovaries produce the ovum, it is less evident that they also **increte** \[**secrete** internally\] a marvelous endocrinal substance that vitalizes women and makes them feminine.

It is true, observable, and real that the testicles have the Ens Seminis (the entity of [semen](http://sacred-sex.org/facts/terminology/85-semen.html)) as an external secretion in which the spermatozoa float. In fact, the latter amount to the vital germs of existence.

The intimate hormonal incretion from the **testicle** cortex is the wonderful power that gives energy to males and makes them essentially masculine.

A normal male is he who has normal male gonads. A normal female is she who has normal female gonads.

The ovaries very wisely regulate the distribution of calcium in women. This has already been proven. An excessive number of pregnancies “because of circumstances,” causes the terrible cases of osteomalacia, or deformities due to soft bones, which are so common in the densely populated countries of the world in which we live. It has been scientifically verified that when pregnancies are very frequent they truly exhaust the calcium reserves and so the bones are weakened. Any doctor can make it evident that many women suffer from trouble with their teeth during pregnancy.

In men, the testicles (also called interstitial glands) regulate the calcium in bones, giving them strength and stability. Through many years of observation and experience, sages have been able to verify that males with very strong bones are generally very virile sexually.

It has already been fully ascertained by means of profound scientific observations that some of the endocrines act intelligently as accelerators of the sexual glands and that others diminish such action. Eminent biologists, whom we cannot doubt, think that the thymus **gland** stops sexual appetite.

The most powerful hormones in the human organism are in the sexual glands of incretion.

Think for a moment: what are, for example, the sexual hormones.

Clearly - and let us focus on facts - the testicles have three layers. The first testicular layer has cells that manifest their activity especially during the first seven years of infancy. Then, one is under the rulership of the Moon \[which influences the first seven years of life\]. Later on, from seven to fourteen years, under the rulership of Mercury, the second testicular layer moves into activity and fully defines the **sex** of that male. And much later on, from fourteen to twenty-one, under the rulership of Venus, the third testicular layer becomes active, producing abundant zoo-sperms. The zoo-sperms that are produced in the third testicular layer ascend by the adjacent testis and continue along the spermatic cord towards the seminal vesicles, until they reach the prostate.

It is most interesting to know that those zoo-sperms become electrified as they ascend trough the spermatic cords. They gradually become charged with a lot of electricity, and what is even more interesting is that they become magnetized, so to speak, from south to north; that is, they work like a compass, south-to-north; they also orientate themselves towards the north, being electrified, magnetized...

It is most interesting to see how they pass from one small vessel to another, climbing through the spermatic cords, until they reach the seminal vesicles, where the sperm is purified even more (all those zoo-sperms), until they reach the prostate, where there is a very important chakra. Obviously, the Svadisthana chakra, which is fundamental for the mastery of sexual **[transmutation](http://sacred-sex.org/methodology/getting-started/126-what-is-transmutation.html)**, is located there. Sexual **[transmutation](http://sacred-sex.org/methodology/getting-started/126-what-is-transmutation.html)** is based on the prostate. The prostatic chakra is, therefore, most important, and most interesting.

That sperm becomes totally purified. It becomes completely radioactive, it becomes completely electromagnetic... \[This is what impels the male to utilize it: i.e. the sexual drive or libido.\] And with **[transmutation](http://sacred-sex.org/methodology/getting-started/126-what-is-transmutation.html)**, it turns into energy.

### badger — 2014-11-05T15:46:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sexual-organs-and-hormones.12066/post-146207>

With **[transmutation](http://sacred-sex.org/methodology/getting-started/126-what-is-transmutation.html)**, the zoo-sperms break down into hormones, which go through the membranes and finally enter the blood, actively stimulating the glands of inner secretion. They make them work in an extraordinarily intensive way. Then the hormones ascend, they continue on their way to the brain. There they activate the brain areas that do not work anymore, that are degenerated; they make all the brain cells dynamic and activate certain powers latent in man, etc.

Unfortunately, and this is the most serious thing, my dear friends, people are degenerated. Vulgar, ordinary men do not let the zoo-sperms rise to the prostate - not even for a second: they eliminate them from the sperm sacks to satisfy their brutal impulses.

Under such circumstances, the brain degenerates awfully, due to the lack of rising hormones. It does not receive sexual hormones and loses its capacity for action. The faculties latent in man degenerate because there is not enough stimulus from sexual hormones, which are so powerful, as I said. They have the power to move all the glands of inner secretion into activity, to make them produce all sorts of hormones, which make the living organism dynamic.

Besides, there are other factors that must be taken into account: the sexual force is what produces the phenomenon of “mitosis” in the fetus. Mitosis is the splitting of cells to gestate humans, animals... Well, the presence of creative energy makes the original cell, which has the well-known 48 chromosomes \[science has only found 46 because they ignore the energetic or fourth dimensional aspect\], split into two; it makes the two split into four, the four into eight, etc., etc., etc., and this is how the tissues, the organs and so on are formed. This is the process of gestation, the formation of creatures in the maternal womb.

Now from the point of view of sexual **[transmutation](http://sacred-sex.org/methodology/getting-started/126-what-is-transmutation.html)**, I want to tell you that the sexual hormones (active in the blood, charged with electricity and magnetism) produce mitosis in the adult human organism: new splitting of cells, formation of new tissues, etc. This is how you can achieve - from a biological standpoint - a complete regeneration or rejuvenation of the human organism.

What is the difference, for example, between the young “new waves” \[young people\] and the antiquated and backward-minded old people? One: a young man’s third testicular layer produces large amounts of sexual hormones, lots of zoo-sperms. All these zoo-sperms, transformed into hormones, go to the brain and renew it. That is why young people have a revolutionary way of thinking and open up to what is new, etc. On the other hand, the poor old men who have already wasted the sacred sperm, who have deteriorated through sexual abuse, are really ruined: the third testicular layer now produces very few zoo-sperms. Quite often it takes them days and months to have enough zoo-sperms to achieve a simple erection. And of course, when they manage to achieve an erection, they immediately seek the coitus and eliminate these zoo-sperms. They spend days, a month, or more, accumulating new zoo-sperms; once they have them, they immediately waste them (for the passionate satisfaction of a moment), and that is how they spend their lives, till they die. Since they do not have what produces great amounts of zoo-sperms, the third testicular layer gradually becomes totally atrophied. It produces its cells or hormones, but due to its atrophy these cannot pass on to another stage. Then there may even appear certain inflammations and swellings between the second and the third layers and, worst of all, this logically comes to affect the prostate. Those poor, degenerated old men always have their prostates operated on because of their abuses. This is clear and I want you to understand it specifically and definitely. So, since their third testicular layer is not producing enough zoo-sperms, the poor old men do not have new ideas, because their brain cells are atrophied and certain areas are not working. They become stuck in what they learned in the past, and turn into old-fashioned people who do not accept anything new; they continue with their ancient ideas from yesterday, dated and clumsy. Obviously, they clash with young people, because the young ones are producing new zoo-sperms and so they have new ideas and open up to what is new; then comes the clash between what is new and what is old. That is then the difference between young people of “the new wave” and old people: it is wholly a sexual, biological difference. Understand that clearly.

The internal secretions of the testicles and the ovaries are definitive for the life of the human being on the Earth. The fundamental differences between men and women are due to the secretions of the testicles and the ovaries.

The non-ejaculation of [semen](http://sacred-sex.org/facts/terminology/85-semen.html) (not reaching the [orgasm](http://sacred-sex.org/facts/terminology/228-orgasm.html)) is radical in order to avoid conception and in order to intensify the hormonal incretion. If the male avoids the ejaculation of [semen](http://sacred-sex.org/facts/terminology/85-semen.html) and if the woman also avoids the [orgasm](http://sacred-sex.org/facts/terminology/228-orgasm.html), then, control of the problem of conception is resolved. Thelema (willpower) is what is required during the sexual act, in order to withdraw in time, prior to the sexual spasm. The refrained sexual desire will make the creative energy ascend. This is how we perform the semination of the brain and the cerebration of the [semen](http://sacred-sex.org/facts/terminology/85-semen.html). It is obvious that the [semen](http://sacred-sex.org/facts/terminology/85-semen.html) can transform itself into energy. It is unquestionable that the sexual energy rises up towards the brain. Specific nervous canals for the ascension of the sexual energy exist. Unfortunately, the scalpel cannot find them because these canals belong to the fourth dimension. I am now concretely referring to the pair of nervous cords known in India with the names of Ida and Pingala. In the male, Ida starts in the right **testicle** and Pingala starts in the left one. In the female, they start in the ovaries and are reversed. These two very fine nervous canals become amiably knotted in the coccygeal bone. Then afterwards, they ascend as two entwined serpents throughout the dorsal spine until the brain. The continuous ascension of the sexual energy throughout these nervous canals radically transforms us by converting us into mutants (Genii). We are concretely referring to the Sahaja Maithuna, **sex** yoga, White [Tantra](http://sacred-sex.org/facts/terminology/95-tantra.html).

[sexual magic](http://sacred-sex.org/facts/terminology/177-sexual-magic.html) is intensified sexual **[transmutation](http://sacred-sex.org/methodology/getting-started/126-what-is-transmutation.html)**. Through Sexual Alchemy, the alchemist absorbs, transmutes and sublimates the totality of the sexual energy-matter. The rich and abundant sexual hormones inundate the circulatory system of the blood and reach the different glands of internal secretion, stimulating and inciting our **endocrine** system to work intensely. Thus, with intensified sexual **[transmutation](http://sacred-sex.org/methodology/getting-started/126-what-is-transmutation.html)**, the **endocrine** glands are super-stimulated, naturally producing a greater number of hormones that animate and modify the entire fluid nervous system.

\- See more at: [http://sacred-sex.org/facts/anatomy/235-sexual-organs-and-hormones.html#sthash.3l7yX4qN.dpuf](http://sacred-sex.org/facts/anatomy/235-sexual-organs-and-hormones.html#sthash.3l7yX4qN.dpuf)

# Thread: Using outlet timers to kill electronics at night
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/using-outlet-timers-to-kill-electronics-at-night.12092/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jeff Power — 2014-11-07T15:34:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/using-outlet-timers-to-kill-electronics-at-night.12092/post-146433>

I put analog outlet timers on all of my outlets that feed into surge protectors. So at 11 PM all the electronics and wifi in my house is off including wifi.

Do you think this is worthwhile in lowering EMF or is too much EMF leaking out of the outlets anyways?

### Optimal Rod — 2014-11-08T04:25:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/using-outlet-timers-to-kill-electronics-at-night.12092/post-146528>

Doing anything to lower EMF is good but you still have some Electrical Radiation from from the lines unless the breaker is shut off. You need to do everything in your power to mitigate EMF and Increase DHA more than the loss of of it (electrons). This is just what I have gathered from my short time from here.

### Da-mo — 2014-11-08T05:57:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/using-outlet-timers-to-kill-electronics-at-night.12092/post-146535>

Yes it is worthwhile. Having the Wi-fi shut off is good in itself. Magnetic feild strength is proportional to the current in the conductor and decreases with distance from it. By turning things off you have decreased magnetic feilds due to reduced current.

However, with AC circuits you still have the reversing of polarity going on in open circuits creating magnetic feilds of lesser magnitude. These are the wires running through your walls/ceiling/floor to your power points and lights. By killing those circuits at the switchboard you eliminate these as well. The switchboard busbar will still be live but hopefully is further away from you so the feild there is diminished by distance.

There will still be feilds along the mains coming into the home to the meter box and switchboard unless you kill the power to the house at the pole fuse (you wouldnt do that coz that is power authority territory).

Thw worst case scenario would be if you sleep near the mains cable, meter box or switchboard.

I kill everything in my bedroom but havent gone as far as killing the supply circuits at the switchboard yet.

My larger concern is the electric fence unit mounted maybe 6m from my pillow - its high voltage but low current if the fences are in good order. It pulses at around 1 Hz. Hmmm . . . . I wonder if I could get it to pulse at the schumann resonance of 7.83 Hz . . . .

# Thread: Grilled Salmon even kids will like
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grilled-salmon-even-kids-will-like.12117/>
**Subforum:** Epi-Paleo Recipes
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### JMO — 2014-11-11T02:08:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grilled-salmon-even-kids-will-like.12117/post-146854>

Grilled Salmon

Filet of salmon (preferably with skin)
Sprinkle generously with Tony Chachere's original creole seasoning(sold by spices). Sprinkle lightly with salt, black pepper, onion powder, garlic powder, and cayenne pepper. Grill on greased grill pan.

Tartar sauce
3 Heaping T Mayo (preferably homemade)
1 T mustard
1/2 t onion powder, garlic powder,salt, pepper
1 t fresh lemon juice
1 T dill relish
1 t dill pickle juice

Sweet potato skillet
Cut up a couple sweet potatoes in small cubes and fry in coconut oil. When potatoes are almost tender, add a red pepper cut in small pieces and add a handful of fresh spinach. Add 1 T of balsamic vinegar and put lid on until spinach is wilted.

### fitness@home — 2014-11-11T02:31:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grilled-salmon-even-kids-will-like.12117/post-146858>

I have an Alaskan salmon fillet unthawing in the fridge tonight

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grilled-salmon-even-kids-will-like.12117/post-146861>

I like macerated cranberries on my salmon right now.

### JoeBranca — 2014-11-13T18:26:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rivers-and-tides.12142/post-147266>

This documentary is a worthy meditation.

Rarely are both artist and his art this engaging, and working within the transient elements of nature, a gentle soul navigating the push and pull forces to carve out some things of beauty. The work and the sentiment behind it taps into something deep , primal.

I am still in awe at the project 6 minutes in, assembling early morning ice before the sun's heat whisks his work away.

# Thread: Photorepair
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/photorepair.12153/>
**Subforum:** Factor X
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### NeilBB — 2014-11-14T16:47:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/photorepair.12153/post-147385>

According to current science, plants and earlier animals use an enzyme called photolyase to repair DNA damage induced by UV-light. But supposedly this particular ability was lost (replaced?) in eutherian mammals. Here, researchers used marsupial-photolyase in human cells and were able to repair DNA:

[http://cancerres.aacrjournals.org/content/60/9/2458.full](http://cancerres.aacrjournals.org/content/60/9/2458.full)

So I would think Factor X was involved somehow in altering the course of mammalian photorepair...

### fitness@home — 2014-11-18T20:10:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/feedback-please-new-gi-effects-test-results.12154/post-147736>

I had some time to look at this on my way to/from Michigan this weekend. The free wifi in airports at least let me do some Googling.

So, the cholesterol was High which can signify mucosal malabsorption. I do utilize betaine HCL, Pepsin, Ox Bile, and Cholacol for heavy meals.

The Eosinophil Protein X was High which can signify too many things! Inflammation, allergens, parasitic activity, food allergy, IBD, unlcerative colitis, Chron's Disease, GERD, Atopic Dermatitis, and gastrointestinal cancer. Is that list long enough for you?

I have had previous issues with IBS. The last GI Effects test indicated some sort of parasite - but unidentified. Have also slipped the past couple of months and some gluten has made it into my mouth and body. Must rectify that.

Four of them on the Commensal Bacteria (PSR) are High. Barnesiellam Odorbacter, Prevotella, and Fusobacterium.

My previous test showed high Fusobacterium. I have seen TWO specialty dentists to rule out the possibility of dental infections (I am clear.)

Page 4 of the test show "No Growth" for Lactobacillus spp. and Escherichia coli. _So my question is should I get a probiotic with these included and take it for a short period to reintroduce it into my body?_

Lactobacillus is a friendly...not sure about the other one. Still need to do more research.

# Thread: Quantum Cookstove
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quantum-cookstove.12155/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### nonchalant — 2014-11-14T21:55:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quantum-cookstove.12155/post-147397>

When building a fire, the type of wood matters less than how dry it is, and it's shape. You start with tinder (often paper), and put twigs on top, plus a few sticks. Arrange these items so that oxygen can reach everything readily, and the fire doesn't get smothered. After the fire is started, add larger pieces. Splitting the wood with an axe can make rough edges that catch fire easily. Large round logs are difficult to get started, so save them for when the fire is larger.

When you have a cookstove like this, however, there are other options.
{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

This stove has vents to control the air intake (on the left side) and the air exhaust (on the chimney centered in the back). When the fire is started (in the box on the upper left side of the cook surface) restricting air flow through the intake and exhaust is helpful, because it speeds air flow through the fire (and also restricts heat loss through the chimney.

But the most interesting feature is a small sliding lever that you cannot see, that controls a door that redirects air flow. It is at the back of the cook surface, towards the right side. I think of it as the Maxwell Demon door. This door is normally open, allowing air to flow directly from the fire toward the chimney. After the fire is getting fairly established, you can start closing this door in increments. It blocks exit of the air through the upper part of the rear of the cook stove. The air then starts filling the inside of the cookstove, and you would have a big problem with smoke in the house if the air didn't find a second exit, cleverly positioned in the lower part of the rear of the cook stove. The hot air has to circulate to the left under the cook top, and then downwards around the oven, then across the bottom until it finds the other exit. Then it zooms upward, joins other air that manages to get past the door, and all of it runs up the chimney.

This door is used to 'turn on' the oven. You can use it to regulate the temp of the oven. But also, since the door sends hot air to the left under the cooktop, it also increases the heat on that part of the cooktop. You can then heat 6-8 pots or pans at the same time, all from a small fire on the left side.

This lever was broken on our stove when we bought it, but we got it anyway because DH can fix anything.
Perhaps the lever isn't a maxwell demon, because it requires a human to use it, and knowledge and experience to use it correctly (without putting out the fire or ending up with a smokey kitchen). But perhaps it is. The metal door, the fire, the human with experience, and also the ingenuity of whoever came up with the design of a downdraft oven in the first place -- all together form a maxwell demon?

There are some other quantum aspects of the stove, like the humans that huddle around it in the morning to absorb IR light, the plate warmer on top to take advantage of waste heat, and even me spreading the cooled ashes (from the bin at the lower left of the stove) around the yard so that nutrients join the shallow water table which then nourishes the nearby trees which will eventually find their way into the stove.

### Da-mo — 2014-11-14T22:16:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quantum-cookstove.12155/post-147399>

I grew up wit stoves like this - ours also had a heat exchanger in it for heating our hot water cylinder.
Last house we owned had a free standing wood heater with space on top to keep pots warm/hot. During winter we'd have a big pot of soup on top full of ham bones and veges and we'd just keep topping it up.

My Fijian relatives that still live in the villages in the mountains still make me humble with their ability to cook cakes, puddings and deserts on an open fire.

I like that you are using ashes as soil nutrients. In mongolia they have used animal dung for fuel forever and they spread the ashes on garden beds that have been producing for millenia without any other form of fertiliser. I have two horses that produce up to 20 tons of dung per year - I compost most of it but am also looking at making it into bricks to dry in the sun - then burning it and fertilising the land with the ashes. There are companies beginning to do this commercially now and some large equine facilities are using the heat produced for energy as well - saving energy costs, disposal costs fertiliser costs and reducing the dependance on chemically produced fertilisers and use of fossil fuels.

Been toying with the idea of rewarming after CT in front of a wood fire last few days . . . .

[http://m.tn.com.ar/sociedad/el-extrano-caso-del-superbebe-de-santiago-del-estero\_544502](http://m.tn.com.ar/sociedad/el-extrano-caso-del-superbebe-de-santiago-del-estero_544502)

A few details in English.
Birthweight 4 kilos approx 10 lbs.
\- since then has added 2 kilos or 4.5 lbs on mothers milk only.
\- 2 months ago he was switched to solids.
\- he is now 9 months old and weighs 20 kilos or 40 lbs.
\- he has elevated glucose,trigs and recently been diagnosed with a fatty liver.
\- no more studies as the family is poor.
\-so poor that often the only food is fish from the nearby river.
\- docs have told the mother that this is due to her drinking a lot of mate with sugar during pregnancy...

Poor kid!

# Thread: Electrodermal Testing or Vega Testing
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/electrodermal-testing-or-vega-testing.12170/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### PaulG — 2014-11-17T04:55:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/electrodermal-testing-or-vega-testing.12170/post-147586>

> This unorthodox method of diagnosing allergic and other diseases uses a Vega machine, a galvanometer that compares the electrical resistance of skin in contact with a hand electrode and skin in contact with a measuring stylus. There is an electrical source providing a direct voltage of 0.87 volts through the patient, and the gadget is completed by a metal honeycomb in which ampoules can be placed in series with the circuit. A dial graded 1-100 reads 100 when the connected resistance is 0, and 0 when the resistance is infinity. During testing, a piezoelectric spark generator producing 400 volts per second is applied to the patient. This is alleged to stress the patient and unmask weaknesses in the body.

Just back from my local Naturapath who used a [Vega Testing](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Vega_machine) machine to determine my son allegedly has the following:

-   Candida
-   Adrenal Fatigue
-   Low Magnesium, Vit-B, Zinc, Molydenum, Chromium

To determine this the Naturapath placed various bottles (glass and plastic, large and small) containing the substance being tested for onto a metal plate, if the meter hovered mid-way that was a problem (eg low in the substance or body has reaction to it), if it went all the way to the end that was ok.

The Hair Mineral Analysis test revealed adequate magnesium levels and the naturopath explained that the body can bioacummulate minerals in one area because of poor utilization in other areas.

Have I been scammed (according to [QuackWatch](http://www.quackwatch.com/01QuackeryRelatedTopics/electro.html), [QScepticsBook](http://scepticsbook.com/2009/03/09/the-vega-test-a-spectacular-rip-off/) and [this](http://theness.com/neurologicablog/index.php/vegatest-high-tech-pseudoscience/))?

http://data:image/jpeg;base64,

### Josh — 2014-11-17T05:53:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/electrodermal-testing-or-vega-testing.12170/post-147587>

My mentor has used it for 20 years as well as several other people that I know. It is the second machine that Schimmel developed. Fairly accurate and open to the correlations that the practitioner and or programmers make regarding the readings. I have seen good results and if one is in doubt, all of the above can be cross referenced with blood and or stool tests. It makes a circuit through the body along a chosen meridian and then the remedies are put into the circuit to see whether it improves.

# Thread: Italian Pot Roast
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/italian-pot-roast.12192/>
**Subforum:** Epi-Paleo Recipes
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### fitness@home — 2014-11-18T21:16:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/italian-pot-roast.12192/post-147744>

This was delicious and had my entire house smelling absolutely divine. Highly recommend if you have a lazy weekend day at home. Perfect for a fall/winter day.

**Italian Pot Roast
(Caveman Books)**

**Ingredients**
2-lb rump or chuck beef roast
½ - t TBL sea salt
4 TBL extra-virgin olive oil, divided
2 medium carrots, diced (about 1C)
2 medium celery stalks, diced (about 1C)
1-2 medium red onions, diced(about 1.5-2C)
2-3 cloves garlic, minced
¼ flat leaf parsley, chopped fine
1 bay leave
2 TBL fresh sage, chopped fine
2 C medium-bodied Italian red wine or red wine vinegar, divided

**Directions**
1\. Trim some fat if desired and then pat dry with paper towels, Sprinkle generously with sea salt to season.
2\. Place 2TBL olive oil in a large heavy-bottomed stockpot or Dutch oven and allow it to heat up over medium-high heat. When oil begins to shimmer, but is not smoking, add the roast to pot and cook for 10-12 minutes, turning as needed until meat is browned on all sides.
3\. Transfer meat to platter and set aside.
4\. Return pot to stove and place over medium heat. Add 2 TBL olive oil and heat until shimmery. Add in the diced carrot, celery, and onion. Cook for 8-10 minutes, stirring occasionally until veggies start to lightly brown and begin to stick to pot. Then add minced garlic and the herbs. Move herbs around pot for about 1 minute or until they become fragrant.
5\. Add in 1.5C of the red wine and begin stirring quickly, scooping up any veggies that try to stick to bottom of pot. When wine has mostly evaporated and the veggies are heavily coated in the wine herb mixture, return the roast to pan and turn roast to coat it well on all sides with the soffritto base. Make sure the roast’s surface is coated with the base, brushing more of the mixture on and rubbing it into the roast.
6\. **MY STEP:** Pour yourself a glass of wine – there is leftover in the bottle! Enjoy
7\. Increase heat to high and add in the remaining ½ C wine and the bay leaf. Bring to boil. Once boiling is reached cover pan with a lid and reduce heat to simmer only. Contiune turning roast every 30 minutes, until the meat is very tender and flakes easily when pierced with a fork – about 3 hours.
8\. Turn off heat and let roast rest in juices for about an hour.
9\. Transfer to platter and cover loosely with foil. If sauce is too thin – increase heat, bring to boil, and reduce to a medium-thick consistency. Adjust seasoning now (salt, pepper).
10\. Cut roast into thick slices and drizzle each slice with the remaining juices.

Start of cooking process:

Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" } [](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/start-jpg.1097/)

### Cpt.Tired — 2015-04-07T05:00:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/smart-meter-protection.12217/post-160469>

> [Hope said:](/goto/post?id=160463)
>
> That's what I read, I think. Smart meter = bad, period.

I have an RF meter (10Mhz - 8Ghz) and I measured around the smart meter that they installed on our house. Outside there was some radiation pulsating off of it but I was more concerned about INSIDE the house. I could not get any reading from the smart meter inside our house but I also wrapped the meter with a few layers of tin foil and just left a small opening in the front. I did that several months back - before the meter came in so I don't really know how much radiation was transmitted before the tin foil.
The biggest ones in our house (now that I disabled the wifi) is the cordless phone and the microwave. My cellphone will pick up a few wifi signals from our neighbours - about 3 - 5 signals, but my meter doesn't pick them up (It's a good quality meter, one that Michael Neuert recommends)
Nevertheless, I still have a 20 gauss Magnetico on order and I'm going to put a main power switch in our bedroom because it's just going to get worse, not better!

### JMO — 2015-04-29T03:38:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/smart-meter-protection.12217/post-162706>

Poor customer service as they didn't even call in advance to schedule an appt and they needed me to be home so they could relight the pilot lights. ### JMO — 2015-04-29T04:33:13+0300

### Cpt.Tired — 2015-04-29T07:03:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/smart-meter-protection.12217/post-162728>

> [JMO said:](/goto/post?id=162713)
>
> The front door to the house is abt 50% glass and 50% iron...just wondering if It's attracting all the rf in the neighborhood?? I have a rf meter but need to have a consult with Michael.

Sorry to hear that... on a positive note, you've now joined the CLUB!
(I know.. not very funny)

I wrapped mine in aluminum foil and left just a small opening in the front. Works good on the sides but there is still a blast from the opening which then reflects off of the neighbour's house and bounces back and forth between our houses.

It seems that there is nothing (or very little) in the house but by far the worst culprit was the CORDLESS PHONE!

Get rid of it! Or at least unplug it at night.

I bought the electrosmog TES 593 meter and it has been great. I also got a Trimeter 100XE and they have really helped a lot but then again I am an electrician so that also helps a lot.
Michael has some you tube tutorials for the meters.
The 593 was about $500.00 and the Trifield 100XE about $160.00

### Psyche — 2015-09-22T23:20:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/smart-meter-protection.12217/post-177617>

I called my electric company and asked about the type of meter I have. They told me it is hard wired and uses power line communication (PLC). From what I've read:

**_Electrical meters using PLC technology communicate by transmitting signals via the electrical wires. Communication is normally transmitted on cables designed for it-power lines along the streets are not designed to carry communication signals. So the PLC signals are "dirty electricity" which turns the household wiring and the power lines in the streets into giant transmitting antennas. This is caused by the modification of the electrical and/ or magnetic fields created by the PLC signals._**
From reading about PLC meters, it seems mitigation is much more difficult with this type- mostly used in rural areas.. Opt out doesn't help, because PLC signals still come in to the house from the outside. The signals get inside on the utility line and also as direct radiation from outside the power line near the house. Apparently, you can't shield PLC technology in the US, only Europe, due to the use of lower frequencies (i.e. unaffordable, not practical).

My fix- most nights, I am sleeping on the property in a tent , approximately 200 feet away from the meter on the house and any power line. I'm also at a lower line of sight from the house (down a small hill).

Smart meters- really SUCK.

### JMO — 2014-12-04T02:56:12+0200

Then she just started asking for 3 oysters every night before bed. Surprised the heck out of me (just hope she doesn't have her calculator going..lol).

### yewwei.tan — 2014-11-26T09:47:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deep-ct-required-for-skin-flushing-and-how-this-relates-to-the-menstrual-cycle.12231/post-148533>

I want to move a question originally posted by Gretchen in the Quantum Health Facebook Group because I think it's important, and because searching on Facebook seems to be impossible  (I need the power of Google to look up older but still relevant threads )

Original Question by Gretchen (@[Shijin13](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/969/)) -- [https://www.facebook.com/groups/872967099382852/permalink/911038668909028/](https://www.facebook.com/groups/872967099382852/permalink/911038668909028/)

[Jack](https://www.facebook.com/jack.kruse) - Question for you - why doesn't sitting in 50 deg water activate my alpha MSH as effectively as colder water? I get the most alpha MSH Activation in water below 45 degs. what mechanism is going on? is this good/bad?
​

Jack's initial reply -- [https://www.facebook.com/groups/872...id=911067665572795&offset=0&total\_comments=25](https://www.facebook.com/groups/872967099382852/permalink/911038668909028/?comment_id=911067665572795&offset=0&total_comments=25)

[https://www.facebook.com/groups/872...id=911067665572795&offset=0&total\_comments=25](https://www.facebook.com/groups/872967099382852/permalink/911038668909028/?comment_id=911067665572795&offset=0&total_comments=25)
​

My initial reply -- [https://www.facebook.com/groups/872...id=911203462225882&offset=0&total\_comments=25](https://www.facebook.com/groups/872967099382852/permalink/911038668909028/?comment_id=911203462225882&offset=0&total_comments=25)

Hmm, I always thought that flushing was a sign of eNOS upregulation more than anything else --[http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php...](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads%2Fniacin-flushing.6849%2F#post-84654)

The linked thread is about Niacin flushing, and the CPC#9 blog post actually talks about how Niacin is useless in people with low redox, which is also where -- [http://jackkruse.com/hashimotos-and-melasma/](http://jackkruse.com/hashimotos-and-melasma/)

I personally think that Niacin's action (more NAD+) is mediated by cAMP and AMPk, and Jack has talked about the mechanisms of how those 2 pathways suck when you are Leptin Resistent:

\- [http://jackkruse.com/how-to-find-your-inner-masterpiece/](http://jackkruse.com/how-to-find-your-inner-masterpiece/)
\- [http://jackkruse.com/intermittent-fasting-and-leptin/](http://jackkruse.com/intermittent-fasting-and-leptin/)

Peter on Hyperlipid mentions how AMPk can generate superoxide --[http://high-fat-nutrition.blogspot.com/.../the-crabtree...](http://high-fat-nutrition.blogspot.com/2014/10/the-crabtree-effect-and-superoxide-in.html)

Quote: "But the central link, the activation of AMKP, is mitochondrial derived superoxide. And, oddly enough, one of the functions of AMPK activation is the generation of mitochondrial superoxide. A self sustaining loop."

Another quote: "Let's be very specific: Glucose, under the Crabtree effect, triggers a cascade which ends up with failure to generate superoxide and this maintains mitochondrial shutdown"

My guess as to why colder CT works, is that you need the glycogen depleting and magnetism increasing effects of the colder temps to stimulate the proper hormone cascade that allows for alpha MSH, eNOS, etc .... Essentially more superoxide and better cellular signalling as [Jack Kruse](https://www.facebook.com/jack.kruse) mentioned.

**Stuff to Possibly Biohack**

[https://www.facebook.com/groups/872...id=911204875559074&offset=0&total\_comments=25](https://www.facebook.com/groups/872967099382852/permalink/911038668909028/?comment_id=911204875559074&offset=0&total_comments=25)

\- If I'm not wrong, Chromium helps diabetics generate superoxide using the same mechanism which would be helpful to you.

\- HIIT before CT to further deplete glycogen before cold exposure. Timed to circadian cycles if possible, say 5 mins of HIIT during the last 2 hours of sunlight, then CT immediately after. (then maybe a meal containing at least 25g of protein after)

\- Nicotine before CT for BAT upregulation. I think 1mg is enough for the effect, though up to 4mg is useful.

\- Spot CT while sitting in warmer water. Example: Ice bag around your neck while sitting in 50F water. I personally found this very helpful, and was as simple as putting a big ice block next to my neck while in the tub. Definitely a quicker way to induce a shivering response in myself.
​

Jack referenced Chromium in Tensegrity #9 -- [https://www.facebook.com/groups/872...id=911266945552867&offset=0&total\_comments=25](https://www.facebook.com/groups/872967099382852/permalink/911038668909028/?comment_id=911266945552867&offset=0&total_comments=25)

Why does Chromium help diabetics? It is the only transition metal that is antiferromagnetic. Antimagnetic materials can cause a net magnetic effect to manifest when mitochondria have lost their normal ability to generate a magnetic field. Antiferromagnets can couple to ferromagnets, through a mechanism known as exchange bias. This stabilizes a bad magnet and slows ubiquination rates in diabetics. The Fe-S clusters in T2D function poorly therefore they stimulate signaling to increase ubiquination rates in protein polymers and protein synthesis. Anytime we increase protein synthesis we are increasing the times our cells have to measure the quantum state of its local environment as its redox potential changes. When we measure things in QED it alters the experiment.

(from Tensegrity #9 section '**More Metal Magic**' -- [http://jackkruse.com/tensegrity-9-magnetic-mitochondria-memory-creates-coherence/](http://jackkruse.com/tensegrity-9-magnetic-mitochondria-memory-creates-coherence/))
​

Reply by Gretchen -- [https://www.facebook.com/groups/872...id=911270635552498&offset=0&total\_comments=25](https://www.facebook.com/groups/872967099382852/permalink/911038668909028/?comment_id=911270635552498&offset=0&total_comments=25)

I think that in my case superoxide and alpha MSH decrease across my cycle, which shows a slower response at the mitochondria b/c of the PCOS/Estrogen dominance thing, especially since estrogen is hydrophobic. all of this is a result of a disconnection from magnetism. women are sensitive to the environment for a reason..... its all about the magnetism at the mitochondrial level...​

\=====

I had a post talking about Estrogen and Ketosis awhile ago -- [http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/todays-webinar-with-dave-asprey.10486/#post-120686](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/todays-webinar-with-dave-asprey.10486/#post-120686)

Strong encourage a re-read of the section '**Iodine, Estrogen and Ketosis: The Wake-Up Call for Menopausal Women**' in EE7 -- [http://jackkruse.com/energy-epigenetics-7/](http://jackkruse.com/energy-epigenetics-7/)

There was a thread about Tensegrity #5 and Hashi's, in which we talked about the need for T3 as the seasons change -- [http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/tensegrity-5-and-thyroid-hashi-question.11842/](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/tensegrity-5-and-thyroid-hashi-question.11842/)

Summary by Gretchen -- [http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.ph...yroid-hashi-question.11842/page-2#post-143162](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/tensegrity-5-and-thyroid-hashi-question.11842/page-2#post-143162)

So. If you're eating a seafood rich diet and you're in ketosis. YOU DON'T Need T3 to assist with ETC across Cytochrome 1. BOOM! When you add Seasonally appropriate carbs, T3 is you little helper to leverage cytochrome 1 during High Light cycles.

So what happens when you live in Artificial light? you mimic summer. the problem is when you eat like summer when its winter, your magnetic sense can't function properly to support ETC.
​

Gretchen is currently in winter when she posted the question.

\----

**Random
**
ECT was mentioned in the latest podcast with Daniel Stickler as a possible CT biohack that Jack may embark on in the future -- [http://www.ephysiologix.com/living-the-optimized-life-episode-33-jack-kruse-cold-thermogenesis/](http://www.ephysiologix.com/living-the-optimized-life-episode-33-jack-kruse-cold-thermogenesis/)

Obviously this podcast is very relevant to discussion, but I only listened to it once. I'll probably listen to it again tomorrow.

\----

I mentioned in my reply to Gretchen above that putting an Ice Block around my neck while in the cold tub caused me to shiver a lot more despite relatively warm water. So for example, I wouldn't shiver in 12C (54F) water normally, but if I put the ice block close to my neck, I'd start shivering, and my skin would also start to flush within a lesser amount of time.

Ben Greenfield's latest podcast on freediving made me think about how the mammalian dive reflex is a huge deal -- [http://www.bengreenfieldfitness.com/2014/11/how-to-start-freediving/](http://www.bengreenfieldfitness.com/2014/11/how-to-start-freediving/)

This could be a bit of the effect of Jack's fournier effect setup, where we keeps his head and chest in the freezing wind, but keeps his lower body cold.

Of course, I'm thinking about what if you actively used water to induce the dive reflex. For some reason, the first idea that came to me was grabbing a cotton (not wool) balaclava, stuffing it full of ice, making it wet with cold water, and then wearing the thing during CT (possibly exposed to the wind), would cool the face pretty well and induce this dive reflex. No idea if that works of course, and I don't have the resources to try it on myself right now ....

\----

Why is "Skin Flushing" good anyway?

@[Josh (Paleo Osteo)](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/9815/) made this comment -- [http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.ph...s-over-exposure-to-the-sun.11796/#post-142231](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/sunscreen-allowing-hazardous-over-exposure-to-the-sun.11796/#post-142231)

when skin goes red it has absorbed enough blue light #optics​

I posted a sun flushing thread as it pertains to the sun awhile ago -- [http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/sun-exposure-and-skin-blushing.10569/](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/sun-exposure-and-skin-blushing.10569/)

Now we know that repelling excess sun and repelling excess cold makes use of the same mechanisms -- either enough NO or Sulfur in the skin to create enough electric current.

So what is skin flushing anyway? Is the redness an indicator that the skin is reflecting red and infrared light, and thus "reflecting excess heat" (hot case), and "preventing loss of heat" (cold case)?

\=====

I'll let these topics sit in my head on the plane ride tomorrow and see what comes up.

> -
>     No one seems to see that aspect of life. What else do I see? Your mind determines, judges, and creates meme's that you operate under. Gravity vs the electromagnetic force. I divorced my beliefs from a gravity based world ten years ago because of what I learned about electric and magnetic fields. The land and seas of planet Earth are alive with an endless and constantly replenished supply of electrons. The specific conditions on the surface of the earth make it unique to other anodes in the solar system. Life has to organize around the conditions here and the electric and magnetic condition set forth by the sun. Since all life forms make some kind of direct contact with the surface of the planet—the skin of our bodies touching the skin of the Earth—our conductive bodies naturally equalize with the Earth and sun's charges. Your mitochondria is that sensor in the system of interaction. It senses both parts of the system with specificity and sensitivity. Figuratively speaking, life is designed by evolution to refill the electron level in our tanks that have become low. DHA is the main supercapacitor in the system because it turns light to a DC electric current in both plants and animals.
>
>     How do we know that the body absorbs those electrons? In the 2005 study I spoke about in EMF 1, it was proved with a simple experiment. Mr. Applewhite showed a voltage drop across an in-line resistor, measured with an oscilloscope, provided ample evidence of an exchange of electrons between the Earth and the body during grounding. Today, the physics of plasma cosmology has shown that sunlight is a cathode ray. The sun is positively charge cathode and the source of all life's forces. How it gets spliced, divided, and used are all quantized in the subsystems of our body.
>
>     The Earth is a negatively charged anode in this circuit. It has an endless supply of negative-charged free electrons. The Birkeland currents of both objects connect them via the solar wind bi-directionally. Anytime you have two conductive objects and they make contact, such as your bare feet and the ground, electrons will flow from the place where they are abundant to the place where there are fewer of them. The more disconnected your mind is from the light and magnetism that are formalized in circadian signaling, the more altered your perceptions of reality become. If you're well connected to nature, inflammation levels are low and your body acts in coherent fashion. In this state, you create thoughts and intuitions of what life looks like and feels like is acceptable for you. Conversely in sickness, the opposite happens and the minds thoughts become disconnected from the operations in your cells. The irony most are unaware of is that the body doesn’t give a hoot what you think about this process; it goes on about the business of survival. All of our goal's in life should be to learn what we need to know to coherently link our thoughts to how our mitochondria fundamentally work in quantum fashion. When we do, we begin to realize that our body conforms to what our mind thinks is optimal based upon the conditions we allow. Once we learn who the master of our body really is, then and only then will we come to know how the quantum world manifests in everyday life.
