Which I think is directly related to this... b/c @[cantweight](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1283/) mentioned to me she thought that excessive exposure to IR last winter made her feel the same way Tristan mentioned above. The above FB thread, as well as Christi's comment has been rolling around in my head for the past several weeks. If you allow yourself to follow circadian cycles - regardless of sex, you should be naturally drawn to the cold. and that cold will directly influence you heath, as well as your ability to become pregnant. If you ignore the cold, your body gets the incorrect signal thus turning on/off epigenetics.

### cantweight — 2014-11-27T03:37:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deep-ct-required-for-skin-flushing-and-how-this-relates-to-the-menstrual-cycle.12231/post-148619>

I was in a bad way last winter. Extreme fatigue, could not walk up a flight of stairs without my leg muscles seizing. Looking back I can see a lot of mismatch.

Was still pushing 180mg Armour, high fat extreme keto, and I lived in front of a burning fire all winter long, little to no CT. I stayed in my family room with a roaring fire daily as I studied....the temp was high and exposure to IR light constant.

When we ran out of wood early spring I slowly began to come around. I blamed the moldy wood, the IR light/heat never even occurred to me until reading Tristans thread. SMH.

### Shijin13 — 2014-11-27T17:50:37+0200

It took me reading Tristan's comments, talking to you @[cantweight](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1283/) to understand how even small things can establish a mismatch. blows my mind I really don't think we truly understand the magnitude disconnection to seasonal and circadian cycles can eff us up, especially as women given we're passing the mitochondria on to the next generation....

### kovita — 2014-11-27T18:05:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deep-ct-required-for-skin-flushing-and-how-this-relates-to-the-menstrual-cycle.12231/post-148648>

> [Shijin13 said:](/goto/post?id=148570)
>
> Thanks for posting @[yewwei.tan](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/6824/) ! I'm still puzzling this out. Women are more sensitive to the environment - the abundance or lack of magnetism, and that fluctuates through out a woman's cycle as does superoxide. These environmental impacts directly influence how women's mitochondria function to directly support gestation, or preclude it. This directly influences if a women gains/looses mass, becomes diseased, or becomes pregnant.
>
> Obviously I'm still trying to figure this out.
>
> One thing that keeps rolling around my head is the possibility that if you pay attn to circadian cycles, embrace cold, and are eating a seafood based ketotic diet is it possible a woman's cycle would naturally shorten in the leutal phase to preclude pregnancy, if she's not already pregnant going into "Winter" thus limiting a woman's chance of getting pregnant when there's limited access the food, similar to how hibernating animals gestate. My thinking on this is that it would support conception during high light cycles, with greater food availability and limiting it during low light cycles/cold just like our hibernating ancestors. This would also support natural child spacing associated with breastfeeding that extended until the child was 5-6yo. yet this probably changed as we evolved and began implementing farming, food storage techniques, as well as living indoors with some semblance of controlled heat to keep temps elevated in the living space. This wouldn't have eliminated the circadian signals from shorter days, but would epigenetically impact the cold signals - possibly allow women to get pregnant during the winter due to the "perceived" summer due to spending the night next to all that IR heat from the fire.

interesting ideas. Well I sort of fit into this model. I was cold and zero carbs the whole winter. It did some harm to my hormones, but the summer and summer carbs fixed this slowly and I got pregnant in impossible way (first random accident). It is interesting to note what was the behaviour of DHEAs and IGF1. IGF1 got very high during the ketotic and cold winter, top range, DHEAs was good around 300 (I am 36). In the summer the IGF1 went to the upper 1/3 rd of the range and DHEAs to the absolute top range. Estrogen went rather lower in the summer, lower third of the range and
progesterone upper third range and I got pregnant. I do not represent complete health at all, this is what I have witnessed this year.

Kovita - I think if more women tracked their cycles, paid attention to seasonal eating, light, and circadian cycles - we'd see a quick shift in pregnancy when women would become pregnant easily. Where most women today - are in some state of STRESS - thus precluding their ability to get pregnant, and its why we're now seeing a reliance on intervention to get pregnant.

The interesting thing is our dog breeder (Labrador Retrievers) is having a hard time getting her bitches to come into heat. She's mentioned this several times, as well as other breeders in other breeds in other areas of the country also having similar issues. So this problem isn't just happening with humans it's happening with other species as well....

If we don't start paying attention, mother nature is going to solve our problem for us.... and we're not going to get a say in it...

### Haley — 2015-01-15T00:16:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deep-ct-required-for-skin-flushing-and-how-this-relates-to-the-menstrual-cycle.12231/post-152771>

Interesting there is a post on this...as I was wondering why my hot red cheeks have been coming back at night. I went down to TX for christmas and upon my returning to CO (and more emphasis on decreasing carbs) I started getting this flush at night. It has been going on for over a week now, happening randomly. I don't do CT in baths/showers but have been keeping my house in the 50s and turning no heat on in the car (keeping it around 30 deg). Is the flushing a good thing?

### notsoperfick — 2014-11-28T12:02:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/techy-issue.12242/post-148686>

Whenever I post to a thread, it automatically watches it for me without my selecting it. It's not a huge problem as I don't post very much but I wondered if there was a way to switch it off? Also, does this site not allow pms to other members, I can't see the facility anywhere?

### caroline — 2014-12-01T02:04:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/techy-issue.12242/post-148891>

Have you contacted mission control?

you should be able to pm other members and I don't think the "watch" thing is automatic - it isn't for me anyway.

Have you tried clicking on someones pic? and then it shows you how to start a discussion ...
try it with me....

### Lahelada — 2014-12-01T02:50:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/techy-issue.12242/post-148893>

Have a look in your account for the heading "preferences" there is a tick box that says "automatically watch when.." Un-tick it and all will be well.

# Thread: Reaction from Betaine HCL
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/reaction-from-betaine-hcl.12255/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### seanb4 — 2014-11-30T22:15:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/reaction-from-betaine-hcl.12255/post-148863>

Hi,

I have been taking betaine hcl (with pepsin) again over the last week. Anyway I've been taking it in higher amounts without burning. I have found it helps with some of my symptoms.

Anyway today I took 20 x 500mg for breakfast and the same for tea. About 1.5 hours after taking the last dose I came up in something similar to a niacin flush reaction. My body got really warm and itchy, I could feel lots of blood pumping to my head and it felt sun burned. At no point have I felt burning or hotness in my stomach. I also came up in little almost like spots.

What is the cause of this reaction?

### seanb4 — 2014-12-01T17:00:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/reaction-from-betaine-hcl.12255/post-148935>

It seemed very similar to a niacin flush but my throat is still tight and it is painful to breath deep. Wonder if it is a reaction to the sheer amount of acid or some of the ingredients... Betaine Hydrochloride, Pepsin (Porcine), Hypromellose capsule, Leucine, Silicon Dioxide.

### kovita — 2014-12-01T19:00:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/reaction-from-betaine-hcl.12255/post-148944>

have you changed brand, lot, package, for how long have you been using this product etc...if you can exclude reaction to components, you can start to look into what happened internally. It sounds like massive histamin release. Remember it could happen even with a substance you have been taking regulary for years. I remember last time you were mentioning helicobacter p. as being probably culprit in your story. Maybe look into whether there are any cases, situations where this bug could be related to histamin release? I am just trying to give ideas.

### seanb4 — 2014-12-01T20:06:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/reaction-from-betaine-hcl.12255/post-148948>

Thanks for the advice kovita.

It seems like a milder version of Anaphylaxis. Probably something to do with histamine like you said.

Anyway it seems that histamine is released when the stomach wants to secrete acid. I was taking high amounts of acid so I doubt this is the case.

If I do have h pylori then maybe it was unhappy with the higher acid levels and released histamine to somehow defend itself. Apparently it reduces diamine oxidase which helps detoxify histamine so that could have influenced it.

Thing is if it was due to h pylori being irratated, I thought I would have had a smaller reaction when taking less dose....

# Thread: CONFIRMED EBOLA CASE TREATED AND QUICKLY RESOLVED DIRECT IV OZONE
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/confirmed-ebola-case-treated-and-quickly-resolved-direct-iv-ozone.12262/>
**Subforum:** Redox Rx
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ColinGorham — 2014-12-01T17:41:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/confirmed-ebola-case-treated-and-quickly-resolved-direct-iv-ozone.12262/post-148937>

[https://www.facebook.com/DrRobertJRowen?fref=ts](https://www.facebook.com/DrRobertJRowen?fref=ts)

CONFIRMED EBOLA CASE TREATED AND QUICKLY RESOLVED
Dear Facebook Family,
You all have been devoted to our mission, and therefore you all should be the first to know the most fabulous news you will hear regarding the Ebola crisis. Dr. Robert Rowen and Dr. Howard Robins now openly announce to the world the first CONFIRMED case of Ebola which has been apparently cured with the Rowen-Robins Ebola protocol, which features the Robins method of DIV ozone administration.
If you’ve been reading the news, Dr. Komba Songu M’Briwa, a senior military physician in Sierra Leone, and one who had been with the now deceased Dr. Salia, contracted the disease, likely from embracing Dr. Salia when Salia’s first test came back negative. Dr. M’Briwa made international news on November 27, 2014 with the announcement that he contracted Ebola.
[http://www.washingtonpost.com/world...1fbd54-7627-11e4-a755-e32227229e7b\_story.html](http://www.washingtonpost.com/world/sierra-leone-physician-who-treated-martin-salia-diagnosed-with-ebola/2014/11/27/af1fbd54-7627-11e4-a755-e32227229e7b_story.html)
Dr. M’Briwa was presented with the offer of our ozone protocol, and signed the informed consent. This is the email Rowen got on November 27, 2014, from Dr. M. Jalloh, who became the managing physician of this case:
“Hello Dr. Rowen, This is Dr. M I Jalloh the guy who brought you the tennis shoes at Hastings \[Ebola treatment center\]. Hope you are fine.
I am not the one directly treating Dr. Songu M'brewa but I coordinate his treatment. He is presently stable with no diarrhea or vomiting. He is receiving the ozone (55 mls) twice a day. He is also receiving Advance Cellular Silver, Vitamin C and Glutathione. The management is promising. They will probably run another Ebola test after ten doses of ozone to check if he is still positive and the viral load…Will keep you updated.”

Dr. Jalloh has indeed kept us updated. Today, November 30, only 3 days later, Dr. Jalloh has informed Dr. Rowen that Dr. M’Briwa promptly resolved his fever and had no further development of the usual Ebola symptoms of horror. In fact, he’s already been moved out of the Ebola sick unit into the “recovery” unit. Furthermore, a second test (for viral load) after treatment commenced was negative for Ebola, and a third test will be performed on Monday, December 1. You will be sure the naysayers will, like they often do when a confirmed cancer case “mysteriously” recovers at the hands of unconventional doctors, that they might say the same here. But it will be hard to do on this one. He had direct contact with Dr. Salia, he was personally treating Ebola, and he was housed in the Ebola treatment unit after testing positive. No one will be able to deny this one. And, he recovered exactly as we predicted would be the course of ozone-treated Ebola.
Now, here is an interesting twist to the story. You will remember our announcement of the first doctor we reported who jabbed himself with an infected needle? That was Dr. Sekou Kanneh, who was scared for his livelihood and life should he get a positive test, be quarantined, and then be denied ozone. Dr. Kanneh was the hands-on treating physician for Dr. M’Briwa, and was supervised in the program by Dr. Jalloh.
And finally, today, Dr. Rowen spontaneously got a delightful email from someone he didn’t recognize. It said, “Hi Dr . Its been a while since u left sierra Leone. How are u doing? We miss d ozone therapy.” When an email was sent inquiring as to the identity of the sender, this returned: “Am d head nurse nurse at Hastings ebola treatment centre. U gave me your email d last time u went there. Actually, we are taking ozone but my colleagues at other treatment centres around d country are not .
Sent from my BlackBerry® smartphone from Airtel Sierra Leone.
This appears to be the most promising advance in the Ebola crisis, and we are hopeful that everything we are being told from our sources is accurate. We are anxiously awaiting additional information regarding Dr. M'Briwa (and Dr. Kanneh). Folks, we wish to thank you for your support, your prayers and your love. We are bringing this case to the world now, and you can certainly help by getting it to news media, friends and family.
Sincerely, Robert Rowen, MD and Howard Robins, DPM

### ColinGorham — 2014-12-01T17:46:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/confirmed-ebola-case-treated-and-quickly-resolved-direct-iv-ozone.12262/post-148938>

From Dr. Robert Rowen's FACEBOOK page
Ebola vaccine is in the works:
[http://www.washingtonpost.com/natio...b63c42-75d6-11e4-8893-97bf0c02cc5f\_story.html](http://www.washingtonpost.com/national/health-science/ebola-vaccine-seems-safe-in-first-stage-testing/2014/11/26/6bb63c42-75d6-11e4-8893-97bf0c02cc5f_story.html)

Now I want you all to consider the press this piece is getting compared to the silence we have gotten. Here is an international story on an Ebola vaccine under way. Yes, it was done on human volunteers. And yes, it showed promise in that antibodies developed in the subjects. Now consider the flack Howard and I are taking for not actually having in hand, as yet, a blood test on our first patient. This vaccine story makes international news even though we will NEVER EVER see proper scientific testing of the effectiveness of this vaccine, in that we can NEVER EVER tempt fate by exposing those vaccinated to real Ebola. Why? They could still get infected. The vaccine might not provide full immunity, or it might provide immunity to a different strain than what the virus has morphed to. So, no proof of anything here with relation to a real case, but the story makes huge news as a promising approach.

You might ask, “Why?” I’ll tell you the same answer as always. Follow the money trail!. The vaccine will make bazillions for the maker in Africa, and perhaps even more here from people scared out of their minds about Ebola, or if/when we see cases coming up from bioterrorism. If there is bioterrorism, and Ebola starts making the rounds where it should not, watch the pundits run for this product. In the meantime, Dr. Robins and I have shown a MOST promising therapy to treat the actual disease which deserves attention and formal study, which no one will profit from since you cannot patent ozone.

[Newsscience.org](http://l.facebook.com/l.php?u=http%3A%2F%2FNewsscience.org%2F&h=6AQFs4rac&s=1) tells us: “The driver \[of the vaccine process\] is really the productivity of the cell line,” Ballou says. He puts the most optimistic timeline for 100,000 to 500,000 doses at 9 months—and the cost at $25 million.

Do the math, one dose of the vaccine now underway by PHARMA giant GSK, will cost, at best, $50 and at worst $250. And, it’s a crap-shoot. Even if it works, it could cover this Ebola outbreak only. Not others, and not offer a scrap of protection against other plagues that will certainly come.

So, let’s round the cost figure off to just $100. If just one million doses are prepared for Sierra Leone, that’s $100,000,000 cost for just that country. And, if Ebola becomes a bioterrorist phenomenon, well, consider taking out a mortgage on the entire world. On the other hand, if there is a $40 risk free treatment for just those who get the disease, and you don’t need to take the risk of the vaccine, and the $40 treatment can also treat all the potential strains that might emerge, that might put a crimp into the profiteering on this malady, and take the Heavenly reward away from the terrorists who won’t be able to kill people with a cure down the street.

Oh, sorry. I don’t mean to be jumping to conclusions. Just want to present the facts.
Stay tuned. I think I’ll have some more hot information for you soon.

Hope you all have a wonderful and SAFE holiday weekend.

### ColinGorham — 2014-12-02T20:38:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/confirmed-ebola-case-treated-and-quickly-resolved-direct-iv-ozone.12262/post-149028>

From Robert Rowen's FB page:
“Suppose they gave a cure for Ebola, and nobody came.” I think that might be the name of the movie that comes out of the adventure Dr. Robins and I had in Sierra Leone. Based on the information we are able to get on our two cases, the Ebola virus, as horrifically lethal as it is left alone in the human body (about 60% mortality) in the current epidemic, this virus appears to have an Achilles heel so vulnerable, but yet so simple, that scientists won’t consider it, and the press ignores it.
Mind you, the Rowen-Robins protocol called for treatment of active Ebola at least 4 times daily with the powerful ozone treatment of direct intravenous gas (DIV) of the Robins method. Dr. Robins and I believed the virus so lethal that it would take several days of frequent administration to cure it, but that we would see improvement in as little as 48-72 hours. It appears, based on the feedback from Sierra Leone, that we were wrong. Dr. Kanneh was better almost immediately and did continue his treatments for at least the 10 days we recommended. But Dr. M’Briwa, who had a confirming test for Ebola, was 100% better after just 2-3 days (possibly less) of ozone therapy, and after a posting was made on Facebook in Sierra Leone which was attempting to raise money for ZMAPP to save his life. Furthermore, a follow up test for Ebola was negative in just 2 days.

Remember the War of the Worlds by H.G. Wells? In that tale, Martians with invincible power came to the planet and went on an unstoppable roll. It was so ghastly that people who listened to the fictional story on radio (broadcast by Orson Wells) actually committed suicide. Those who killed themselves might have survived if they listened just a bit longer. As the story unfolded, within just a few days of the invasion, the Martians were stopped! And suddenly! How? By the simplest of all things on the planet – microbes, to which they had no immunity, but we do!

We are just two cases into Ebola. The unstoppable march of symptoms virtually instantly stopped in both these cases. And in the one documented positive case, the patient became negative on a second test just a few days after the first. The Facebook page calling for donations for ZMAPP to save the life of Dr. M’Briwa announced his personal recovery. Additionally, I got an email from the chief nurse at the Hastings Ebola unit telling me they were rejoicing. And yes, he did get ozone, but not nearly what we thought was necessary.
So, could Ebola be the equivalent of “invincible” Martians? Does it really have a target painted on it that ozone or other oxidants might move on like a “smart bomb”? We won’t know unless and until those responsible for the lives of others take note – including organizations wedded to Pharma, like governments bought by Pharma, like the WHO, whose Ebola officer (Theo Grace) emailed me that the use of ozone on humans is CRIMINAL, like news media fully biased by Pharma bought ads, etc. Zmapp treatment may cost many hundreds of dollars, perhaps more, and it takes many months to grow it in a GMO tobacco plant. While thousands die, Theo Grace, presented with ozone as a definite safe, and highly possible cure for Ebola deemed ozone use to be criminal, yet permits a 60% death rate to rampage.

Ozone? It’s here right now. It’s in the atmosphere. It’s in many city water purification systems. In Sierra Leone the material cost to administer treatment is about $0.75 per session. Based on two doctors recoveries and the limited information we have at hand from their cases, the material cost for cures for them cost less than $10 (not including supplements).
I guess ozone is just not sexy enough like “ZMAPP” for the Pharma owned/controlled authorities/press to consider. Hmmm, maybe because its simply not costly enough or patentable either. Perhaps because unknown “nobodies” like Rowen and Robins can do it absent their control?
Honestly, while it appears we have two full quick cures under our belt, the treatment deserves study. Until then, we don’t know ABSOLUTELY for sure/ And, our line of communication from Sierra Leone is about as good as a satellite transmission from Neptune. But, with the WHO in charge, and huge Profit motivated interests at large, and millions coming in to incinerate bodies and bury the dead, getting ozone into use to actually SAVE lives has been painfully slow in coming, even as more people die, children orphaned and families broken.
However, while hundreds are still dying of Ebola, you can be sure that when a government official gets scared, as exactly happened when I was on the ground there in October (see my previous postings), he will come running for ozone to save his xxx. Then “there will be a cure for Ebola and “he” will have come”.

Finally, I close by referring you to the solicitations for donations for the military doctor for the expensive and scarcely available ZMAPP and the doctor's own announcement that he has recovered. Interesting that the ONLY note about ozone on that page came from me.
[https://www.facebook.com/…/ZMAPP-for-a-life…/754066924678683](https://www.facebook.com/pages/ZMAPP-for-a-life-saver-Captain-Dr-Komba-Songu-Mbriwa/754066924678683)

I solicit your comments, please. And, I will post the extraordinarily hostile comments of WHO Ebola official Theo Grace in the very near future.

# Thread: Ketogenic overeating in winter
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ketogenic-overeating-in-winter.12272/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Butters — 2014-12-02T21:40:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ketogenic-overeating-in-winter.12272/post-149031>

What happens when you overeat on a ketogenic diet in winter and you're resistance training?

I can watch my hunger disappearing but force myself to eat to build muscles. I try to gorge on fat and have a moderat protein approach with carbs mainly in the evening meal up to 50g max out of vegetables or chocolate (I will ditch some vegetables and chocolate soon because I do not feel to eat so much anymore in the cold times).

### Hansen Kenimer — 2014-12-04T00:39:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ketogenic-overeating-in-winter.12272/post-149137>

Butters, I imagine if your redox is good, you'd burn off the excess calories at night when you're sleeping.

As an aside, I'm eating ketogenic and I lift heavy and never force myself to eat. I eat till I'm full. And then I don't eat until I'm hungry again. The better my redox gets and the more I CT, the less I want to eat, really. Starting CT for me really seemed to make building muscle ridiculously easy. Too easy. My legs have suddenly gotten too big and I need to back off.

You'll eventually get past what's been ingrained into our minds about "eating big to get big" and realize it's all about improving your redox and becoming metabolically efficient. The more efficient you are, the less actual food you'll need, and you'll get a whole lot more out of the food you DO consume. Water, however, I have found to become even more critical as you start to creep closer and closer to being a zero entropy system.

### Butters — 2014-12-04T11:55:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ketogenic-overeating-in-winter.12272/post-149200>

Thanks for the answer. Thought no one would answer it.

I posted the question, because I read the CT blogpost 6,7 and there Jack mentioned that cold, short light cycles, low carbohydrates and low calories have all to be present to get the most out of CT. Then I thought about my consume behavior and my hunger was declining despite I am working out and make gains like crazy for me. I'm making such gains I haven't for a long time but I also was eating a lot of fat.

Its coming slowly but after rereading some posts I begin to understand.

In Winter protein and fats also are rare, so we are low calorie, dependend of the latitude its more or less.

But in his podcast with Ben Greenfield Jack mentioned also that some kids use CT and eat tons of bacon etc. So that is not fitting in the picture.


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: Where I am at with CT?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-i-am-at-with-ct.12273/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Butters — 2014-12-02T21:48:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-i-am-at-with-ct.12273/post-149033>

How can I find out if I am cold adapted?

What happens when my BF is already low and I am CTing extensivly through the day with less clothing and sessions on the top? My BF through BF caliper is roundabout 12%.

I thought I am adapting very good but as temperature is dropping to -3°C I find myself shivering almost all the time outside when wearing only longsleeve or T-shirt and inside in the unheated room. I can not get the feeling of heating up all the time. Plus my feet and hands are cold almost all the time.

When I use the cool gut buster and fat burner I can wear it all the time on the pure skin and take it off with covered red skin and numb in places. I don't develop cold urticaria. Also most of the time I can gulp on ice water without getting brain freezes.

Also, should I eat a high fat/high protein meal before at my status or better not?

### nonchalant — 2014-12-03T02:45:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-i-am-at-with-ct.12273/post-149069>

I think you are partially adapted.
If you are having difficulty with CT, eating a high fat meal before could help. I don't usually bother now. I CT when ever it is convenient; sometimes just after a meal, sometimes not.
I think you need to be patient with the shivering. Your body will adapt when it can.

### Butters — 2014-12-05T14:28:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-i-am-at-with-ct.12273/post-149301>

Tonight I got very intense heat generation also during the day after a period of fasting I recognized more heat generation.
I thought shivering isn't bad at all because it releases Irisin and this browns WAT fast to BAT.
Maybe I drink to much ice water during the session. Sometime to 2l. Jacks writes about 1l ice water maximum during a session.

# Thread: Brain freeze explanation
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brain-freeze-explanation.12274/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Butters — 2014-12-02T22:39:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brain-freeze-explanation.12274/post-149041>

Why One gets brainfreezes with high omega 6 tissue content?

### nonchalant — 2014-12-02T23:52:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brain-freeze-explanation.12274/post-149047>

The O6 tissue (from artificial oils) is not able to respond correctly to cold temperatures. Skin that has O6 from nuts or other natural source, and O3 from seafood is quite resilient in cold temps. They are like antifreeze for your cell membranes.

### Butters — 2014-12-03T09:14:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brain-freeze-explanation.12274/post-149084>

Yeah, I know Jack mentioned that. But is there any scientific evidence? I have a friend who wants to take fish-oil. I told him to eat fish but he don't want to. As he wants to know how much he have to take, I said it varies from person to person. But he should tell me if he gets brain freeze when eating/drinking cold things. I would like to send him some study or article about that.

# Thread: Take Back Your Power's Josh Del Sol EMF summit interview today and tomorrow
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/take-back-your-powers-josh-del-sol-emf-summit-interview-today-and-tomorrow.12279/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Penny — 2014-12-03T01:51:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/take-back-your-powers-josh-del-sol-emf-summit-interview-today-and-tomorrow.12279/post-149064>

[http://emfsummit.com/josh-del-sol/](http://emfsummit.com/josh-del-sol/)

This interview had some interesting legal things you could do if you didn't want to smart meter... excellent -

### Terminator — 2014-12-09T03:17:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/take-back-your-powers-josh-del-sol-emf-summit-interview-today-and-tomorrow.12279/post-149560>

DTE just installed my home area but not my house and I covered two meters of two neighbors in double layer alluminum mesh all around and one layer of alluminum foil around it (not on the face). I spent no more than 5 minutes each around them Saturday and today and got a headache both times.

I know a DTE employee that installs meters and does other technical electrical services for them. I called him today and asked if he has headaches from working around these meters, his reply was ;

_Yes, I do. My ears are ringing also and cannot sleep. I often wakeup in the middle of the night and just cannot go back to sleep. H also told me that he asked other meter installers that confirmed that they have same issues !_

EMF situation is getting out of control quickly !

### PaulG — 2014-12-09T04:03:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/take-back-your-powers-josh-del-sol-emf-summit-interview-today-and-tomorrow.12279/post-149575>

Here is a lesson for you I have been living with smart meter for 15 months now, when I first moved in I phoned the power company, exchanged emails, looked into ways of protecting me and the family having read that you cannot get them swapped out. Josh (Paleo Osteo) mentioned that he managed to get his 'powered down' which I filled in the back of my brain and forgot about. Last week I looked into getting a canopy and was again told that you can get them powered down to 10% of the max RF output or moved to front of your property.

So, I phoned the company but they said they had not heard of this powering down but was then asked whether I wanted the meter to be swapped for the older digital style where you have to report the usage every two months...almost fainted...now kicking myself for my lack of assertion and apathy.. (probably caused by EMFs lol).

Aluminium foil reflects EMF where more solid metals can absorb the signal. You can also use YShield paint. Best to buy a meter and test levels around your sleeping areas and then take steps to mitigate.

### Josh (Paleo Osteo) — 2014-12-09T04:10:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/take-back-your-powers-josh-del-sol-emf-summit-interview-today-and-tomorrow.12279/post-149576>

did you swap it out???
i might try that...

### PaulG — 2014-12-09T04:41:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/take-back-your-powers-josh-del-sol-emf-summit-interview-today-and-tomorrow.12279/post-149579>

Takes two weeks to get the crew to do it - so hoping it will happen next week, although apparently the replacement Digital meters also emit RF though I have no idea why they would. Certainly cant see me getting an analog one.

Might be worth contacting the **[http://stopsmartmeters.com.au/contact-us/](http://stopsmartmeters.com.au/contact-us/)**

### Josh (Paleo Osteo) — 2014-12-09T08:35:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/take-back-your-powers-josh-del-sol-emf-summit-interview-today-and-tomorrow.12279/post-149586>

Hi Josh

Can you please tell which Distributor has allowed him to do this as this is not allowed by the AER.

We cannot offer this at all as set out by the Victorian Government. Please find attached Victorian Government confirmation regarding this.

# Thread: Weird thing happened after CT today...
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weird-thing-happened-after-ct-today.12292/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Hansen Kenimer — 2014-12-04T18:13:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weird-thing-happened-after-ct-today.12292/post-149228>

Spent an hour in the tub, no problems. Warm, pink, happy. About 5 minutes after getting out I started to get this feeling in my quads and hamstrings that I can only describe as a mix of cramping and the "pump" you get when lifting. It happened when I either stretched the muscles or tightened them, but seemed normal when they relaxed. I've never had this sensation before, but this was my first hour-long CT session. I'd only done 30 min up to this point. I don't think it has anything to do with being low in Mg or K, because I NEVER have issues when lifting with cramping.

My guess it has something to do with increasing NO levels from the longer CT. Anyone ever had anything like this?

### Jonathin — 2014-12-04T20:47:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weird-thing-happened-after-ct-today.12292/post-149238>

A couple of years ago after doing a CT bath, I was trying to warm up and it felt like I was in a pulsating wind tunnel. Eventually it died down but felt truly weird.

### JMO — 2014-12-05T01:03:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weird-thing-happened-after-ct-today.12292/post-149252>

Yes...something like that happened to me after I took a couple H2O2 baths. Weird feelings in my calves....but the good thing was I started warming up really quick after that and have stayed like that. Maybe all the DHA went to my lower half in the cold water and I warm up quick. I need to flip it so it goes to my head (need a hot tub).

### Brett Jacques ND — 2014-12-07T02:27:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weird-thing-happened-after-ct-today.12292/post-149386>

Yes, I too can get a little crampy when spending too much time in the ocean that resolves after eating breakfast of avocado, coconut and some other nuts.

# Thread: CRP and birth control
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/crp-and-birth-control.12308/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Dombant — 2014-12-05T14:21:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/crp-and-birth-control.12308/post-149300>

So after a year and two blood tests showing very high CRP (11 in the one a few months after my c-section and 7 a few months ago) I read recently that birth control and IUD's can cause elevated CRP (pregnancy does too.) I recently found out that my IUD was protruding (I was expelling it so they had to put a new one in OUCH!!) so now I'm not quite trusting what my inflammation read out was. Just wanted to point this out to any of the ladies here using these methods. It is making me question whether using the copper iud is a good choice, though. I wonder if chronic minor inflammation of the uterine walls is a good idea now (though, weighing that against the cortisol elevation from an unwanted pregnancy, hahahaha.) I was just trying to take a non-hormone route. I take a little extra zinc picolinate and eat plenty of red meat and offal to keep my balance in check. Anyway, as always, context is everything..

[\[url=https://plus.google.com/+JeangreyCofan/posts\]dr jean grey](https://helpouts.google.com/113838601398151265676/ls/8ac079cb1db4077d)\[/URL\]

### Shijin13 — 2014-12-05T15:20:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/crp-and-birth-control.12308/post-149304>

There's an entire thread some where here on the forum about charting and using that to prevent pregnancy. I used it, as have many others....

# Thread: OZ Gathering @ The Barossa Vintage Festival
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oz-gathering-the-barossa-vintage-festival.12319/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ssj3 — 2014-12-07T11:58:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oz-gathering-the-barossa-vintage-festival.12319/post-149412>

As it seems like all the gatherings are in a land far far away, I think it would be awesome if us cooler people in the Southern Hemisphere had an opportunity to get gather and cause mayhem.

The week long Barossa Vintage Festival is being held in April 2015 and on Saturday 18th April, the famous Vintage Festival Parade is held.

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

The idea is that a bunch of us would stake out a spot along the parade route; and over some interesting conversation share a few dozen oysters and some great Barossa vino.

Send me a PM or reply below if you are interested and we can discuss details further and organise logistics.

Hope many of you can make it!

### ssj3 — 2014-12-10T10:14:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oz-gathering-the-barossa-vintage-festival.12319/post-149676>

Here some extra details for those interested.

The vintage festival runs from 15th-19th April. The main parade is on Saturday 18th April.

There are quite a few options to think about depending on where everyone wants to stay and how long you want to enjoy Adelaide ...
1\. Stay in Adelaide and travel up for day to Barossa on the 18th April.
2\. Stay in Barossa from 16th-19th April or longer.
3\. Stay in Adelaide before and/or after the 16th-19th April and stay in Barossa during the 16th-19th April.

There are a few options in the Barossa that will be able to accommodate a large group of us for a reasonable cost of ~$60pp/night. This is with a 3 night booking with check-in on Thursday 16th and check-out on Sunday 19th April. These a linked below for you to review:

[https://www.airbnb.com.au/rooms/3491789](https://www.airbnb.com.au/rooms/3491789)
[http://www.seppeltsviewcabins.com.au/index.html](http://www.seppeltsviewcabins.com.au/index.html)
[http://www.barossamotorlodge.com.au/](http://www.barossamotorlodge.com.au/)
[http://vineinn.com.au/](http://vineinn.com.au/)

In Adelaide there is accommodation both before, during and after the 16th-19th April. Below are some links to available accommodation.

[http://www.stayz.com.au/accommodation/sa/adelaide/henley-beach/83688](http://www.stayz.com.au/accommodation/sa/adelaide/henley-beach/83688)
[http://www.stayz.com.au/accommodation/sa/adelaide/henley-beach/104704](http://www.stayz.com.au/accommodation/sa/adelaide/henley-beach/104704)
[http://www.stayz.com.au/accommodation/sa/adelaide/henley-beach/132112](http://www.stayz.com.au/accommodation/sa/adelaide/henley-beach/132112)
[http://www.stayz.com.au/accommodation/sa/adelaide/henley-beach/64801](http://www.stayz.com.au/accommodation/sa/adelaide/henley-beach/64801)
[https://www.airbnb.com.au/rooms/247024](https://www.airbnb.com.au/rooms/247024)
[https://www.airbnb.com.au/rooms/267804](https://www.airbnb.com.au/rooms/267804)
[https://www.airbnb.com.au/rooms/4720674](https://www.airbnb.com.au/rooms/4720674)

# Thread: Alcohol

**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 9

### Butters — 2014-12-07T13:50:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/alcohol.12320/post-149417>

Is Alcohol harmful in Winter and a no go?

Jack talks about red wine often and I wonder if there are some spirits that I should avoid?

### Inger — 2014-12-07T22:13:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/alcohol.12320/post-149449>

iirc alcohol is not so great in winter time... Jack has mentioned it somewhere. I try to avoid it then and rather drink wine in summer if I want some. But sure no rules without exceptions
I think most spirits are not good for health but red wine like malbec that is high in resveratrol is good for health when you drink one or two glasses no more - then it turns to poison. Yeah. And no wine after 5 PM as it disturbs your sleep.
If you live in high EMF you should drink no alcohol at all... as it dehydrates you too... iirc

### Haley — 2015-01-10T01:29:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/alcohol.12320/post-152271>

I'm wondering the same thing as Jack has mentioned several times of drinking wine lately and considering we are in the middle of winter I am confused. Maybe this is because of where he lives?

### Da-mo — 2015-01-10T02:04:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/alcohol.12320/post-152280>

I think its a case - like most things - of how good your redox potential is. Contra-indicated in some instances like when engaging in CT for example.

If I have a wine its usually in the evening and my biggest concern with it is the carbs it contains and hoow that might affect the hormone cascade during sleep.

### Josh (Paleo Osteo) — 2015-01-11T05:21:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/alcohol.12320/post-152378>

> [Da-mo said:](/goto/post?id=152280)
>

# Thread: Foot structure
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/foot-structure.12323/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### nonchalant — 2014-12-08T03:08:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/foot-structure.12323/post-149468>

I see some changes in my feet lately. Well, one change has been going on for a while -- the second toe on both feet has moved more toward the big toe, which has also moved away from the smaller toes. So the gap between my big toe and the next has increased, and there is also a gap after that next toe. The big toe movement started with grounding and changes to the next toe started with CT. So it's been going on for a couple years.

The other change I've noticed in the past couple months. When I walk barefoot on a hard floor, it feels like I'm poised on three points. Heel, base of big toe, and base of little toe. This is a completely new feeling for me, and it still feels...surprising. I don't remember it as a kid, though I've always had a high arch between my heel and toes. The base of my little toe was never used much, though I went barefoot plenty until my 30's.

A few years ago I suddenly started developing a callous under the second toe, next to the big one. But I was recovering from plantar faciitis, and going barefoot for the first time in years. But now that callous is almost gone, and now I have new callouses at the base of the little toes. Good for me, because walking on 3 points can get painful. The callouses under the big toes have decreased, though they were always huge since I was quite young.

I don't notice any difference in my footprint, but I don't have anything to compare it to.
I know, I know Dr. K, I shouldn't have callouses at all. I'm still dehydrated.


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: Cold repelling effect
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-repelling-effect.12327/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Butters — 2014-12-08T12:09:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-repelling-effect.12327/post-149497>

Hey there.
I'm diving deeper and deeper in this CT thing. For me, I have to understand the mechanism of things to do it better.

So why a magnetic or electric field repels the cold?

Moving currents create a magnetic field in a right angle to the flow of direction. For repelling only the surface charges on the mitochondria membrane are effective I think. Also we are not cooling the core, so it has to happen near the skin.
But how does this repel the cold?
Also wouldn't it be more a heat holding mechanism than a repelling mechanism, because thermodynamically heat diffuses to cold and not the other direction. So the magnetic field must hold back the diffusing mechanism.
So I have to ask: How does a magnetic field is able to break the diffusion? It has to make a neutral zone without charges is my thought, but I would like to know how this can be physically explained.

# Thread: Zero Point -Documentary series
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/zero-point-documentary-series.12333/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### sjoshua — 2014-12-08T19:02:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/zero-point-documentary-series.12333/post-149527>

I really enjoyed this 4-part documentary series by Zero Point, available for free viewing on youtube:

Volume I - Messages from Our Past:

\- Discusses some incredible mathematical alignments between various 'earlier' cultures, as well as related to the design and sizes of the pyramids and similar style monuments found around the world.

Volume II - The Structure of Infinity:

\- Discusses the common fractal patterns that can be found everywhere in nature, even within the constructs of human designs, such as the stock markets. Everything scales by the same basic ideas.

Volume III - The Legacy of the Ark:

\- Discusses the Ark of the Covenant from a scientific analysis - i.e. it was a giant capacitor; also discusses monatomic gold/ORMUS and its funky 'superconductive' abilities/tendencies.

Volume IV - Beyond:

\- Discusses our observations of a global/collective consciousness - i.e. hours before the 9/11/01 attacks at the World Trade Center, the 'randomness' around NYC became a lot less... random, and the same can be said about natural disasters and other worldwide events.

Watching these helped a lot of dots connect around my understands of all things. Enjoy

### sjoshua — 2014-12-08T21:39:44+0200

Geez, that's a real bummer! Here is a link to their channel, all of the videos are posted by the same company so I'm unsure why the rights would be different: [https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLWlK1ZorBxmy8K5M6zDALL0EGiYS51Z1d](https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLWlK1ZorBxmy8K5M6zDALL0EGiYS51Z1d)

# Thread: Pentacyclic triterpenes
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pentacyclic-triterpenes.12353/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Dombant — 2014-12-10T13:40:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pentacyclic-triterpenes.12353/post-149684>

I was searching for the effects of Pentacyclic Triterpenes on brown fat generation and found that ursolic acid, oleanolic acid, and betulinic acid have wide ranging health effects. Ursolic acid can increase brown fat, at least in mice. UA seems to convert between Oleanolic acid and UA in the body. Betulinic acid, UA, and OA are being studied for wide ranging health effects including obesity reduction and immunity. It is interesting that birch bark and chaga mushroom which grows on the birch bark in the far cold north have relatively high amounts of 1 or more of these PT's. Chaga is considered a medicinal or adaptogenic mushroom. Rosemary and apple peels are high in PT's, too.

Do PT's have a place in the overall Rx?

[\[url=https://plus.google.com/+JeangreyCofan/posts\]dr jean grey](https://helpouts.google.com/113838601398151265676/ls/8ac079cb1db4077d)\[/URL\]

Is there a valid test for omega fat composition of cell walls? Maybe someone does an RBC test for this? Or is the technology even there to do this measurement?

Jansz had a thread about his DHA being too high. Let's ask him. @[JanSz](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/933/)? Calling @Jaansz! Ring, Ring.

### persistence — 2014-12-16T00:01:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-way-to-measure-dha-in-cell-membranes.12364/post-150231>

> [JMO said:](/goto/post?id=150150)
>
> Ya sorry was off topic but Grechens outline was a trigger. As for ur question...I brought a related question up in another thread as my doc mentioned that when she does testing for Patricia Canes protocol that most people have too much DHA. I initially Tght that sounded absurd but guess it may be due to people taking fish oil. So I take it as not being the correct form SN-2 position.

There are all kinds of questions about what are the right levels of individual fatty acids, and of course the big question everyone tries to answer is what is the right ratio of Omega-6 to Omega-3. While those are very interesting questions, those are not the questions I was asking.

Jack makes a big deal in some of his podcasts about the unique role of DHA in the cell membrane. It's ability to support ION channels and electron transport gives it a unique and critical role. And because Jack is all about the electron and molecular biochemistry, this is a core issue. What I want to find is a lab that will directly \*measure\* the \*amount of DHA in the cell membrane\*. What most labs do is give you values in your plasma. That probably approximates the membrane value, but if you have a metabolic condition that affects the cell membrane, maybe not. Since DHA in the membrane is what gives you the benefit, DHA in the membrane is what I want to measure.

Apparently I am not the only crazy who has asked this question. This researcher realized the same issue and did a custom measurement of DHA in the cell membrane:
[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2785093/](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2785093/)
[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/17875307/](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/17875307/)

So, attempting to get this thread back to where it started: does anyone know of a commercial lab that will give a fatty acid profile of your cell membranes, rather than relying on plasma.

### persistence — 2014-12-16T00:51:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-way-to-measure-dha-in-cell-membranes.12364/post-150237>

I found a researcher who suggested to contact an Omega-3 specialty lab named OmegaQuant. Their 24 fatty acid profile looks great in any case:

[http://www.omegaquant.com/omega-3-index/](http://www.omegaquant.com/omega-3-index/)
[http://www.omegaquant.com/sample-report-omega-3-index/](http://www.omegaquant.com/sample-report-omega-3-index/)

Talked to one of their technicians, and they are telling me if you have a lab send them an EDTA tube, they will centrifuge the RBC and get a reading on fatty acids in the membrane. I'm still trying to get clarification on that.

### JanSz — 2014-12-17T00:24:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-way-to-measure-dha-in-cell-membranes.12364/post-150330>

I do my Fatty Acids testing at LabCorp, but the test is done at Mayo Clinic.
**Test ID: FAPCP**
**Fatty Acid Profile, Comprehensive (C8-C26), Serum**

[http://www.mayomedicallaboratories.com/test-catalog/Overview/82042](http://www.mayomedicallaboratories.com/test-catalog/Overview/82042)

Specimen is spinned.
3\. Spin down within 45 minutes of draw.
[http://www.mayomedicallaboratories.com/test-catalog/Specimen/82042](http://www.mayomedicallaboratories.com/test-catalog/Specimen/82042)

**Results are for individual fatty acids**

This is not test that Patricia Kane is using.
It is the best I can get.
\==================================
**I think Mayo Clinic should be better that the test done by OmegaQuant,**
**because:**
[http://www.omegaquant.com/omega-3-index/](http://www.omegaquant.com/omega-3-index/)
**gives:**
**The Omega-3 Index is a measure of omega-3 fatty acids, EPA+DHA, in red blood cells,**

That is at extreme one can have DHA=0(zero) and EPA 4x over the limit
**and the test will tell that there is excess of EPA+DHA.**
\============

Many people (including me in the past) supplement with fish and/or krill oils.
They have excessive amounts of EPA.

I still have a hard time to get rid of my excessive EPA, working on it for about 3 years now.

/
\--------------------------------------
Not only fish and kril oils, but also:
salmon and other seafood have mostly lots of EPA and little bit of DHA (at the wrong sn location),
so, .............................????????????????????/

.....

### persistence — 2014-12-17T00:41:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-way-to-measure-dha-in-cell-membranes.12364/post-150334>

Can you explain this more? Mayo test instructs the person taking the sample to spin down the blood to serum. This is the blood plasma minus the fibrinogens, so by definition there is no cell membrane measurement!! I talked to OmegaQuant and they have an option to spin down blood drawn to an EDTA tube to get RBC cell membranes and do the measurement on the membrane directly. That's what we want, isn't it, since the membrane is where the omega fatty acids are incorporated?

> Many people (including me in the past) supplement with fish and/or krill oils.
> They have excessive amounts of EPA.
>
> I still have a hard time to get rid of my excessive EPA, working on it for about 3 years now.
>

> salmon and other seafood have mostly lots of EPA and little bit of DHA (at the wrong sn location),

2) What is the issue with SN location? What are the different types of DHA and which foods have the right type?

### persistence — 2014-12-17T00:48:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-way-to-measure-dha-in-cell-membranes.12364/post-150335>

> [persistence said:](/goto/post?id=150237)
>
> I found a researcher who suggested to contact an Omega-3 specialty lab named OmegaQuant. Their 24 fatty acid profile looks great in any case:
>
> [http://www.omegaquant.com/omega-3-index/](http://www.omegaquant.com/omega-3-index/)
> [http://www.omegaquant.com/sample-report-omega-3-index/](http://www.omegaquant.com/sample-report-omega-3-index/)
>
> Talked to one of their technicians, and they are telling me if you have a lab send them an EDTA tube, they will centrifuge the RBC and get a reading on fatty acids in the membrane. I'm still trying to get clarification on that.

Okay, just had an interesting discussion with OmegaQuant. If your intent is to understand how DHA affects electron transport chain, then you need to measure fatty acid composition of the mitochondria, not the outer cell membrane. This then raises the questions:

1) How the heck are you going to get a tissue sample that contains mitochondria, and which tissue do you sample? Short of doing an autopsy, you would need to probably get a biopsy, maybe a muscle biopsy.

2) Then you need to worry about how do you isolate mitochondria in your tissue sample. For sure no commercial lab is doing that. You are in the realm of research project. Would need to look if any researcher has done this.

3) Assuming you solve these problems, how would you interpret the result from a mitochondrial measurement. You would need a baseline value, presumably extracted from research papers. But has any researcher even profiled fatty acid composition of mitochondrial membranes on humans?

The "good news" is OmegaQuant told me "If you can get us an isolated sample of your mitochondrial membranes, we'll measure it for you."

### JanSz — 2014-12-17T01:27:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-way-to-measure-dha-in-cell-membranes.12364/post-150348>

I have a problem when someone is giving me combined results for (EPA+DHA).
At this time this overrides my other concerns.

This is the sample test report for the OmegaQuant 24 fatty acid report:

[http://omegaquant.com/sample-report-omega-3-index/](http://omegaquant.com/sample-report-omega-3-index/)

They list each fatty acid separately, and very nicely they give you percentage totals of each individual fatty acid as well as Omega 6 to Omega 3 ratio at bottom.

Where are you seeing EPA and DHA combined?

> There is variety of mitochondria.
> Likely the one in the brain is of most concern to us.
>
> There is two types of membranes in mitochondria.
> Outside eukaryotic.
> Inside prokaryotic, as in bacteria
> .

Does anyone know if electron transport chain is on both types of mitochondrial membranes?

I think if DHA is incorporating correctly into your outer mitochondrial membrane, it probably also incorporates onto the inner membrane. I'm also going to assume that - if you are lucky - researchers have baseline values for the entire mitochondrial membrane complex, and I doubt that anyone went to the trouble to isolate the inner membrane. How would you even do it?

I'm going to assume that if you can isolate mitochondria, that would be sufficient to get a very good reading of how well you have incorporated DHA into the structures used in the electron transport chain.

>
> Where are you seeing EPA and DHA combined?

In the link you posted I see individual fatty acids. GOOD
but I do not see there recommended ranges for each individual fatty acid. BAD

On the same page, on left I see:

The Omega-3 Index is a measure of omega-3 fatty acids, EPA+DHA

,,,,,,,,,,,
''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''
On same page there is:

We do not list “normal” values for any fatty acid or ratio except for the Omega-3 Index on the report. This is not an oversight. We are acutely aware that, although there are “average” values for blood fatty acids in America, these are clearly not “optimal” levels. Average American levels of palmitic, stearic, oleic, trans oleic, linoleic, arachidonic, etc. are not necessarily “healthy” levels. At OmegaQuant, we don’t want our clients to aim for a target that is off-target.

### persistence — 2014-12-17T04:23:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-way-to-measure-dha-in-cell-membranes.12364/post-150380>

> [JanSz said:](/goto/post?id=150376)
>
> In the link you posted I see individual fatty acids. GOOD
> but I do not see there recommended ranges for each individual fatty acid. BAD

I think this recommendation is beyond the scope of the report. This is in fact one of the most contentious issues in science. Biochemists will line up and tell you that your omega 6 to omega 3 should be as low as you can get it because of the inflammation and pro cancer effects of omega 6. Clinicians will line up and point to all of the studies that show omega 6 lowering heart disease and ask you to load up on it.

Why would I trust any recommendation that OmegaQuant gives me? It's a point of study and you have to make a decision about what is the right goal. Personally I want Omega-6 to Omega-3 not above 4:1, and you will see recommendations from 9:1 to 2:1.

> On the same page, on left I see:
>
> The Omega-3 Index is a measure of omega-3 fatty acids, EPA+DHA

This is a non issue because they give you every one of the separate 24 fatty acids and you can quickly do your own calculations using any combination of those that fit to the science you want to use.

Out of curiosity, what combination of Omega-3 do you want to use to make an Omega-3 Index?

Keep in mind that research studies simply compare Omega-3 to Omega-6, so if you choose a metric that no one has published research for, what good does it do you?

> On same page there is:
>
> We do not list “normal” values for any fatty acid or ratio except for the Omega-3 Index on the report. This is not an oversight. We are acutely aware that, although there are “average” values for blood fatty acids in America, these are clearly not “optimal” levels. Average American levels of palmitic, stearic, oleic, trans oleic, linoleic, arachidonic, etc. are not necessarily “healthy” levels. At OmegaQuant, we don’t want our clients to aim for a target that is off-target.

There is some confusion here. "Normal" means "normalized". And what they are trying to say is that they use a dried blood drop on a piece of spotter paper to do their standard test. This ends up measuring 50% plasma and 50% RBC membranes, and they are reporting those non-normalized values. For the one line that says "Omega-3 Index" they are doing a calculation to "normalize" the value to what you would get if you had done an RBC membrane test. Notice in their sample how EPA + DHA does NOT mathematically add up to the number in the Omega-3 Index line? That's because they normalized the value.

What I would do when getting this test is send them an EDTA tube, which would then let them spin down and get RBC membranes. I would get a test result where every individual value on the report needs no adjustment to normalize.

I want high quality data, taken from the right part of the cell. I'm also not interested in their Omega-3 Index, but just remove that from your thoughts and what is left is pretty interesting because of where they are measuring the values (from the cell membrane and not from plasma).

> Like I said before, I prefer to follow dr Patricia Kane.

I did some quick scan of her online, and she looks very interesting. I'm going to start on phospholipids after reading some of their work.

Is this the test you have been using:

[http://www.bodybio-wellness.com/report-fatty-acid.html](http://www.bodybio-wellness.com/report-fatty-acid.html)

Note Dr Kane also has her critics:

[http://articles.chicagotribune.com/...m-autism-recovery-movement-autism-treatment/3](http://articles.chicagotribune.com/2009-11-23/health/chi-autism-science-nov23_1_defeat-autism-autism-recovery-movement-autism-treatment/3)

Her credentials are from a nonaccredited academic institution, and the common criticism I read was that she uses the IV infusions as a way to charge patients huge amounts of money. But I think the idea of supporting phospholipids by oral supplementation is pretty low risk and reasonable cost.

Code:

```
Is this the test you have been using:

http://www.bodybio-wellness.com/report-fatty-acid.html
```

I would like to use this test, but have a problem.
I as private person cannot order this test.
This test have to be ordered by someone who have account with bodybio.
I called bodybio, they have given me few addresses in my area, I called them.
I was told that I can come (and pay for visit), but I will get the test ordered only if professional will make investigation, and as result of that investigation he/she will decide that I need such a test.

It looks to me like a scheme designed for profit.
I am not desperate enough to go that route.

I am able to get script from my doc for fatty acids from Mayo Clinic, that have to do.
\====================================

Code:

```
cell membrane and not from plasma
```

OmegaQuant is using dry blood

Mayo Clinic is using serum that is spinned within 45 minutes of draw.
I think (not sure) that they keep red blood cells and discard plasma, so they test fatty acids in plasma.

At this time I am not able to agree or disagree which one is better, but definitely the results will not tell me anything because OmegaQuant is not providing any values/ranges to compare.

Mayo Clinic does provide ranges for each individual fatty acid that they test.

### Shijin13 — 2014-12-12T21:59:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/time-and-space-in-our-brain.12367/post-149943>

> [av8r said:](/goto/post?id=149942)
>
> Makes me ponder that maybe verbal language isn’t primordial and only came about as we became more disconnected….

disconnected from what?

I ask this b/c in one of my philosophy classes with a great professor, in fact @[QuantumRadical](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/165/) had the same instructor I did several years earlier, who asked the question regarding the biblical story of the tower of babble - why would god make different languages to disrupt communications between his creations? and If God created us all in his image would his message differ if it were in different languages? Thought provoking discussion followed.

But when I think about disconnection - I don't just think its from the earth, but from the universe at large. the ability to perceive what's going on beneath the current of perceived reality.... the connection with who you are now, and who you were in the past and who you are in the future. no end, no beginning. and constant stream of consciousness regardless of what form you take in matter. consciousness is the energy of the universe.

As Havoc told me one day in the car: "Mommy you're God." To which I replied: "um dude, no I'm not." To which he replied: " Yes, you are. God made you, therefore you are God." my reply back to him: "you know dude, you're pretty smart." His response: "I know"

Energy = God = Us = Energy = Universe = Energy

disconnection is from our true selves, the untapped energy within each and everyone of us.

### NeilBB — 2014-12-14T15:15:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/time-and-space-in-our-brain.12367/post-150068>

Language is not primordial to life as such. But I would argue that language is fundamental to humanity. I consider it the distinctive feature of normal human cognition. Not only for communication, but for conceptual _cognition_ as well. Language is merely conceptual abstraction, which is the tool ma nature innovated in us, so that we could transcend the immediate perceptual moment in a number of respects.

Sure, it is still possible to be a human without much or even any language ability. From strokes, tumors, trauma, or developmental anomalies, etc. I see these people all the time in my work. Non-verbal functioning is all that is possible to them. But I think if you talk to their families, you will find that this is a massive impairment for almost all of them, not any kind of liberation. (There may be some rare "savant" type exceptions to some extent. True.)

But quite frankly, I think the new age idea of desire to "transcend language" is mostly an excuse for wanting to be freed from the responsibility of understanding epistemology and the deeper meanings of concepts and principles. A significant portion of the expanded human neocortex accommodates symbolic language function and it works pretty damn well. Could we evolve beyond language one day? Sure. But why not try to better appreciate the unique conceptual tool we already have? Very few do...

### av8r — 2014-12-14T16:24:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/time-and-space-in-our-brain.12367/post-150072>

> [NeilBB said:](/goto/post?id=150068)
>
> Language is not primordial to life as such. But I would argue that language is fundamental to humanity. I consider it the distinctive feature of normal human cognition. Not only for communication, but for conceptual _cognition_ as well. Language is merely conceptual abstraction, which is the tool ma nature innovated in us, so that we could transcend the immediate perceptual moment in a number of respects.
>
> Sure, it is still possible to be a human without much or even any language ability. From strokes, tumors, trauma, or developmental anomalies, etc. I see these people all the time in my work. Non-verbal functioning is all that is possible to them. But I think if you talk to their families, you will find that this is a massive impairment for almost all of them, not any kind of liberation. (There may be some rare "savant" type exceptions to some extent. True.)
>
> But quite frankly, I think the new age idea of desire to "transcend language" is mostly an excuse for wanting to be freed from the responsibility of understanding epistemology and the deeper meanings of concepts and principles. A significant portion of the expanded human neocortex accommodates symbolic language function and it works pretty damn well. Could we evolve beyond language one day? Sure. But why not try to better appreciate the unique conceptual tool we already have? Very few do...

I totally agree that language is fundamentally to humanity, it’s essential; by primordial I meant could there have been a more efficient means of communication before verbal language came on the scene? I only ask this because of what I observe in nature, schools of fish and flocks of birds moving in total coherence without verbal cues … not that we would move in flocks or schools of coherence  but maybe our brains could be resonate coupled giving an efficient means of instant communication…exactly how that communication would emerge… I am not sure. BTW this is just a thought experiment of mine.

Intra and extra cellular communication in an optimum organism is instantaneous via longitudinal waves, as long as there is resonance coupling through a common medium, I could be wrong, but that’s my understanding. Fractal up to an optimum human coupled to the earth, I then wonder if two or more brains could share instant information if resonant coupled through the same medium.

### NeilBB — 2014-12-14T16:43:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/time-and-space-in-our-brain.12367/post-150076>

> [av8r said:](/goto/post?id=150072)

I only ask this because of what I observe in nature, schools of fish and flocks of birds moving in total coherence without verbal cues … not that we would move in flocks or schools of coherence  but maybe our brains could be resonate coupled giving an efficient means of instant communication…exactly how that communication would emerge… I am not sure. Fractal up to an optimum human coupled to the earth, I then wonder if two or more brains could share instant information if resonant coupled through the same medium.

Oh yeah, totally agree. I guess it depends on what we mean by more efficient though. I think these instantaneous "quantum" forms of communication are lightening fast, but probably also quite limited in scope, unlike language, so perhaps "efficient," but not "robust" maybe?

[http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/rush-song-of-day-for-gretchen.12246/page-4#post-150095](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/rush-song-of-day-for-gretchen.12246/page-4#post-150095)

### NeilBB — 2014-12-14T18:11:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/time-and-space-in-our-brain.12367/post-150080>

> [CTforlife said:](/goto/post?id=150014)
>
> I would say our idea of a god compared to the ancient civilizations of the world is dramatically different. People think we moved forward as a whole in every area besides health on this website. Ha, omg , ha.
>
> I think we haven't even learned 1% of the knowledge we use to have...... There is evidence of laser semiconductors being used 14,000 years ago. Yea, we are so advanced with our phones and cancer! The great flood took everyone out (different day).
>
> Call me stupid but here it goes. People make huge assumptions with the pyramids. They couldn't be bigger mistakes (different day). We still have many different theories to how they were built. How could we know something's true purpose if we can barely even fathom how they constructed it. Where did they get their gold? How did they have the fractal structure of the whole universe under the paw of many sphinx around the globe. Spend a day plotting the pyramids around the world on the globe you will literally shit yourself. We aren't being told the great ARCANUM because of the worlds powers. There you have it. What's in the Vatican that they couldn't show 7 billon non believers ? Maybe we need to play catch up instead of thinking we are moving forward. Our de evolution has created de coherence to the point that we can't understand the basic fractal laws of the universe. It gets way more fundamental. You can get the speed of light in the Giza pyramids and pi and the golden ratio just by dividing the dimensions. They knew the speed of light down to the Fenta second 10,000 years ago before we did. They matched magnetic north very very accurately. Pyramids around the world line up with Orion's Belt, why? Our idea of being disconnected and the idea of having to come before a god is radically different than our pasts. I don't even believe e=mc^2 is right or a full equation. It's a very brief description of a great observers perspective on reality. I think I know why entangling happens faster than the speed of light and why light must travel through what you call space time but my idea is RADICALLY different. Just because we can google something doesn't make it right.

Interesting line of thought. I agree that ancient cultures likely had a lot of knowledge that has been lost and that there is always a bias that current humans "are now smarter than those dumb people in the past" that is mostly unjustified. However, to my knowledge, there is not a lot of evidence from the past of these specific kinds of advanced technologies that we have today. Perhaps there were superior but totally different technologies that have been lost. It seems feasible. What evidence of laser semiconductors from 14,000 years ago? Please continue to enlighten us...

### sjoshua — 2014-12-15T19:44:23+0200

> [NeilBB said:](/goto/post?id=150076)
>

Interesting to ponder indeed. I'd posit though, in the same way we can learn better verbal communication (i.e. an infant learning their first words vs. an 'average' adult who is likely well versed in their common language), or considering other facets of our bodies like that we can train our muscles to run further, faster, harder etc, if our form of communication was though a different medium I don't see why we also wouldn't get better at that as well. We are quite elastic, especially the more exposure there is to things at younger ages.

...What if this is really how infants are trying to communicate to us, but due to our disconnection they must resort to crying, gesturing, etc until they fumble their way into our verbal construct?

> [NeilBB said:](/goto/post?id=150080)
>
> Interesting line of thought. I agree that ancient cultures likely had a lot of knowledge that has been lost and that there is always a bias that current humans "are now smarter than those dumb people in the past" that is mostly unjustified. However, to my knowledge, there is not a lot of evidence from the past of these specific kinds of advanced technologies that we have today. # Thread: Grounding to Water
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-to-water.12375/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### persistence — 2014-12-13T11:53:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-to-water.12375/post-149996>

In some podcasts, Jack makes the point that grounding by stepping into ocean water up to your knees is one of the best ways to add electrons. Is this due to the surface area coverage by grounded water, or is this because of ions in the water?

Assuming that the grounding is from surface area, I wonder if it would make any sense to run a grounding wire from a grounding rod outside into a tub of water. If you had an outside sauna, you could get your near infrared light and at the same time be up to your knees in a tub of grounded water. You would be adding electrons from the grounded water at the same time you treat with the infrared on the upper body.

### nonchalant — 2014-12-13T16:59:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-to-water.12375/post-150008>

I believe I can ground by just washing the dishes, with the water from our deep well, judging by the way my back reacts. It's almost like a chiropractor is adjusting it while I work. Also I get the same sensation, but faster and better, when I CT in our outdoor tub, using the same water. So it could be both surface area, and the energy in the water. I look forward to my next trip to the ocean, though, because it is far better than my well water.

When I used to CT in the tub in the city, unfortunately I didn't notice this benefit, though I used a grounding strap connected to the dirt outside. Jack did mention using a grounding strap while in the tub, long ago. I connected the strap to my ankle.

hello everybody,
i can't take ice baths since i only have shower in my bathroom but i was wondering if i should take daily cold showers for 30-day straight ( kinda experience that some folks brag about with youtube vids) or is it still okay to do them once in awhile like 5-10 mins twice or three times a week?
I do not live by the ocean but when i do visit my relatives near the french city of Nice (south of france nearby italy) in the winter time, i can see ederly people taking baths in the sea around christmas time and they say it is a great experience so i might do that as well soon (cold sea (thus salted water) exposure) in winter.

nice weekend to all
