# Thread: Non Soy Source for phosphatidylcholine?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/non-soy-source-for-phosphatidylcholine.12408/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 14
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### persistence — 2014-12-17T07:14:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/non-soy-source-for-phosphatidylcholine.12408/post-150406>

I have a problem digesting soy. Is there a non-soy (sunflower?) source for phospholipids such as phosphatidylcholine?

### Kristi Lambert — 2014-12-17T14:30:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/non-soy-source-for-phosphatidylcholine.12408/post-150418>

I used to use this in my raw food days: [https://www.mysunflowerlecithin.com/product/100-organic-liquid-sunflower-lecithin/](https://www.mysunflowerlecithin.com/product/100-organic-liquid-sunflower-lecithin/)

### persistence — 2014-12-17T14:56:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/non-soy-source-for-phosphatidylcholine.12408/post-150421>

> [Kristi Lambert said:](/goto/post?id=150418)
>
> I used to use this in my raw food days: [https://www.mysunflowerlecithin.com/product/100-organic-liquid-sunflower-lecithin/](https://www.mysunflowerlecithin.com/product/100-organic-liquid-sunflower-lecithin/)

Nonono!!! Placing a phospholipid into a fat base DISABLES ITS BIOAVAILABILITY!!!! This is one of the biggest scams in nutrition. The large industrial seed companies all convinced the regulators to allow them to take their engineered lecithin formulas and put 30% phospholipids into those and then call the resulting goo a "phosphatidylcholine". But it isn't bioavailable! It's just lecithin. Yes, it contains phospholipids, but your body cannot incorporate them into cell structure because of how they were packaged. They conveniently forget to tell you that on the product label. To my thinking, it is an obscene violation of consumer trust.

Here is a great video, and I am pointing you into the part of the video that explains how this scam works:

If you want bioavailable phospholipids, you need to buy a product where those have been extracted out of the lecithin, and packaged alone, not in a fat base. BodyBio makes a great one, but theirs is from soy. I found this one on Amazon that looks good from sunflower lecithin:

[http://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B00DILLV0G](http://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B00DILLV0G)

### SeaHorse — 2014-12-17T15:37:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/non-soy-source-for-phosphatidylcholine.12408/post-150423>

> [persistence said:](/goto/post?id=150406)
>
> I have a problem digesting soy. Is there a non-soy (sunflower?) source for phospholipids such as phosphatidylcholine?

Pure Encapsulations has a phosphatidylserine that is sunflower derived, but as for a choline supplement….why not eat eggs and sardines?

### Kristi Lambert — 2014-12-17T16:04:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/non-soy-source-for-phosphatidylcholine.12408/post-150425>

> [SeaHorse said:](/goto/post?id=150423)
>
> Pure Encapsulations has a phosphatidylserine that is sunflower derived, but as for a choline supplement….why not eat eggs and sardines?

Thanks so much for the info, SeaHorse, now I know what to put into my frittata tomorrow

### persistence — 2014-12-17T23:43:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/non-soy-source-for-phosphatidylcholine.12408/post-150458>

> [SeaHorse said:](/goto/post?id=150423)
>
> Pure Encapsulations has a phosphatidylserine that is sunflower derived, but as for a choline supplement….why not eat eggs and sardines?

To be clear, we don't want the choline. We want phosphatidylcholine, which is directly used to build cell membranes.

If you are fighting a metabolic disease that attacks cell membrane structure, there aren't enough hours in a day to eat all of the whole foods needed to supply enough bioavailable phospholipids.

I have noticed that most all encompassing phospholipids include just three out of the four on the label and leave out phosphatidylserine. Then you see other supplements that offer just phosphatidylserine. Any idea why no one puts it all together in one supplement?

### Kristi Lambert — 2014-12-18T02:34:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/non-soy-source-for-phosphatidylcholine.12408/post-150467>

These guys say they remove the soy components: [http://www.thepowermall.com/thecenterforhealth/healthy\_bodies/phosphatidyl\_choline.htm](http://www.thepowermall.com/thecenterforhealth/healthy_bodies/phosphatidyl_choline.htm)

### persistence — 2014-12-18T02:59:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/non-soy-source-for-phosphatidylcholine.12408/post-150474>

This is soy lecithin, not isolated phospholipids. It's all there in the ingredients list:

Each 1 teaspoon (3.3 grams) serving contains:
• Soy Lecithin 3,333 mg
• Phosphatidyl Choline 1,333 mg

The phosphatidylcholine in this product will not be bioavailable to reconstruct cell membranes because of this packaging.

# Thread: Clever Product For Determining Mineral Needs
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clever-product-for-determining-mineral-needs.12409/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### persistence — 2014-12-17T12:44:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clever-product-for-determining-mineral-needs.12409/post-150416>

This has got to be one of the cleverest products I have ever seen:

[http://www.bodybio.com/BodyBio/docs/BodyBioBulletin-LiquidMinerals.pdf](http://www.bodybio.com/BodyBio/docs/BodyBioBulletin-LiquidMinerals.pdf)

They provide eight minerals in homeopathic doses and in liquid form, and they are relying on the fact that if your body needs a mineral it will taste sweet, and if your body has too much the taste will turn bitter. The same mineral in the same person will taste sweet or bitter based on how much your body needs and has absorbed.

The beauty of this testing is that you can back off on minerals that you are taking too much of, and you can up your doses on minerals your body is not absorbing. Backing off of overdoses is at least as important as increasing intake of minerals you don't absorb. Minerals often compete against each other and affect critical enzyme systems, so getting a healthy balance is important.

I'm totally sold on the idea for this product. Test once a week, and keep adjusting dosage until you satisfy the body's need and get a neutral flavor during the test.

Now if only we had reliable home-based testing for other nutrients. Half of the stuff we are taking is probably killing us and we don't know it because we don't know what to measure.

### fitness@home — 2014-12-17T14:54:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clever-product-for-determining-mineral-needs.12409/post-150420>

Interesting. Thanks for posting

# Thread: How to Turn Off Email Notifications as DEFAULT Behavior?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-turn-off-email-notifications-as-default-behavior.12412/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### persistence — 2014-12-18T03:19:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-turn-off-email-notifications-as-default-behavior.12412/post-150477>

How do I turn off email notifications of new messages in Watched Threads?

I have seen this software used elsewhere, and how it is normally done is that you select "Watched Threads" and then "Show All Watched Threads" and then there is a pull down menu where you disable email notifications globally.

Someone here changed that default behavior. In this forum there is a pull down at the bottom of the all threads list
[http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?watched/threads/all](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?watched/threads/all)
that says:
"With selected
...
Disable email notifications"

The implication appears to be that only the specific threads you select will stop sending email notifications.

Is there a way to turn this off globally, for future posts as well as current ones? It's way too much hassle to keep resetting each thread manually.

### Lahelada — 2014-12-18T11:20:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-turn-off-email-notifications-as-default-behavior.12412/post-150500>

Click on your name in the top right hand corner or thereabouts. A menu comes up . Select "alert settings" and voilá.

### persistence — 2014-12-18T15:38:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-turn-off-email-notifications-as-default-behavior.12412/post-150520>

> [Lahelada said:](/goto/post?id=150500)
>
> Click on your name in the top right hand corner or thereabouts. A menu comes up . Select "alert settings" and voilá.

Did you mean "Alert Preferences"? That controls what notifications you receive when you are online, and I DO want to receive most of those when I am online.

Nothing on that page selectively controls receipt of notifications to email.

# Thread: Distance to Wifi Router

**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### persistence — 2014-12-18T08:58:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/distance-to-wifi-router.12416/post-150493>

If you are forced to live with a Wifi router in your home, turned on 24 hours a day, is there a recommendation about the minimum distance that this device should be from where you sit and use the computer?

### Josh (Paleo Osteo) — 2014-12-21T01:41:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/distance-to-wifi-router.12416/post-150707>

as far as humanly possible imo

### persistence — 2014-12-21T02:30:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/distance-to-wifi-router.12416/post-150709>

> [Josh (Paleo Osteo) said:](/goto/post?id=150707)
>
> as far as humanly possible imo

That's really escaping the question. The core issue is what is the distance under which you would receive some acute dose of EMF, enough to cause even 5% dysregulation of your normal.

Remember even if I turn my wifi off, I am still being inundated by wifi from all of my neighbors. At some point you have to commit to a specific recommendation or the idea just becomes some amorphous and not actionable concept.

### Josh (Paleo Osteo) — 2014-12-21T03:41:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/distance-to-wifi-router.12416/post-150716>

i cant give you the answer you want.

### Da-mo — 2014-12-21T04:17:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/distance-to-wifi-router.12416/post-150717>

Here's an answer, but you need to understand the question and the only way to know for sure is to test.

> **B14. What levels are considered safe?**
>
> RF/microwaves in the home vary greatly, usually depending on the use of cell phones, cordless phones, wireless and Wi-Fi. Average levels vary from 0.01 to 0.5 microwatts per centimeter squared (μW/cm²). **We generally use a precautionary level of 0.1 μW/cm² as suggested by the Bioinitiative Report, and 0.001 or less for sensitive individuals**. [http://www.emfcenter.com/emffaqs.htm#B10](http://www.emfcenter.com/emffaqs.htm#B10).

### persistence — 2014-12-22T03:27:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/distance-to-wifi-router.12416/post-150767>

> [Da-mo said:](/goto/post?id=150717)
>
> Here's an answer, but you need to understand the question and the only way to know for sure is to test.

Thanks for that. I found a great article through Google:

[http://www.emfwise.com/distance.php](http://www.emfwise.com/distance.php)

Based on this, for a wifi router you would need 48 meters (!) to get to the Salzburg 2002 limit. But - more pragmatically - if you can put just 6 meters (about 20 feet) between you and the router you are getting rid of most of the EMF.

Reading this article I referenced above is like an advertisement for Jack. It's terrifying the amount of EMF we are being exposed to. Look how far you need to be from a cell phone tower to meet the Salzburg 2002 limit. No one living in a US suburb is more than 3 km from more than one cell phone tower.

### persistence — 2014-12-22T03:30:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/distance-to-wifi-router.12416/post-150768>

> [Da-mo said:](/goto/post?id=150717)
>
> Here's an answer, but you need to understand the question and the only way to know for sure is to test.

Which EMF meters would you recommend for a home user? Do you think it is important to have it be digital?

### Da-mo — 2014-12-22T04:45:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/distance-to-wifi-router.12416/post-150772>

Check the FAQs at the link I posted it covers the meters [http://www.emfcenter.com/emffaqs.htm#B10](http://www.emfcenter.com/emffaqs.htm#B10)

Thanks for the emfwise link - some very good info in there.

Kinda like our problem...only way we can get internet...so have the modem+cradle 8meters away in outside garage with trees in between it and house, and a long ethernet cable to the house. Fortunately no neighbours and we turn ours off when not in use. Cheap way to protect your sleep could be to paper your bedroom walls with aluminium foil?

# Thread: Mineral and vitamin depletion in soil
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mineral-and-vitamin-depletion-in-soil.12444/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Ben1983 — 2014-12-22T16:30:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mineral-and-vitamin-depletion-in-soil.12444/post-150799>

I've heard Jack say that generally he isn't a fan of supplements. I often hear people talk about how we need to supplement minerals and vitamins because our plants are lacking in these things due to the depletion of it in the soil. I'm just wondering how you guys would respond to this?

### Jonathin — 2014-12-23T00:23:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mineral-and-vitamin-depletion-in-soil.12444/post-150816>

Many soils have less than optimum minerals even before modern farming. Why do you think taking a supplement might be a better option? Healthy animals (sea and land) have to be mineraly sufficient or they would not be healthy. So eating them would be beneficial. As far as vegetables and fruits, I eat what I produce from plants grown in mineral rich, biologically active soil. If you don't grow your own then at least know what healthy, vital produce looks and tastes like. There is a lot of garbage out there even if it has an organic seal of approval. Your tongue can tell you a lot.

### sebbie — 2015-01-20T19:35:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mineral-and-vitamin-depletion-in-soil.12444/post-153441>

The way I've personally responded to this is moving to a place where I can grow food. Then study, study, study, practice, practice, practice. Soil chemistry and microbiology will affect the nutrition of your crops.

Been on it for 3 years.

I'm actually surprised that Dr. Kruse hasn't written extensively about growing and raising healthy food. What makes a healthy egg? What makes a healthy carrot? Certainly not the labels that you see at a store.

### kovita — 2015-01-20T20:03:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mineral-and-vitamin-depletion-in-soil.12444/post-153444>

Ben, I am soon to move to a place where I can grow at least some of my veggies in a good soil. The truth is that I am not able to gete everything. i seem to need just from food. My diet is excellent but still magnesium is my particular weak point. I do not supplement iodine, DHA, cystein or sulphur in general....but without high dose magnesium I cannot go on. So for myself I say one should try to do his best to get covered all his nutritional needs by the best food choices and leave for supplements only what really cannot be particulary covered byt the real food. Epipaleo is not particulary rich in magnesium and it happens to be as well my weak point, however the dose I need to keep my levels optimal is lowering with the time. I am fully aware of the fact that no supps will ever replace the real food in any way and i think this should be the core of the overall approach.

### Kristi Lambert — 2015-01-21T22:53:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mineral-and-vitamin-depletion-in-soil.12444/post-153561>

Hi, Kovita, what is your preference on the form and dosage of magnesium? I am still trying to get my iodine up, then hopefully the magnesium will kick in. I put epsom salts in my CT tub and take 4 160mg magnesium citrate per day.

### BTA — 2015-01-22T19:02:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mineral-and-vitamin-depletion-in-soil.12444/post-153613>

> [Ben1983 said:](/goto/post?id=150799)
>
> I've heard Jack say that generally he isn't a fan of supplements. I often hear people talk about how we need to supplement minerals and vitamins because our plants are lacking in these things due to the depletion of it in the soil. I'm just wondering how you guys would respond to this?

that's why you need to find a quality ionic trace mineral product that has over 80 trace minerals. Organic vs Inorganic matters. You need some gold, arsenic, tin, ect..... but in the correct form. Dead doctors don't lie............

### Martin — 2015-01-22T21:49:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mineral-and-vitamin-depletion-in-soil.12444/post-153623>

Our environment has changed through our farming practices. So we have to study, test, evaluate, and then decide if and when we need to plug in where we have screwed it up so bad we need to supplement.

# Thread: ? about my chronic insomniac daughter...

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/about-my-chronic-insomniac-daughter.12447/post-150808>

When she actually eats enough seafood, she starts to sleep again, but has nightmares unless she sleeps with her table lamp on. Literally every time she turns it off she'll have nightmares that night and then I can't get her to keep trying to sleep with it turned off. I cannot understand for the life of me what would cause this!

### caroline — 2014-12-24T07:00:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/about-my-chronic-insomniac-daughter.12447/post-150916>

> When she actually eats enough seafood, she starts to sleep again, but has nightmares unless she sleeps with her table lamp on. Literally every time she turns it off she'll have nightmares that night and then I can't get her to keep trying to sleep with it turned off. I cannot understand for the life of me what would cause this!

i have a child patient like this who also has juvenile rheumatoid arthritis. she was a twin to her sister who died at birth, the spirit of whom is her "imaginary" best friend.
she will not sleep in the dark, which i believe is the reason for the JRA onset...light on the skin all day etc etc

### kovita — 2014-12-25T12:16:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/about-my-chronic-insomniac-daughter.12447/post-150971>

i have a friend with thee same issues daughter. Not sleeping for many years now aged 13. Resistant to tryptophan and melatonin attempts to treat the condition. Anecdotically I do not recall any non sleeping friend while a child and nowadays I hear this story more and more often. I am convinced the lights, emf etc...the usual story is the culprit.

### Terminator — 2014-12-27T18:01:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/about-my-chronic-insomniac-daughter.12447/post-151054>

The issue may be poor electrical wiring in the home. This means high voltage / magnetic fields around sleep area. These can be far worse than cell / cordless phones, wifi, etc.

Dr. Kruse had this issue in his home in Nashville.

Part of the fix is to turn off the circuit breaker at least in bedrooms.

A simple voltage meter can confirm this. I had this issue in my home and had it partially fixed and I turn off all circuit breakers except fridge and furnace at night. This can make a huge difference in reduction of electron loss.

### Hansen Kenimer — 2014-12-29T21:09:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/about-my-chronic-insomniac-daughter.12447/post-151150>

She's 18. Poor wiring is highly likely...the house was built in 1930.

### Hansen Kenimer — 2014-12-29T21:22:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/about-my-chronic-insomniac-daughter.12447/post-151151>

> [Terminator said:](/goto/post?id=151054)
>
> The issue may be poor electrical wiring in the home. This means high voltage / magnetic fields around sleep area. These can be far worse than cell / cordless phones, wifi, etc.
>
> Dr. Kruse had this issue in his home in Nashville.
>
> Part of the fix is to turn off the circuit breaker at least in bedrooms.
>
> A simple voltage meter can confirm this. I had this issue in my home and had it partially fixed and I turn off all circuit breakers except fridge and furnace at night. This can make a huge difference in reduction of electron loss.

It just occurred to me that all of the outlets in her room are ungrounded. Hmmm.....in my room, too. Shoot, I think everywhere but in the kitchen. Is this relevant? I know nothing about electrical wiring!

### Terminator — 2014-12-31T19:43:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/about-my-chronic-insomniac-daughter.12447/post-151254>

> [Hansen Kenimer said:](/goto/post?id=151151)
>
> It just occurred to me that all of the outlets in her room are ungrounded. Hmmm.....in my room, too. Shoot, I think everywhere but in the kitchen. Is this relevant? I know nothing about electrical wiring!

Ungrounded outlets is a major issue if the wires don't have the ground wire (meaning 3 wires total in one line). You will have high voltage & amgnetic fields in your home ! Just buy a cheap digital volt meter to confirm. Let us know what you find if you do check.

I measured my home before the fix and found up to 7 volts just standing in the middle of a room ! My 11 year old daughter had sleep issues also ! She's sleeping all night now.

# Thread: Neurofibromatosis type 1
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/neurofibromatosis-type-1.12460/>
**Subforum:** Success Stories
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Bob Smith — 2014-12-24T22:54:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/neurofibromatosis-type-1.12460/post-150938>

Dr. Kruse, Thank you very much for sharing your amazing insights.

Through internet searches I finally tied my correct symptoms together. I'm pretty sure I have neurofibromatosis-1 (NF-1). My problems with diagnosis surround the fact that I've been progressively cutting back on specific carbs for almost 30 years .....fructose, then lactose, polyphenol dyes, wheat, polyphenol dyes, cheese, nightshades, and legumes. I did so because of recurrent autoimmune symptoms, headache and neck ache.

This progression, along with exercise, caused a subsidence of most of the symptoms, such as skin discoloration, associated with most cases of NF-1. I've been beating back the metabolic symptoms with a stick.

Elimination of wheat in this progression happened in 2007 after news spread that Dr. Fasano had discovered zonalin and leaky gut. As I discovered progressively more proteins which caused me autoimmune trouble other scientists discovered that 1) the zonulin switch remains open in autoimmune types like me, and 2) wheat doesn't directly elicit zonulin. The bad gut bacteria fostered by wheat and many other carbs causes zonulin release.

I've been hell-bent on eating "safe" starches while chasing down offending carb proteins. It hasn't worked. Finally the realization that NF-1 tumors are slowly permeating my neurons and nerve endings has caused me to invoke cancer dieting .....starving the tumors of sugar. NF-1 might explain why my immune system has been in overdrive.

My pertinent symptoms are tiny bumpy tumors around my feet, but extending everywhere, a dime-size darkened turmor and a couple of scaly patches on my back, right-headache and neck ache, ringing and "whooshing" in my right ear, sinusitis around my right eye, left-side nose bleed, occasionally slightly blurred vision, and a kidney stone about four years ago. Before some carb protein eliminations there were other transient symptoms, like vision discoloration in my right eye. I'm guessing my right optic nerve has one or more neurofibromas which are causing adema roughly around where it passes by my hypothalamus.

I read your Organizational Structural Failure # 5 "Prions Are Compliant Design Flaws" [http://jackkruse.com/organizational-structural-failure-5-prions-are-compliant-design-flaws/](http://jackkruse.com/organizational-structural-failure-5-prions-are-compliant-design-flaws/)

It points to leaky gut and prion protein transport to the fourth ventricle via swolen vagus nerves. It's apparent that I have had a lot of swelling and associated Arnold Chiari related neck pain. I started drastically decreasing all remaining carbs about two weeks ago, and it seems to be working well. I'm down to about 20 grams per day, and I suspect those will have to go.

Thanks for helping me sort this out.

### nonchalant — 2014-12-25T03:52:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/neurofibromatosis-type-1.12460/post-150955>

Hi Bob, welcome to the forum. Can you post a pic for your account?
How is your sleep?

### Bob Smith — 2014-12-25T16:04:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/neurofibromatosis-type-1.12460/post-150977>

> [nonchalant said:](/goto/post?id=150955)
>
> Hi Bob, welcome to the forum. Can you post a pic for your account?
> How is your sleep?

Hi Nonchalant. My sleep was growing uncontrollably erratic. It's gotten much better. I forgot to mention that I had a butterfly rash, indicating advancement in autoimmune disease. That's subsiding now along with the NF-1 polyps.

I just did a web search on "NF1 vagus nerve" and the whole first page was filled with case reports of malignant tumors.

# Thread: Vision effects
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vision-effects.12478/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 23
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### nonchalant — 2014-12-28T02:39:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vision-effects.12478/post-151078>

Early this morning, soon after awakening, I noticed a vision sensation. I was still in bed, with my eyes closed, and I noticed a ring of light around my peripheral vision that got smaller and moved toward the center of my vision and vanished. Then it was replaced by another circle, and so forth. Like the reverse of most of the standing wave videos I've seen. The ring was white to light grey light on a dark grey background. It looked like a smoke ring, because its shape changed slightly as it moved toward the center. Sometimes the light was fairly bright. Each ring lasted about 5 seconds. This continued for about 5 minutes, I suppose. It was fascinating.

But for the last minute or two, the rings gradually lost their coherence. Instead of decreasing steadily in size until it vanished, some parts would lag, distorting the circle, but it would all come together at the last instant. Finally, though, I just got blobs of light with some movement, but no organization. The light was still bright, but wacky. More like a lava lamp than a standing wave. Then the show was over.

Thinking back, I realized that a little before this neat stuff, I had opened my eyes briefly to see if the sun was up or not. I guessed that sunrise was 30-45 minutes away, so I closed my eyes again. That's it. The whole show resulted from a couple seconds of low light.

I have seen this before, but I didn't recognize the potential so I disregarded it, or opened my eyes and spoiled the effect.

This sensation could be similar to the fluttering I saw before, just in slow motion.
[http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/kruses-theorum.11382/page-3](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/kruses-theorum.11382/page-3)
The fluttering came from longer exposure to brighter light.

So were these waves from the retina headed towards the optic nerve? Visual Purple and all that?

### Martin — 2014-12-28T15:51:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vision-effects.12478/post-151084>

Fascinating. I've gotten "ceiling fans" spinning in my peripheral vision in the morning. Rotating and squished up into a corner of my vision. Color was smokey. Lasted a few minutes, if that.

NSH

### nonchalant — 2014-12-28T16:51:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vision-effects.12478/post-151085>

> [Martin said:](/goto/post?id=151084)
>
> Fascinating. I've gotten "ceiling fans" spinning in my peripheral vision in the morning. Rotating and squished up into a corner of my vision. Color was smokey. Lasted a few minutes, if that.
>
> NSH

Interesting, Martin. Sounds like a vortex. Actually it sounds like the fluttering I see occasionally. But I've had my eyes open, so I wouldn't have seen the smoke, just whatever is in front of me, with a visual effect to it.
I think the most important variable, for me, is that it is morning. Everything is closer together then. But wait, that's when cortisol is running rampant... Maybe my vision is freshly repaired then?

One thing I forgot to mention, I had my closed eyes pointed towards the dim light yesterday morning. That may have helped me see it.

### Shijin13 — 2014-12-28T17:24:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vision-effects.12478/post-151090>

> [nonchalant said:](/goto/post?id=151078)
>
> Early this morning, soon after awakening, I noticed a vision sensation. I was still in bed, with my eyes closed, and I noticed a ring of light around my peripheral vision that got smaller and moved toward the center of my vision and vanished. Then it was replaced by another circle, and so forth. Like the reverse of most of the standing wave videos I've seen. The ring was white to light grey light on a dark grey background. It looked like a smoke ring, because its shape changed slightly as it moved toward the center. Sometimes the light was fairly bright. Each ring lasted about 5 seconds. This continued for about 5 minutes, I suppose. It was fascinating.
>
> But for the last minute or two, the rings gradually lost their coherence. Instead of decreasing steadily in size until it vanished, some parts would lag, distorting the circle, but it would all come together at the last instant. Finally, though, I just got blobs of light with some movement, but no organization. The light was still bright, but wacky. More like a lava lamp than a standing wave. Then the show was over.
>
> Thinking back, I realized that a little before this neat stuff, I had opened my eyes briefly to see if the sun was up or not. I guessed that sunrise was 30-45 minutes away, so I closed my eyes again. That's it. The whole show resulted from a couple seconds of low light.
>
> I have seen this before, but I didn't recognize the potential so I disregarded it, or opened my eyes and spoiled the effect.
>
> This sensation could be similar to the fluttering I saw before, just in slow motion.
> [http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/kruses-theorum.11382/page-3](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/kruses-theorum.11382/page-3)
> The fluttering came from longer exposure to brighter light.
>
> So were these waves from the retina headed towards the optic nerve? Visual Purple and all that?

Sally I experience these when I'm going to sleep.... and they run counter clockwise when it happens....

### Jude — 2014-12-29T00:15:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vision-effects.12478/post-151106>

Have a similar story...this time last yr developed a detached vitreous humor in left eye with significent bleeding ..left with large dark floaters occluding vision.
11mths later my dr can't believe the difference in my floaters ..have reduced in size and colour..vision has improved so much thank goodness....he even commented that my long standing corneal scar has almost disappeared ..really had to go look for it.....
He said he supposed I was still following JK!
Daily tinned sardines and salmon does the trick as well!

### cinnamon — 2014-12-29T04:10:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vision-effects.12478/post-151113>

This past summer I noticed some interesting vision effects with reflective materials.

The first time it happened, I was walking toward a stop sign. I don't think there was anything special about this stop sign - I've walked past it many times before and since. But that afternoon it looked huge and glowing. I could see the pattern of reflective coating that made up the red part of the sign. I had never really thought about why you can see stop signs at night with your headlights until that moment! LOL I walked right up to it and stared. It was afternoon and the sun was behind me, shining toward the sign.

After that, I noticed many times when I was out walking around that the white paint used for the pedestrian crossing lanes at intersections is reflective. I had never noticed before, but now I often found it glowing brightly.

### Sue-UK — 2014-12-29T10:54:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vision-effects.12478/post-151124>

I've been playing card games with the family over Christmas and DD downloaded an app for me to learn the rules of a new game. On Saturday evening as an experiment I played using the app on the ipad late into the evening, switching off just before I went to bed. Got into bed, room very dark and closed my eyes..... For several minutes I could see circles of playing cards going round and round .....  Really showed me the effect of technology on vision  Last night was computer technology off as soon as it got dark and blue blockers on. Remembered my dreams this morning

It did look like ceiling fan flutter, but in all "corners" of my vision. I closed my eyes and watched (they lasted only a minute or so) but I didn't detect any spin. \[If I turned toward the light with my eyes closed, they disappeared, only to reappear when I turned back towards the dark room.\] No rotation on their own axis, or about the center of my vision. ### nonchalant — 2015-01-15T00:25:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vision-effects.12478/post-152773>

A couple mornings ago I got another light show behind my eyelids. But this time the peripheral light ring got 'stuck' at the 10 o'clock position. The rest of the light band would contract towards the center of vision, but not that part. Then I noticed a clockwise sweep of light, like from a weather radar screen, that seemed to solve the problem. Over and over it happened, the same way each time. I was happy to see the rotation, which was a first for me.

But since I had read Tensegrity 13, I just had to try something. I quickly moved my closed eyes back and forth once. Then I watched for more smoke rings. But they would not reappear.  The movement had killed the show. I didn't even see any lava-lamp type effects.

### nonchalant — 2015-03-15T22:36:30+0200

That leads to another question. Does one have a 'dominant eye' (or dominant hand) if they lack energy to coordinate the vision from both eyes? Do we rely mostly on one eye to reduce chaos? Should ambidexterity be the norm for humans?

# Thread: Neurological conditions can cause constipation in newborns?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/neurological-conditions-can-cause-constipation-in-newborns.12490/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Kids
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Lucian Toma — 2014-12-29T21:44:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/neurological-conditions-can-cause-constipation-in-newborns.12490/post-151152>

My two mounths newborn cannot passing his poo only with a glycerin suppository. Strange that are not hard. But after that, he has some seizures and agitation which last for many hours. He is fed with expresed milk mainly and with soy formula sometimes. Can the gut brain to control the defecation and because my wife has a C section does he not have an apropiate gut flora and his nervos system is overloaded?

What can be done in this case? Thank you

### Jude — 2014-12-30T04:02:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/neurological-conditions-can-cause-constipation-in-newborns.12490/post-151163>

Hi Lucian, just hope you have been reading all of the early JK blogs...looks like mum and bub are going to need your knowledge to get out of this difficulty....modern medicine will offer you pharmaceuticals to alleviate condition......but this is the best place to learn how to reverse engineer all health problems and in particular your babes.
Do you have JK's book..The epi-paleo prescription?

Your baby needs tons of breast milk and no soy ...ever....feed mum up on lots of good fatty fish...salmon, sardines,oysters to support good milk supply...JK recommends breastfeeding for 2yrs.
I know it's a lot to take in ...keep asking questions
Lots of folk here with tons of info ...keep us up to date re bub

### nonchalant — 2014-12-30T05:00:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/neurological-conditions-can-cause-constipation-in-newborns.12490/post-151166>

Welcome, Lucian. I have to wonder about your baby's circadian cycle. And his mother's, also. We normally have higher levels of cortisol in the morning. The rising sun, bright in our eyes, causes a huge spike in cortisol, which helps set the circadian rhythm. It also increases our energy levels and affects many other hormone levels.

I had some constipation problems a couple years ago, and I found that just looking towards the sun, or even at just blue sky, would help the situation in a few minutes. If you are in the northern hemisphere, your wife might be reluctant to take the herself and the baby outside very often. But I think it would be helpful.

### Penny — 2015-01-01T07:14:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/neurological-conditions-can-cause-constipation-in-newborns.12490/post-151295>

Hi Lucian - too many glutamates - like in soy/wheat/dairy/processed food - causes excitotoxicity in the brain (migraines/seizures) - avoid glutamates and it's smooth sailing - you can also use slippery elm to soften the stool - and a good prebiotic and probiotic - your kid didn't get mom's flora - not good - who knows what he picked up in the hospital -

get this probiotic:
[http://www.amazon.com/ProBiota-ProB...id=1420088115&sr=8-32&keywords=seeking+health](http://www.amazon.com/ProBiota-ProBiotic-Absorbs-Quickly-Dairy-Free/dp/B003RY51GO/ref=sr_1_32?ie=UTF8&qid=1420088115&sr=8-32&keywords=seeking+health)
and this prebiotic which has galactooligosacharides that feed bifida bacteria - the predominant bacteria in a newborn:
[http://www.amazon.com/ProBiota-Immu...&sr=8-1-spell&keywords=seeking+health+prebiot](http://www.amazon.com/ProBiota-Immune-grams-Seeking-Health/dp/B004JCL3E2/ref=sr_1_sc_1?ie=UTF8&qid=1420088207&sr=8-1-spell&keywords=seeking+health+prebiot)
also - watch these videos for what horrors glutamates to to us:
[http://unblindmymind.org/](http://unblindmymind.org/)
They also have a glutamate list at the above site - glutamates are put in all processed food because they "tickle" your neurons and make them think the food tastes good and make you want more of it... then the neuron dies...

The opposite of glutamates is GABA - this affects the motility of the colon -

This is why wheat is a cork Happy New Year

Also, if you want to know the exact flora of your child, go to the [www.americangut.org](http://www.americangut.org) project and they will get the DNA of your child's gut flora so you can see what is in there...it's only $99.00 and you can do it from many parts of the globe

Not sure if mom eats glutamates if it transfers to the baby, but look it up and report back to us

# Thread: Productivity Tools and Tips to help deconstructing the Blogs
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/productivity-tools-and-tips-to-help-deconstructing-the-blogs.12519/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### PaulG — 2015-01-05T03:42:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/productivity-tools-and-tips-to-help-deconstructing-the-blogs.12519/post-151637>

Please can we share any tools or tips we use to help when reading/researching the [Jack Kruse Blogs](http://jackkruse.com/).

Personally, when reading the JK blogs (or anyone internet research session) I typically end up with many many browser tabs as I get easily taken off task due to my inquisitive nature and I am finding as a consequence my attention and powers of concentration diminishes. Good book on this phenomena is [The Shallows - What the Internet is doing to our brains](http://www.nytimes.com/2010/06/06/books/review/Lehrer-t.html?_r=0)

I think perhaps the issue may be that when I open a new tab, my brain interprets this as follows
1) The information on the previous tab is not so important and consider the topic 'closed'
2) The new tab is far more important/interesting so direct attention there and 'forget' or at least de-prioritize previous context.

This is not conducive to learning and in my opinion promotes a form of attention deficit

In terms of the JK blogs there are a lot of technical terms and phrases that I would love to have little popup reminder windows to jog my memory what it is and how it relates to what has been written/discussed before (WIKI/Glossary alert). Ten minutes of googling and I found [ezLinkPreview](https://chrome.google.com/webstore/detail/ezlinkpreview/nnkcfbiefgdaceeplickkkmifpicbpcc) for Desktop Chrome and [Firefox CoolPreviews](https://addons.mozilla.org/en-US/firefox/addon/coolpreviews/) that just might solve this problem for me and others.

So I would be interested in learning how others go about tackling the blogs, my methodology is failing me as the blogs get more detailed. (being honest ).

**Good**
taking notes like @**[yewwei.tan](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?members/yewwei-tan.6824/)**
dual monitors
Printing out article (Though can no longer do this)
Setting aside dedicated quiet time (2hrs)

**Bad**
Using an IPad/tablet
Skim reading and saying you will get back to it later

### Josh (Paleo Osteo) — 2015-01-05T04:13:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/productivity-tools-and-tips-to-help-deconstructing-the-blogs.12519/post-151640>

i have previously printed and bound whole series into a booklet, and i use a highlighter for main points.

### PaulG — 2015-01-05T04:20:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/productivity-tools-and-tips-to-help-deconstructing-the-blogs.12519/post-151642>

Using the ezLinkPreview Chrome extension now and you can highlight some text and search in wiki - which is way cool..

### Josh (Paleo Osteo) — 2015-01-05T04:27:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/productivity-tools-and-tips-to-help-deconstructing-the-blogs.12519/post-151645>

pretty cool feature but i agree, it will ruin the flow

### Josh (Paleo Osteo) — 2015-01-05T04:28:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/productivity-tools-and-tips-to-help-deconstructing-the-blogs.12519/post-151646>

if it had a highlight ctrl function where it came up with summary definition on same page in a box that would be l33t tho

### Lahelada — 2015-01-05T11:43:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/productivity-tools-and-tips-to-help-deconstructing-the-blogs.12519/post-151673>

I was reading an article recently that your brain learns best when you teach it that what it is being fed is important. So to my mind reading on screen is too easy. Print off, use highlighters,highlight in red what is important,another color the words concepts you had to look up. Write in the margin in a condensed form what you found. Re read as if you are cramming for an exam.

### SeaHorse — 2015-01-05T17:42:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/productivity-tools-and-tips-to-help-deconstructing-the-blogs.12519/post-151688>

I think it is really such an individual thing…my daughter has to write out what she wants to remember…the act of writing is key….I like to print out things and have them in a concrete form. I hate reading on the computer, but I do end up doing that for some blogs….others I've printed. I'm very grateful for Kirakitty's pdf. formatting.

It is staggering to think about the amount of writing JK has released through the blogs and forum…..

I really like the idea of the wiki format for ongoing research and easier access for newcomers. I like the idea of somebody being attracted to the quantum stuff and working backwards towards an understanding of hormones, Vit D etc etc…..not everybody needs to be inched towards the quantum world in the way the blog was unfolded. There are a lot of energy medicine people who are right there at the end line, but don't know how to explain what they do or why. It's all good however you come to the realization that our health is a creative, intuitive dance between the energy we call our bodies and the energy we call the environment.

### QuantumRadical — 2015-01-05T20:41:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/productivity-tools-and-tips-to-help-deconstructing-the-blogs.12519/post-151713>

I hope Dr. Kruse's next book compiles all of this great information into one volume. The world needs a good textbook on Quantum Biology & It's Implications for Human Health.

Randy

### yewwei.tan — 2015-01-05T21:29:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/productivity-tools-and-tips-to-help-deconstructing-the-blogs.12519/post-151725>

When I was a budding coder, the best advice that I got came from Joe Armstrong

Siednote: Armstrong is usually credited as one of the creators of the Erlang programming language, and wrote the first Abstract Machine for the language. Erlang and the BEAM, and now OTP (open telecomms platform) are what powers all the network switches of Ericsson, which support literally billions of phone communications. Erlang/OTP is my platform of choice for building robust, scalable, and highly-available systems.
​

His advice was to pick up a programming book, and type out every line of code word-for-word, while writing your own comments.

In the case of Jack's blogs, writing/copying chunks of text and editing the grammar  literally "makes the text your own".

### Jonathin — 2015-01-05T21:31:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/productivity-tools-and-tips-to-help-deconstructing-the-blogs.12519/post-151726>

I've copied and reformated every blog in Word, printed, re-read and manually highlighted whatever seems most significant at the moment. Working on my 3rd thick binder. I make sure all the cites are active links that I can click on them (Ctrl-K in Word). Any downloadable links are downloaded. Things that are not directly downloadable I highlight and copy using UnHMT for Firefox \[[http://www.unmht.org/unmht/en\_index.html\]](http://www.unmht.org/unmht/en_index.html]). This way I have later access to these documents saved on the hard drive. Some longer docs are printed for careful reading. I have also saved all wikipedia entries to folder that I've looked up at some point relevant to biology, physics and chemistry so that I can quick reference for some forgotten factoid.

### ssj3 — 2015-01-06T00:01:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/productivity-tools-and-tips-to-help-deconstructing-the-blogs.12519/post-151751>

If you create a pdf of the blog post and have the correct setting ticked in Adobe you will be able to generate a summary page of everything you highlight.

### Jude — 2015-01-06T00:13:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/productivity-tools-and-tips-to-help-deconstructing-the-blogs.12519/post-151753>

> [Jonathin said:](/goto/post?id=151726)
>
> I've copied and reformated every blog in Word, printed, re-read and manually highlighted whatever seems most significant at the moment. Working on my 3rd thick binder. I make sure all the cites are active links that I can click on them (Ctrl-K in Word). Any downloadable links are downloaded. Things that are not directly downloadable I highlight and copy using UnHMT for Firefox \[[http://www.unmht.org/unmht/en\_index.html\]](http://www.unmht.org/unmht/en_index.html]). This way I have later access to these documents saved on the hard drive. Some longer docs are printed for careful reading. I have also saved all wikipedia entries to folder that I've looked up at some point relevant to biology, physics and chemistry so that I can quick reference for some forgotten factoid.

That is so impressive Jonathin...am hoping, like Quantam R, that JK's book is going to be the THE volume to go to!

# Thread: Doc uses ZRT labs, calling for my list soon, don't know what to request
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/doc-uses-zrt-labs-calling-for-my-list-soon-dont-know-what-to-request.12527/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 17
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Hope2Learn — 2015-01-05T19:52:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/doc-uses-zrt-labs-calling-for-my-list-soon-dont-know-what-to-request.12527/post-151705>

Hi all.. I have a very detailed journal over in the journals forum which gives specifics. [http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/nowhere-to-go-but-up.12501/](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/nowhere-to-go-but-up.12501/) But here's the bottom line. My doc referred me to a compounding pharmacy whose pharmacist does patient consults regarding BHRT. Then the pharmacist faxes the recommendations back to my doc, who will write prescriptions for me based on the consult.

The pharmacist is calling me back soon to get a list of labs I want run. He uses ZRT labs. [https://store.zrtlab.com/](https://store.zrtlab.com/)

I'm 42, female, reason for going is for BHRT purposes. No sex drive, poor sleep (already reduced EMF greatly, blue blockers /blue light issues being worked on as we speak, only one cell tower within four miles of my home, etc). Thyroid tests always at the bottom-end of normal.

(TSH Was last done in August.. they always seem to pick and choose one or another of them to do. Haven't had any of the others done in years probably. I have an appointment to see my GP on Thursday regarding sleep issues and long-standing fatigue that he is already treating me for, and I hope he will be able to get my thyroid and vitamin D and maybe even adrenals tested/covered by insurance so that I only need ZRT to do my sex hormones and 'oddball' stuff you all may recommend).

_\[August TSH labs:
TSH- normal range: 0.47-4.68 uIU/mL - Mine:2.03 \]
\[they did a lipid profile the same day - here are those results in case they're helpful - normal range is in parentheses and my result is afterwards:

Cholesterol(<200 mg/dL)150
HDL Cholesterol(35-60 mg/dL)41
Triglycerides(<150 mg/dL)110
LDL Cholesterol(<100 md/dl)87
Cholesterol / Hdl Ratio3.7
LDL / HDL Ratio2.1 \]_

I really don't know what I need them to order for me, and normally they don't even ORDER any labs, she said the pharmacist usually knows by what a woman's symptoms are, what she will need. I didn't feel comfortable with that though, I want info to start with.. so time is of the essence. No matter how fast I try to read (and I have been reading almost non-stop here for over a week now), no matter if I have podcasts running all day while everyone is at work and school (as I have been for several days), I still need just a bit of help here because he's calling back faster than I can find the information I need.

And the tests are expensive so I can only order what I truly need. I will not skimp, but it must be justified thoroughly.

Can ANYONE help me based on what ZRT labs offers? They have a lot of different test to choose from and I just don't know where to start.

[https://store.zrtlab.com/](https://store.zrtlab.com/)

Thank you so much.. I will gladly pay it forward in any way I can, for now I already started by ordering a second copy of Jack's book for my oldest son, who is away from home right now.

Thanks again, and for more info please check out my journal:
[http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/nowhere-to-go-but-up.12501/](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/nowhere-to-go-but-up.12501/)

### caroline — 2015-01-06T00:39:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/doc-uses-zrt-labs-calling-for-my-list-soon-dont-know-what-to-request.12527/post-151759>

an ASI - saliva test ...cortisol x 4 \[diurnal\] ......great start for now and not too expensive - my opinion btw!
this one tests hormones and cortisol.....
cortisol is important to tell us how the Rolex in our brain is working

you may want to test melatonin ........

### Josh (Paleo Osteo) — 2015-01-06T07:32:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/doc-uses-zrt-labs-calling-for-my-list-soon-dont-know-what-to-request.12527/post-151796>

hey
i use zrt labs for hormones/thyroid and ASI
these are the only real ones i know from them, they are saliva and blood spot tests.

i would do the blood spot for the hormones including thyroid $350 here in Aus
also the salivary ASI (maybe $140 here in Aus)

you can also tick various additions on the requisition form

### Josh (Paleo Osteo) — 2015-01-06T07:33:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/doc-uses-zrt-labs-calling-for-my-list-soon-dont-know-what-to-request.12527/post-151797>

multi test kit might be the go

### Hope2Learn — 2015-01-06T16:48:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/doc-uses-zrt-labs-calling-for-my-list-soon-dont-know-what-to-request.12527/post-151821>

ASI is adrenal stress index? They don't call it that there but pretty sure thats' what it is.

I'm having real trouble figuring this out because I am going to see my GP on Thursday (day after tomorrow) to see if he can order any tests for me and get my insurance to cover them. I don't know which ones he will order and which ones he won't. So I don't know what to order myself!

I find it likely that he can and will order all the thyroid stuff for me because he has before. I can't imagine him saying no to vitamin d testing either. But I don't know what adrenal testing consists of. Can he just send me for fasting bloodwork the next morning and have them do that or is it different? All my doctors tests will be bloodwork.

I don't have any cash on hand so anything I order will have to go on credit and I really hate to do that. I feel like I Should forgo testing and just trust the pharmacist to throw stuff at me based on a telephone interview of my symptoms, because I can maybe afford that. But maybe not.. I guess whatever they prescribe I will have to pay cash for...? What does BHRT cost anyway?

so many questions and nobody to ask... no sisters, mom passed away ten years ago, no friends to speak of... \*sigh\*

I know I want my hormones tested and DHEA and DHA. And cortisol maybe? But I don't understand cortisol... everyone says it's important to have enough in the morning but then say it's bad because it's related to stress. So am I not supposed to stress in the morning? Because i thought you WANTED cortisol in the morning so I do my bills and stuff at that point and spend all morning in a crappy stressed out mood.. but see, you all say you want cortisol in the morning right?

Likewise I read articles about people who are like me, over 40 and with glaucoma, and it says they have BAD eyes because they DON"T let in blue light. So.. I thought I was supposed to never have blue light, ever. No lightbulbs, use blueblockers, etc. But now I read that my eyes a re bad because they DON"T LET blue light in!

I am so confused. I only ever found this website because I googled BHRT and landed in a monster thread all about it, so I signed up. Now I find that Jack has a whole thing about eating special, special EMF limits, all kinds of things I never head of before and never had a concern about.

Whic his GREAT and I'm incorporating much of it into my own life because it reads sensibly and seems legit... but it has led to overwhelming information overload and has made it so that now I don't know where to start. It had seemed so simple at first.. I'm constantly a bitch, so I need more hormones. Well okay that's counter intuitive, I've always lamented that I need LESS hormones because I'm so impossible. But now I think I need more because... no sex drive.

Oh I am just so confused and sad this morning at the fact that when the pharmacist calls all I can really tell him is please don't order any test for me because I don't know what I need yet. Some days I feel like I'm making progress and other days it hits me that I have changed nothing ,except I now spend MORE time on the couch on the computer, because I'm reading here and trying to learn. But I haven't. I still don't know what to do about BHRT or testing. On one hand my days have been less boring and more fulfilling because I found some things that sound interesting to try, which should keep me busy buut on the other hand they're more depressing and frustrating because I'm not getting anywhere, and now I'm more confused than ever. I thought I'd just ask to be put on the pill, because that should be completely balanced hormones right?

But now I don't know anymore after reading here.

Most people sign up here, I bet, because they wanted to lose weight and had tried everything and then decided to try Jack's book/diet/Rxes. So I can understand why everyone is so quick to advise me to do all the stuff they do. But my goals aren't the same.... so I feel like I don't know how to pick and choose what to do now. My n=1 is different, which I initially thought would be a great fit here but now I just.. well.. I'm used to being the smart one. Not so much here. I don't like that feeling.

hopefully the day will improve from here. This isn't like me. bleh.

### Hope2Learn — 2015-01-06T18:44:03+0200

Oh boy. I need to apologize... I'm so sorry for being in such a mood this morning. I surely shouldn't have had such an outburst. I'm sorry.

I have since turned off the laptop (wifi connection + blue light from wake up to bed time has to be the worst thing for me yet I just seem to have completely not even thought about that until now.. OMG can't see the forest for the trees... now that some trees are systematically being knocked down and hauled out of the way, the forest suddenly is so much more obvious. \*sigh\* live and learn.. know better, then do better... I have many ethernet cables and will dig one out once I'm done posting this).. (and holy run-on sentence, Batman!).. and showered, which always makes me feel a million times better, and realized the apology was due, then asked myself - why was I so positive yesterday and not today?

Yesterday morning I had steak for breakfast with raw greens and cucumber.

This morning I threw two slices of cinnamon raisin bread from the (discount) grocery store into the toaster and then used "light margarine spread" on them, then added on a banana.

...

Ya think this is it?

(could also be that my almost-blind, diabetic, 13 year old rat terrier, Daisy, had a warm and fragrant "accident" on the floor right in front of me this morning quite deliberately because I told her "not right now" when she wanted to go out. Touche!)

here is a picture of a tanked ASI (adrenal fatigue)
you want it high in the AM as it is what wakes you up
it should follow like a bell curve skewed to the left (high am, tapering away to hardly anything at night)
if it is high late day or night time it indicates cortisol is raised due to a stressor etc
cortisol is magical when it works properly, we NEED it
it saves our lives in times of stress, but it gives your doc a good idea of where the stressors are when it is spiked everywhere or flat like the one above which is one of my patients

# Thread: Affect of a ketogenic diet on performance
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/affect-of-a-ketogenic-diet-on-performance.12528/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### justin potier — 2015-01-05T21:57:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/affect-of-a-ketogenic-diet-on-performance.12528/post-151728>

When talking about the performance gains associated with a ketogenic style diet such as the vo2 max gain after 2 to 3 years of utilizing the diet how much of a gain is this? considering the average vo2 max for an athletic person is 50-60 would the increase be minor such as a two point increase or would it be a large increase such as 20-30 points bringing someone to the level of cyclings greats like armstrong and indurain who both had vo2 max's of above 80? I heard your vo2 max is largely genetic which is what made me think about this. and if this has not been studied, scientifically speaking what type of gain should be seen?

### Shijin13 — 2015-01-05T22:49:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/affect-of-a-ketogenic-diet-on-performance.12528/post-151736>

Welcome @[justin potier](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/12037/) Start with this thread here: Barry nicely sums up the answer across this epic thread [http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/fasted-state-training-adaptations.297/](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/fasted-state-training-adaptations.297/)

### sjoshua — 2015-01-05T22:53:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/affect-of-a-ketogenic-diet-on-performance.12528/post-151738>

Hey Justin, welcome to the site!

As you explore, you'll probably notice that the mindset around here is that genetics are more of a potential blueprint, per se, where true expression of said blueprint is determined by the entire lifestyle and environment of the individual - i.e. epi-genetics. This makes it possible for situations like two identical twins to each be unique - because it is impossible for them to experience the same exact environmental conditions, even if they were always within inches of each other for their entire lifetimes. But, this also makes predicting others' results quite difficult, since the environmental conditions are quite improbably or likely impossible to replicate...

What VO2Max 'YOU' are able to achieve is so unique that the only real way to determine is to try it for yourself. I realize you are likely looking for some ballpark gauge, but I'd suggest that this would be almost useless considering it may be worlds below your individual potential, or worlds above - and setting the bar in the wrong place or making choices about health based on a specific metric instead of looking at the entire picture may be detrimental in the long term... Sure, elite athletes may choose to focus on bettering times, lifts, or other max outputs, but it is worth noting that elite athletes perform at elite levels for short periods of time, NOT their entire lives. They may choose to sacrifice none, little, some, or all of their long-term optimal potential for the short term elite athletic endeavor, but unless you are pushing into elite athletics I'd suggest a better pursuit for us regular folk would be to focus on overall optimal health.

Also, ketogenic diets are not all equal. Drinking canola oil would be ketogenic by definition, as would drinking pastured pork fat.... but which would you want to do? The difference in how our body reacts to these two situations is the important part. Here is a blog that talks directly to this: [http://jackkruse.com/organization-structural-failure-8-ketosis-appears-fail/](http://jackkruse.com/organization-structural-failure-8-ketosis-appears-fail/) - but that may not be a good starting point for you to dive in. This may be a better stepping stone to find information you desire: [http://jackkruse.com/getting-started-guide/](http://jackkruse.com/getting-started-guide/)

Hope this helps
